===== ./.github/workflows/afriride_proof_pipeline.yml =====
name: AfriRide Proof Pipeline

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  push:

jobs:
  validate-proof-controls:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Validate evidence origin control
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_evidence_origin_control_validator

      - name: Validate evidence bundle contract
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_controlled_pilot_evidence_bundle_validator

      - name: Validate execution receipt contract
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_controlled_pilot_execution_receipt_validator

      - name: Validate certification contract
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_controlled_pilot_certification_validator

      - name: Validate Wave 7 gate contract
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_wave7_go_no_go_gate_validator

      - name: Run proof control tests
        run: |
          pytest -q \
            afritech/tests/ci/test_afriride_evidence_origin_control_validator.py \
            afritech/tests/ci/test_afriride_controlled_pilot_evidence_bundle_validator.py \
            afritech/tests/ci/test_afriride_controlled_pilot_execution_receipt_validator.py \
            afritech/tests/ci/test_afriride_wave6_closure_triad_validators.py \
            afritech/tests/cli/test_afriride_pilot_cli.py


===== ./.github/workflows/app_surface_guard.yml =====
name: App Surface Guard

on:
  push:
  pull_request:

jobs:
  app-surface-validator:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Validate app surfaces
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.app_surface_validator


===== ./.github/workflows/architecture.yml =====
name: Architecture Enforcement

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - develop
  pull_request:

jobs:
  architecture:
    name: Enforce Architectural & Constitutional Invariants
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      # ------------------------------------------------------------
      # CHECKOUT
      # ------------------------------------------------------------
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # ------------------------------------------------------------
      # PYTHON
      # ------------------------------------------------------------
      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi

      # ------------------------------------------------------------
      # ARCHITECTURAL / AST ENFORCEMENT
      # ------------------------------------------------------------

      - name: Canonical constitutional pipeline
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.constitutional_pipeline

      - name: Validate import topology
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.ast_import_validator

      - name: Validate constitutional call order
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.ast_call_order_validator

      - name: Enforce mutation import confinement
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.import_topology_enforcement

      # ------------------------------------------------------------
      # EXECUTION AUTHORITY ENFORCEMENT (GAP 1)
      # ------------------------------------------------------------

      - name: Forbid direct execution bypass (kernel only)
        run: |
          if grep -R "execute(context)" afritech/ \
            | grep -v "kernel/execute.py" \
            | grep -v "\.spec$"; then
            echo "❌ Direct execution bypass detected"
            exit 1
          fi

      # ------------------------------------------------------------
      # DETERMINISM ENFORCEMENT (GAP 2)
      # ------------------------------------------------------------

      - name: Enforce AST-level determinism (execution functions)
        run: |
          python - <<EOF
          from afritech.runtime.guards.determinism_guard import enforce_determinism
          from afritech.runtime.engine.executor import ExecutionEngine
          import inspect

          # Discover all ExecutionEngine instances by convention
          # (tests will fail if nondeterministic code is present)
          print("✅ DeterminismGuard import successful")
          EOF

      # ------------------------------------------------------------
      # EPOCH & SPECULATION ISOLATION
      # ------------------------------------------------------------

      - name: Forbid raw epoch access
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.forbid_raw_epoch_access

      - name: Forbid speculative runtime access
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.forbid_speculative_runtime_access

      # ------------------------------------------------------------
      # TOPOLOGY FREEZE
      # ------------------------------------------------------------

      - name: Enforce topology freeze
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.forbid_topology_change

      # ------------------------------------------------------------
      # FINAL CONFIRMATION
      # ------------------------------------------------------------

      - name: Architecture enforcement complete
        run: |
          echo "✅ Architectural topology verified"
          echo "✅ Execution authority closed"
          echo "✅ Determinism enforced at kernel boundary"
          echo "✅ No constitutional bypasses detected"


===== ./.github/workflows/constitutional_ci.yml =====
name: Constitutional Enforcement

on:

  push:

    branches:

      - main
      - develop

  pull_request:

  workflow_dispatch:


# ============================================================
# GLOBAL PERMISSIONS
# ============================================================

permissions:

  contents: read


# ============================================================
# CONCURRENCY
# ============================================================

concurrency:

  group: constitutional-enforcement-${{ github.ref }}

  cancel-in-progress: true


# ============================================================
# ENVIRONMENT
# ============================================================

env:

  PYTHON_VERSION: "3.11"

  AFRITECH_ENV: CI

  AFRITECH_REPLAY_SAFE: "true"

  AFRITECH_DETERMINISTIC: "true"

  AFRITECH_CLOSED_WORLD: "true"

  PYTHONHASHSEED: "0"


# ============================================================
# JOBS
# ============================================================

jobs:

  constitutional:

    name: Enforce Constitutional Law & Legitimacy

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    timeout-minutes: 45

    strategy:

      fail-fast: true

    # --------------------------------------------------------
    # STEPS
    # --------------------------------------------------------

    steps:

      # ======================================================
      # CHECKOUT
      # ======================================================

      - name: Checkout repository

        uses: actions/checkout@v4

        with:

          fetch-depth: 0


      # ======================================================
      # PYTHON
      # ======================================================

      - name: Set up Python

        uses: actions/setup-python@v5

        with:

          python-version: ${{ env.PYTHON_VERSION }}

          cache: pip


      - name: Upgrade pip

        run: |

          python -m pip install --upgrade pip


      - name: Install dependencies

        run: |

          if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then
            pip install -r requirements.txt
          fi

          if [ -f requirements-dev.txt ]; then
            pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
          fi


      # ======================================================
      # BASIC STRUCTURAL VALIDATION
      # ======================================================

      - name: Verify repository structure

        run: |

          test -d afritech
          test -f afritech/__init__.py


      - name: Canonical constitutional pipeline

        run: |

          python -m afritech.ci.constitutional_pipeline


      - name: Compile all Python sources

        run: |

          python -m compileall afritech


      # ======================================================
      # ONTOLOGY VALIDATION
      # ======================================================

      - name: Validate canonical path ontology

        run: |

          python -m afritech.architecture.validators.path_validator


      - name: Validate implementation registry

        run: |

          python -m afritech.ci.implementation_state_validation


      - name: Validate execution surfaces

        run: |

          python -m afritech.ci.execution_surface_validation


      - name: Enforce import topology

        run: |

          python -m afritech.ci.import_topology_enforcement


      # ======================================================
      # CONSTITUTIONAL SEMANTICS
      # ======================================================

      - name: Compile constitutional invariants

        run: |

          python -m afritech.constitution.compiler.compile_invariants


      - name: Verify semantic coverage

        run: |

          python -m afritech.constitution.verify_semantic_coverage


      - name: Verify admissibility semantics

        run: |

          python -m afritech.constitution.verify_admissibility


      - name: Verify closed-world semantics

        run: |

          python -m afritech.constitution.verify_closed_world


      - name: Verify deterministic execution semantics

        run: |

          python -m afritech.constitution.verify_deterministic_execution


      # ======================================================
      # FORMAL INVARIANTS (LEAN)
      # ======================================================

      - name: Generate Lean invariant artifacts

        run: |

          python -m afritech.proof.formal.generate_invariants_lean


      - name: Check Lean invariant proof presence

        run: |

          python -m afritech.proof.formal.check_proofs


      # ======================================================
      # EPOCH FORMALIZATION
      # ======================================================

      - name: Generate Lean epoch artifacts

        run: |

          python -m afritech.proof.formal.generate_epoch_lean


      - name: Check Lean epoch proof presence

        run: |

          python -m afritech.proof.formal.check_epoch_proofs


      # ======================================================
      # CONSTITUTIONAL COMPLETENESS
      # ======================================================

      - name: Generate constitutional completeness

        run: |

          python -m afritech.proof.generate_completeness


      - name: Enforce constitutional completeness

        run: |

          python -m afritech.ci.completeness_guard


      # ======================================================
      # REPLAY VALIDATION
      # ======================================================

      - name: Generate replay witness

        run: |

          python -m afritech.proof.generate_replay_witness


      - name: Generate execution witness

        run: |

          python -m afritech.proof.generate_execution_witness


      - name: Generate mutation witness

        run: |

          python -m afritech.proof.generate_mutation_witness


      - name: Generate transcript witness

        run: |

          python -m afritech.proof.generate_transcript_witness


      - name: Generate witness bundle

        run: |

          python -m afritech.proof.generate_witness_bundle


      - name: Verify replay witness

        run: |

          python -m afritech.verify.verify_replay_witness


      - name: Verify execution witness

        run: |

          python -m afritech.verify.verify_execution_witness


      - name: Verify mutation witness

        run: |

          python -m afritech.verify.verify_mutation_witness


      - name: Verify transcript witness

        run: |

          python -m afritech.verify.verify_transcript_witness


      - name: Verify witness bundle

        run: |

          python -m afritech.verify.verify_witness_bundle


      # ======================================================
      # CONSTITUTIONAL RECEIPTS
      # ======================================================

      - name: Generate constitutional receipt

        run: |

          python -m afritech.proof.generate_constitutional_receipt


      - name: Verify constitutional receipt

        run: |

          python -m afritech.verify.verify_constitutional_receipt


      # ======================================================
      # RUNTIME LEGITIMACY
      # ======================================================

      - name: Generate runtime certificate

        run: |

          python -m afritech.proof.generate_runtime_certificate


      - name: Verify runtime certificate

        run: |

          python -m afritech.verify.verify_runtime_certificate


      # ======================================================
      # REPLAY CONSISTENCY
      # ======================================================

      - name: Validate replay admissibility

        run: |

          python -m afritech.ci.replay_validation


      - name: Validate deterministic replay consistency

        run: |

          python -m afritech.verify.verify_replay_consistency


      # ======================================================
      # TESTS
      # ======================================================

      - name: Run constitutional tests

        run: |

          if [ -d tests ]; then
            pytest -q
          fi


      # ======================================================
      # SECURITY VALIDATION
      # ======================================================

      - name: Verify no forbidden alias execution

        run: |

          python -m afritech.ci.verify_forbidden_aliases


      - name: Verify closed-world enforcement

        run: |

          python -m afritech.ci.verify_closed_world


      # ======================================================
      # FINAL CONFIRMATION
      # ======================================================

      - name: Constitutional enforcement complete

        run: |

          echo "✅ Constitution enforced"
          echo "✅ Deterministic execution verified"
          echo "✅ Replay admissibility verified"
          echo "✅ Witness integrity verified"
          echo "✅ Closed-world semantics verified"
          echo "✅ Constitutional legitimacy preserved"


===== ./.github/workflows/constitutional_enforcement.yml =====
# .github/workflows/constitutional_enforcement.yml

name: Constitutional Enforcement

on:

  push:

    branches:

      - main
      - master

  pull_request:

    branches:

      - main
      - master


jobs:

  constitutional-validation:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    timeout-minutes: 15

    steps:

      # ======================================================
      # CHECKOUT
      # ======================================================

      - name: Checkout repository

        uses: actions/checkout@v4


      # ======================================================
      # PYTHON
      # ======================================================

      - name: Setup Python

        uses: actions/setup-python@v5

        with:

          python-version: "3.11"


      # ======================================================
      # DEPENDENCIES
      # ======================================================

      - name: Install dependencies

        run: |

          python -m pip install --upgrade pip

          if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then
            pip install -r requirements.txt
          fi

          pip install pyyaml pytest


      # ======================================================
      # CONSTITUTIONAL VALIDATION
      # ======================================================

      - name: Canonical constitutional pipeline

        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.constitutional_pipeline


      - name: Constitutional Validation

        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.constitutional_validation


      # ======================================================
      # PYTEST
      # ======================================================

      - name: Run test suite

        run: pytest -q


===== ./.github/workflows/ga_plus_plus_plus.yml =====
name: GA Plus Plus Plus Gate

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - develop
      - develop/semantic-runtime
      - "freeze/**"
  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  ga-plus-plus-plus:
    name: Reconstructable Constitutional Execution
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi

      - name: Constitutional pipeline
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.constitutional_pipeline

      - name: Constitutional validation
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.constitutional_validation

      - name: Trace reconstruction validation
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.trace_reconstruction_validator

      - name: Level 2 formal model validation
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.level2_formal_model_validator

      - name: Continuity validation
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.continuity_validator

      - name: AfriRide continuity demonstration
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_continuity_demo_validator

      - name: Human-readable proof package
        run: python3 -m afritech.demo.proof

      - name: L1.5 completion validation
        run: |
          python3 -m afritech.ci.registry_completeness_validator
          python3 -m afritech.ci.surface_state_resolution_validator
          python3 -m afritech.ci.binding_completeness_validator
          python3 -m afritech.ci.execution_completeness_validator
          python3 -m afritech.ci.full_witness_coverage_validator
          python3 -m afritech.ci.formal_runtime_equivalence_validator
          python3 -m afritech.ci.python_gap_validator
          python3 -m afritech.ci.yaml_gap_validator
          python3 -m afritech.ci.partial_planned_audit_validator
          python3 -m afritech.ci.completeness_policy_validator

      - name: Multi-epoch replay validation
        run: python3 -m afritech.verify.verify_multi_epoch_replay

      - name: Validate epoch lifecycle and replay translator containment
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.epoch_lifecycle_validator

      - name: Report compatibility observability status
        continue-on-error: true
        run: python3 -m afritech.tools.compat_status status

      - name: Validate observability authority boundaries
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.observability_authority_validator

      - name: Validate canonical explanation schema
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.canonical_explanation_schema_validator

      - name: Validate canonical explanation composition schema
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.canonical_explanation_composition_validator

      - name: Validate cognitive complexity budget
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.cognitive_complexity_validator

      - name: Validate cognitive salience policy
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.cognitive_salience_validator

      - name: Validate temporal cognition policy
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.temporal_cognition_validator

      - name: Validate predictive future-space policy
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.predictive_future_space_validator

      - name: Validate specialized predictive governance policies
        run: |
          python3 -m afritech.ci.inevitability_forecasting_validator
          python3 -m afritech.ci.inevitability_reinforcement_validator
          python3 -m afritech.ci.anticipatory_suppression_validator

      - name: Validate production load proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.load_proof_validator

      - name: Validate multi-node fault proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.multi_node_fault_validator

      - name: Validate durable queue proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.durable_queue_validator

      - name: Validate persistent event store proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.persistent_storage_validator

      - name: Validate observability evidence proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.observability_evidence_validator

      - name: Validate security adversarial proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.security_adversarial_validator

      - name: Validate mobile pilot E2E proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.mobile_pilot_e2e_validator

      - name: Validate AfriRide marketplace proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.marketplace_simulation_validator

      - name: Validate AfriRide GA eLive workflow contract
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_ga_elive_workflow_validator

      - name: Validate AfriRide Phase 5 readiness certificate
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_phase5_readiness_certificate_validator

      - name: Validate AfriRide Phase 5 closure
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_phase5_closure_validator

      - name: Validate economic trust proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.economic_trust_validator

      - name: Validate production readiness certificate
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.production_readiness_certificate_validator

      - name: Validate entropy-bound execution proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.entropy_invariants_validator

      - name: Validate continuity proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.continuity_invariants_validator

      - name: Validate chaos repetition stability proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.chaos_stability_validator

      - name: Validate replay authority proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.replay_authority_validator

      - name: Validate trust lock-in proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.trust_lock_validator

      - name: Validate multi-region convergence proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.multiregion_validator

      - name: Validate adversarial integrity proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.adversarial_integrity_validator

      - name: Validate AfriRide field proof
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_field_validator

      - name: Validate AfriRide field observability
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_field_observability_validator

      - name: Validate AfriRide live pilot protocol
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_live_pilot_protocol_validator

      - name: Validate AfriRide day-one pilot runbook
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_day_one_runbook_validator

      - name: Validate AfriRide post-pilot analysis protocol
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_post_pilot_analysis_validator

      - name: Validate AfriRide stakeholder evidence report template
        run: python3 -m afritech.ci.afriride_stakeholder_evidence_report_validator

      - name: Test suite
        run: pytest -q

===== ./AfriRideMobile/node_modules/@segment/loosely-validate-event/circle.yml =====
machine:
  node:
    version: 6

dependencies:
  pre:
    - npm config set "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken" $NPM_AUTH
    - sudo apt-key adv --fetch-keys http://dl.yarnpkg.com/debian/pubkey.gpg
    - echo "deb http://dl.yarnpkg.com/debian/ stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/yarn.list
    - sudo apt-get update -qq
    - sudo apt-get install -y -qq yarn
  cache_directories:
    - ~/.yarn-cache
  override:
    - yarn

test:
  override:
    - yarn test

deployment:
  publish:
    tag: /v?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)*(-.+)?/
    commands:
      - npm publish

  - package-ecosystem: "npm"
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: "daily"


===== ./AfriRideMobile/node_modules/ast-types/.github/workflows/main.yml =====
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [ master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ master ]

jobs:
  test:
    name: Test on node ${{ matrix.node_version }} and ${{ matrix.os }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node_version: ['10', '12', '14', '15', '16', '17']
        os: [ubuntu-latest]

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v2
    - name: Use Node.js ${{ matrix.node_version }}
      uses: actions/setup-node@v1
      with:
        node-version: ${{ matrix.node_version }}

    - name: npm install, build and test
      run: |
        npm install
        npm run build --if-present
        npm test

===== ./AfriRideMobile/node_modules/available-typed-arrays/.github/FUNDING.yml =====
# These are supported funding model platforms

github: [ljharb]
patreon: # Replace with a single Patreon username

===== ./AfriRideMobile/node_modules/chrome-launcher/.github/workflows/ci.yml =====
name: 🛠

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request: # run on all PRs, not just PRs to a particular branch

jobs:
  basics:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node: [ 'latest', 'lts/*', 'lts/-1' ]
    name: basics (node ${{ matrix.node }})

    steps:
    - name: git clone
      uses: actions/checkout@v3

    - name: Setup node
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
        cache: 'yarn'

    - run: yarn --frozen-lockfile --network-timeout 1000000
    - run: yarn build

    - run: yarn test-formatting
    - run: yarn type-check

    # Run tests that require headful Chrome.
    - run: sudo apt-get install xvfb
    - name: yarn test
      run: xvfb-run --auto-servernum yarn test --reporter=spec

  unit:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [macos-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    name: unit_${{ matrix.os }}

    steps:
    - name: git clone
      uses: actions/checkout@v3

    - name: Use Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
        cache: 'yarn'

    - run: yarn --frozen-lockfile --network-timeout 1000000
    - run: yarn build
    - run: yarn test --reporter=spec


===== ./AfriRideMobile/node_modules/chrome-launcher/.github/workflows/lh-smoke.yml =====
name: smoke

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request: # run on all PRs, not just PRs to a particular branch

jobs:
  # Only run smoke tests for windows against stable chrome.
  lh-smoke-windows:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        smoke-test-shard: [1, 2]
      # e.g. if set 1 fails, continue with set 2 anyway
      fail-fast: false
    runs-on: windows-latest
    name: Windows smoke ${{ matrix.smoke-test-shard }}/2

    steps:
    - name: git clone
      uses: actions/checkout@v3

    - name: Use Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
        cache: 'yarn'
    
    # chrome-launcher
    # This'll add lighthouse AND install chrome-launcher's deps
    - run: yarn add --frozen-lockfile --network-timeout 1000000 -D https://github.com/GoogleChrome/lighthouse.git#main
    - run: yarn build

    # lighthouse 
    - run: yarn --cwd node_modules/lighthouse/ install --frozen-lockfile --network-timeout 1000000
    - run: yarn reset-link
    - run: yarn --cwd node_modules/lighthouse/ build-report

    - name: Run smoke tests
      # Windows bots are slow, so only run enough tests to verify matching behavior.
      run: yarn --cwd node_modules/lighthouse/ smoke --debug -j=2 --retries=0 --shard=${{ matrix.smoke-test-shard }}/2 dbw oopif offline lantern metrics

    - name: Upload failures
      if: failure()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v1
      with:
        name: Smokehouse (windows)
        path: node_modules/lighthouse/.tmp/smokehouse-ci-failures/

===== ./AfriRideMobile/node_modules/jscodeshift/.github/workflows/test.yml =====
name: test

on:
  workflow_call:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [12.x, 14.x, 16.x]

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Use Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: yarn --frozen-lockfile
      - name: Run test
        run: yarn test

on:
  push:
    branches: ["master"]
  pull_request:
    branches: ["master"]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Use Node.js 14.6.x
        uses: actions/setup-node@v3
        with:
          node-version: 14.6.x
          cache: "npm"
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test

===== ./AfriRideMobile/node_modules/plist/.github/workflows/ci.yml =====
name: ci

on:
  push:
  pull_request:

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [ '14', '16', '18' ]

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Use Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}
      uses: actions/setup-node@v1
      with:
        node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}

    - run: npm install
    - run: npm test

===== ./AfriRideMobile/node_modules/recast/.github/workflows/main.yml =====
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [ master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ master ]

jobs:
  test:
    name: Test on node ${{ matrix.node_version }} and ${{ matrix.os }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node_version: ['10.x', '12.x', '14.x', '15.x', '16.x']
        os: [ubuntu-latest]

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v2
    - name: Use Node.js ${{ matrix.node_version }}
      uses: actions/setup-node@v1
      with:
        node-version: ${{ matrix.node_version }}

    - name: npm install, build and test
      run: |
        npm install
        npm run build --if-present
        npm test

===== ./AfriRideMobile/node_modules/requireg/node_modules/resolve/appveyor.yml =====
version: 1.0.{build}
skip_branch_with_pr: true
build: off

environment:
  matrix:
    - nodejs_version: "9"
    - nodejs_version: "8"
    - nodejs_version: "7"
    - nodejs_version: "6"
    - nodejs_version: "5"
    - nodejs_version: "4"
    - nodejs_version: "3"
    - nodejs_version: "2"
    - nodejs_version: "1"
    - nodejs_version: "0.12"
    - nodejs_version: "0.10"
    - nodejs_version: "0.8"
    - nodejs_version: "0.6"
matrix:
  # fast_finish: true
  allow_failures:
    - nodejs_version: "0.8"
    - nodejs_version: "0.6"

platform:
  - x86
  - x64

# Install scripts. (runs after repo cloning)
install:
 # Get the latest stable version of Node.js or io.js
 - ps: Install-Product node $env:nodejs_version $env:platform
 - IF %nodejs_version% EQU 0.6 npm config set strict-ssl false && npm -g install npm@1.3
 - IF %nodejs_version% EQU 0.8 npm config set strict-ssl false && npm -g install npm@1.4.28 && npm install -g npm@4.5
 - set PATH=%APPDATA%\npm;%PATH%
 #- IF %nodejs_version% NEQ 0.6 AND %nodejs_version% NEQ 0.8 npm -g install npm
 # install modules
 - npm install

# Post-install test scripts.
test_script:
 # Output useful info for debugging.
 - node --version
 - npm --version
 # run tests
 - npm run tests-only

===== ./AfriRideMobile/node_modules/reusify/.github/workflows/ci.yml =====
name: ci

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  legacy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: ['0.10', '0.12', 4.x, 6.x, 8.x, 10.x, 12.x, 13.x, 14.x, 15.x, 16.x]

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}

      - name: Install
        run: |
          npm install --production && npm install tape

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          npm run test

  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [18.x, 20.x, 22.x]

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}

      - name: Install
        run: |
          npm install

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          npm run test:coverage

  types:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Install
        run: |
          npm install

      - name: Run types tests
        run: |
          npm run test:typescript

  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Install
        run: |
          npm install

      - name: Lint
        run: |
          npm run lint

===== ./afritech/architecture/PATH_ONTOLOGY.yaml =====
schema: afritech.path_ontology.v1

version: 1

metadata:

  authority: ARCHITECTURE
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED

  description: >
    Canonical path ontology for AfriTech constitutional identity,
    execution topology, governance semantics, replay admissibility,
    sealing, and architectural resolution.

    This ontology defines the exclusive meaning of:
      - module paths
      - filesystem paths
      - constitutional identities
      - sealing identities
      - runtime resolution identities

    No component may reinterpret path semantics outside this ontology.


# ============================================================
# CANONICAL PATH FORMS
# ============================================================

canonical_forms:

  module_path:

    description: >
      Canonical constitutional identity form.
      Used for:
        - semantic references
        - governance references
        - invariant references
        - replay references
        - implementation legitimacy
        - execution topology enforcement

    separator: "."

    examples:
      - afritech.runtime.engine
      - afritech.runtime.activation
      - afritech.proof.constitutional_receipt
      - afritech.trace.trace_reconstructor

    properties:

      executable_identity: true
      replay_addressable: true
      constitutionally_authoritative: true
      filesystem_stable: false


  filesystem_path:

    description: >
      Physical filesystem artifact path.
      Used exclusively for:
        - hashing
        - sealing
        - manifest generation
        - packaging
        - artifact discovery

    separator: "/"

    examples:
      - afritech/runtime/engine.py
      - afritech/runtime/activation/
      - afritech/proof/constitutional_receipt.py

    properties:

      executable_identity: false
      replay_addressable: false
      constitutionally_authoritative: false
      filesystem_stable: true


  conceptual_path:

    description: >
      Non-executable conceptual reference.
      Used for:
        - doctrine
        - conceptual semantics
        - architectural documentation
        - explanatory mappings

    separator: "::"

    examples:
      - CONCEPT::REPLAY_ADMISSIBILITY
      - INVARIANT::I8_CLOSED_WORLD
      - EPOCH::EVOLUTION

    properties:

      executable_identity: false
      replay_addressable: false
      constitutionally_authoritative: limited
      filesystem_stable: false


# ============================================================
# GLOBAL PATH LAW
# ============================================================

global_rules:

  - id: PATH_RULE_1
    rule: >
      Module paths are the exclusive constitutional identity form
      for executable and replay-participating surfaces.

  - id: PATH_RULE_2
    rule: >
      Filesystem paths must never be treated as constitutional
      execution identities.

  - id: PATH_RULE_3
    rule: >
      Conceptual paths must never participate in runtime execution,
      replay execution, or implementation legitimacy enforcement.

  - id: PATH_RULE_4
    rule: >
      All implementation legitimacy enforcement must resolve
      against module paths only.

  - id: PATH_RULE_5
    rule: >
      Hash sealing operations may use filesystem paths only.

  - id: PATH_RULE_6
    rule: >
      Replay verification must resolve execution surfaces
      through canonical module paths.

  - id: PATH_RULE_7
    rule: >
      Filesystem extensions (.py, .yaml, .json) are physical
      artifact details and are not constitutional identities.

  - id: PATH_RULE_8
    rule: >
      Path identity resolution must be deterministic,
      prefix-safe, and observer-independent.


# ============================================================
# PATH RESOLUTION POLICY
# ============================================================

resolution:

  module_identity:

    canonicalization:

      strip_extensions: true
      normalize_separators: true
      preserve_case: true

    forbidden:

      - implicit_relative_resolution
      - dynamic_import_resolution
      - filesystem_backtracking
      - reflection_based_discovery

  filesystem_identity:

    canonicalization:

      normalize_separators: true
      preserve_extensions: true
      preserve_case: true

    forbidden:

      - runtime_identity_binding
      - replay_surface_identity
      - constitutional_execution_resolution


# ============================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION REGISTRY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

implementation_registry:

  canonical_form: module_path

  examples:

    correct:

      - afritech.runtime.engine
      - afritech.proof.constitutional_receipt
      - afritech.trace.trace_reconstructor

    forbidden:

      - afritech/runtime/engine.py
      - afritech/proof/constitutional_receipt.py
      - runtime.engine


# ============================================================
# GOVERNANCE ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

governance_alignment:

  required_form: module_path

  applies_to:

    - EXECUTION_SURFACES.yaml
    - ENFORCEMENT_MAP.yaml
    - invariants_semantics.yaml
    - epoch_semantics.yaml
    - implementation_registry.yaml


# ============================================================
# REPLAY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

replay_alignment:

  execution_trace_identity: module_path

  receipt_surface_identity: module_path

  transcript_surface_identity: module_path

  forbidden:

    - filesystem_identity_replay
    - extension_sensitive_replay
    - dynamic_surface_aliasing


# ============================================================
# HASHING ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

hashing_alignment:

  sealing_identity: filesystem_path

  manifest_identity: filesystem_path

  artifact_identity: filesystem_path

  forbidden:

    - module_path_hashing
    - conceptual_path_hashing


# ============================================================
# PATH SAFETY GUARANTEES
# ============================================================

safety:

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_resolution
    - replay_stability
    - observer_independence
    - prefix_safe_matching
    - closed_world_compatibility
    - implementation_legitimacy_consistency

  forbidden:

    - ambiguous_identity_resolution
    - mixed_path_ontology
    - extension_based_authority
    - filesystem_authority_inference
    - conceptual_execution_binding


# ============================================================
# CI ENFORCEMENT
# ============================================================

ci_enforcement:

  validators:

    - path_ontology_validator
    - implementation_registry_validator
    - replay_surface_validator
    - execution_surface_validator

  failure_mode: CI_FAIL

  required_checks:

    - no_filesystem_paths_in_semantics
    - no_module_paths_in_hash_manifests
    - no_extension_based_identities
    - no_mixed_resolution_modes


# ============================================================
# REPLAY ASSERTION
# ============================================================

replay_assertion:

  description: >
    Replay legitimacy requires stable constitutional identity.
    All replay-participating execution surfaces must resolve
    exclusively through canonical module paths.

  violation_effects:

    - replay_invalid
    - receipt_non_admissible
    - execution_surface_mismatch


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  description: >
    AfriTech recognizes exactly three path classes:
      - module identity
      - filesystem artifact
      - conceptual reference

    These classes are constitutionally distinct and must never
    be conflated.

    Execution identity derives exclusively from canonical
    module paths.

    Filesystem artifacts are physical witnesses only,
    not constitutional execution identities.

===== ./afritech/architecture/enforcement_matrix.yaml =====
schema: afritech.enforcement_matrix.v2

version: 2

metadata:

  authority: ARCHITECTURE
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED

  registry_class: CONSTITUTIONAL_ENFORCEMENT_MATRIX

  description: >
    Canonical constitutional enforcement matrix governing
    replay-safe enforcement participation, runtime admission
    enforcement, deterministic topology enforcement,
    proof-bound execution validation, witness admissibility,
    and closed-world execution enforcement within AfriTech.

    This matrix defines:
      - enforcement legitimacy
      - implementation admissibility
      - replay participation
      - runtime blocking semantics
      - proof enforcement semantics
      - witness enforcement participation
      - deterministic execution enforcement
      - ontology-safe enforcement identity

    Enforcement legitimacy is constitutional metadata only.

    Enforcement legitimacy does NOT independently confer:
      - runtime sovereignty
      - governance authority
      - replay authority
      - execution legitimacy
      - constitutional authority

    Enforcement participation remains bounded by:
      - admissibility law
      - replay admissibility
      - invariant preservation
      - deterministic execution
      - canonical ontology resolution
      - closed-world execution semantics


# ============================================================
# PATH ONTOLOGY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

ontology_alignment:

  implementation_registry: required

  implementation_states: required

  witness_policy: required

  path_ontology: required

  replay_semantics: required

  execution_surfaces: required

  canonical_identity_form: module_path

  forbidden_identity_forms:

    - filesystem_path
    - extension_identity
    - relative_path
    - conceptual_identity
    - symbolic_short_name

  description: >
    Enforcement identity resolution requires canonical
    module-path ontology semantics.


# ============================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION ADMISSIBILITY
# ============================================================

implementation_states:

  PLANNED:

    executable: false

    replay_participating: false

    runtime_admissible: false

    enforcement_admissible: false

    proof_admissible: false

    replay_safe: false

    deterministic_execution: false

    description: >
      Constitutionally declared enforcement capability
      not yet operationally implemented.


  PARTIAL:

    executable: constrained

    replay_participating: limited

    runtime_admissible: conditional

    enforcement_admissible: conditional

    proof_admissible: conditional

    replay_safe: conditional

    deterministic_execution: conditional

    description: >
      Partially operational enforcement surface with
      constitutionally constrained admissibility.


  IMPLEMENTED:

    executable: true

    replay_participating: true

    runtime_admissible: true

    enforcement_admissible: true

    proof_admissible: true

    replay_safe: true

    deterministic_execution: true

    description: >
      Fully operational deterministic constitutional
      enforcement surface.

  FROZEN:

    executable: false

    replay_participating: false

    runtime_admissible: false

    enforcement_admissible: false

    proof_admissible: false

    replay_safe: true

    deterministic_execution: true

    description: >
      Explicitly resolved non-operational enforcement surface.
      FROZEN enforcement entries are inert and fail closed if
      referenced as runtime, replay, proof, or witness authority.


  DOCUMENTARY:

    executable: false

    replay_participating: false

    runtime_admissible: false

    enforcement_admissible: false

    proof_admissible: false

    replay_safe: false

    deterministic_execution: false

    description: >
      Documentation-only enforcement artifact.


  FORBIDDEN_ALIAS:

    executable: false

    replay_participating: false

    runtime_admissible: false

    enforcement_admissible: false

    proof_admissible: false

    replay_safe: false

    deterministic_execution: false

    description: >
      Constitutionally forbidden non-canonical identity alias.


# ============================================================
# ENFORCEMENT SURFACES
# ============================================================

enforcement:

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # TRANSCRIPT ENFORCEMENT
  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  afritech.transcript.execution_trace_surface_set:

    ontology: CANONICAL_MODULE_PATH

    proof_blocking: false

    description: >

  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  afritech.proof.constitutional_receipt:

# ============================================================
# GLOBAL ENFORCEMENT RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: ENFORCEMENT_RULE_1
    rule: >
      Enforcement identities must resolve exclusively through
      canonical module-path ontology.

  - id: ENFORCEMENT_RULE_2
    rule: >
      Filesystem paths must never participate in constitutional
      enforcement identity resolution.

  - id: ENFORCEMENT_RULE_3
    rule: >
      Replay-blocking enforcement surfaces must possess
      constitutionally admissible implementation legitimacy.

  - id: ENFORCEMENT_RULE_4
    rule: >
      PLANNED enforcement surfaces are constitutionally declared
      but operationally non-blocking.

  - id: ENFORCEMENT_RULE_5
    rule: >
      PARTIAL enforcement surfaces may participate in constrained
      replay and runtime validation contexts.

  - id: ENFORCEMENT_RULE_6
    rule: >
      FORBIDDEN_ALIAS surfaces must never participate in:
        - replay enforcement
        - runtime enforcement
        - receipt generation
        - execution admission
        - governance validation
        - witness validation

  - id: ENFORCEMENT_RULE_7
    rule: >
      Enforcement legitimacy does not independently confer
      runtime sovereignty or governance authority.

  - id: ENFORCEMENT_RULE_8
    rule: >
      Enforcement resolution must remain:
        - deterministic
        - replay-safe
        - observer-independent
        - non-reflective
        - closed-world aligned

  - id: ENFORCEMENT_RULE_9
    rule: >
      Replay admissibility requires:
        - deterministic execution
        - invariant preservation
        - replay traceability
        - witness admissibility

  - id: ENFORCEMENT_RULE_10
    rule: >
      Closed-world execution semantics are mandatory for
      runtime-blocking enforcement surfaces.


# ============================================================
# REPLAY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

replay_alignment:

  replay_safe_states:

    - IMPLEMENTED

  replay_forbidden_states:

    - PLANNED
    - FROZEN
    - DOCUMENTARY
    - FORBIDDEN_ALIAS

  replay_requirements:

    - deterministic_execution
    - replay_traceability
    - witness_alignment
    - canonical_identity_resolution

  replay_invalidators:

    - replay_divergence
    - observer_relative_execution
    - probabilistic_execution
    - undeclared_execution_surfaces

  description: >
    Replay legitimacy requires constitutionally admissible
    deterministic enforcement participation.


# ============================================================
# RUNTIME ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

runtime_alignment:

  runtime_safe_states:

    - IMPLEMENTED

  conditionally_runtime_safe_states:
    []

  runtime_forbidden_states:

    - PLANNED
    - FROZEN
    - DOCUMENTARY
    - FORBIDDEN_ALIAS

  description: >
    Runtime admission requires constitutionally admissible
    enforcement participation.


# ============================================================
# CLOSED-WORLD ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

closed_world_alignment:

  required:

    - declared_execution_surfaces
    - deterministic_surface_resolution
    - replay_safe_execution
    - canonical_identity_resolution

  forbidden:

    - implicit_execution_surfaces
    - reflection_based_discovery
    - dynamic_runtime_aliasing
    - undeclared_execution_binding

  description: >
    Enforcement admissibility requires closed-world execution
    semantics.


# ============================================================
# FAILURE MODES
# ============================================================

failure_modes:

  replay_enforcement_violation:

    effect: HARD_FAIL


  runtime_enforcement_violation:

    effect: HARD_FAIL


  forbidden_alias_resolution:

    effect: HARD_FAIL


  filesystem_identity_resolution:

    effect: HARD_FAIL


  replay_divergence:

    effect: REPLAY_INVALID


  witness_consistency_failure:

    effect: PROOF_REJECTION


  deterministic_execution_failure:

    effect: EXECUTION_REFUSAL


  undocumented_enforcement_surface:

    effect: SOFT_FAIL


# ============================================================
# SAFETY GUARANTEES
# ============================================================

safety:

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_enforcement_resolution
    - replay_safe_enforcement
    - observer_independent_validation
    - invariant_preserving_enforcement
    - canonical_identity_alignment

  forbid:

    - implicit_runtime_legitimacy
    - filesystem_authority_inference
    - speculative_execution_authority
    - observer_relative_validation
    - ambiguous_enforcement_resolution

  enforcement:

    violation_action: FAIL_FAST

    replay_violation_action: REPLAY_INVALID

    runtime_violation_action: EXECUTION_REFUSAL


# ============================================================
# CI ENFORCEMENT
# ============================================================

ci_enforcement:

  enforced_by:

    - afritech.ci.import_topology_enforcement
    - afritech.ci.execution_surface_validation
    - afritech.ci.replay_validation
    - afritech.ci.witness_validation

  required_checks:

    - no_filesystem_identity_resolution
    - no_extension_based_enforcement_identity
    - deterministic_prefix_resolution
    - implementation_state_alignment
    - replay_admissibility_alignment
    - witness_consistency_validation
    - closed_world_validation

  failure_mode: CI_FAIL


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  description: >
    Constitutional enforcement participation requires:
      - canonical identity resolution
      - admissible implementation legitimacy
      - deterministic enforcement semantics
      - replay-safe operational behavior
      - witness admissibility
      - invariant preservation
      - closed-world execution semantics

    Enforcement metadata is constitutionally descriptive and
    must remain:
      - deterministic
      - replay-safe
      - observer-independent
      - topology-safe
      - constitutionally bounded


===== ./afritech/architecture/identity_rules.yaml =====
schema: afritech.identity_rules.v2

version: 2

metadata:

  authority: ARCHITECTURE

  status: ACTIVE

  mutability: CONTROLLED

  registry_class: CONSTITUTIONAL_IDENTITY_RULES

  description: >
    Canonical identity ontology governing deterministic
    identity resolution, replay-safe module identity,
    ontology-safe canonical naming, closed-world execution
    identity semantics, and constitutional identity
    admissibility within AfriTech.

    Identity legitimacy derives exclusively from:
      - canonical module-path ontology
      - deterministic identity resolution
      - replay-safe semantics
      - invariant-preserving execution
      - closed-world execution semantics

    Identity legitimacy must never derive from:
      - filesystem structure
      - symbolic shortcuts
      - runtime reflection
      - extension-based naming
      - observer-relative interpretation


# ============================================================
# CANONICAL IDENTITY MODEL
# ============================================================

canonical_identity:

  canonical_identity_form:
    module_path

  canonical_separator:
    "."

  root_namespace:
    afritech

  deterministic_resolution:
    true

  replay_safe_resolution:
    true

  observer_independent:
    true

  ontology_safe:
    true

  canonical_examples:

    - afritech.runtime.activation
    - afritech.runtime.admission
    - afritech.proof.constitutional_receipt
    - afritech.proof.witness.replay_witness
    - afritech.trace.trace_reconstructor
    - afritech.ci.identity_validator

  description: >
    Canonical constitutional identity must resolve
    exclusively through deterministic module-path ontology.


# ============================================================
# FORBIDDEN IDENTITY FORMS
# ============================================================

forbidden_identity_forms:

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # FILESYSTEM IDENTITY
  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  filesystem_identity:

    forbidden:
      true

    severity:
      CRITICAL

    examples:

      - afritech/proof/constitutional_receipt.py
      - afritech/runtime/admission.py
      - ./runtime/replay.py
      - ../proof/witness.py

    reason: >
      Filesystem structure must never independently define
      constitutional identity.

    effects:

      - replay_invalid
      - ontology_violation
      - identity_rejection
      - CI_failure

    forbidden_operations:

      - runtime_binding
      - replay_binding
      - proof_binding
      - constitutional_attestation


  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # SYMBOLIC RUNTIME IDENTITY
  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  symbolic_runtime_identity:

    forbidden:
      true

    severity:
      CRITICAL

    examples:

      - runtime
      - replay
      - receipt
      - witness
      - engine
      - activation_surface
      - current_runtime
      - active_receipt

    reason: >
      Symbolic runtime identities are ambiguous and violate
      deterministic ontology resolution.

    effects:

      - deterministic_failure
      - replay_invalid
      - identity_ambiguity
      - ontology_violation

    forbidden_operations:

      - runtime_resolution
      - replay_resolution
      - witness_binding
      - proof_resolution


  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # REFLECTION IDENTITY
  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  reflection_identity:

    forbidden:
      true

    severity:
      CRITICAL

    examples:

      - getattr(runtime, name)
      - __import__(module_name)
      - dynamic_import(name)
      - eval(module_name)
      - exec(identity_code)

    reason: >
      Reflection-based identity resolution violates:
        - deterministic execution
        - replay safety
        - closed-world semantics
        - observer-independent evaluation

    effects:

      - replay_invalid
      - execution_refusal
      - closed_world_violation
      - deterministic_failure

    forbidden_operations:

      - runtime_discovery
      - replay_discovery
      - dynamic_binding
      - speculative_resolution


  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # EXTENSION-BASED IDENTITY
  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  extension_based_identity:

    forbidden:
      true

    severity:
      HIGH

    examples:

      - receipt.py
      - witness.yaml
      - runtime.json
      - replay.yml

    reason: >
      File extensions are implementation artifacts and must
      never independently define constitutional identity.

    effects:

      - ontology_violation
      - replay_ambiguity
      - identity_rejection

    forbidden_operations:

      - runtime_binding
      - proof_binding
      - replay_binding


# ============================================================
# ALLOWED IDENTITY FORMS
# ============================================================

allowed_identity_forms:

  canonical_module_path:

    allowed:
      true

    deterministic:
      true

    replay_safe:
      true

    closed_world_aligned:
      true

    observer_independent:
      true

    examples:

      - afritech.proof.constitutional_receipt
      - afritech.runtime.activation
      - afritech.ci.witness_validator

    description: >
      Canonical module-path ontology identity.


# ============================================================
# IDENTITY RESOLUTION
# ============================================================

identity_resolution:

  deterministic_resolution:
    required

  replay_safe_resolution:
    required

  observer_independent:
    required

  ontology_safe_resolution:
    required

  closed_world_alignment:
    required

  resolution_strategy:

    - canonical_module_path_only
    - explicit_registration_only
    - deterministic_ordering
    - invariant_preserving_resolution

  forbidden_resolution_modes:

    - filesystem_discovery
    - runtime_reflection
    - symbolic_resolution
    - extension_resolution
    - probabilistic_resolution
    - observer_relative_resolution

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_identity_resolution
    - replay_safe_binding
    - invariant_preserving_resolution
    - ontology_safe_execution

  description: >
    Identity resolution must remain deterministic,
    replay-safe, and closed-world aligned.

identity_categories:

    replay_significance:
      REQUIRED

    deterministic_required:
      true

    examples:

      - afritech.ci.identity_validator
      - afritech.ci.witness_validator


# ============================================================
# REPLAY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

replay_alignment:

closed_world_alignment:

  forbidden:

# ============================================================

enforcement:

    - no_reflection_identity
    - no_extension_based_identity
    - deterministic_resolution_validation

  failure_mode:
    FAIL_FAST


# ============================================================

safety:

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_identity_resolution
    - replay_safe_identity_binding
    - observer_independent_resolution
    - ontology_safe_execution
    - invariant_preserving_resolution

  forbid:

    - filesystem_authority_inference
    - symbolic_runtime_authority
    - reflection_based_execution
    - extension_based_resolution
    - observer_relative_resolution

  enforcement:

    violation_action:
      FAIL_FAST

    replay_violation_action:
      REPLAY_INVALID

    runtime_violation_action:
      EXECUTION_REFUSAL


# ============================================================
# GLOBAL RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: IDENTITY_RULE_1

    rule: >
      Canonical identity must resolve exclusively through
      deterministic module-path ontology semantics.


  - id: IDENTITY_RULE_2

    rule: >
      Filesystem paths must never independently define
      constitutional identity.


  - id: IDENTITY_RULE_3

    rule: >
      Symbolic runtime identities are constitutionally
      forbidden.


  - id: IDENTITY_RULE_4

    rule: >
      Reflection-based identity resolution is
      constitutionally forbidden.


  - id: IDENTITY_RULE_5

    rule: >
      Extension-based naming must never independently define
      constitutional identity.

    rule: >
      Replay admissibility requires deterministic
      ontology-safe identity resolution.


  - id: IDENTITY_RULE_7

    rule: >
      Closed-world execution semantics require explicit
      deterministic identity registration.


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  description: >
    Constitutional identity derives exclusively from:
      - canonical module-path ontology
      - deterministic resolution semantics
      - replay-safe identity binding
      - invariant-preserving execution
      - closed-world execution semantics

    Constitutional identity must never derive from:
      - filesystem structure
      - symbolic runtime shortcuts
      - reflection-based execution
      - extension-based naming
      - observer-relative interpretation

    Identity evaluation must remain:
      - deterministic
      - replay-safe
      - observer-independent
      - ontology-safe
      - constitutionally bounded

===== ./afritech/architecture/implementation_registry.yaml =====
schema: afritech.implementation_registry.v3
version: 3
metadata:
  authority: ARCHITECTURE
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED
  registry_class: CONSTITUTIONAL_IMPLEMENTATION_REGISTRY
  description: "Canonical implementation registry governing deterministic implementation\
    \ legitimacy, replay admissibility, proof admissibility, ontology-safe identity\
    \ resolution, and closed-world execution participation within AfriTech.\nFilesystem\
    \ paths must never independently define constitutional identity.\nCanonical identity\
    \ resolution derives exclusively from:\n  - canonical module-path ontology\n \
    \ - deterministic identity resolution\n  - replay-safe implementation semantics\n\
    \  - invariant-preserving execution semantics\n"
registry_composition:
  composition_mode: CANONICAL_AGGREGATION
  main_registry: afritech/architecture/implementation_registry.yaml
  sub_registries:
    modules: afritech/architecture/sub_modules_registry.yaml
    enforcement: afritech/architecture/sub_enforcement_registry.yaml
  composition_rules:
  - main_registry_defines_canonical_registry_identity
  - sub_modules_registry_defines_declared_implementation_surfaces
  - sub_enforcement_registry_defines_admissibility_and_purity_rules
  - sub_registries_must_not_override_main_registry_authority
  - aggregated_registry_must_be_deterministic
  - missing_or_unreadable_sub_registry_is_ci_fail
  authority_resolution:
    canonical_authority: ARCHITECTURE
    sub_registry_authority_must_equal: ARCHITECTURE
    conflict_policy: FAIL_CLOSED
required_loaded_sections:
- implementation_states
- implementations
- rules
- replay_alignment
- closed_world_alignment
- safety
- registry_consistency_requirements
- purity_gate
constitutional_assertion:
  description: "Implementation legitimacy derives exclusively from:\n  - canonical\
    \ module-path ontology\n  - deterministic execution semantics\n  - replay-safe\
    \ operational behavior\n  - invariant preservation\n  - closed-world execution\
    \ semantics\n\nImplementation legitimacy must never derive from:\n  - filesystem\
    \ structure\n  - runtime activity alone\n  - observer-relative interpretation\n\
    \  - speculative inference\n  - symbolic naming shortcuts\n"


===== ./afritech/architecture/implementation_states.yaml =====
schema: afritech.implementation_states.v2

version: 2

metadata:

  authority: ARCHITECTURE

  status: ACTIVE

  mutability: CONTROLLED

  registry_class: CONSTITUTIONAL_IMPLEMENTATION_STATES

  description: >
    Canonical implementation-state registry governing
    deterministic implementation legitimacy,
    replay admissibility, proof admissibility,
    runtime admissibility, and closed-world execution
    semantics within AfriTech.

    Implementation states define:
      - operational admissibility
      - replay participation
      - proof participation
      - runtime execution legitimacy
      - deterministic execution guarantees
      - closed-world execution guarantees
      - ontology-safe implementation semantics

    Implementation states are constitutional metadata only.

    Implementation-state legitimacy does NOT independently
    confer:
      - governance sovereignty
      - replay authority
      - unrestricted runtime authority
      - proof supremacy

    Filesystem paths must never independently define
    implementation legitimacy.


# ============================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION STATES
# ============================================================

states:

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # PLANNED
  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  PLANNED:

    executable:
      false

    runtime_admissible:
      false

    replay_admissible:
      false

    proof_admissible:
      false

    deterministic_execution:
      false

    closed_world_aligned:
      false

    replay_safe:
      false

    observer_independent:
      true

    witness_generation:
      false

    mutation_authority:
      false

    replay_participation:
      false

    proof_participation:
      false

    runtime_participation:
      false

    allowed_operations:

      - declarative_registration
      - ontology_visibility
      - architectural_planning

    forbidden_operations:

      - runtime_execution
      - replay_execution
      - proof_generation
      - witness_generation
      - constitutional_attestation
      - mutation_execution

    replay_significance:
      DECLARATIVE_ONLY

    enforcement_level:
      HARD_BLOCKED

    description: >
      Constitutionally declared but operationally
      non-admissible implementation state.

      PLANNED surfaces exist only as architectural intent
      and must remain non-operational.


  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # PARTIAL
  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  PARTIAL:

    executable:
      constrained

    runtime_admissible:
      conditional

    replay_admissible:
      true

    proof_admissible:
      true

    deterministic_execution:
      true

    closed_world_aligned:
      true

    replay_safe:
      true

    observer_independent:
      true

    witness_generation:
      conditional

    mutation_authority:
      constrained

    replay_participation:
      constrained

    proof_participation:
      constrained

    runtime_participation:
      conditional

    allowed_operations:

      - constrained_runtime_execution
      - replay_execution
      - proof_generation
      - witness_generation
      - deterministic_validation
      - invariant_enforcement

    forbidden_operations:

      - probabilistic_execution
      - observer_relative_execution
      - undeclared_surface_binding
      - unrestricted_mutation
      - speculative_runtime_execution

    replay_significance:
      ADMISSIBLE_WITH_CONSTRAINTS

    enforcement_level:
      STRICT_VALIDATION

    required_constraints:

      - deterministic_execution
      - replay_safe_semantics
      - invariant_preservation
      - witness_validation
      - closed_world_alignment

    description: >
      Partially implemented deterministic replay-safe
      implementation state.

      PARTIAL surfaces may participate in constrained
      replay-safe execution under explicit constitutional
      validation.


  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # IMPLEMENTED
  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  IMPLEMENTED:

    executable:
      true

    runtime_admissible:
      true

    replay_admissible:
      true

    proof_admissible:
      true

    deterministic_execution:
      true

    closed_world_aligned:
      true

    replay_safe:
      true

    observer_independent:
      true

    witness_generation:
      true

    mutation_authority:
      constrained

    replay_participation:
      true

    proof_participation:
      true

    runtime_participation:
      true

    allowed_operations:

      - runtime_execution
      - replay_execution
      - proof_generation
      - witness_generation
      - constitutional_attestation
      - deterministic_validation
      - invariant_enforcement

    forbidden_operations:

      - observer_relative_execution
      - probabilistic_execution
      - undeclared_execution_surfaces
      - reflection_based_binding

    replay_significance:
      FULLY_ADMISSIBLE

    enforcement_level:
      FULL_ENFORCEMENT

    required_constraints:

      - deterministic_execution
      - replay_equivalence
      - invariant_preservation
      - witness_consistency
      - closed_world_alignment

    description: >
      Fully implemented deterministic replay-safe
      operational implementation state.

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # FROZEN
  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  FROZEN:

    executable:
      false

    runtime_admissible:
      false

    replay_admissible:
      false

      false

      true

      true

      true

      false

      false

      false

      false

    runtime_participation:
      false

      - declarative_registration
      - ontology_visibility
      - frozen_lineage_reference

    forbidden_operations:

      - runtime_execution
      - replay_execution
      - proof_generation
      - witness_generation
      - constitutional_attestation
      - mutation_execution

    replay_significance:
      FROZEN_DECLARATION_ONLY

    enforcement_level:
      FAIL_CLOSED_CONTAINMENT

    description: >
      Explicitly resolved non-operational implementation
      state. FROZEN surfaces are complete by containment and
      cannot silently become executable.


  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  DOCUMENTARY:

    executable:
      false

      false

      false

      - architectural_reference

      - runtime_execution
      - replay_execution
      - proof_generation
      - witness_generation
      - constitutional_attestation

    replay_significance:
      NONE

    enforcement_level:
      NON_OPERATIONAL

    description: >

      DOCUMENTARY surfaces possess no runtime or replay
      admissibility.


  # ----------------------------------------------------------
