      - runtime_execution
      - replay_execution
      - proof_generation
      - witness_generation
      - constitutional_attestation
      - identity_resolution
      - runtime_binding

    replay_significance:
      FORBIDDEN

    enforcement_level:
      HARD_REJECTION

    description: >
      Constitutionally forbidden alias implementation state.

      FORBIDDEN_ALIAS identities must never participate in:
        - runtime execution
        - replay execution
        - proof generation
        - witness generation
        - constitutional attestation


# ============================================================
# GLOBAL RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: IMPLEMENTATION_STATE_RULE_1

    rule: >
      Replay admissibility requires:
        - deterministic execution
        - replay-safe semantics
        - invariant preservation
        - closed-world alignment


  - id: IMPLEMENTATION_STATE_RULE_2

    rule: >
      PLANNED implementations must remain operationally inert.


  - id: IMPLEMENTATION_STATE_RULE_3

    rule: >
      PARTIAL implementations may participate in constrained
      replay-safe execution only when explicitly validated.


  - id: IMPLEMENTATION_STATE_RULE_4

    rule: >
      IMPLEMENTED surfaces require deterministic replay-safe
      operational behavior.


  - id: IMPLEMENTATION_STATE_RULE_5

    rule: >
      FORBIDDEN_ALIAS identities must never participate in:
        - runtime execution
        - replay execution
        - proof generation
        - witness generation


  - id: IMPLEMENTATION_STATE_RULE_6

    rule: >
      Observer-relative execution semantics are
      constitutionally forbidden.


  - id: IMPLEMENTATION_STATE_RULE_7

    rule: >
      Reflection-based identity resolution is
      constitutionally forbidden.


# ============================================================
# REPLAY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

replay_alignment:

  replay_safe_states:

    - IMPLEMENTED

  replay_forbidden_states:

    - PLANNED
    - FROZEN
    - DOCUMENTARY
    - FORBIDDEN_ALIAS

  replay_requirements:

    - deterministic_execution
    - replay_safe_semantics
    - invariant_preservation
    - canonical_identity_resolution

  replay_invalidators:

    - probabilistic_execution
    - observer_relative_execution
    - undeclared_execution_surfaces
    - filesystem_identity_resolution


# ============================================================
# CLOSED-WORLD ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

closed_world_alignment:

  required:

    - declared_execution_surfaces
    - deterministic_identity_resolution
    - replay_safe_execution
    - invariant_preservation

  forbidden:

    - implicit_runtime_discovery
    - reflection_based_execution
    - dynamic_runtime_aliasing
    - undeclared_surface_binding


# ============================================================
# SAFETY GUARANTEES
# ============================================================

safety:

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_state_evaluation
    - replay_safe_execution
    - ontology_safe_resolution
    - observer_independent_validation
    - invariant_preserving_execution

  forbid:

    - speculative_runtime_authority
    - filesystem_authority_inference
    - observer_relative_execution
    - ambiguous_state_resolution
    - probabilistic_execution

  enforcement:

    violation_action:
      FAIL_FAST

    replay_violation_action:
      REPLAY_INVALID

    runtime_violation_action:
      EXECUTION_REFUSAL


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  description: >
    Implementation-state legitimacy derives exclusively from:
      - deterministic execution semantics
      - replay-safe operational behavior
      - invariant preservation
      - canonical ontology resolution
      - closed-world execution semantics

    Implementation-state legitimacy must never derive from:
      - filesystem structure
      - runtime activity alone
      - speculative interpretation
      - observer-relative evaluation
      - symbolic naming shortcuts


===== ./afritech/architecture/path_aliases.yaml =====
schema: afritech.architecture.path_aliases.v3

version: 3

metadata:

  authority: ARCHITECTURE

  status: ACTIVE

  mutability: CONTROLLED

  registry_class: PATH_ALIAS_REGISTRY

  replay_safe: true

  deterministic: true

  closed_world_aligned: true

  description: >
    Replay-safe alias normalization registry governing
    compatibility-token resolution toward canonical
    ontology identities.

    Alias keys are compatibility tokens only.

    Alias keys MUST NOT:
      - encode filesystem topology
      - encode runtime paths
      - encode repository structure
      - define constitutional identity

    Canonical targets MAY represent:
      - ontology module identities
      - replay-safe filename normalization targets

    This registry exists exclusively to:
      - preserve migration continuity
      - normalize historical identifiers
      - maintain replay-safe resolution semantics
      - support deterministic canonicalization

    Runtime legitimacy derives exclusively from:
      - canonical ontology identity
      - replay admissibility
      - invariant legality
      - execution surface governance


# ============================================================
# ONTOLOGY
# ============================================================

ontology:

  alias_key_semantics:

    definition: COMPATIBILITY_TOKEN

    topology_independent: true

    replay_safe: true

    filesystem_paths_forbidden: true

    examples:

      valid:

        - constitutional_receipt.py
        - trace_reconstructor.py
        - attestation.yaml
        - EPOCH_0008.yaml

      invalid:

        - afritech/proof/constitutional_receipt.py
        - afritech/trace/trace_reconstructor.py
        - registry/attestation.yaml


  canonical_target_semantics:

    ontology_identity:

      definition: CANONICAL_MODULE_PATH

      examples:

        - afritech.proof.constitutional_receipt
        - afritech.trace.trace_reconstructor
        - afritech.registry.attestation


    filename_normalization:

      definition: REPLAY_SAFE_FILENAME_TARGET

      examples:

        - EPOCH_0008.yaml
        - EPOCH_0009.yaml


# ============================================================
# STATUS MODEL
# ============================================================

status_model:

  CANONICAL:

    replay_safe: true

    admissible: true

    description: >
      Canonical replay-safe identity target.


  MIGRATING:

    replay_safe: true

    admissible: true

    description: >
      Transitional compatibility alias retained for
      deterministic replay continuity and migration
      normalization.


  DEPRECATED:

    replay_safe: conditional

    admissible: false

    description: >
      Historical compatibility alias retained only
      for replay reconstruction visibility.


  FORBIDDEN:

    replay_safe: true

    admissible: false

    description: >
      Explicitly forbidden executable alias retained
      solely for governance visibility and replay-safe
      ontology completeness.


# ============================================================
# ALIASES
# ============================================================

aliases:

  attestation.py:

    canonical: afritech.registry.attestation

    status: FORBIDDEN

    ontology: FORBIDDEN_EXECUTABLE_ALIAS

    replay_safe: true

    description: >
      Executable attestation aliases are constitutionally
      forbidden. Attestation authority must derive from
      canonical registry ontology only.


  attestation.yaml:

    canonical: afritech.registry.attestation

    status: MIGRATING

    ontology: TOKEN_TO_MODULE_PATH

    replay_safe: true

    description: >
      Historical registry compatibility token preserved
      for deterministic replay continuity.


  constitutional_receipt.py:

    canonical: afritech.proof.constitutional_receipt

    status: MIGRATING

    ontology: TOKEN_TO_MODULE_PATH

    replay_safe: true

    description: >
      Compatibility alias preserved for deterministic
      ontology migration normalization.


  trace_reconstructor.py:

    canonical: afritech.trace.trace_reconstructor

    status: MIGRATING

    ontology: TOKEN_TO_MODULE_PATH

    replay_safe: true

    description: >
      Historical replay-trace compatibility alias
      preserved for deterministic canonicalization.


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: PATH_ALIAS_RULE_1

    rule: >
      Alias keys must remain topology-independent
      compatibility identifiers.


  - id: PATH_ALIAS_RULE_2

    rule: >
      Filesystem paths are forbidden as alias keys.


  - id: PATH_ALIAS_RULE_3

    rule: >
      Canonical ontology identity must remain
      independent from repository topology.


  - id: PATH_ALIAS_RULE_4

    rule: >
      Alias normalization must remain deterministic
      and replay-safe.


  - id: PATH_ALIAS_RULE_5

    rule: >
      Forbidden aliases may remain declared for
      governance visibility but are runtime
      inadmissible.


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTIONS
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertions:

  - >
    Canonical ontology identity must remain
    independent from filesystem topology.

  - >
    Alias keys are compatibility artifacts only
    and must never define constitutional identity.

  - >
    Replay-safe canonicalization requires
    deterministic alias normalization.

  - >
    Governance legitimacy derives from canonical
    ontology identity and replay admissibility,
    not from compatibility aliases.

===== ./afritech/architecture/planned_surface_policy.yaml =====
schema: afritech.planned_surfaces_policy.v2

version: 2

metadata:

  authority: ARCHITECTURE
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED

  registry_class: CONSTITUTIONAL_PLANNED_SURFACES_POLICY

  description: >
    Canonical constitutional policy governing PLANNED
    execution surfaces, speculative implementation
    containment, replay admissibility boundaries,
    proof admissibility boundaries, runtime loading
    restrictions, and deterministic ontology-safe
    execution semantics within AfriTech.

    Planned surfaces define:
      - declared architectural intent
      - non-operational execution boundaries
      - replay participation restrictions
      - proof participation restrictions
      - deterministic containment semantics
      - closed-world isolation guarantees
      - ontology-safe declaration semantics
      - implementation legitimacy boundaries

    Planned surfaces are declarative constitutional
    artifacts only.

    Planned surfaces do NOT independently confer:
      - runtime legitimacy
      - replay admissibility
      - proof admissibility
      - execution authority
      - governance authority
      - mutation authority

    Planned-surface participation remains bounded by:
      - admissibility law
      - deterministic ontology resolution
      - replay-safe containment
      - invariant preservation
      - closed-world execution semantics


# ============================================================
# PATH ONTOLOGY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

ontology_alignment:

  canonical_identity_form: module_path

  deterministic_resolution_required: true

  replay_safe_resolution_required: true

  observer_independent_required: true

  forbidden_identity_forms:

    - filesystem_path
    - extension_identity
    - relative_identity
    - symbolic_short_name
    - reflection_based_identity

  description: >
    Planned-surface identity resolution requires canonical
    deterministic module-path ontology semantics.

planned_surfaces:

  executable:

    false

    false

    false

    operationally non-admissible.

# ============================================================

proof_policy:

# ============================================================

# ============================================================

closed_world_alignment:

  isolation_required:

    true

  execution_surface_registration_forbidden:

    true

    true

  reflection_based_binding_forbidden:

    true

    - deterministic_containment
    - replay_safe_isolation
    - observer_independent_resolution
    - ontology_safe_declaration

  description: >
    Planned surfaces must remain isolated from admissible
    execution topology.


# ============================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION TRANSITION POLICY
# ============================================================

transition_policy:

  allowed_transitions:

    - PLANNED -> PARTIAL
    - PLANNED -> DOCUMENTARY
    - PLANNED -> FROZEN

  forbidden_transitions:

    - PLANNED -> IMPLEMENTED
    - PLANNED -> RUNTIME_ACTIVE
    - PLANNED -> REPLAY_ACTIVE

  transition_requirements:

    - explicit_implementation_registration
    - deterministic_surface_validation
    - replay_safety_validation
    - invariant_alignment_validation
    - canonical_identity_validation

  description: >
    Planned surfaces require explicit constitutional
    transition validation before operational participation.


# ============================================================

# ============================================================

determinism_alignment:

  deterministic_runtime_execution:

    forbidden

  deterministic_replay_execution:

    forbidden

  observer_independent_required:

    true

  replay_safe_required:

    true

  forbidden:

    - probabilistic_execution
    - observer_relative_execution
    - runtime_dynamic_resolution
    - speculative_execution

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_containment
    - ontology_safe_isolation
    - replay_safe_non_execution

  description: >
    Planned surfaces must remain operationally inert and
    replay-safe through deterministic containment.


# ============================================================
# SAFETY GUARANTEES
# ============================================================

safety:

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_surface_containment
    - replay_safe_non_execution
    - observer_independent_declaration
    - ontology_safe_resolution
    - closed_world_isolation

  forbid:

    - implicit_runtime_legitimacy
    - speculative_execution_authority
    - filesystem_authority_inference
    - observer_relative_execution
    - ambiguous_surface_resolution

  enforcement:

    violation_action: FAIL_FAST

    replay_violation_action: REPLAY_INVALID

    runtime_violation_action: EXECUTION_REFUSAL


# ============================================================
# FAILURE EFFECTS
# ============================================================

failure_effects:

  planned_runtime_execution:

    effect:

      - runtime_refusal
      - replay_invalid
      - CI_failure


  planned_replay_participation:

    effect:

      - replay_rejection
      - witness_rejection


  planned_proof_generation:

    effect:

      - proof_non_admissible
      - constitutional_receipt_rejection


  reflection_based_binding:

    effect:

      - ontology_failure
      - execution_refusal


  observer_relative_resolution:

    effect:

      - replay_invalid
      - deterministic_failure


# ============================================================
# GLOBAL CONSTITUTIONAL RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: PLANNED_SURFACE_RULE_1
    rule: >
      Planned surfaces are constitutionally declarative and
      operationally non-admissible.

  - id: PLANNED_SURFACE_RULE_2
    rule: >
      Planned surfaces must never participate in:
        - runtime execution
        - replay execution
        - proof generation
        - witness generation
        - constitutional attestation

  - id: PLANNED_SURFACE_RULE_3
    rule: >
      Planned surfaces possess no mutation authority.

  - id: PLANNED_SURFACE_RULE_4
    rule: >
      Planned surfaces must remain isolated under
      closed-world execution semantics.

  - id: PLANNED_SURFACE_RULE_5
    rule: >
      Filesystem artifacts must never independently define
      planned-surface legitimacy.

  - id: PLANNED_SURFACE_RULE_6
    rule: >
      Reflection-based execution binding is constitutionally
      forbidden.

  - id: PLANNED_SURFACE_RULE_7
    rule: >
      Observer-relative execution evaluation is constitutionally
      forbidden.

  - id: PLANNED_SURFACE_RULE_8
    rule: >
      Planned-surface resolution must remain:
        - deterministic
        - replay-safe
        - observer-independent
        - ontology-safe
        - constitutionally bounded


# ============================================================
# CI ENFORCEMENT
# ============================================================

ci_alignment:

  enforced_by:

    - afritech.ci.execution_surface_validation
    - afritech.ci.import_topology_enforcement
    - afritech.ci.replay_validation
    - afritech.ci.witness_validation

  required_checks:

    - no_planned_runtime_execution
    - no_planned_replay_participation
    - no_planned_proof_generation
    - deterministic_identity_validation
    - closed_world_isolation_validation

  failure_mode: CI_FAIL


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  description: >
    Planned surfaces are deterministic declarative
    constitutional artifacts representing architectural
    intent only.

    Planned-surface legitimacy derives exclusively from:
      - canonical ontology declaration
      - deterministic containment semantics
      - replay-safe non-execution
      - closed-world isolation
      - observer-independent resolution

    Planned surfaces must never derive operational legitimacy
    from:
      - filesystem structure
      - runtime activity
      - speculative interpretation
      - observer-relative execution
      - semantic inference

    Planned-surface evaluation must remain:
      - deterministic
      - replay-safe
      - observer-independent
      - ontology-safe
      - constitutionally bounded


===== ./afritech/architecture/sub_enforcement_registry.yaml =====
schema: afritech.sub_enforcement_registry.v1
version: 1
metadata:
  authority: ARCHITECTURE
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED
  registry_class: CONSTITUTIONAL_SUB_ENFORCEMENT_REGISTRY
  parent_registry: afritech/architecture/implementation_registry.yaml
  description: Enforcement, replay, closed-world, safety, and purity-gate rules for
    implementation registry admissibility.
rules:
- id: IMPLEMENTATION_RULE_1
  rule: 'Canonical implementation identity must resolve exclusively through deterministic
    module-path ontology.

    '
- id: IMPLEMENTATION_RULE_2
  rule: 'Filesystem paths must never independently define constitutional implementation
    identity.

    '
- id: IMPLEMENTATION_RULE_3
  rule: "Replay admissibility requires:\n  - deterministic execution\n  - replay-safe\
    \ semantics\n  - invariant preservation\n  - closed-world alignment\n"
- id: IMPLEMENTATION_RULE_4
  rule: "PLANNED implementations must never participate in:\n  - runtime execution\n\
    \  - replay execution\n  - proof generation\n  - witness generation\n"
- id: IMPLEMENTATION_RULE_5
  rule: 'PARTIAL implementations may participate in replay-safe constrained execution
    only when explicitly declared.

    '
- id: IMPLEMENTATION_RULE_6
  rule: 'Observer-relative implementation semantics are constitutionally forbidden.

    '
- id: IMPLEMENTATION_RULE_7
  rule: 'Reflection-based implementation identity resolution is constitutionally forbidden.

    '
replay_alignment:
  replay_safe_states:
  - PARTIAL
  - IMPLEMENTED
  replay_forbidden_states:
  - PLANNED
  - FROZEN
  - DOCUMENTARY
  - FORBIDDEN_ALIAS
  replay_requirements:
  - deterministic_execution
  - replay_safe_semantics
  - invariant_preservation
  - canonical_identity_resolution
  replay_invalidators:
  - observer_relative_execution
  - probabilistic_execution
  - undeclared_execution_surfaces
  - filesystem_identity_resolution
closed_world_alignment:
  required:
  - declared_execution_surfaces
  - deterministic_identity_resolution
  - replay_safe_execution
  - invariant_preservation
  forbidden:
  - implicit_runtime_discovery
  - reflection_based_execution
  - dynamic_runtime_aliasing
  - undeclared_surface_binding
  constitutional_domain:
    included:
    - afritech/
    excluded:
    - node_modules/
    - .venv/
    - build/
    - dist/
    - afriride_system/mobile/*/node_modules/
  external_dependency_rule:
    may_support_execution: true
    may_define_authority: false
    may_define_identity: false
    may_define_replay_truth: false
safety:
  guarantees:
  - deterministic_implementation_resolution
  - replay_safe_execution
  - observer_independent_identity
  - ontology_safe_binding
  - invariant_preserving_execution
  forbid:
  - filesystem_authority_inference
  - speculative_runtime_authority
  - observer_relative_execution
  - probabilistic_execution
  - ambiguous_identity_resolution
  enforcement:
    violation_action: FAIL_FAST
    replay_violation_action: REPLAY_INVALID
    runtime_violation_action: EXECUTION_REFUSAL
registry_consistency_requirements:
  required_implementation_fields:
  - implementation_state
  - category
  - ontology
  - replay_significance
  - canonical_path
  - authority_scope
  - semantic_properties
  - witness_dependencies
  - enforced_by
  - generated_artifacts
  - completed_capabilities
  - forbidden_behaviors
  - description
  single_authority: ARCHITECTURE
  fail_closed_on_missing_fields: true
  forbid_top_level_implementation_entries: true
  forbid_filesystem_identity_authority: true
  forbid_undeclared_execution_surfaces: true
purity_gate:
  command: python3 -m afritech.ci.constitutional_purity_gate
  failure_mode: CI_FAIL
  checks:
  - single_authority
  - implementation_schema_completeness
  - closed_world_boundary_integrity
  - canonical_module_path_identity
  - replay_alignment_consistency
  - proof_admissibility_consistency
  - planned_surface_isolation
  - forbidden_alias_isolation


===== ./afritech/architecture/sub_modules_registry.yaml =====
schema: afritech.sub_modules_registry.v1
version: 1
metadata:
  authority: ARCHITECTURE
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED
  registry_class: CONSTITUTIONAL_SUB_MODULES_REGISTRY
  parent_registry: afritech/architecture/implementation_registry.yaml
  description: Declared AfriTech implementation/module surfaces. This registry answers
    what exists, what is implemented, and which module surfaces are admitted for runtime,
    replay, and proof participation.
implementation_states:
  PLANNED:
    executable: false
    runtime_admissible: false
    replay_admissible: false
    proof_admissible: false
    deterministic_execution: false
    closed_world_aligned: false
    description: 'Constitutionally declared but operationally non-admissible implementation
      state.

      '
  PARTIAL:
    executable: constrained
    runtime_admissible: conditional
    replay_admissible: true
    proof_admissible: true
    deterministic_execution: true
    closed_world_aligned: true
    description: 'Partially implemented deterministic replay-safe implementation state.

      '
  IMPLEMENTED:
    executable: true
    runtime_admissible: true
    replay_admissible: true
    proof_admissible: true
    deterministic_execution: true
    closed_world_aligned: true
    description: 'Fully implemented deterministic replay-safe implementation state.

      '
  FROZEN:
    executable: false
    runtime_admissible: false
    replay_admissible: false
    proof_admissible: false
    deterministic_execution: true
    closed_world_aligned: true
    description: 'Constitutionally declared and explicitly non-operational surface.
      FROZEN surfaces are complete as inert artifacts and may not participate in runtime,
      replay, proof, or witness generation.

      '
  DOCUMENTARY:
    executable: false
    runtime_admissible: false
    replay_admissible: false
    proof_admissible: false
    deterministic_execution: false
    closed_world_aligned: false

===== ./afritech/architecture/surface_authority_registry.yaml =====
schema: afritech.execution_surfaces.v2

version: 2

metadata:

  authority: ARCHITECTURE
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED

  registry_class: CONSTITUTIONAL_EXECUTION_SURFACES

  description: >
    Canonical constitutional execution-surface registry
    governing deterministic execution authority,
    replay participation semantics, mutation authority,
    proof participation, runtime activation legitimacy,
    and closed-world execution topology within AfriTech.

    Execution surfaces define:
      - constitutional execution authority
      - replay participation semantics
      - mutation authority boundaries
      - proof participation boundaries
      - deterministic execution legitimacy
      - ontology-safe surface identity
      - closed-world execution participation
      - invariant-preserving operational authority

    Execution surfaces are constitutional operational
    boundaries only.

    Surface legitimacy does NOT independently confer:
      - governance sovereignty
      - constitutional supremacy
      - unrestricted mutation authority
      - observer-relative execution authority

    Execution participation remains bounded by:
      - admissibility law
      - invariant preservation
      - deterministic execution
      - replay-safe semantics
      - canonical ontology resolution
      - closed-world execution semantics


# ============================================================
# PATH ONTOLOGY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

ontology_alignment:

  canonical_identity_form: module_path

  forbidden_identity_forms:

    - filesystem_path
    - extension_identity
    - relative_identity
    - symbolic_short_name
    - reflection_based_identity

  deterministic_resolution_required: true

  replay_safe_resolution_required: true

  observer_independent_required: true

  description: >
    Execution surface identity resolution requires
    deterministic canonical module-path ontology semantics.


# ============================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION STATES
# ============================================================

implementation_states:

  PLANNED:

    executable: false

    runtime_admissible: false

    replay_participating: false

    proof_admissible: false

    replay_safe: false

    deterministic_execution: false


  PARTIAL:

    executable: constrained

    runtime_admissible: conditional

    replay_participating: conditional

    proof_admissible: conditional

    replay_safe: conditional

    deterministic_execution: conditional


  IMPLEMENTED:

    executable: true

    runtime_admissible: true

    replay_participating: true

    proof_admissible: true

    replay_safe: true

    deterministic_execution: true

  FROZEN:

    executable: false

    runtime_admissible: false

    replay_participating: false

    proof_admissible: false

    replay_safe: true

    deterministic_execution: true


  DOCUMENTARY:

    executable: false

    runtime_admissible: false

    replay_participating: false

    proof_admissible: false

    replay_safe: false

    deterministic_execution: false


  FORBIDDEN_ALIAS:

    executable: false

    runtime_admissible: false

    replay_participating: false

    replay_safe: false

    deterministic_execution: false

# ============================================================
# REPLAY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

replay_alignment:

  replay_safe_states:

    - IMPLEMENTED

  replay_forbidden_states:

    - PLANNED
    - FROZEN
    - DOCUMENTARY
    - FORBIDDEN_ALIAS

  replay_requirements:

    - deterministic_execution
    - replay_traceability
    - witness_admissibility
    - invariant_preservation
    - canonical_identity_resolution

  replay_invalidators:

    - replay_divergence
    - observer_relative_execution
    - speculative_runtime_execution
    - undeclared_execution_surfaces

  description: >
    Replay participation requires constitutionally
    admissible deterministic execution surfaces.


# ============================================================
# MUTATION AUTHORITY POLICY
# ============================================================

mutation_authority_policy:

  allowed_mutation_scopes:

    - epoch_evolution_only
    - proof_artifact_generation_only
    - deterministic_state_transition
    - replay_trace_generation

  forbidden_mutation_scopes:

    - unrestricted_runtime_mutation
    - observer_relative_mutation
    - hidden_mutation
    - probabilistic_mutation
    - undeclared_state_transition

  mutation_requirements:

    - invariant_preservation
    - deterministic_ordering
    - replay_traceability
    - witness_generation

  description: >
    Mutation authority is constitutionally bounded and
    replay-traceable.


# ============================================================
# CLOSED-WORLD ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

closed_world_alignment:

  required:

    - declared_execution_surfaces
    - canonical_identity_resolution
    - deterministic_surface_binding
    - replay_safe_execution

  forbidden:

    - implicit_surface_discovery
    - reflection_based_execution
    - dynamic_runtime_aliasing
    - undeclared_execution_binding

  description: >
    Execution surface admissibility requires closed-world
    execution semantics.


# ============================================================
# SAFETY GUARANTEES
# ============================================================

safety:

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_surface_resolution
    - replay_safe_execution
    - observer_independent_evaluation
    - invariant_preserving_mutation
    - ontology_safe_execution

  forbid:

    - implicit_runtime_legitimacy
    - filesystem_authority_inference
    - speculative_execution_authority
    - observer_relative_execution
    - ambiguous_surface_resolution

  enforcement:

    violation_action: FAIL_FAST

    replay_violation_action: REPLAY_INVALID

    runtime_violation_action: EXECUTION_REFUSAL


# ============================================================
# FAILURE EFFECTS
# ============================================================

failure_effects:

  replay_divergence:

    effect:

      - replay_invalid
      - proof_rejection
      - execution_refusal


  closed_world_violation:

    effect:

      - runtime_refusal
      - ontology_failure


  forbidden_mutation_scope:

    effect:

      - mutation_rejection
      - replay_invalid


  observer_relative_execution:

    effect:

      - proof_non_admissible
      - replay_rejection


  forbidden_alias_resolution:

    effect:

      - identity_rejection
      - CI_failure


# ============================================================
# GLOBAL CONSTITUTIONAL RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: EXECUTION_SURFACE_RULE_1
    rule: >
      Execution surface identity must resolve exclusively
      through canonical module-path ontology semantics.

  - id: EXECUTION_SURFACE_RULE_2
    rule: >
      Runtime activation requires:
        - deterministic execution
        - replay-safe semantics
        - invariant preservation
        - closed-world execution alignment

  - id: EXECUTION_SURFACE_RULE_3
    rule: >
      Mutation authority must remain constitutionally bounded
      and replay-traceable.

  - id: EXECUTION_SURFACE_RULE_4
    rule: >
      Filesystem artifacts must never independently define
      execution-surface legitimacy.

  - id: EXECUTION_SURFACE_RULE_5
    rule: >
      Replay participation requires deterministic replay-safe
      execution semantics.

  - id: EXECUTION_SURFACE_RULE_6
    rule: >
      Reflection-based execution binding is constitutionally
      forbidden.

  - id: EXECUTION_SURFACE_RULE_7
    rule: >
      Observer-relative execution evaluation is constitutionally
      forbidden.

  - id: EXECUTION_SURFACE_RULE_8
    rule: >
      Execution surface resolution must remain:
        - deterministic
        - replay-safe
        - observer-independent
        - ontology-safe
        - constitutionally bounded


# ============================================================
# CI ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

ci_alignment:

  enforced_by:

    - afritech.ci.execution_surface_validation
    - afritech.ci.import_topology_enforcement
    - afritech.ci.replay_validation
    - afritech.ci.witness_validation

  required_checks:

    - canonical_identity_validation
    - replay_safe_surface_validation
    - deterministic_resolution_validation
    - mutation_authority_validation
    - closed_world_validation

  failure_mode: CI_FAIL


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  description: >
    Constitutional execution surfaces govern deterministic
    replay-safe operational participation within AfriTech.

    Surface legitimacy derives exclusively from:
      - canonical ontology resolution
      - deterministic execution
      - replay-safe semantics
      - invariant preservation
      - closed-world execution semantics

    Surface legitimacy must never derive from:
      - filesystem structure
      - runtime activity alone
      - speculative interpretation
      - observer-relative execution
      - semantic inference

    Execution surface resolution must remain:
      - deterministic
      - replay-safe
      - observer-independent
      - ontology-safe
      - constitutionally bounded


===== ./afritech/architecture/surface_implementation_binding.yaml =====
schema: afritech.binding_registry.v2

version: 2

metadata:

  authority: ARCHITECTURE
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED

  registry_class: CONSTITUTIONAL_BINDING_REGISTRY

  description: >
    Canonical constitutional binding registry governing
    replay-safe implementation bindings, execution
    admissibility bindings, runtime activation semantics,
    proof participation semantics, evaluation semantics,
    speculative doctrine containment, and deterministic
    binding resolution within AfriTech.

    Bindings define:
      - implementation legitimacy
      - execution admissibility
      - replay participation
      - proof participation
      - runtime activation authority
      - speculative containment semantics
      - deterministic binding resolution
      - closed-world binding alignment

    Binding legitimacy is constitutional metadata only.

    Binding legitimacy does NOT independently confer:
      - runtime sovereignty
      - governance authority
      - replay authority
      - execution legitimacy
      - constitutional authority

    Binding participation remains bounded by:
      - admissibility law
      - replay admissibility
      - invariant preservation
      - deterministic execution
      - canonical ontology resolution
      - closed-world execution semantics


# ============================================================
# PATH ONTOLOGY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

ontology_alignment:

  canonical_identity_form: module_path

  deterministic_resolution_required: true

  replay_safe_resolution_required: true

  observer_independent_required: true

  forbidden_identity_forms:

    - filesystem_path
    - extension_identity
    - relative_identity
    - symbolic_short_name
    - reflection_based_identity

  description: >
    Binding resolution requires canonical module-path
    ontology semantics.


# ============================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION STATES
# ============================================================

implementation_states:

  PLANNED:

    executable: false

    runtime_admissible: false

    replay_participating: false

    proof_admissible: false

    deterministic_execution: false

    replay_safe: false

    description: >
      Constitutionally declared but operationally
      non-admissible implementation state.


  PARTIAL:

    executable: constrained

    runtime_admissible: conditional

    replay_participating: conditional

    proof_admissible: conditional

    deterministic_execution: conditional

    replay_safe: conditional

    description: >
      Constitutionally constrained partially implemented
      operational state.


  IMPLEMENTED:

    executable: true

    runtime_admissible: true

    replay_participating: true

    proof_admissible: true

    deterministic_execution: true

    replay_safe: true

    description: >
      Fully operational replay-safe deterministic
      implementation state.

  FROZEN:

    executable: false

    runtime_admissible: false

    replay_participating: false

    proof_admissible: false

    deterministic_execution: true

    replay_safe: true

    description: >
      Explicitly non-operational but constitutionally resolved
      surface state. FROZEN bindings are complete by containment
      and cannot execute, replay, emit proof, or emit witnesses.


  DOCUMENTARY:

    executable: false

    runtime_admissible: false

    replay_participating: false

    proof_admissible: false

    deterministic_execution: false

    replay_safe: false

    description: >
      Documentation-only non-operational implementation
      state.


  FORBIDDEN_ALIAS:

    executable: false

    runtime_admissible: false

    replay_participating: false

    proof_admissible: false

    deterministic_execution: false

    replay_safe: false

    description: >
      Constitutionally forbidden non-canonical alias state.

# ============================================================
# REPLAY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

replay_alignment:

  replay_safe_states:

    - IMPLEMENTED

  replay_forbidden_states:

    - PLANNED
    - FROZEN
    - DOCUMENTARY
    - FORBIDDEN_ALIAS

  replay_requirements:

    - deterministic_execution
    - replay_traceability
    - invariant_preservation
    - canonical_identity_resolution

  replay_invalidators:

    - replay_divergence
    - observer_relative_execution
    - speculative_runtime_execution
    - undeclared_execution_surfaces

  description: >
    Replay participation requires constitutionally admissible
    deterministic bindings.


# ============================================================
# CLOSED-WORLD ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

closed_world_alignment:

  required:

    - declared_execution_surfaces
    - canonical_identity_resolution
    - deterministic_binding_resolution
    - replay_safe_execution

  forbidden:

    - implicit_surface_discovery
    - reflection_based_binding
    - dynamic_runtime_aliasing
    - undeclared_execution_binding

  description: >
    Binding admissibility requires closed-world execution
    semantics.


# ============================================================
# SAFETY GUARANTEES
# ============================================================

safety:

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_binding_resolution
    - replay_safe_binding_semantics
    - observer_independent_binding
    - invariant_preserving_execution
    - ontology_safe_resolution

  forbid:

    - implicit_runtime_legitimacy
    - filesystem_authority_inference
    - speculative_execution_authority
    - observer_relative_binding
    - ambiguous_binding_resolution

  enforcement:

    violation_action: FAIL_FAST

    replay_violation_action: REPLAY_INVALID

    runtime_violation_action: EXECUTION_REFUSAL


# ============================================================
# FAILURE EFFECTS
# ============================================================

failure_effects:

  speculative_runtime_execution:

    effect:

      - runtime_refusal
      - replay_invalid
      - proof_rejection


  forbidden_alias_resolution:

    effect:

      - ontology_failure
      - identity_rejection


  replay_divergence:

    effect:

      - replay_invalid
      - deterministic_failure


  closed_world_violation:

    effect:

      - execution_refusal
      - proof_non_admissible


  observer_relative_binding:

    effect:

      - replay_rejection
      - proof_rejection


# ============================================================
# GLOBAL CONSTITUTIONAL RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: BINDING_RULE_1
    rule: >
      Binding identity must resolve exclusively through
      canonical module-path ontology semantics.

  - id: BINDING_RULE_2
    rule: >
      Runtime activation requires:
        - deterministic execution
        - replay-safe semantics
        - invariant preservation
        - closed-world execution alignment

  - id: BINDING_RULE_3
    rule: >
      Speculative bindings must never participate in:
        - runtime execution
        - replay execution
        - witness generation
        - proof generation
        - constitutional attestation

  - id: BINDING_RULE_4
    rule: >
      Filesystem artifacts must never independently define
      binding legitimacy.

  - id: BINDING_RULE_5
    rule: >
      Replay admissibility requires deterministic replay-safe
      binding semantics.

  - id: BINDING_RULE_6
    rule: >
      Reflection-based binding resolution is constitutionally
      forbidden.

  - id: BINDING_RULE_7
    rule: >
      Observer-relative binding evaluation is constitutionally
      forbidden.

  - id: BINDING_RULE_8
    rule: >
      Binding resolution must remain:
        - deterministic
        - replay-safe
        - observer-independent
        - ontology-safe
        - constitutionally bounded


# ============================================================
# CI ENFORCEMENT
# ============================================================

ci_alignment:

  enforced_by:

    - afritech.ci.import_topology_enforcement
    - afritech.ci.execution_surface_validation
    - afritech.ci.replay_validation
    - afritech.ci.witness_validation

  required_checks:

    - canonical_identity_validation
    - replay_safe_binding_validation
    - deterministic_resolution_validation
    - no_speculative_runtime_execution
    - closed_world_validation

  failure_mode: CI_FAIL


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  description: >
    Constitutional bindings govern deterministic replay-safe
    participation semantics within AfriTech.

    Binding legitimacy derives exclusively from:
      - canonical ontology resolution
      - deterministic evaluation
      - replay-safe semantics
      - invariant preservation
      - closed-world execution semantics

    Binding legitimacy must never derive from:
      - filesystem structure
      - runtime activity alone
      - speculative interpretation
      - observer-relative evaluation
      - semantic inference

    Binding resolution must remain:
      - deterministic
      - replay-safe
      - observer-independent
      - ontology-safe
      - constitutionally bounded

===== ./afritech/bootstrap/BOOTSTRAP_AUTHORITY.yaml =====
bootstrap_authority:

  authoritative_entrypoints:

    - afritech.bootstrap.engine.constitutional_bootstrap

  responsibilities:

    - constitutional_bootstrap
    - runtime_initialization
    - invariant_validation
    - semantic_guard_loading
    - replay_guard_loading
    - authority_guard_loading

  forbidden:

    - direct_runtime_execution
    - replay_mutation
    - epoch_mutation
    - speculative_runtime_loading

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_bootstrap_order
    - invariant_prevalidation
    - authority_before_execution

===== ./afritech/ci/CI_AUTHORITY.yaml =====
schema: afritech.ci.authority.v1
status: PROVEN_GOVERNANCE
classification: AUTHORITY_COMPRESSION

canonical_gate:
  id: canonical_constitutional_pipeline
  command: python3 -m afritech.ci.constitutional_pipeline
  authority: CI_ADMISSIBILITY_GATE
  rule: Only the canonical constitutional pipeline defines CI admissibility.

supporting_gates:
  - id: constitutional_validation
    command: python3 -m afritech.ci.constitutional_validation
    role: supporting_validation
  - id: afritech_demo_proof
    command: python3 -m afritech.demo.proof
    role: proof_surface_confirmation
  - id: claim_discipline_validator
    command: python3 -m afritech.ci.claim_discipline_validator
    role: claim_boundary_confirmation
  - id: enforcement_integrity_validator
    command: python3 -m afritech.ci.enforcement_integrity_validator
    role: enforcement_boundary_confirmation

workflow_roles:
  .github/workflows/architecture.yml:
    role: supporting_architecture_adapter
    must_invoke_canonical_gate: true
  .github/workflows/constitutional_ci.yml:
    role: supporting_deep_constitutional_adapter
    must_invoke_canonical_gate: true
  .github/workflows/constitutional_enforcement.yml:
    role: supporting_enforcement_adapter
    must_invoke_canonical_gate: true
  .github/workflows/ga_plus_plus_plus.yml:
    role: supporting_ga_reconstruction_adapter
    must_invoke_canonical_gate: true

rules:
  - id: ci_authority_single_source
    statement: Only canonical_pipeline defines CI admissibility; workflows are adapters or supporting gates.
  - id: no_workflow_truth_redefinition
    statement: Workflows may invoke validators but may not redefine proof truth, invariants, or admissibility.
  - id: no_supporting_gate_independence
    statement: Supporting workflows must invoke the canonical constitutional pipeline before making completion claims.
    

===== ./afritech/ci/completeness_policy.yaml =====
schema: afritech.ci.completeness.v1
status: IMPLEMENTED
authority: CI

rules:

  - id: COMP_001
    rule: No empty YAML nodes allowed
    enforced_by:
      - afritech.ci.yaml_gap_validator
      - afritech.ci.registry_completeness_validator

  - id: COMP_002
    rule: All surfaces must have binding
    enforced_by:
      - afritech.ci.binding_completeness_validator
      - afritech.ci.execution_completeness_validator

  - id: COMP_003
    rule: No PLANNED surfaces in runtime topology
    enforced_by:
      - afritech.ci.surface_state_resolution_validator
      - afritech.ci.partial_planned_audit_validator

  - id: COMP_004
    rule: All surfaces must emit witnesses
    enforced_by:
      - afritech.ci.full_witness_coverage_validator
      - afritech.ci.ast_witness_validator

  - id: COMP_005
    rule: Formal model must match runtime behavior
    enforced_by:
      - afritech.ci.formal_runtime_equivalence_validator
      - afritech.ci.level2_formal_model_validator

failure:
  block_merge: true
  severity: CRITICAL
  action: CI_FAIL

completion:
  empty: false
  active: true
  validator: afritech.ci.completeness_policy_validator


===== ./afritech/ci/execution_completion_matrix.yaml =====
schema: afritech.ci.execution_completion_matrix.v1
status: IMPLEMENTED
authority: CI
surfaces:
  runtime_activation:

===== ./afritech/constitution/AFRITECH_CONSTITUTION_V1.yaml =====
schema: afritech.constitution.ecosystem.v1
version: v1.0
status: LOCKED
authority: FOUNDATIONAL_DOCUMENT
scope: AFRITECH_ECOSYSTEM

foundational_principle: >
  AfriTech exists to help organizations build, govern, execute, and scale
  intelligent systems through proof-aware engineering, executable governance,
  operational capability, and AI-ready enterprise intelligence.

canonical_statement: >
  AfriTech has four constitutional pillars: Deterministic Truth, Orchestration,
  Data Locality, and Observability. AfriTech also has four ecosystem pillars:
  AfriCPPT governs. AfriTPPS executes. AfriProgramming builds. AFRIPower
  explains. The mature AfriTech core model organizes eighteen core pillars
  across constitutional kernel, runtime, distributed scale, governance, and
  human/ecosystem layers.

constitutional_pillars:
  - id: DETERMINISTIC_TRUTH
    name: Deterministic Truth
    constitutional_function: Replay Governance
    purpose: Defines canonical truth through deterministic replay.
    question_answered: What is true?
    authority_boundary: Truth is produced by replay governance, not assertion.
  - id: ORCHESTRATION
    name: Orchestration
    constitutional_function: Replay-Safe Execution
    purpose: Coordinates execution while preserving replay safety.
    question_answered: How does execution proceed safely?
    authority_boundary: Orchestration coordinates execution but does not redefine truth.
  - id: DATA_LOCALITY
    name: Data Locality
    constitutional_function: Compute Near Data
    purpose: Keeps compute near bounded, declared data scope.
    question_answered: Where should computation happen?
    authority_boundary: Data movement must remain declared, bounded, and replay-safe.
  - id: OBSERVABILITY
    name: Observability
    constitutional_function: Explain Without Authority
    purpose: Explains system behavior without creating decision authority.
    question_answered: What happened and why?
    authority_boundary: Observability may explain but must not govern, mutate, or decide.

core_pillar_layers:
  - id: CONSTITUTIONAL_KERNEL
    name: Constitutional Kernel
    purpose: Defines truth.
    legitimacy_boundary: Truth remains narrow and replay-governed.
    pillars:
      - id: DETERMINISTIC_EXECUTION
        name: Deterministic Execution
        summary: Same execution produces same replay and same legitimacy.
      - id: REPLAY_LEGITIMACY
        name: Replay Legitimacy
        summary: Replay reconstructs constitutional truth.
      - id: CLOSED_WORLD_ADMISSIBILITY
        name: Closed-World Admissibility
        summary: Undeclared surfaces are inadmissible.
      - id: CANONICAL_IDENTITY
        name: Canonical Identity
        summary: Identity derives from ontology, not observation.
      - id: CONSTITUTIONAL_AUTHORITY
        name: Constitutional Authority
        summary: Authority must be explicit, bounded, and enforceable.
  - id: RUNTIME
    name: Runtime and Operational
    purpose: Defines lawful execution behavior.
    legitimacy_boundary: Operations remain replay-safe and cannot redefine truth.
    pillars:
      - id: DATA_LOCALITY
        name: Data Locality
        summary: Compute near the data.
      - id: CONTINUITY_PRESERVATION
        name: Continuity Preservation
        summary: Identity survives disruption.
      - id: ENTROPY_CONTAINMENT
        name: Entropy Containment
        summary: External uncertainty cannot redefine truth.
      - id: OBSERVABILITY_ISOLATION
        name: Observability Isolation
        summary: Observation may describe execution but may not mutate legitimacy.
  - id: DISTRIBUTED_SCALE
    name: Distributed Scale
    purpose: Defines lawful scaling.
    legitimacy_boundary: Scaling must converge to replay-equivalent truth.
    pillars:
      - id: PARTITIONED_DETERMINISTIC_SCALE
        name: Partitioned Deterministic Scale
        summary: Scale without replay divergence.
      - id: WITNESS_INTEGRITY
        name: Witness Integrity
        summary: Every legitimacy-bearing action must be reconstructable.
      - id: MULTI_NODE_CONVERGENCE
        name: Multi-Node Convergence
        summary: Distributed execution must converge to replay-equivalent truth.
  - id: GOVERNANCE
    name: Governance and Proof
    purpose: Prevents conceptual inflation.
    legitimacy_boundary: Governance remains evidence-bound and authority-minimal.
    pillars:
      - id: PROOF_BOUND_GOVERNANCE
        name: Proof-Bound Governance
        summary: Claims require executable evidence.
      - id: PRESERVE_OR_ISOLATE
        name: Preserve-or-Isolate
        summary: Unproven expansion must not contaminate proven truth.
      - id: AUTHORITY_COMPRESSION
        name: Authority Compression
        summary: Minimal authority surface, maximal replay verifiability.
  - id: HUMAN_ECOSYSTEM
    name: Human and Ecosystem
    purpose: Defines why the system exists.
    legitimacy_boundary: Ecosystem expansion cannot redefine legitimacy backward.
    pillars:
      - id: HUMAN_CONTINUITY
        name: Human Continuity
        summary: People retain identity, agency, and continuity through disruption.
      - id: ECONOMIC_CONTINUITY
        name: Economic Continuity
        summary: Economic participation survives instability.
      - id: INFRASTRUCTURE_SOVEREIGNTY
        name: Infrastructure Sovereignty
        summary: Critical execution infrastructure remains governable by constitutional truth.

ecosystem_pillars:
  - id: AfriCPPT
    role: GOVERNANCE
    canonical_action: governs
  - id: AfriTPPS
    role: EXECUTION
    canonical_action: executes
  - id: AfriProgramming
    role: ENGINEERING
    canonical_action: builds
  - id: AFRIPower
    role: INTELLIGENCE
    canonical_action: explains

core_definitions:
  afritech:
    role: PARENT_ECOSYSTEM
    definition: >
      An intelligent enterprise operating ecosystem that orchestrates
      governance, execution, intelligence, and engineering to enable
      organizations to build and scale intelligent systems.
  afri_programming:
    role: ENGINEERING
    definition: >
      AfriProgramming is a proof-aware autonomous engineering platform that
      combines AI software agents, software lifecycle automation, verification,
      testing, constitutional governance, and engineering intelligence to build,
      validate, and evolve intelligent software systems.
  afri_cppt:
    role: GOVERNANCE
    definition: >
      A governance and transformation framework that defines and enforces
      constitutional control over systems.
  afri_tpps:
    role: EXECUTION
    definition: >
      An operational capability framework that enables structured execution
      through alignment of technology, processes, people, and skills.
  afri_power:
    role: INTELLIGENCE
    definition: >
      An intelligent enterprise readiness framework that structures content,
      data, and workflows for AI-driven decision-making and automation.

hierarchy:
  root: AfriTech
  layers:
    - id: AfriCPPT
      role: GOVERNANCE
    - id: AfriTPPS
      role: EXECUTION
    - id: AfriProgramming
      role: ENGINEERING
    - id: AFRIPower
      role: INTELLIGENCE

constitutional_rules:
  - AfriProgramming is the core engineering engine.
  - AfriTech is the parent ecosystem.
  - AfriCPPT, AfriTPPS, and AFRIPower operate around and above the engine.

layer_responsibilities:
  AfriProgramming:
    domain: ENGINEERING
    purpose: Builds and verifies software systems.
    question_answered: How do we build it?
    responsibilities:
      - Software engineering.
      - AI engineering.
      - Autonomous code generation.
      - Testing.
      - Verification.
      - CI/CD.
      - Runtime governance.
      - Proof-aware engineering.
      - System implementation.
      - Multi-agent engineering.
      - Codebase intelligence.
      - Autonomous software development lifecycle automation.
      - Constitutional engineering.
      - Sandbox execution.
      - Pull request intelligence.
      - Developer workspace integration.
      - Agent skills marketplace.
      - Security engineering.
      - Explainable engineering.
      - Knowledge graph engineering.
      - Enterprise portfolio management.
      - AfriTech ecosystem integration.
    outputs:
      - Code
      - Tests
      - Validators
      - Runtime Systems
      - Proof Artifacts
      - Software Platforms
  AfriCPPT:
    domain: GOVERNANCE
    purpose: Defines what is allowed.
    question_answered: What should be done?
    responsibilities:
      - Governance.
      - Policy.
      - Constitutional rules.
      - Standards.
      - Compliance.
      - Decision frameworks.
      - Enterprise transformation governance.
      - Risk controls.
      - Authority structures.
    outputs:
      - ADR
      - Invariant
      - Rule
      - Binding
      - Guard
      - Policy
      - Governance Model
    canonical_chain:
      - ADR
      - INVARIANT
      - RULE
      - BINDING
      - GUARD
      - CI
  AfriTPPS:
    domain: EXECUTION
    purpose: Defines how work gets executed.
    question_answered: How should it be executed?
    responsibilities:
      - Operational capability.
      - Process execution.
      - Workflow orchestration.
      - Delivery management.
      - Service operations.
      - Program execution.
      - Capability maturity.
      - Performance management.
    outputs:
      - Capabilities
      - Workflows
      - Processes
      - Programs
      - Operational Models
      - Execution Metrics
  AFRIPower:
    domain: INTELLIGENCE
    purpose: Transforms evidence into intelligence.
    question_answered: What can we learn from it?
    responsibilities:
      - Enterprise intelligence.
      - Analytics.
      - Dashboards.
      - Explainability.
      - Knowledge graphs.
      - Read-only insights.
      - Strategic visibility.
      - Decision support.
    outputs:
      - Insights
      - Dashboards
      - Reports
      - Graph Projections
      - Explanations
      - Enterprise Intelligence Views
  AfriTech:
    domain: ORCHESTRATION
    responsibilities:
      - Integrate all layers.
      - Scale systems across organizations.
      - Ensure alignment with the Foundational Principle.

doctrines:
  governance:
    - All system changes must be traceable.
    - All systems must obey invariants.
    - Governance is executable.
    - Validation is continuous.
    - No uncontrolled evolution is permitted.
  execution:
    - Execution requires alignment across technology, processes, people, and skills.
    - Capability precedes scaling.
    - Work must be structured through defined workflows.
    - Performance must be measurable.
  intelligence:
    - Content must be structured and machine-readable.
    - Systems must be AI-ready by design.
    - Data must form relationships.
    - Automation is mandatory where possible.
    - Insights must drive action.
  afri_programming:
    - All systems originate from intent.
    - Execution is mandatory.
    - Verification is required.
    - Proof is an artifact.
    - Engineering is autonomous but governed.

system_dynamics:
  core_flow:
    - actor: AfriCPPT
      action: defines governance
    - actor: AfriTPPS
      action: executes capability
    - actor: AfriProgramming
      action: builds systems
    - actor: AFRIPower
      action: generates intelligence
    - actor: AfriTech
      action: orchestrates and scales
  feedback_loop:
    - Insights
    - Improvements
    - New Systems
    - Re-verification
  constitutional_law: >
    All systems must be capable of continuous improvement through feedback
    integration.

expansion_rules:
  - All new modules must align with the Foundational Principle.
  - No duplication of responsibilities across layers is permitted.
  - All additions must integrate into the hierarchy.
  - All systems must remain governable via AfriCPPT.
  - All expansions must remain interoperable with AfriProgramming.

example_future_modules:
  - id: AfriFin
    domain: FINANCIAL_SYSTEMS
  - id: AfriLogix
    domain: LOGISTICS_INTELLIGENCE
  - id: AfriGrid
    domain: INFRASTRUCTURE_SYSTEMS

canonical_language:
  Build: Generate systems via AfriProgramming.
  Verify: Prove correctness through computation.
  Govern: Enforce rules through AfriCPPT.
  Execute: Operate through AfriTPPS.
  Intelligence: Learn and optimize via AFRIPower.
  Ecosystem: AfriTech as the orchestrator.


===== ./afritech/constitution/CLAIM_DISCIPLINE.yaml =====
schema: afritech.constitution.claim_discipline.v1
version: 1

allowed_classification: >
  Bounded deterministic continuity validation architecture
  with executable, replay-verifiable operational recovery
  demonstrations under controlled disruption scenarios.

core_rules:
  - id: CLAIM_RULE_001
    rule: No expansion without compression.
    requires:
      - compression_target
      - complexity_justification
  - id: CLAIM_RULE_002
    rule: No claim without executable evidence.
    requires:
      - scope
      - evidence
      - validator
  - id: CLAIM_RULE_003
    rule: No proof without a harder counter-test waiting behind it.
    requires:
      - counter_test

canonical_compression:
  schema: afritech.constitution.compression_basis.v1
  objective: >
    Reduce high-load concepts to a minimal basis without weakening determinism,
    replay validity, or epistemic discipline.
  minimal_basis:
    identity:
      primitive_refs:
        - SEM-ID-001
      preserves:
        - canonical_identity
        - identity_stability_under_replay
    determinism:
      primitive_refs:
        - SEM-DET-001
      preserves:
        - stable_execution_order
        - observer_independent_state
    replay:
      primitive_refs:
        - SEM-RPL-001
      preserves:
        - reconstruction
        - replay_equivalence
    closed_world:
      primitive_refs:
        - SEM-CW-001
      preserves:
        - declared_surface_only
        - bounded_execution_authority
    epistemic_discipline:
      primitive_refs:
        - CLAIM_RULE_001
        - CLAIM_RULE_002
        - CLAIM_RULE_003
      preserves:
        - scoped_claims
        - executable_evidence
        - counter_test_pressure
  reductions:
    admissibility:
      expression: function(identity, determinism, replay, closed_world)
      replaces:
        - replay_admissibility
        - execution_admissibility
        - surface_admissibility
    continuity:
      expression: function(identity, determinism, replay, epistemic_discipline)
      replaces:
        - continuity_integrity
        - operational_recovery_claim
    proof_surface:
      expression: function(replay, closed_world, epistemic_discipline)
      replaces:
        - claim_snapshot
        - proof_package
        - demonstration_summary

forbidden_claim_terms:
  - final form
  - complete crystallization
  - truth system
  - global resilience infrastructure
  - production survivability
  - universal deployment readiness

claims:
  - id: CLAIM_CONTINUITY_001
    statement: Continuity under simulated disruption
    scope: controlled_simulated_disruption
    evidence:
      - afritech.ci.continuity_validator
      - afritech.ci.afriride_continuity_demo_validator
      - python3 -m afritech.demo.proof
    validator: afritech.ci.claim_discipline_validator
    counter_test: hostile_partition_healing_under_byzantine_coordination
    falsification_tests:
      - inject_network_partition
      - inject_duplicate_authority
      - inject_delayed_replay_rejoin
    lineage:
      epoch: 6
      validated_by:
        - afritech.ci.continuity_validator
        - afritech.ci.afriride_continuity_demo_validator

  - id: CLAIM_REPLAY_001
    statement: Deterministic replay for declared evidence paths
    scope: declared_evidence_paths
    evidence:
      - afritech.verify.engine.verify_replay
      - afritech.ci.trace_reconstruction_validator
      - afritech.verify.verify_multi_epoch_replay
    validator: afritech.ci.claim_discipline_validator
    counter_test: cross_epoch_replay_under_protocol_change
    falsification_tests:
      - inject_clock_drift
      - inject_execution_delay
      - inject_mutation_reordering
    lineage:
      epoch: 6
      validated_by:
        - afritech.verify.engine.verify_replay
        - afritech.ci.trace_reconstruction_validator

  - id: CLAIM_AFRIRIDE_001
    statement: AfriRide is a controlled continuity demonstration environment
    scope: afriride_controlled_demo
    evidence:
      - ecosystems.afriride.continuity.index.yaml
      - ecosystems.afriride.continuity.scenarios
      - ecosystems.afriride.tests.continuity
    validator: afritech.ci.claim_discipline_validator
    counter_test: low_connectivity_trip_recovery_with_adversarial_duplicate_acceptance
    falsification_tests:
      - inject_offline_driver_rejoin
      - inject_passenger_state_mutation
      - inject_conflicting_acceptance
    lineage:
      epoch: 6
      validated_by:
        - afritech.ci.afriride_continuity_demo_validator
        - ecosystems.afriride.tests.continuity

new_surfaces:
  - id: SURFACE_DEMO_PROOF_001
    surface: afritech.demo.proof
    compression_target: one_command_human_readable_proof_surface
    complexity_justification: >
      Compresses validator lattice evidence into one bounded proof command and
      one machine-readable output without adding new runtime authority.

  - id: SURFACE_AFRIRIDE_CONTINUITY_001
    surface: ecosystems.afriride.continuity
    compression_target: controlled_mobility_continuity_demo
    complexity_justification: >
      Compresses operational continuity evidence into five mobility disruption
      scenarios that reuse deterministic AfriRide execution and replay.

readiness_boundary:
  global_deployment_readiness_claimed: false
  production_survivability_claimed: false
  distributed_byzantine_consensus_claimed: false

negative_capabilities:
  - id: NEG_CAP_001
    cannot_claim: global_scaling
    reason: No large-scale node coordination or hostile public network evidence.
  - id: NEG_CAP_002
    cannot_claim: non_deterministic_optimization
    reason: Optimization outside deterministic replay scope is not admitted.
  - id: NEG_CAP_003
    cannot_claim: external_runtime_guarantees
    reason: Current evidence is bounded to controlled local proof surfaces.
  - id: NEG_CAP_004
    cannot_claim: byzantine_fault_tolerance
    reason: Byzantine consensus is named as counter-test pressure, not proven capability.

===== ./afritech/constitution/CONTINUITY_PROFILE.yaml =====
schema: afritech.constitution.continuity_profile.v1
version: 1

mission:
  id: CONTINUITY_MISSION_001
  statement: >
    AfriTech is a deterministic continuity execution layer that preserves
    verifiable operations under system disruption.
  additive_to:
    - afritech.constitution.level2_formal_model
  replaces:
    - none

invariant_class:
  id: CONTINUITY_INVARIANTS
  definition: >
    For every admissible disruption, the system preserves identity continuity,
    coordination continuity, economic participation continuity, and
    deterministic recoverability.
  required_properties:
    - identity_continuity
    - coordination_continuity
    - economic_participation_continuity
    - deterministic_recoverability

formal_to_operational_mapping:
  replay:
    operational_meaning: recoverability
    level2_refs:
      - SEM-RPL-001
      - AX-RPL-002
  determinism:
    operational_meaning: trust_under_coordination
    level2_refs:
      - SEM-DET-001
      - AX-DET-001
      - AX-DET-002
  witnesses:
    operational_meaning: verifiable_history
    level2_refs:
      - witness_completeness
      - trace_complete
  closed_world:
    operational_meaning: attack_surface_reduction
    level2_refs:
      - SEM-CW-001
      - AX-CW-001
      - AX-CW-002
  identity_ontology:
    operational_meaning: portability
    level2_refs:
      - SEM-ID-001
      - AX-ID-001
      - AX-ID-003
  mutation_governance:
    operational_meaning: controlled_change
    level2_refs:
      - invariant_preserving
      - mutation_traceability

disruption_classes:
  partition_survival:
    description: Independent partition execution must merge into one canonical replay state.
    required_metrics:
      - execution_admissible
      - replay_converges
      - identity_preserved
      - coordination_rebuilds
      - recovery_deterministic
  replay_divergence_recovery:
    description: Divergent branches must be rejected or repaired before admission.
    required_metrics:
      - execution_admissible
      - replay_converges
      - identity_preserved
      - coordination_rebuilds
      - recovery_deterministic
  identity_drift:
    description: Alias and node disruption must not create canonical identity drift.
    required_metrics:
      - execution_admissible
      - replay_converges
      - identity_preserved
      - coordination_rebuilds
      - recovery_deterministic
  delayed_event_convergence:
    description: Delayed events must converge under canonical causal ordering.
    required_metrics:
      - execution_admissible
      - replay_converges
      - identity_preserved
      - coordination_rebuilds
      - recovery_deterministic
  coordination_rebuild:
    description: Coordinator loss must rebuild deterministically without violating replay.
    required_metrics:
      - execution_admissible
      - replay_converges
      - identity_preserved
      - coordination_rebuilds
      - recovery_deterministic
  offline_admissibility:
    description: Offline local execution may rejoin only when deterministic replay convergence holds.
    required_metrics:
      - execution_admissible
      - replay_converges
      - identity_preserved
      - coordination_rebuilds
      - recovery_deterministic

success_rule:
  valid_if_all:
    - execution_remains_admissible
    - replay_converges
    - identity_preserved
    - coordination_rebuilds
    - recovery_deterministic
  hard_fail_if_any:
    - replay_divergence
    - identity_drift
    - nondeterministic_recovery
    - unbound_coordination_state

validators:
  ci:
    - afritech.ci.continuity_validator
  simulation:
    - afritech.simulation.continuity.runner

failure_effects:
  replay_divergence: CONTINUITY_INVALID
  identity_drift: CONTINUITY_INVALID
  coordination_rebuild_failure: CI_FAIL
  nondeterministic_recovery: HARD_FAIL

constitutional_assertion:
  description: >
    Continuity validation is an additive operational survival layer. It does not
    weaken or replace Level 2 correctness; it requires Level 2 correctness to
    remain true under disruption.


===== ./afritech/constitution/FIVE_INVARIANT_CONTRACT.yaml =====
schema: afritech.constitution.five_invariant_contract.v1
status: PROVEN_GOVERNANCE
authority: afritech.demo.proof
operating_law:
  name: preserve_or_isolate
  internal_domain: preserve_exactly
  external_domain: remain_fully_decoupled
  mixed_or_ambiguous: drift
invariants:
  - id: INV-001
    name: preserve_proof_meaning
    description: Proof semantics must remain unchanged.
  - id: INV-002
    name: preserve_authority_boundaries
    description: Layer authority must remain unchanged.
  - id: INV-003
    name: preserve_afriride_scope
    description: AfriRide remains the only active execution domain.
  - id: INV-004
    name: preserve_claim_discipline
    description: Claims must remain scoped, evidenced, and bounded.
  - id: INV-005
    name: preserve_enforcement_integrity
    description: Enforcement paths must remain intact and uncompromised.
authority_chain:
  - proof_defines_truth
  - core_decides
  - api_exposes
  - polling_confirms
  - websockets_notify
  - apps_display
behavioral_constraint:
  websocket_event: trigger_poll
  ui_update_source: confirmed_api_state


===== ./afritech/constitution/INVARIANTS.yaml =====
schema: afritech.constitution.invariants.v1

meta:
  description: >
    Canonical registry of constitutional invariants.
    Defines execution, replay, and governance constraints.
  authority: registry
  enforcement: REQUIRED
  mutability: FORBIDDEN
  deterministic: true
  replay_safe: true
  ordering: canonical
  canonical_invariant_count: 36


invariants:

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# EXECUTION BOUNDARY
# ---------------------------------------------------------

- id: I1_EXPLICIT_INPUT_BOUNDARY
  category: EXECUTION_BOUNDARY
  runtime_scope: PRE
  description: >
    Execution MUST operate only on explicitly provided RuntimeContext inputs.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    ALL(runtime_context.inputs ARE explicit AND declared)
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I2_EXPLICIT_OUTPUT_BOUNDARY
  category: EXECUTION_BOUNDARY
  runtime_scope: POST
  description: >
    All outputs MUST be fully contained within ExecutionResult.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    outputs subset_of ExecutionResult.outputs
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I14_BOUND_EXECUTION_SURFACE
  category: EXECUTION_BOUNDARY
  runtime_scope: PRE
  description: >
    Execution MUST remain within declared runtime surfaces.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    execution_surface subset_of declared_surfaces
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: false, governance: false }

- id: I15_NO_DYNAMIC_BEHAVIOR_INJECTION
  category: EXECUTION_BOUNDARY
  runtime_scope: PRE
  description: >
    Execution logic MUST NOT be injected dynamically.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    execution_logic IS static_during_runtime
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: false, governance: false }


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# STATE INTEGRITY
# ---------------------------------------------------------

- id: I3_NO_SILENT_MUTATION
  category: STATE_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: DURING
  description: >
    No state mutation without trace visibility.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    ALL(state_changes ARE recorded_in_trace)
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I19_STATE_TRANSITION_EXPLICIT
  category: STATE_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: DURING
  description: >
    All state transitions MUST be declared.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    ALL(state_transitions ARE explicitly_declared)
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I20_NO_HIDDEN_SIDE_EFFECTS
  category: STATE_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: DURING
  description: >
    No hidden side effects allowed.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    effects subset_of observable_trace
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# REPLAY INTEGRITY
# ---------------------------------------------------------

- id: I4_DETERMINISTIC_EXECUTION
  category: REPLAY_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: POST
  description: >
    Identical inputs MUST produce identical outputs and traces.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    inputs₁ = inputs₂ implies outputs₁ = outputs₂ AND trace₁ = trace₂
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I5_REPLAY_REQUIRED
  category: REPLAY_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: REPLAY
  description: >
    All executions MUST be replayable.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    for_all execution exists replay(execution)
  enforcement: { runtime: false, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I6_REPLAY_AUTHORITY
  category: REPLAY_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: REPLAY
  description: >
    Replay results are authoritative.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    replay_result = canonical_truth
  enforcement: { runtime: false, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I7_TRANSCRIPT_COMPLETENESS
  category: REPLAY_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: POST
  description: >
    Execution traces MUST fully reconstruct behavior.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    trace implies reconstruct(execution)
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I8_TRANSCRIPT_HASH_STABILITY
  category: REPLAY_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: REPLAY
  description: >
    Replay hashes MUST remain stable.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    hash(trace₁) = hash(trace₂) IF equivalent_execution
  enforcement: { runtime: false, replay: true, governance: false }


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# CLOSED WORLD
# ---------------------------------------------------------

- id: I9_CLOSED_WORLD
  category: CLOSED_WORLD_ENFORCEMENT
  runtime_scope: PRE
  description: >
    No undeclared external access allowed.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    execution_inputs subset_of declared_inputs
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I10_EXPLICIT_DEPENDENCIES
  category: CLOSED_WORLD_ENFORCEMENT
  runtime_scope: GOVERNANCE
  description: >
    All dependencies MUST be declared.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    dependencies subset_of declared_dependencies
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# GOVERNANCE
# ---------------------------------------------------------

- id: I11_AUTHORITY_DECLARATION
  category: GOVERNANCE_CONTROL
  runtime_scope: GOVERNANCE
  description: >
    Authority MUST be explicitly declared.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    authority ∈ approved_ADRs
  enforcement: { runtime: false, replay: false, governance: true }

- id: I12_NO_AUTHORITY_ESCALATION
  category: GOVERNANCE_CONTROL
  runtime_scope: GOVERNANCE
  description: >
    No component may escalate authority.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    authority_runtime ≤ authority_declared
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: false, governance: true }

- id: I13_ADR_REQUIRED_FOR_MUTATION
  category: GOVERNANCE_CONTROL
  runtime_scope: GOVERNANCE
  description: >
    Changes require ADR approval.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    mutation implies approved_ADR
  enforcement: { runtime: false, replay: false, governance: true }


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# EXECUTION FINALITY
# ---------------------------------------------------------

- id: I16_TOTAL_OUTCOME_DECLARATION
  category: EXECUTION_FINALITY
  runtime_scope: POST
  description: >
    Execution MUST resolve to final outcome.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    execution implies exists final_outcome
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I17_DETERMINISTIC_FAILURE
  category: EXECUTION_FINALITY
  runtime_scope: POST
  description: >
    Failures MUST be reproducible.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    failure(inputs₁) = failure(inputs₂)
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I18_REPLAYABLE_REFUSAL
  category: EXECUTION_FINALITY
  runtime_scope: POST
  description: >
    Refusals MUST be replay-consistent.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    refusal_replay(inputs) = refusal_original(inputs)
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# TEMPORAL INTEGRITY
# ---------------------------------------------------------

- id: I21_DETERMINISTIC_ORDERING
  category: TEMPORAL_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: DURING
  description: >
    Execution ordering MUST be deterministic.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    order(execution₁) = order(execution₂)
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I22_TIME_SOURCE_CONTROL
  category: TEMPORAL_INTEGRITY

  description: >
    Time MUST be controlled and replay-stable.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    time_source ∈ controlled_sources
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }
