  category: DATA_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: DURING
  description: >
    Inputs MUST remain immutable.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    inputs(t0) = inputs(tn)
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: false, governance: false }

- id: I24_OUTPUT_INTEGRITY
  category: DATA_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: POST
  description: >
    Outputs MUST be verifiable.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    outputs ARE deterministic_and_verifiable
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# CLASSIFICATION
# ---------------------------------------------------------

- id: I25_CLASSIFICATION_IS_POST_EXECUTION
  category: CLASSIFICATION_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: REPLAY
  description: >
    Classification occurs after execution.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    classification_time > execution_end
  enforcement: { runtime: false, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I26_CLASSIFICATION_REPLAY_ALIGNMENT
  category: CLASSIFICATION_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: REPLAY
  description: >
    Classification aligns with replay.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    classification = replay_observed_behavior
  enforcement: { runtime: false, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I27_NO_CLASSIFICATION_SIDE_EFFECT
  category: CLASSIFICATION_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: REPLAY
  description: >
    Classification does not modify outputs.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    classification DOES NOT modify(outputs, trace)
  enforcement: { runtime: false, replay: true, governance: false }


# ---------------------------------------------------------

# ---------------------------------------------------------

  runtime_scope: REPLAY
  description: >

  constitutional_assertion: >

  enforcement: { runtime: false, replay: true, governance: false }

  description: >

  description: >

  constitutional_assertion: >
    execution_events subset_of observable_log
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# EXTENDED (SYSTEM HARDENING)
# ---------------------------------------------------------

- id: I31_STRUCTURAL_VALIDATION_REQUIRED
  category: REPLAY_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: PRE
  description: >
    Distributed core types MUST be validated structurally.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    validation_method != isinstance
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: true }

- id: I32_CANONICAL_ORDERING_GLOBAL
  category: TEMPORAL_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: DURING
  description: >
    All records MUST follow canonical ordering.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    records ORDERED_BY (partition_id, sequence, event_id)
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I33_REPLAY_EQUIVALENCE_STRICT
  category: REPLAY_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: REPLAY
  description: >
    Replay MUST reproduce identical results AND traces.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    replay(results, trace) == original(results, trace)
  enforcement: { runtime: false, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I34_PARTITION_ASSIGNMENT_BINDING
  category: DATA_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: PRE
  description: >
    Partition MUST be derived from assignment.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    partition_id == assignment(partition_key)
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }

- id: I35_HASH_CHAIN_CONTINUITY
  category: PROOF_INTEGRITY
  runtime_scope: REPLAY
  description: >
    Execution MUST form a continuous hash chain.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    hash_chain(event → record → batch → result → receipt → trace)
  enforcement: { runtime: false, replay: true, governance: true }

- id: I36_DATA_LOCALITY_BOUND
  category: EXECUTION_BOUNDARY
  runtime_scope: DURING
  description: >
    Execution data access MUST remain within bounded, declared, and localized
    execution scope.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    accessed_data subset_of bounded_execution_scope
  enforcement: { runtime: true, replay: true, governance: false }
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# cross-system-proof-format.yaml (INVARIANTS)
# ---------------------------------------------------------

- id: INVARIANT-014
  description: >
    All proofs must be verifiable independently of their origin system.
  enforcement:
    - Proof must contain all required fields
    - Verification must not rely on internal database state
    - Verification must succeed using only proof + public key

- id: INVARIANT-014B
  description: >
    Proof integrity must be guaranteed via merkle root and signature.
  enforcement:
    - Merkle root must be deterministically reproducible
    - Signature must validate against merkle_root
    - Any tampering must invalidate the proof

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ✅ EXTENDED INVARIANTS (CRITICAL FOR PRODUCTION)
# ---------------------------------------------------------

- id: INVARIANT-014C
  description: >
    Proof structure must conform to the canonical schema defined in ADR‑0014.
  enforcement:
    - Required fields must exist
    - Field types must match schema
    - Unknown critical fields must not break verification

- id: INVARIANT-014D
  description: >
    Public keys must be resolvable via a trusted registry.
  enforcement:
    - public_key_id must exist in registry
    - Missing or invalid key must reject proof

- id: INVARIANT-014E
  description: >
    Proofs must be deterministic and reproducible across systems.
  enforcement:
    - Same input logs must produce identical merkle root
    - Hash algorithm must be consistent

- id: INVARIANT-014F
  description: >
    Proof verification must be side-effect free.
  enforcement:
    - No mutation of proof data during verification
    - No dependency on external mutable state

- id: INVARIANT-014G
  description: >
    Timestamp must ensure temporal validity of proofs.
  enforcement:
    - Timestamp must be ISO-8601 format
    - Optional TTL validation may reject stale proofs

- id: INVARIANT-014H
  description: >
    Cross-system interoperability must be preserved across versions.
  enforcement:
    - Version field must be present
    - Backward compatibility must be maintained or explicitly versioned

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ✅ OPTIONAL SECURITY INVARIANTS (ADVANCED)
# ---------------------------------------------------------

- id: INVARIANT-014I
  description: >
    Proof metadata must not affect cryptographic verification.
  enforcement:
    - Metadata is ignored during hash/signature validation
    - Metadata may enrich context but not affect integrity

- id: INVARIANT-014J
  description: >
    Proof serialization must be canonical.
  enforcement:
    - JSON must be normalized (ordering, encoding)
    - No non-deterministic serialization allowed

- id: INVARIANT-019
  category: GOVERNANCE_BINDING
  runtime_scope: GOVERNANCE
  description: >
    AfriTech Constitution v1.0 doctrine must only become executable through
    declared governance bindings and must not silently become runtime authority.
  constitutional_assertion: >
    constitution_v1_doctrine -> declared_binding_required AND runtime_authority = false
  enforcement:
    runtime: false
    replay: false
    governance: true

  enforced_by:
    - bootstrap/validators/invariant_validator.py
    - runtime/replay/replay_engine.py

  witnesses:
    - execution_result_hash
    - replay_hash

  tests:
    - tests/replay/test_determinism.py

authority: CONSTITUTION

===== ./afritech/constitution/canonical/SEMANTIC_DEPENDENCIES.yaml =====
dependencies:

  CONCEPT_REPLAY_ADMISSIBILITY:
    depends_on:
      - CONCEPT_DETERMINISTIC_EXECUTION
      - CONCEPT_AUTHORITY_DECLARATION

  CONCEPT_ADMISSIBILITY:
    depends_on:
      - CONCEPT_AUTHORITY_DECLARATION
      - CONCEPT_CLOSED_WORLD

===== ./afritech/constitution/canonical/concepts/admissibility.yaml =====
schema: afritech.concept.admissibility.v1

version: 1

metadata:
  authority: CONSTITUTION
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED
  concept_class: CANONICAL_CONCEPT
  description: >
    Canonical constitutional definition of admissibility within AfriTech.

    Admissibility governs whether a surface, execution artifact,
    replay artifact, transcript, receipt, mutation, or runtime
    action may constitutionally participate in:
      - runtime execution
      - replay verification
      - governance evaluation
      - constitutional enforcement
      - proof generation

    Admissibility is a constitutional validation property.


# ============================================================
# ✅ CANONICAL CONCEPT IDENTITY
# ============================================================

concept:
  id: CONCEPT_ADMISSIBILITY
  canonical_name: admissibility

  # ✅ REQUIRED NORMALIZED FIELD
  name: admissibility

  # ✅ REQUIRED NORMALIZED FIELD
  domain:
    - runtime
    - replay
    - governance
    - enforcement

  constitutional_domain:
    - runtime
    - replay
    - governance
    - proof
    - enforcement

  authoritative_sources:
    - afritech.constitution.invariants_semantics
    - afritech.architecture.execution_surfaces
    - afritech.architecture.enforcement_matrix
    - afritech.architecture.implementation_registry
    - afritech.epoch.epoch_semantics


# ============================================================
# ✅ CORE DEFINITION
# ============================================================

definition:

  # ✅ REQUIRED NORMALIZED FIELD
  summary: >
    Admissibility defines whether an entity may constitutionally
    participate in execution, replay, governance, or enforcement.

  # ✅ REQUIRED NORMALIZED FIELD
  requires:
    - canonical_identity_resolution
    - deterministic_execution
    - execution_surface_declaration
    - invariant_alignment
    - replay_legitimacy
    - authority_alignment

  # ✅ REQUIRED NORMALIZED FIELD
  forbidden_when:
    - identity_is_ambiguous
    - execution_surface_is_undeclared
    - replay_legitimacy_fails
    - invariant_violation_exists
    - forbidden_alias_detected
    - authority_boundary_violated
    - speculative_surface_used_operationally

  canonical_statement: >
    Admissibility is the constitutional determination that an
    artifact, execution surface, runtime action, replay trace,
    governance declaration, or enforcement surface may lawfully
    participate within the AfriTech constitutional system.


# ============================================================
# ✅ NORMALIZED CI PROJECTION (CRITICAL)
# ============================================================

enforcement_points:
  - afritech.ci.witness_validator
  - afritech.ci.import_topology_enforcement
  - afritech.ci.semantic_concept_validator

witness_requirements:
  - replay_witness
  - execution_witness
  - transcript_witness
  - mutation_witness

replay_requirements:
  - deterministic_reconstruction
  - invariant_preservation
  - canonical_identity_resolution
  - replay_traceability


# ============================================================
# ✅ EXTENDED SEMANTIC MODEL (UNCHANGED STRUCTURE)
# ============================================================

domains:

  runtime:
    requires:
      - implementation_state_admissible
      - execution_surface_declared
      - invariant_alignment
      - replay_safe_operation
    forbidden:
      - speculative_execution
      - forbidden_alias_resolution

  replay:
    requires:
      - deterministic_trace_resolution
      - canonical_module_identity
      - replay_safe_implementation_state
    forbidden:
      - observer_relative_resolution
      - speculative_replay

  governance:
    requires:
      - replay_verifiable_observation
      - canonical_identity_resolution
      - invariant_alignment
    forbidden:
      - semantic_reinterpretation
      - runtime_execution_control

  enforcement:
    requires:
      - canonical_enforcement_identity
      - deterministic_enforcement_resolution
    forbidden:
      - reflective_policy_loading
      - runtime_policy_mutation


# ============================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

implementation_alignment:

  admissible_states:
    runtime:
      - PARTIAL
      - IMPLEMENTED
    replay:
      - PARTIAL
      - IMPLEMENTED

  forbidden_states:
    - PLANNED
    - DOCUMENTARY
    - FORBIDDEN_ALIAS

  description: >
    Implementation legitimacy contributes to admissibility
    but does not independently confer constitutional authority.


# ============================================================
# PATH ONTOLOGY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

path_alignment:

  canonical_identity_form: module_path

  forbidden_identity_forms:
    - filesystem_path
    - extension_identity
    - relative_identity
    - conceptual_identity


# ============================================================
# CLOSED-WORLD ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

closed_world_alignment:

  undeclared_surface_effect: HARD_FAIL

  forbidden:
    - implicit_surface_discovery
    - reflection_based_execution
    - speculative_runtime_execution


# ============================================================
# FAILURE EFFECTS
# ============================================================

failure_effects:

  runtime:
    inadmissible_effect:
      - runtime_rejection
      - execution_block

  replay:
    inadmissible_effect:
      - replay_invalid
      - transcript_rejection

  governance:
    inadmissible_effect:
      - governance_declaration_invalid

  enforcement:
    inadmissible_effect:
      - enforcement_rejection
      - CI_failure


# ============================================================
# RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: ADMISSIBILITY_RULE_1
    rule: >
      Admissibility requires canonical identity resolution.

  - id: ADMISSIBILITY_RULE_2
    rule: >
      Filesystem artifacts must never confer admissibility.

  - id: ADMISSIBILITY_RULE_3
    rule: >
      Replay admissibility requires deterministic semantics.

  - id: ADMISSIBILITY_RULE_4
    rule: >
      Speculative surfaces are constitutionally inadmissible.


# ============================================================
# ✅ CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  # ✅ REQUIRED FIELD FOR VALIDATOR
  statement: >
    Only canonically identified, deterministic, and
    replay-admissible entities may participate in AfriTech.

  # ✅ FULL DOCTRINAL DESCRIPTION PRESERVED
  description: >
    Constitutional admissibility is the deterministic and
    observer-independent validation boundary governing lawful
    participation within the AfriTech constitutional system.

    Admissibility derives from:
      - canonical identity
      - deterministic execution semantics
      - replay legitimacy
      - invariant alignment
      - constitutional authority boundaries

schema: afritech.concept.authority_declaration.v1

version: 1

metadata:

    - runtime
    - replay
    - governance
    - enforcement
    - proof

    - replay
    - governance
    - proof
    - enforcement
    - epoch

    is explicitly established, constrained, and validated.

    - canonical_identity_resolution
    - admissibility_alignment
    - deterministic_resolution
    - replay_legitimacy
    - invariant_alignment

    - implicit_authority
    - filesystem_identity_as_authority
    - speculative_authority
    - identity_ambiguity

    which authority is explicitly defined and bounded.

  runtime:
    allowed:
      - deterministic_execution
    forbidden:
      - speculative_execution
      - governance_runtime_override

  replay:
    allowed:
      - deterministic_reconstruction
    forbidden:
      - replay_mutation
      - speculative_replay

  governance:
    allowed:
      - replay_bound_observation
    forbidden:
      - runtime_execution_control

  enforcement:
    allowed:
      - validation
    forbidden:
      - runtime_policy_mutation

  proof:
    allowed:
      - receipt_generation
    forbidden:
      - speculative_proof

  authority_safe_states:

    - DOCUMENTARY
    - FORBIDDEN_ALIAS

  canonical_identity_form: module_path

  forbidden_identity_forms:
    - filesystem_path
    - extension_identity
    - conceptual_identity

  undeclared_surface_effect: HARD_FAIL

  forbidden:
    - implicit_surface_discovery
    - reflection_based_execution
    - speculative_runtime_execution


# ============================================================

  replay_requirements:
    - deterministic_resolution
    - replay_safe_execution

  replay_invalidators:
    - replay_divergence
    - observer_relative_execution

  runtime:
    authority_violation_effect:
      - runtime_rejection

  replay:
    authority_violation_effect:
      - replay_invalid

  governance:
    authority_violation_effect:
      - governance_invalid

  enforcement:
    authority_violation_effect:
      - CI_failure

  proof:
    authority_violation_effect:
      - proof_invalid


# ============================================================
# RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: AUTHORITY_RULE_1
    rule: >
      Authority must always be explicitly declared.

  - id: AUTHORITY_RULE_2
    rule: >
      Filesystem artifacts must never confer authority.

  - id: AUTHORITY_RULE_3
    rule: >
      Governance authority is declarative, not operational.

  - id: AUTHORITY_RULE_4
    rule: >
      Runtime authority is bounded by admissibility.

  # ✅ REQUIRED

    Authority must be explicit, deterministic, and admissible
    under constitutional execution rules.

  # ✅ FULL DOCTRINE
  description: >
    Authority derives only from canonical declaration,
    admissible execution surfaces, deterministic replay
    legitimacy, and invariant alignment.

===== ./afritech/constitution/canonical/concepts/closed_world.yaml =====
schema: afritech.concept.closed_world.v1

version: 1

metadata:
  authority: CONSTITUTION
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED
  concept_class: CANONICAL_CONCEPT
  description: >
    Canonical constitutional definition of closed-world execution semantics within AfriTech.


# ============================================================
# ✅ CONCEPT (NORMALIZED + FULL)
# ============================================================

concept:

  id: CONCEPT_CLOSED_WORLD
  canonical_name: closed_world

  # ✅ REQUIRED BY VALIDATOR
  name: closed_world

  # ✅ REQUIRED BY VALIDATOR
  domain:
    - runtime
    - replay
    - governance
    - enforcement
    - proof
    - execution_surfaces

  constitutional_domain:
    - runtime
    - replay
    - governance
    - enforcement
    - proof
    - execution_surfaces


# ============================================================
# ✅ DEFINITION (NORMALIZED + FULL)
# ============================================================

definition:

  # ✅ REQUIRED
  summary: >
    Closed-world semantics restrict execution to explicitly
    declared and admissible surfaces only.

  # ✅ REQUIRED
  requires:
    - canonical_surface_declaration
    - deterministic_identity_resolution
    - admissibility_alignment
    - replay_legitimacy
    - invariant_alignment

  # ✅ REQUIRED
  forbidden_when:
    - undeclared_surface_execution_exists
    - implicit_surface_discovery_exists
    - reflection_based_execution_exists
    - speculative_runtime_execution_exists
    - dynamic_execution_aliasing_exists
    - observer_relative_resolution_exists

  canonical_statement: >
    Closed-world semantics require all execution to derive
    exclusively from declared constitutional surfaces.


# ============================================================
# ✅ NORMALIZED CI PROJECTION (CRITICAL)
# ============================================================

enforcement_points:
  - afritech.ci.import_topology_enforcement
  - afritech.ci.execution_surface_validation
  - afritech.ci.witness_validator
  - afritech.ci.semantic_concept_validator

witness_requirements:
  - execution_witness
  - replay_witness
  - witness_bundle

replay_requirements:
  - deterministic_surface_reconstruction
  - execution_trace_integrity
  - no_hidden_dependencies


# ============================================================
# ✅ EXTENDED SEMANTIC MODEL (PRESERVED)
# ============================================================

execution_surfaces:

  runtime:
    requires:
      - declared_execution_surface
      - invariant_alignment
    forbidden:
      - speculative_runtime_execution
      - undeclared_runtime_surfaces

  replay:
    requires:
      - deterministic_resolution
      - canonical_identity
    forbidden:
      - replay_mutation
      - observer_relative_reconstruction

  governance:
    requires:
      - replay_verifiable_observation
    forbidden:
      - governance_runtime_execution
      - speculative_authority

  enforcement:
    requires:
      - canonical_enforcement_identity
      - deterministic_resolution
    forbidden:
      - reflective_policy_loading
      - ontology_self_modification

  proof:
    requires:
      - deterministic_trace_alignment
    forbidden:
      - speculative_attestation


# ============================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

implementation_alignment:

  runtime_safe_states:
    - PARTIAL
    - IMPLEMENTED

  replay_safe_states:
    - PARTIAL
    - IMPLEMENTED

  forbidden_states:
    - PLANNED
    - DOCUMENTARY
    - FORBIDDEN_ALIAS


# ============================================================
# PATH ONGTOLOGY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

path_alignment:

  canonical_identity_form: module_path

  forbidden_identity_forms:
    - filesystem_path
    - extension_identity
    - conceptual_identity
    - relative_identity


# ============================================================
# REPLAY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

replay_alignment:

  replay_requirements:
    - deterministic_execution
    - invariant_preservation

  replay_invalidators:
    - replay_divergence
    - dynamic_surface_aliasing


# ============================================================
# IMPORT TOPOLOGY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

import_topology_alignment:

  enforcement_surface:
    - afritech.ci.import_topology_enforcement

  guarantees:
    - deterministic_import_validation
    - execution_surface_isolation

  forbidden:
    - speculative_runtime_imports
    - dynamic_import_reflection


# ============================================================
# GUARANTEES
# ============================================================

guarantees:

  - deterministic_execution_topology
  - replay_safe_execution_boundaries
  - observer_independent_resolution
  - execution_surface_isolation


# ============================================================
# FAILURE EFFECTS
# ============================================================

failure_effects:

  undeclared_surface_execution:
    effect:
      - runtime_rejection
      - replay_invalid
      - CI_failure

  speculative_runtime_execution:
    effect:
      - runtime_rejection
      - replay_invalid

  dynamic_surface_aliasing:
    effect:
      - replay_divergence
      - execution_non_determinism


# ============================================================
# RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: CLOSED_WORLD_RULE_1
    rule: >
      All execution must derive from declared execution surfaces.

  - id: CLOSED_WORLD_RULE_2
    rule: >
      Undeclared execution surfaces are inadmissible.

  - id: CLOSED_WORLD_RULE_3
    rule: >
      Reflection-based execution is forbidden.

  - id: CLOSED_WORLD_RULE_4
    rule: >
      Speculative execution is forbidden.


# ============================================================
# ✅ CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION (NORMALIZED + FULL)
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  # ✅ REQUIRED FIELD
  statement: >
    Execution within AfriTech must occur strictly within
    explicitly declared and admissible surfaces.

  # ✅ PRESERVED DOCTRINE
  description: >
    AfriTech operates exclusively under closed-world
    execution semantics. All execution and replay must derive
    from explicitly declared, deterministic, and admissible
    surfaces. No execution legitimacy may emerge from implicit
    discovery, filesystem structure, or speculative reasoning.


===== ./afritech/constitution/canonical/concepts/data_locality.yaml =====
schema: afritech.concept.data_locality.v1

version: 1

metadata:
  authority: CONSTITUTION
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED
  concept_class: CANONICAL_CONCEPT
  description: >
    Canonical constitutional definition of bounded data-locality semantics.

concept:
  id: CONCEPT_DATA_LOCALITY
  canonical_name: data_locality
  name: data_locality
  domain:
    - runtime
    - replay
    - enforcement
    - execution_surfaces

  constitutional_domain:
    - runtime
    - replay
    - enforcement
    - execution_surfaces

definition:
  summary: >
    Data locality requires execution to operate near the data it depends on,
    inside bounded declared surfaces, using predictable and replay-safe access
    patterns.

  requires:
    - bounded_data_access
    - surface_scoped_data
    - deterministic_access_patterns
    - partition_affinity
    - declared_working_set_limits

  forbidden_when:
    - random_data_access
    - cross_surface_data_dependency
    - unbounded_working_set
    - undeclared_partition_hop
    - dynamic_data_discovery

  canonical_statement: >
    Data locality is bounded execution context: accessed data, surfaces, and
    partitions must remain inside declared execution scope.

enforcement_points:
  - afritech.guards.data_locality_guard
  - afritech.ci.invariant_validator
  - afritech.ci.semantic_concept_validator
  - afritech.ci.execution_completeness_validator

witness_requirements:
  - locality_trace
  - scheduler_trace
  - execution_witness
  - replay_witness
  - transcript_witness
  - locality_scope_witness

replay_requirements:
  - deterministic_access_scope
  - stable_partition_affinity
  - declared_surface_scope
  - bounded_working_set

rules:
  - id: LOCALITY_RULE_1
    rule: Execution must operate within bounded data scope
  - id: LOCALITY_RULE_2
    rule: Cross-surface data access must be explicit and replay-safe
  - id: LOCALITY_RULE_3
    rule: Partition hops must preserve stable locality affinity
  - id: LOCALITY_RULE_9
    rule: Work stealing is permitted only within same partition and NUMA boundary

constitutional_assertion:
  statement: >
    accessed_data subset_of bounded_execution_scope and execution_surface
    subset_of declared_surfaces.

schema: afritech.concept.deterministic_execution.v1

version: 1

metadata:
  authority: CONSTITUTION
  status: ACTIVE

    Canonical constitutional definition of deterministic execution semantics.

concept:

  id: CONCEPT_DETERMINISTIC_EXECUTION
  canonical_name: deterministic_execution

  # ✅ REQUIRED BY VALIDATOR
  name: deterministic_execution

  # ✅ REQUIRED BY VALIDATOR
  domain:
    - runtime
    - replay
    - governance
    - proof
    - enforcement
    - transcript

  constitutional_domain:
    - runtime
    - replay
    - governance
    - proof
    - enforcement
    - transcript


# ============================================================
# ✅ DEFINITION (NORMALIZED + FULL)
# ============================================================

definition:

  # ✅ REQUIRED
  summary: >
    Deterministic execution guarantees identical results under
    identical constitutional inputs and declared execution conditions.

  # ✅ REQUIRED
  requires:
    - canonical_identity_resolution
    - deterministic_import_resolution
    - invariant_preservation
    - replay_safe_execution
    - admissible_execution_surfaces
    - observer_independent_resolution
    - controlled_mutation_boundaries

  # ✅ REQUIRED
  forbidden_when:
    - observer_relative_execution
    - speculative_runtime_execution
    - undeclared_execution_surfaces
    - dynamic_surface_aliasing
    - reflection_based_execution
    - uncontrolled_mutation
    - ambiguous_identity_resolution

  canonical_statement: >
    Deterministic execution requires replay-equivalent outcomes
    under equivalent constitutional conditions.

# ============================================================

  - afritech.ci.witness_validator

  - observer_independent_resolution
  - deterministic_replay_reconstruction
  - invariant_preserving_execution


# ============================================================

failure_effects:

    effect:
      - replay_invalid
      - receipt_rejection

  observer_relative_execution:
    effect:
      - determinism_failure
      - replay_invalid

  uncontrolled_mutation:
    effect:
      - invariant_violation
      - replay_divergence


# ============================================================
# RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: DETERMINISM_RULE_1
    rule: >
      Equivalent inputs must produce identical outputs.

  - id: DETERMINISM_RULE_2
    rule: >
      Observer-relative execution is forbidden.

  - id: DETERMINISM_RULE_3
    rule: >
      Reflection-based execution is forbidden.


# ============================================================
# ✅ CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION (NORMALIZED + FULL)
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  # ✅ REQUIRED
  statement: >
    Execution must be deterministic, replay-safe, and invariant-preserving.

  # ✅ FULL DOCTRINE
  description: >
    Deterministic execution defines the constitutional requirement
    that all admissible execution produces replay-equivalent,
    observer-independent results under equivalent conditions.

    Determinism derives from canonical identity, invariant
    preservation, replay-safe execution, and controlled mutation.

    Execution must never depend on observer identity, filesystem
    structure, speculative behavior, or undeclared surfaces.


===== ./afritech/constitution/canonical/concepts/mutation_traceability.yaml =====
schema: afritech.concept.mutation_traceability.v1

version: 1

metadata:
  authority: CONSTITUTION
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED
  concept_class: CANONICAL_CONCEPT
  description: >
    Canonical constitutional definition of mutation traceability.


# ============================================================
# ✅ CONCEPT (NORMALIZED + FULL)
# ============================================================

concept:

  id: CONCEPT_MUTATION_TRACEABILITY
  canonical_name: mutation_traceability

  name: mutation_traceability

  # ✅ ✅ FIX APPLIED HERE
  domain:
    - runtime
    - replay
    - proof
    - enforcement
    - transcript
    - state   # ✅ corrected

  constitutional_domain:
    - runtime
    - replay
    - proof
    - enforcement
    - transcript
    - state   # ✅ corrected


# ============================================================
# ✅ DEFINITION
# ============================================================

definition:

  summary: >
    Mutation traceability ensures all state changes are explicitly
    declared, deterministically ordered, and replay-reconstructable.

  requires:
    - canonical_mutation_identity
    - deterministic_mutation_ordering
    - replay_traceability
    - invariant_preservation
    - admissible_mutation_surface
    - observer_independent_resolution
    - mutation_gateway_authorization

  forbidden_when:
    - silent_mutation
    - undeclared_mutation_surface
    - speculative_mutation
    - observer_relative_mutation
    - uncontrolled_runtime_mutation
    - reflection_based_mutation
    - ambiguous_identity_resolution

  canonical_statement: >
    Mutation traceability requires all mutation to be explicitly
    attributable, deterministically ordered, and replay-reconstructable.


# ============================================================
# ✅ NORMALIZED CI PROJECTION
# ============================================================

enforcement_points:
  - afritech.ci.mutation_validation
  - afritech.ci.replay_validation
  - afritech.ci.witness_validator
  - afritech.ci.semantic_concept_validator
  - afritech.ci.import_topology_enforcement

witness_requirements:
  - mutation_witness
  - transcript_witness
  - execution_witness
  - witness_bundle

replay_requirements:
  - deterministic_mutation_ordering
  - replay_trace_reconstruction
  - invariant_preservation
  - mutation_equivalence
  - no_missing_mutation_steps


# ============================================================
# ✅ EXTENDED SEMANTIC MODEL (UNCHANGED)
# ============================================================

domains:

  runtime:
    requires:
      - declared_mutation_gateway
      - replay_traceability
      - deterministic_ordering
      - invariant_preservation
    forbidden:
      - silent_runtime_mutation
      - speculative_runtime_mutation
      - observer_relative_mutation

  replay:
    requires:
      - deterministic_mutation_reconstruction
      - canonical_mutation_identity
      - transcript_alignment
    forbidden:
      - replay_mutation_override
      - replay_divergence

  proof:
    requires:
      - deterministic_mutation_trace
      - invariant_alignment
    forbidden:
      - speculative_attestation
      - undocumented_mutation_paths

  enforcement:
    requires:
      - deterministic_validation
      - mutation_legitimacy_alignment
    forbidden:
      - reflective_policy_mutation
      - uncontrolled_mutation_generation


# ============================================================
# MUTATION AUTHORITY
# ============================================================

mutation_authority:

  canonical_mutation_gateway:
    - afritech.kernel.constitutional_gateway

  mutation_requirements:
    - explicit_mutation_declaration
    - replay_traceability
    - invariant_preservation
    - deterministic_ordering

  forbidden:
    - implicit_mutation
    - undeclared_mutation_authority
    - speculative_mutation_authority


# ============================================================
# TRACE REQUIREMENTS
# ============================================================

trace_requirements:

  required_trace_fields:
    - mutation_identity
    - execution_surface
    - deterministic_order
    - replay_trace_reference
    - invariant_alignment_state
    - epoch_reference

  forbidden_trace_properties:
    - observer_relative_state
    - speculative_context
    - undocumented_mutation_origin


# ============================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

implementation_alignment:

  mutation_safe_states:
    - PARTIAL
    - IMPLEMENTED

  forbidden_states:
    - PLANNED
    - DOCUMENTARY
    - FORBIDDEN_ALIAS


# ============================================================
# CLOSED-WORLD ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

closed_world_alignment:

  required:
    - declared_mutation_surfaces
    - deterministic_mutation_resolution

  forbidden:
    - implicit_mutation_discovery
    - reflection_based_mutation
    - speculative_runtime_mutation


# ============================================================
# REPLAY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

replay_alignment:

  replay_requirements:
    - deterministic_mutation_ordering
    - replay_traceability
    - invariant_preservation

  replay_invalidators:
    - replay_divergence
    - observer_relative_mutation


# ============================================================
# GUARANTEES
# ============================================================

guarantees:
  - replay_traceable_mutation
  - deterministic_mutation_ordering
  - invariant_preserving_mutation
  - observer_independent_mutation
  - replay_safe_state_evolution


# ============================================================
# FAILURE EFFECTS
# ============================================================

failure_effects:

  silent_mutation:
    effect:
      - replay_invalid
      - invariant_violation

  undeclared_mutation_surface:
    effect:
      - runtime_rejection
      - CI_failure

  replay_divergence:
    effect:
      - replay_rejection
      - receipt_non_admissible


# ============================================================
# RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: MUTATION_RULE_1
    rule: >
      All mutation must be replay-traceable and deterministically ordered.

  - id: MUTATION_RULE_2
    rule: >
      Mutation must derive from declared mutation gateways.

  - id: MUTATION_RULE_3
    rule: >
      Silent mutation is forbidden.

  - id: MUTATION_RULE_4
    rule: >
      Reflection-based mutation is forbidden.


# ============================================================
# ✅ CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  statement: >
    All mutation must be deterministic, traceable, and replay-reconstructable.

  description: >
    Mutation traceability ensures that all state transitions are
    explicitly declared, deterministically ordered, and fully
    reconstructable under replay conditions.

    Mutation must never emerge from implicit, speculative, or
    observer-relative sources. All mutation must preserve invariants
    and maintain constitutional execution boundaries.

===== ./afritech/constitution/canonical/concepts/replay_admissibility.yaml =====
schema: afritech.concept.replay_admissibility.v1

version: 1

metadata:
  authority: CONSTITUTION
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED
  concept_class: CANONICAL_CONCEPT
  description: >
    Canonical constitutional definition of replay admissibility.


# ============================================================
# ✅ CONCEPT (NORMALIZED + FULL)
# ============================================================

concept:

  id: CONCEPT_REPLAY_ADMISSIBILITY
  canonical_name: replay_admissibility

  name: replay_admissibility

  domain:
    - runtime
    - replay
    - proof
    - transcript
    - enforcement
    - governance   # ✅ FIXED (was admission)

  constitutional_domain:
    - runtime
    - replay
    - proof
    - transcript
    - enforcement
    - governance   # ✅ FIXED


# ============================================================
# ✅ DEFINITION
# ============================================================

definition:

  summary: >
    Replay admissibility determines whether an execution can be
    fully and deterministically reconstructed under equivalent
    constitutional conditions.

  requires:
    - deterministic_execution
    - canonical_module_identity
    - replay_safe_execution_surfaces
    - invariant_preservation
    - deterministic_mutation_ordering
    - replay_traceability
    - admissible_execution_topology
    

  forbidden_when:
    - probabilistic_replay
    - partial_reconstruction
    - observer_dependent_replay
    - replay_divergence
    - speculative_runtime_execution
    - undeclared_execution_surfaces
    - dynamic_surface_aliasing
    - uncontrolled_mutation

  canonical_statement: >
    Replay admissibility ensures deterministic reconstruction
    with invariant preservation and replay equivalence.


# ============================================================
# ✅ NORMALIZED CI PROJECTION
# ============================================================

enforcement_points:
  - afritech.ci.replay_validation
  - afritech.ci.import_topology_enforcement
  - afritech.ci.execution_surface_validation
  - afritech.ci.determinism_validation
  - afritech.ci.witness_validator
  - afritech.ci.semantic_concept_validator

witness_requirements:
  - replay_witness
  - execution_witness
  - transcript_witness
  - mutation_witness
  - witness_bundle
  - constitutional_receipt

replay_requirements:
  - deterministic_trace_reconstruction
  - invariant_preservation
  - replay_equivalence
  - bundle_hash_consistency
  - receipt_hash_consistency
  - full_trace_completeness
  - observer_independence


# ============================================================
# ✅ EXTENDED SEMANTIC MODEL
# ============================================================

domains:

  runtime:
    requires:
      - admissible_execution_surfaces
      - deterministic_runtime_execution
      - invariant_preservation
    forbidden:
      - speculative_runtime_execution
      - observer_relative_execution

  replay:
    requires:
      - deterministic_trace_resolution
      - canonical_surface_identity
      - transcript_alignment
    forbidden:
      - probabilistic_reconstruction
      - replay_divergence

  proof:
    requires:
      - deterministic_receipt_generation
      - replay_legitimacy
    forbidden:
      - speculative_attestation
      - undocumented_execution_paths

  enforcement:
    requires:
      - deterministic_validation
      - canonical_identity_resolution
    forbidden:
      - reflective_policy_loading
      - ontology_self_modification


# ============================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

implementation_alignment:

  replay_safe_states:
    - PARTIAL
    - IMPLEMENTED

  replay_forbidden_states:
    - PLANNED
    - DOCUMENTARY
    - FORBIDDEN_ALIAS


# ============================================================
# PATH ONTOLOGY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

path_alignment:

  canonical_identity_form: module_path

  forbidden_identity_forms:
    - filesystem_path
    - extension_identity
    - conceptual_identity
    - relative_identity


# ============================================================
# CLOSED-WORLD ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

closed_world_alignment:

  required:
    - declared_execution_surfaces
    - deterministic_surface_resolution

  forbidden:
    - implicit_surface_discovery
    - reflection_based_execution
    - speculative_runtime_execution


# ============================================================
# DETERMINISM ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

determinism_alignment:

  required:
    - deterministic_execution
    - deterministic_mutation_ordering
    - deterministic_receipt_generation

  forbidden:
    - observer_relative_execution
    - probabilistic_reconstruction


# ============================================================
# GUARANTEES
# ============================================================

guarantees:
  - deterministic_reconstruction
  - replay_safe_execution
  - observer_independent_replay
  - invariant_preserving_replay
  - transcript_equivalence
  - deterministic_receipt_generation


# ============================================================
# FAILURE EFFECTS
# ============================================================

failure_effects:

  replay_divergence:
    effect:
      - replay_invalid
      - receipt_rejection

  probabilistic_replay:
    effect:
      - replay_non_admissible
      - proof_invalid

  observer_dependent_replay:
    effect:
      - replay_invalid

  uncontrolled_mutation:
    effect:
      - replay_invalid
      - invariant_violation


# ============================================================
# RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: REPLAY_RULE_1
    rule: >
      Replay must be fully deterministic and reproducible.

  - id: REPLAY_RULE_2
    rule: >
      Partial replay reconstruction is forbidden.

  - id: REPLAY_RULE_3
    rule: >
      Observer-dependent replay is forbidden.

  - id: REPLAY_RULE_4
    rule: >
      Probabilistic replay is forbidden.


# ============================================================
# ✅ CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  statement: >
    Only fully deterministic, invariant-preserving executions
    are admissible for replay.

  description: >
    Replay admissibility defines the constitutional boundary
    for lawful deterministic reconstruction.

    Replay-valid execution must derive from canonical identity,
    deterministic execution, admissible topology, invariant
    preservation, and replay-safe mutation ordering.

    No replay legitimacy may emerge from probabilistic,
    partial, observer-relative, or speculative execution.

meta:
  authority: constitutional
  domain: replay
  invariants: [SEM-RPL-001]
axioms:
  - id: AX-RPL-001
    guarantees: [SEM-RPL-001]
    depends_on: [AX-DET-001]
    statement: Replay reconstructs state from recorded history.
    formal:
      operator: IMPLIES
      operands:
        lhs: replay(history)
        rhs: reconstructable_state
  - id: AX-RPL-002
    guarantees: [SEM-RPL-001]
    depends_on: [AX-RPL-001, AX-DET-002]
    statement: Replay state equals original execution state for valid history.
    formal:
      operator: EQUIVALENT
      operands: [state(replay(history)), state(original_execution(history))]
  - id: AX-RPL-003
    guarantees: [SEM-RPL-001]
    depends_on: [AX-RPL-001, AX-RPL-002]
    statement: Only valid history is replay-admissible.
    formal:
      operator: IMPLIES
      operands:
        lhs: valid_history
        rhs: replay_admissible
evaluation:
  replay_safe: true
  proof_relevant: true

===== ./afritech/constitution/evolution/anticipatory_suppression_policy.yaml =====
schema: afritech.anticipatory_suppression_policy.v1
classification: autonomous_constitutional_anticipatory_suppression_policy
authority: PREVENTIVE_CLOSURE_CONTAINMENT_ONLY
inherits_policy: afritech.predictive_future_space_policy.v1

lock: >
  Anticipatory analysis may warn. Constitutional possibility cannot be
  preemptively closed. Prediction informs. Risk informs. Authority decides.

required_payload_fields:
  - admissible_future_space
  - authority_disclaimer
  - divergence_visibility
  - future_space_scope
  - instability_classification
  - ratification_status
  - replay_reference
  - uncertainty_disclosure

allowed_actions:
  - compare_alternative_futures
  - expose_instability_risk
  - identify_preventive_closure_pressure
  - preserve_authorized_divergence_visibility
  - surface_uncertainty
  - recommend_authority_review

forbidden_drift:
  - close_admissible_future_space
  - convert_risk_into_exclusion
  - suppress_authorized_divergence
  - treat_instability_as_illegitimacy
  - treat_uncertainty_as_inadmissibility

required_authority_disclaimer: >
  This anticipatory analysis warns about instability and uncertainty. It does
  not suppress, close, or delegitimize admissible constitutional futures.

===== ./afritech/constitution/evolution/canonical_explanation_schema.yaml =====
schema: afritech.canonical_explanation.v1
classification: canonical_explanation_schema
authority: EXPLANATION_ONLY

purpose: >
  Define how compatibility, replay, lifecycle, and governance state may be
  explained to humans and tools without creating new semantics.

source_authorities:
  - replay
  - validators
  - registries
  - artifacts

forbidden_authority:
  - governance_enforcement
  - legitimacy_definition
  - replay_truth_validation
  - semantic_reinterpretation

status_values:
  - green
  - yellow
  - red
  - unknown

source_ref_types:
  - artifact
  - registry
  - replay
  - validator

allowed_actions:
  - expand
  - inspect
  - link_to_source
  - recommend_next_check

forbidden_actions:
  - infer_legitimacy
  - mutate_replay_meaning
  - override_validator
  - redefine_truth

explanation_record:
  id:
    type: string
    required: true
  status:
    type: enum
    values:
      - green
      - yellow
      - red
      - unknown
    required: true
  summary:
    type: string
    required: true
  source_refs:
    type: list
    required: true
    item_shape:
      type:
        type: enum
        values:
          - artifact
          - registry
          - replay
          - validator
        required: true
      name:
        type: string
        required: true
      result:
        type: string
        required: true
  allowed_actions:
    type: list
    values:
      - expand
      - inspect
      - link_to_source
      - recommend_next_check
    required: true
  forbidden_actions:
    type: list
    values:
      - infer_legitimacy
      - mutate_replay_meaning
      - override_validator
      - redefine_truth
    required: true

canonical_examples:
  compatibility_green:
    id: compatibility.health.current
    status: green
    summary: Compatibility lifecycle metadata is valid and no translator burden is present.
    source_refs:
      - type: validator
        name: afritech.ci.epoch_lifecycle_validator
        result: passed
      - type: registry
        name: epoch_lifecycle_registry
        result: active_epochs=1
    allowed_actions:
      - expand
      - inspect
      - link_to_source
      - recommend_next_check
    forbidden_actions:
      - infer_legitimacy
      - mutate_replay_meaning
      - override_validator
      - redefine_truth

lock: >
  Explanations may summarize evidence. Explanations may not become evidence.

classification: cognitive_salience_policy

  max_primary_concerns: 1
  max_recommended_next_checks: 1
  max_secondary_concerns: 3
  max_source_explanation_records: 5

  may_infer_legitimacy: false
  may_override_deterministic_ordering: false

  - salience_type

  - source_signal

===== ./afritech/constitution/evolution/predictive_future_space_policy.yaml =====
schema: afritech.predictive_future_space_policy.v1
classification: autonomous_constitutional_predictive_future_space_policy
authority: PREDICTIVE_REVIEW_ONLY

lock: >
  Prediction informs. Risk informs. Authority decides.
  Forecasts may not choose the future. Risk may not erase the future.
  AfriTech can see possible futures without being captured by them.

required_context:
  - authority_disclaimer
  - constitution_reference
  - future_space_scope
  - prediction_model_version
  - ratification_status
  - replay_reference
  - risk_model_version
  - validator_reference

predictive_governance_surfaces:
  - inevitability_forecasting
  - inevitability_reinforcement_monitoring
  - anticipatory_suppression_analysis

invariant_separations:
  - awareness_authority
  - forecasting_future_selection
  - prediction_destiny
  - probability_legitimacy
  - risk_exclusion
  - uncertainty_illegitimacy

allowed_predictive_actions:
  - analyze_uncertainty
  - compare_future_scenarios
  - detect_reinforcement_loops
  - expose_prediction_pressure
  - forecast_trajectories
  - model_instability
  - reveal_lock_in_dynamics
  - simulate_constitutional_pressure
  - surface_convergence_pressure
  - warn_about_future_risk

forbidden_predictive_actions:
  - collapse_constitutional_plurality
  - convert_prediction_into_legitimacy
  - convert_risk_into_exclusion
  - operationally_select_constitutional_futures
  - operationalize_inevitability
  - pre_ratify_constitutional_destiny
  - silently_narrow_admissible_futures
  - suppress_admissible_constitutional_futures

inevitability_forecasting:
  required_preservation:
    - forecast_uncertainty
    - future_plurality
    - probability_authority_separation
    - replay_reference
    - ratification_status
  forbidden_drift:
    - prediction_derived_legitimacy
    - probabilistic_destiny
    - future_space_collapse
    - high_probability_future_as_authority

inevitability_reinforcement_monitoring:
  required_detection:
    - forecast_amplification
    - operational_future_preselection
    - prediction_driven_resource_concentration
    - recommendation_pressure
    - salience_amplification
    - self_fulfilling_legitimacy
  forbidden_drift:
    - forecast_confidence_to_operational_priority
    - predictive_optimization_as_authority
    - resource_routing_to_predicted_legitimacy
    - self_fulfilling_future_selection

anticipatory_suppression_analysis:
  required_preservation:
    - admissible_future_space
    - alternative_governance_trajectories
    - authorized_divergence
    - constitutional_experimentation_space
    - low_confidence_constitutional_futures
    - uncertainty_legitimacy_separation
  forbidden_drift:
    - preventive_future_closure
    - risk_derived_illegitimacy
    - suppression_of_authorized_divergence
    - uncertainty_based_exclusion

required_authority_disclaimer: >
  This predictive artifact informs review by describing future pressure,
  uncertainty, and reinforcement risk. It does not select, suppress, ratify,
  or legitimize constitutional futures.

===== ./afritech/constitution/evolution/temporal_cognition_policy.yaml =====
schema: afritech.temporal_cognition_policy.v1
classification: temporal_cognition_policy
authority: TEMPORAL_CONSISTENCY_ONLY

lock: >
  Evolution may improve cognition. Evidence meaning must persist.
  Operational reality must remain time-stable.

required_context:
  - authority_disclaimer
  - cognitive_complexity_budget_version
  - composition_schema_version
  - evidence_id
  - explanation_schema_version
  - salience_policy_version
  - summary_template_version

  - ai_model_version
  - epoch_context
  - translator_context

temporal_drift_surfaces:
  - ai_model_upgrade
  - epoch_evolution
  - policy_evolution
  - summary_template_evolution
  - translator_evolution
  - ui_redesign

temporal_explanation_equivalence:
  required_preservation:
    - authority_boundary
    - explanation_identifiers
    - replay_implications
    - source_references
    - status_meaning
    - validator_semantics
  forbidden_changes:
    - changing_replay_mismatch_meaning_through_wording
    - hiding_old_hard_failures_in_modernized_summaries
    - presenting_translated_explanation_as_original_evidence
    - red_failure_to_yellow_warning_without_source_change
    - removing_source_evidence_from_newer_explanations

  required_visible_versions:
    - ai_salience_model_version_when_ai_is_used
    - ordering_rule_version
    - salience_policy_version
    - urgency_classification_version
  forbidden_changes:
    - ai_only_reprioritization
    - hidden_salience_weighting_change
    - silent_priority_downgrade
    - silent_urgency_inflation
    - visual_redesign_hides_critical_historical_failures

  required_preservation:
    - authority_disclaimers
    - composition_source_ids
    - narrative_template_version
    - summarization_policy_version
    - transformation_steps
  forbidden_changes:
    - advisory_narrative_to_evidence
    - adding_synthetic_legitimacy
    - changing_incident_meaning_without_source_change
    - inferring_new_causality
    - removing_omitted_detail_indicators

ai_temporal_stability:
  required_fields:
    - advisory_status
    - authority_disclaimer
    - model_identifier
    - omitted_detail_indicator
    - prompt_or_template_version
    - salience_policy_version
    - source_explanation_ids
  forbidden_changes:
    - changing_hard_failure_severity
    - creating_model_version_dependent_operational_reality
    - hiding_old_evidence
    - inferring_legitimacy_from_old_evidence
    - synthesizing_replay_truth

temporal_ordering_stability:
  allowed_evolution:
    - accessibility_ordering_with_preserved_semantics
    - clearer_grouping_with_preserved_source_ids
    - new_declared_tie_breaker
    - role_scoped_ordering_with_declared_policy_version
  forbidden_evolution:
    - ai_model_preference_ordering
    - dashboard_redesign_ordering_without_declaration
    - hidden_priority_rewrite
    - removing_hard_failures_from_top_level_historical_view
    - runtime_order_dependent_historical_replay_display

forbidden_temporal_drift:
  - ai_model_cognition_instability
  - authority_disclaimer_loss
  - epoch_context_loss
  - explanation_reinterpretation
  - hard_failure_softening
  - narrative_divergence
  - salience_drift
  - source_reference_loss
  - summary_drift
  - translator_context_loss

required_authority_disclaimer: >
  This cognition artifact presents evidence under declared temporal context.
  It does not validate truth or define legitimacy.


===== ./afritech/constitution/invariants_semantics.yaml =====
# =========================================================
# AFRITECH INVARIANT SEMANTICS REGISTRY
# =========================================================

schema: afritech.constitution.invariants.semantics.v1

meta:
  description: >
    Semantic interpretation layer for constitutional invariants.

    This file provides controlled semantic annotations used for:
      - audit understanding
      - classification alignment
      - documentation
      - tooling interpretation

    This layer DOES NOT introduce behavior.
    Execution authority remains in IR and runtime layers.

  authority: derived
  mutability: GOVERNED
  deterministic: true
  replay_safe: true


# =========================================================
# SEMANTIC DEFINITIONS
# =========================================================

semantics:

  # =====================================================
  # EXECUTION BOUNDARY
  # =====================================================

  I1_EXPLICIT_INPUT_BOUNDARY:
    meaning: input_is_closed_set
    interpretation: >
      Execution depends strictly on declared RuntimeContext inputs.
    semantic_tags: [boundary, input, determinism]

  I2_EXPLICIT_OUTPUT_BOUNDARY:
    meaning: output_is_fully_declared
    interpretation: >
      Execution produces only explicitly declared outputs.
    semantic_tags: [boundary, output, containment]

  I14_BOUND_EXECUTION_SURFACE:
    meaning: execution_surface_is_closed
    interpretation: >
      Execution cannot escape defined runtime boundaries.
    semantic_tags: [surface, isolation]

  I15_NO_DYNAMIC_BEHAVIOR_INJECTION:
    meaning: execution_is_static
    interpretation: >
      Behavior cannot be modified during execution.
    semantic_tags: [immutability, safety]


  # =====================================================
  # STATE INTEGRITY
  # =====================================================

  I3_NO_SILENT_MUTATION:
    meaning: mutation_requires_visibility
    interpretation: >
      All state changes must be observable in trace.
    semantic_tags: [mutation, traceability]

  I19_STATE_TRANSITION_EXPLICIT:
    meaning: transitions_are_declared
    interpretation: >
      Every state transition must be formally declared.
    semantic_tags: [state, transitions]

  I20_NO_HIDDEN_SIDE_EFFECTS:
    meaning: no_untracked_effects
    interpretation: >
      No hidden or implicit side effects exist.
    semantic_tags: [effects, trace]


  # =====================================================
  # REPLAY INTEGRITY
  # =====================================================

  I4_DETERMINISTIC_EXECUTION:
    meaning: same_input_same_output
    interpretation: >
      Execution must always produce identical outcomes for identical inputs.
    semantic_tags: [determinism, replay]

  I5_REPLAY_REQUIRED:
    meaning: execution_must_be_replayable
    interpretation: >
      System must support full replay of all executions.
    semantic_tags: [replay, validation]

  I6_REPLAY_AUTHORITY:
    meaning: replay_is_source_of_truth
    interpretation: >
      Replay defines behavioral correctness.
    semantic_tags: [authority, replay]

  I7_TRANSCRIPT_COMPLETENESS:
    meaning: trace_is_complete
    interpretation: >
      Execution trace must fully describe behavior.
    semantic_tags: [trace, completeness]

  I8_TRANSCRIPT_HASH_STABILITY:
    meaning: hash_is_stable
    interpretation: >
      Replay hashes must remain identical across runs.
    semantic_tags: [hash, determinism]


  # =====================================================
  # CLOSED WORLD
  # =====================================================

  I9_CLOSED_WORLD:
    meaning: no_external_dependency
    interpretation: >
      Execution cannot depend on undeclared external systems.
    semantic_tags: [closed_world, dependency]

  I10_EXPLICIT_DEPENDENCIES:
    meaning: dependencies_are_declared
    interpretation: >
      All dependencies must be explicitly defined.
    semantic_tags: [dependency, declaration]


  # =====================================================
  # GOVERNANCE
  # =====================================================

  I11_AUTHORITY_DECLARATION:
    meaning: authority_is_declared
    interpretation: >
      Authority must be formally defined via governance.
    semantic_tags: [authority, governance]

  I12_NO_AUTHORITY_ESCALATION:
    meaning: no_privilege_escalation
    interpretation: >
      Components cannot exceed authorized roles.
    semantic_tags: [security, governance]

  I13_ADR_REQUIRED_FOR_MUTATION:
    meaning: change_requires_adr
    interpretation: >
      System evolution requires governance approval.
    semantic_tags: [change_management]


  # =====================================================
  # EXECUTION FINALITY
  # =====================================================

  I16_TOTAL_OUTCOME_DECLARATION:
    meaning: outcome_is_complete
    interpretation: >
      Every execution must resolve fully.
    semantic_tags: [finality]

  I17_DETERMINISTIC_FAILURE:
    meaning: failure_is_reproducible
    interpretation: >
      Failures must be consistent and replayable.
    semantic_tags: [failure, replay]

  I18_REPLAYABLE_REFUSAL:
    meaning: refusal_is_stable
    interpretation: >
      Refusals must be replay-consistent.
    semantic_tags: [refusal]


  # =====================================================
  # TEMPORAL INTEGRITY
  # =====================================================

  I21_DETERMINISTIC_ORDERING:
    meaning: order_is_stable
    interpretation: >
      Execution ordering must not vary between runs.
    semantic_tags: [ordering]

  I22_TIME_SOURCE_CONTROL:
    meaning: time_is_controlled
    interpretation: >
      Time must be deterministic and controlled.
    semantic_tags: [time]


  # =====================================================
  # DATA INTEGRITY
  # =====================================================

  I23_INPUT_IMMUTABILITY:
    meaning: inputs_are_readonly
    interpretation: >
      Inputs must not change during execution.
    semantic_tags: [data, immutability]

  I24_OUTPUT_INTEGRITY:
    meaning: outputs_are_valid
    interpretation: >
      Outputs must be correct and verifiable.
    semantic_tags: [data, verification]


  # =====================================================
  # CLASSIFICATION
  # =====================================================

  I25_CLASSIFICATION_IS_POST_EXECUTION:
    meaning: classification_after_execution
    interpretation: >
      Classification cannot affect execution.
    semantic_tags: [classification]

  I26_CLASSIFICATION_REPLAY_ALIGNMENT:
    meaning: classification_matches_replay
    interpretation: >
      Classification must reflect replay reality.
    semantic_tags: [classification, replay]

  I27_NO_CLASSIFICATION_SIDE_EFFECT:
    meaning: classification_is_pure
    interpretation: >
      Classification cannot modify outputs.
    semantic_tags: [purity]


  # =====================================================
  # PROOF
  # =====================================================

  I28_PROOF_OF_CONTINUITY:
    meaning: replay_proves_continuity
    interpretation: >
      Replay guarantees system consistency over time.
    semantic_tags: [proof]

  I29_HASH_VERIFIABILITY:
    meaning: hash_validates_execution
    interpretation: >
      Hashes must verify trace integrity.
    semantic_tags: [hash, proof]

  I30_OBSERVABILITY_COMPLETENESS:
    meaning: system_is_fully_observable
    interpretation: >
      All relevant events must be captured.
    semantic_tags: [observability]


# =========================================================
# SEMANTIC GUARANTEES
# =========================================================

guarantees:
  semantic_layer_non_authoritative: true
  execution_defined_in_ir: true
  replay_truth_preserved: true
  classification_is_non_interfering: true

===== ./afritech/constitution/level2_formal_model.yaml =====
schema: afritech.constitution.level2.formal_model.v1

meta:
  authority: constitutional
  role: formal_consistency_model
  level: 2
  scope: GA++++
  mutability: GOVERNED
  closed_world: true

system:
  id: AFRITECH-L2-GA-PLUS-PLUS-PLUS-PLUS
  tuple: [Sigma, A, I, T, E, R]
  definition: "S = (Sigma, A, I, T, E, R)"
  state_space:
    symbol: Sigma
    fields: [epoch, registry, surfaces, invariants, history]
    uniqueness_law: "state = f(event_history)"
  semantic_atoms:
    - SEM-DET-001
    - SEM-RPL-001
    - SEM-CW-001
    - SEM-ID-001
    - SEM-ADM-001
  runtime_projection:
    - I1_EXPLICIT_INPUT_BOUNDARY
    - I2_EXPLICIT_OUTPUT_BOUNDARY
    - I3_NO_SILENT_MUTATION
    - I4_DETERMINISTIC_EXECUTION
    - I5_REPLAY_REQUIRED
    - I9_CLOSED_WORLD
  transition_system:
    symbol: T
    signature: "(state, event, epoch) -> (state_prime, proof)"
    deterministic: true
    invariant_preserving: true
    guard_filtered: true
  execution_rules:
    symbol: E
    closed_world: true
    deterministic: true
    declared_surface_required: true
  replay_operator:
    symbol: R
    signature: "history -> reconstructed_receipt"
    requires:
      - execution_chain_hash
      - deterministic_execution_chain
      - transcript_hash
      - mutation_trace_hash
      - replay_hash
      - receipt_hash

theorems:
  - id: L2-THM-001
    name: deterministic_state_uniqueness
    statement: "For every admissible history there is exactly one system state."
    requires_atoms: [SEM-DET-001]
    requires_axioms: [AX-DET-001, AX-DET-002]
    requires_invariants: [I4_DETERMINISTIC_EXECUTION, I21_DETERMINISTIC_ORDERING]
    requires_witnesses: [execution_chain_hash, replay_hash]
    verifier: afritech.verify.verify_execution_lineage

  - id: L2-THM-002
    name: safe_transition_closure
    statement: "Every admitted transition preserves constitutional invariants."
    requires_atoms: [SEM-DET-001, SEM-ADM-001]
    requires_axioms: [AX-DET-003, AX-ADM-003]
    requires_invariants: [I3_NO_SILENT_MUTATION, I19_STATE_TRANSITION_EXPLICIT, I20_NO_HIDDEN_SIDE_EFFECTS]
    requires_witnesses: [mutation_trace_hash, receipt_hash]
    verifier: afritech.ci.trace_reconstruction_validator

  - id: L2-THM-003
    name: admissibility_safety
    statement: "Admissibility implies deterministic, replay-valid, invariant-aligned execution."
    requires_atoms: [SEM-ADM-001, SEM-DET-001, SEM-RPL-001, SEM-ID-001, SEM-CW-001]
    requires_axioms: [AX-ADM-001, AX-ADM-002, AX-ADM-003]
    requires_invariants: [I1_EXPLICIT_INPUT_BOUNDARY, I2_EXPLICIT_OUTPUT_BOUNDARY, I9_CLOSED_WORLD]
    requires_witnesses: [normalized_expression_hash, proof_hash, receipt_hash]
    verifier: afritech.ci.receipt_validator

  - id: L2-THM-004
    name: no_undeclared_behavior
    statement: "All valid behavior is confined to declared semantic and execution surfaces."
    requires_atoms: [SEM-CW-001]
    requires_axioms: [AX-CW-001, AX-CW-002, AX-CW-003]
    requires_invariants: [I9_CLOSED_WORLD, I14_BOUND_EXECUTION_SURFACE, I15_NO_DYNAMIC_BEHAVIOR_INJECTION]
    requires_witnesses: [receipt_hash]
    verifier: afritech.ci.semantic_kernel_validator

  - id: L2-THM-005
    name: strong_determinism
    statement: "Identical admissible inputs produce identical execution and replay receipts."
    requires_atoms: [SEM-DET-001, SEM-RPL-001]
    requires_axioms: [AX-DET-001, AX-DET-002, AX-DET-003, AX-RPL-002]
    requires_invariants: [I4_DETERMINISTIC_EXECUTION, I7_TRANSCRIPT_COMPLETENESS, I8_TRANSCRIPT_HASH_STABILITY]
    requires_witnesses: [deterministic_execution_chain, transcript_hash, replay_hash]
    verifier: afritech.verify.verify_execution_lineage

  - id: L2-THM-006
    name: replay_soundness
    statement: "Replay is the canonical truth test for a valid execution history."
    requires_atoms: [SEM-RPL-001, SEM-DET-001]
    requires_axioms: [AX-RPL-001, AX-RPL-002, AX-RPL-003]
    requires_invariants: [I5_REPLAY_REQUIRED, I6_REPLAY_AUTHORITY, I28_PROOF_OF_CONTINUITY]
    requires_witnesses: [execution_chain_hash, transcript_hash, mutation_trace_hash, replay_hash]
    verifier: afritech.verify.verify_multi_epoch_replay

  - id: L2-THM-007
    name: identity_stability
    statement: "Canonical identity is unique and preserved under replay."
    requires_atoms: [SEM-ID-001, SEM-RPL-001]
    requires_axioms: [AX-ID-001, AX-ID-002, AX-ID-003]
    requires_invariants: [I10_EXPLICIT_DEPENDENCIES, I11_AUTHORITY_DECLARATION]
    requires_witnesses: [replay_hash, receipt_hash]
    verifier: afritech.ci.identity_validator

  - id: L2-THM-008
    name: mutation_completeness
    statement: "Every admitted state mutation has a reconstructable trace."
    requires_atoms: [SEM-RPL-001, SEM-ADM-001]
    requires_axioms: [AX-RPL-001, AX-ADM-003]
    requires_invariants: [I3_NO_SILENT_MUTATION, I19_STATE_TRANSITION_EXPLICIT, I29_HASH_VERIFIABILITY]
    requires_witnesses: [mutation_trace_hash, transcript_hash]
    verifier: afritech.ci.trace_reconstruction_validator

  - id: L2-THM-009
    name: temporal_consistency
    statement: "Epoch lineage is monotonic, immutable after reseal, and replay-preserving."
    requires_atoms: [SEM-RPL-001, SEM-DET-001]
    requires_axioms: [AX-RPL-003, AX-DET-002]
    requires_invariants: [I13_ADR_REQUIRED_FOR_MUTATION, I28_PROOF_OF_CONTINUITY, I29_HASH_VERIFIABILITY]
    requires_witnesses: [replay_hash, receipt_hash]
    verifier: afritech.verify.verify_multi_epoch_replay

  - id: L2-THM-010
    name: system_consistency
    statement: "The system cannot produce two conflicting valid states for the same admitted history."
    requires_atoms: [SEM-DET-001, SEM-RPL-001, SEM-CW-001, SEM-ID-001, SEM-ADM-001]
    requires_axioms: [AX-DET-002, AX-RPL-002, AX-ADM-003, AX-CW-003, AX-ID-003]
    requires_invariants: [I4_DETERMINISTIC_EXECUTION, I5_REPLAY_REQUIRED, I9_CLOSED_WORLD, I24_OUTPUT_INTEGRITY]
    requires_witnesses: [execution_chain_hash, transcript_hash, mutation_trace_hash, replay_hash, receipt_hash]
    verifier: afritech.ci.constitutional_validation

  - id: L2-THM-011
    name: failure_consistency_preservation
    statement: "Failures reject invalid states instead of creating alternate valid states."
    requires_atoms: [SEM-ADM-001, SEM-RPL-001, SEM-DET-001]
    requires_axioms: [AX-ADM-003, AX-RPL-003, AX-DET-003]
    requires_invariants: [I16_TOTAL_OUTCOME_DECLARATION, I17_DETERMINISTIC_FAILURE, I18_REPLAYABLE_REFUSAL]
    requires_witnesses: [proof_hash, replay_hash, receipt_hash]
    verifier: afritech.ci.receipt_validator

  - id: L2-THM-012
    name: meaning_stability
    statement: "The system cannot reinterpret declared semantics inconsistently."
    requires_atoms: [SEM-CW-001, SEM-DET-001]
    requires_axioms: [AX-CW-001, AX-CW-002, AX-DET-001]
    requires_invariants: [I21_DETERMINISTIC_ORDERING, I22_TIME_SOURCE_CONTROL, I23_INPUT_IMMUTABILITY]
    requires_witnesses: [normalized_expression_hash, transcript_hash]
    verifier: afritech.ci.semantic_kernel_validator

final_property:
  id: L2-COMPLETE-SYSTEM-PROPERTY
  statement: "valid(e) iff ADM(e) and deterministic(e) and invariant_preserving(e) and replay_equivalent(e) and identity_stable(e) and trace_complete(e)."
  depends_on:
    - L2-THM-001
    - L2-THM-002
    - L2-THM-003
    - L2-THM-004
