    - L2-THM-012


===== ./afritech/constitution/meta.yaml =====
#====================================================
# The system governs not only execution,
# but the structure of meaning itself.
#====================================================

schema: afritech.constitution.meta.v3

meta:
  authority: constitutional
  enforcement: REQUIRED
  mutability: FORBIDDEN

  guarantees:
    - SEM-DET-001
    - SEM-RPL-001
    - SEM-CW-001
    - SEM-ID-001
    - SEM-ADM-001


#====================================================
# META PRINCIPLES (CANONICAL + ATOMIC)
#====================================================

meta_principles:

  #--------------------------------------------------
  # SEMANTIC MINIMALITY
  #--------------------------------------------------

  - id: META-001
    guarantees: [SEM-ADM-001]

    rule: SEMANTIC_MINIMALITY

    assertion: |
      semantics = canonical_minimal_set

    enforcement:
      semantic_registry_required: true
      duplicate_semantics_forbidden: true


  #--------------------------------------------------
  # TRACEABLE ENFORCEMENT
  #--------------------------------------------------

  - id: META-002
    guarantees: [SEM-RPL-001, SEM-ID-001]

    rule: ENFORCEMENT_TRACEABILITY

    assertion: |
      enforcement → semantic_atom_reference

    enforcement:
      semantic_reference_required: true
      orphan_enforcement_forbidden: true


  #--------------------------------------------------
  # CLOSED-WORLD SEMANTICS
  #--------------------------------------------------

  - id: META-003
    guarantees: [SEM-CW-001]

    rule: CLOSED_WORLD_SEMANTICS

    assertion: |
      semantics ⊆ declared_semantic_atoms

    enforcement:
      undeclared_semantics_forbidden: true
      dynamic_semantics_forbidden: true


  #--------------------------------------------------
  # DETERMINISTIC INTERPRETATION
  #--------------------------------------------------

  - id: META-004
    guarantees: [SEM-DET-001]

    rule: DETERMINISTIC_MEANING

    assertion: |
      identical_input → identical_interpretation

    enforcement:
      interpretation_deterministic: true
      replay_consistent: true


  #--------------------------------------------------
  # SEMANTIC / EXECUTION SEPARATION
  #--------------------------------------------------

  - id: META-005
    guarantees: [SEM-ID-001]

    rule: SEMANTIC_EXECUTION_SEPARATION

    assertion: |
      semantics ≠ execution_mechanics
      AND semantics → admissibility_constraints

    enforcement:
      execution_independence_required: true
      admissibility_semantic_control: true


  #--------------------------------------------------
  # REPLAY-BASED CONSISTENCY
  #--------------------------------------------------

  - id: META-006
    guarantees: [SEM-RPL-001, SEM-ADM-001]

    rule: REPLAY_SEMANTIC_CONSISTENCY

    assertion: |
      replay ⇒ semantic_consistency

    enforcement:
      replay_verification_required: true
      semantic_alignment_required: true


#====================================================
# META DEPENDENCY GRAPH (STRICT DAG)
#====================================================

meta_dependencies:

  META-002:
    depends_on: [META-001]

  META-003:
    depends_on: [META-001]

  META-004:
    depends_on: [META-003]

  META-005:
    depends_on: [META-002]

  META-006:
    depends_on: [META-002, META-004]


#====================================================
# GLOBAL ENFORCEMENT (CI + RUNTIME)
#====================================================

enforcement:

  semantic_directionality:
    core_only_definition: true
    downward_flow_required: true
    upward_definition_forbidden: true

  semantic_usage:
    atom_reference_only: true
    freeform_semantics_forbidden: true

  system_integrity:
    closed_world_enforced: true
    deterministic_interpretation_enforced: true


#====================================================
# CI POLICY (BLOCKING)
#====================================================

ci:

  required_checks:
    - semantic_atom_reference_required
    - no_duplicate_semantic_expression
    - meta_dependency_acyclic
    - no_undeclared_semantics
    - deterministic_interpretation_verified
    - semantic_traceability_complete

  failure_policy:
    block_merge: true
    block_release: true


#====================================================
# DRIFT PREVENTION
#====================================================

drift_control:

  semantic_equivalence_detection: REQUIRED
  duplicate_meaning_detection: REQUIRED
  semantic_hash_alignment: REQUIRED


#====================================================
# VALIDATION LAYERS (STRUCTURAL)
#====================================================

validation:

  semantic_layer:
    canonical_atoms_only: true
    registry_required: true

  enforcement_layer:
    semantic_trace_required: true
    invariant_mapping_required: true

  interpretation_layer:
    deterministic_only: true
    replay_consistency_required: true

  boundary_layer:
    closed_world_required: true
    undefined_semantics_forbidden: true

canonical_surfaces:
  - afritech.distributed



===== ./afritech/constitution/path_ontology.yaml =====
schema: afritech.path.ontology.v2

rules:

  # =========================================================
  # GLOBAL IGNORE
  # =========================================================
  ignore_patterns:
    - "__pycache__"
    - "*.pyc"
    - ".DS_Store"
    - "*.tmp"
    - "*.bak"

  # =========================================================
  # EXPLICIT ALLOWED FILES
  # =========================================================
  allowed_exceptions:
    - "__init__.py"

  # =========================================================
  # STRICT EXECUTION TOPOLOGY
  #
  # Runtime/core paths are constitutionally enforced.
  # These must remain deterministic and machine-safe.
  # =========================================================
  strict_paths:
    - "ecosystems/**/runtime/**"
    - "ecosystems/**/core/**"
    - "afritech/runtime/**"

  strict_rules:

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # lowercase enforcement
    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    lowercase_only: true

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # snake_case enforcement
    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    snake_case_only: true

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # whitespace forbidden
    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    no_spaces: true

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # allowed file extensions
    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    allowed_extensions:
      - ".py"
      - ".yaml"
      - ".yml"
      - ".json"
      - ".toml"
      - ".md"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # malformed / forbidden naming patterns
    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    forbidden_patterns:

      # duplicated pluralization
      - ".*usecasess.*"
      - ".*casess$"
      - ".*ssess$"

      # double underscore forbidden
      - ".*__.*"

      # temporary artifacts
      - ".*~$"
      - ".*\\.tmp$"

  # =========================================================
  # GOVERNANCE / CONSTITUTION
  #
  # Governance artifacts are typed constitutional objects.
  # =========================================================
  governance_paths:
    - "**/governance/**"
    - "**/constitution/**"
    - "**/registry/**"

  governance_rules:

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # structured constitutional artifacts
    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    structured_patterns:

      # RULE artifacts
      - "^RULE-[0-9]{4}-[a-z0-9\\-]+\\.yaml$"

      # ADR artifacts
      - "^ADR-[0-9]{4}-[a-z0-9\\-]+\\.(md|yaml)$"

      # epoch declarations
      - "^EPOCH-[0-9]{4}-[a-z0-9\\-]+\\.yaml$"

      # invariant declarations
      - "^I[0-9]{1,4}_[a-z0-9_]+\\.yaml$"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # registry-style governance artifacts
    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    registry_patterns:

      - "^[a-z0-9_]+\\.yaml$"
      - "^[a-z0-9_]+\\.yml$"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # python tooling allowed
    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    allow_python: true

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # governance docs allowed
    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    allow_docs: true

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # directories always allowed
    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    allow_directories: true

  # =========================================================
  # DOCUMENTATION
  #
  # Documentation is intentionally relaxed.
  # =========================================================
  docs_paths:
    - "**/docs/**"
    - "*.md"

  docs_rules:

    allow_uppercase: true
    allow_spaces: true
    allow_hyphens: true

  # =========================================================
  # OPTIONAL FUTURE EXTENSIONS
  # =========================================================
  future_rules:

    # enforce deterministic registry ordering
    deterministic_registry_sorting: false

    # enforce ontology checksum manifests
    require_manifest_checksums: false

    # enforce semantic naming lineage
    semantic_lineage_validation: false

    forbids: [NON_DETERMINISTIC_IO, UNDECLARED_SEMANTICS]
    runtime_constraints:
      deterministic_only: true
      allow_side_effects: false
  REPLAY_CRITICAL:
    requires: [SEM-DET-001, SEM-RPL-001, SEM-CW-001]
    forbids: [NON_DETERMINISTIC_IO, HISTORY_REWRITE]
    runtime_constraints:
      deterministic_only: true
      allow_side_effects: false
      replay_required: true
  ADMISSIBILITY_CRITICAL:
    requires: [SEM-ADM-001, SEM-ID-001, SEM-CW-001]
    forbids: [DERIVED_AXIOM, SEMANTIC_REDEFINITION]
    runtime_constraints:
      deterministic_only: true
      allow_side_effects: false
      proof_required: true
binding:
  field: profile
  required_for: [operation, runtime_contract, governance_binding]

  AND:
    type: boolean

  OR:
    type: boolean

  IMPLIES:
    type: logical

  REQUIRES:
    type: dependency

  FORBIDS:
    type: constraint

  EQUIVALENT:
    type: equality

  FORALL:
    type: quantifier

  EXISTS:
    type: quantifier

===== ./afritech/constitution/semantic_primitives.yaml =====
schema: afritech.constitution.semantic_primitives.v2

meta:
  authority: constitutional
  role: irreducible_semantic_atoms
  closed_world: true
  evaluable: true


#====================================================
# FORMAL SEMANTIC PRIMITIVES
#====================================================

primitives:

  PRIM-DET-STATE-001:

    type: PROPERTY

    formal:
      operator: EQUIVALENT
      operands:
        - replay(state_history)
        - replay(state_history)

    invariant: state_determinism

  PRIM-DET-ORDER-001:

    type: PROPERTY

    formal:
      operator: EQUIVALENT
      operands:
        - order(events)
        - canonical_order(events)

    invariant: ordering_stability

  PRIM-RPL-EQUIV-001:

    type: GUANRANTEE

    formal:
      operator: EQUIVALENT
      operands:
        - state(replay(H))
        - state(original_execution(H))

    invariant: replay_equivalence

  PRIM-ADM-INVARIANT-001:

    type: CONSTRAINT

    formal:
      operator: FORALL
      operands:
        - invariant_set
        - preserved(invariant_set)

    invariant: invariant_preservation

  PROOF_BOUNDED:
    guarantee: proof_size_within_limits

enforcement:

===== ./afritech/constitution/theorems.yaml =====
schema: afritech.constitution.theorems.v1

theorems:

  DETERMINISM:

    statement:
      equivalent_histories ⇒ equivalent_states

  REPLAY_SOUNDNESS:

    statement:
      replay(history) ⇒ admissible

  CLOSED_WORLD:

    statement:
      undeclared_semantics ⇒ excluded_from_execution

  ADMISSIBILITY:

    statement:
      satisfiable_semantics ⇒ execution_allowed


===== ./afritech/core/infrastructure/classification.yaml =====
schema: afritech.infrastructure.classification.v1

infrastructure:

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # PURPOSE
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  description: >
    This file defines all non-constitutional infrastructure surfaces
    within the AfriTech system.

    These surfaces support deployment, orchestration, and operations
    but MUST NOT influence:

      - runtime legitimacy
      - execution correctness
      - replay outcomes
      - constitutional state

    They exist strictly outside the constitutional trust model.

  classification: NON_CONSTITUTIONAL_SURFACE
  role: SUPPORT_SYSTEM

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # GLOBAL INVARIANTS
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  invariants:

    - infrastructure_must_not_affect_execution
    - infrastructure_must_not_participate_in_hash_chains
    - infrastructure_must_not_be_replay_relevant
    - infrastructure_must_not_define_authority
    - infrastructure_must_not_modify_registry
    - infrastructure_must_not_bypass_guards

  enforcement:

    violation_effect: HARD_FAILURE
    detection_layer:
      - CI
      - Runtime Admission
      - Replay Verification

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # SURFACE CLASSIFICATION
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  surfaces:

    # -------------------------------------------------------------
    # Docker / Containerization
    # -------------------------------------------------------------

    docker:

      path: afritech/docker/
      type: DEPLOYMENT_ORCHESTRATION

      includes:
        - docker-compose.yml
        - Dockerfile

      constraints:

        - must_not_define_runtime_logic
        - must_not_override_runtime_identity
        - must_not_embed_execution_rules

    # -------------------------------------------------------------
    # CI/CD Pipelines
    # -------------------------------------------------------------

    ci_cd:

      path: .github/workflows/
      type: PIPELINE_ORCHESTRATION

      includes:
        - architecture.yml

      constraints:

        - may_validate_but_not_define_truth
        - must_not_modify_attestation
        - must_not_generate_runtime_state

    # -------------------------------------------------------------
    # Environment Configuration
    # -------------------------------------------------------------

    environment:

      type: RUNTIME_CONFIGURATION
      scope: OPTIONAL

      examples:

        - ENV variables
        - deployment configs
        - secrets management

      constraints:

        - must_not_affect_determinism
        - must_not_change_execution_semantics
        - must_not influence_replay_results

    # -------------------------------------------------------------
    # Observability / Logging
    # -------------------------------------------------------------

    observability:

      type: TELEMETRY

      examples:

        - logging systems
        - metrics
        - monitoring dashboards

      constraints:

        - write_only
        - must_not influence_runtime_execution
        - must_not modify_state

    # -------------------------------------------------------------
    # External Integrations
    # -------------------------------------------------------------

    integrations:

      type: EXTERNAL_SYSTEM_INTERFACE

      examples:

        - APIs
        - message queues
        - third-party services

      constraints:

        - must_be_declarative
        - must_not affect_constitutional_core
        - must_not introduce_nondeterminism

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED FROM CONSTITUTION
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  excluded_from_constitution:

    - docker
    - ci_cd
    - environment
    - observability
    - integrations

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # RUNTIME CONSTRAINTS
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  runtime_constraints:

    invariant: >
      Runtime execution MUST be independent of infrastructure.

    enforcement_rules:

      - runtime_must_execute_identically_without_infrastructure
      - no environment-dependent execution branches
      - no runtime decisions based on deployment context
      - infrastructure failure must not affect replay validity

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # CI ENFORCEMENT
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  ci_enforcement:

    required_rules:

      - INFRASTRUCTURE_NON_INTERFERENCE
      - NO_RUNTIME_DEPENDENCY_ON_ENV
      - NO_INFRASTRUCTURE_HASH_BINDING

    validation:

      description: >
        CI MUST ensure that:
          - no infrastructure files are referenced in execution code
          - no infrastructure surfaces are included in registry hashes
          - runtime remains deterministic across environments

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # NON-OBSERVABILITY EXTENSION
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  non_observability_extension:

    invariant: >
      Infrastructure surfaces MUST NOT introduce hidden state.

    forbidden:

      - hidden_config_flags
      - environment-based branching logic
      - runtime behavioral toggles via infrastructure

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # PHASE COMPATIBILITY
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  lifecycle:

    current_phase: PHASE_2B

    readiness:

      supports:
        - local_runtime_execution
        - CI_validation
        - containerized_deployment

      future_extensions:

        - distributed_node_deployment
        - multi-node orchestration
        - consensus-aware infrastructure

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  constitutional_assertion: >
    Infrastructure surfaces exist solely to support execution,
    not to define or influence it.

    All constitutional guarantees MUST hold regardless of
    deployment environment, orchestration layer, or external systems.

    Any dependency of execution correctness on infrastructure
    SHALL be considered a constitutional violation.

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # FINAL ASSERTION
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  final_assertion: >
    Infrastructure is replaceable.
    Constitution is not.

    Deployment may vary.
    Execution truth must remain invariant.

    AfriTech execution is independent of infrastructure.

===== ./afritech/core/runtime/consensus/consensus_contract.yaml =====

  requirement:

    normalized_expression_hash:
      identical_across_nodes: true

  violation:
    action: reject_execution


===== ./afritech/core/runtime/contracts/normalization_contract.yaml =====
schema: afritech.runtime.contracts.normalization.v1

normalization_contract:
  status: mandatory
  applies_to:
    - semantic_yaml
    - s_ir
    - proof_generation
    - execution_admission

  invariant:
    evaluation_requires_normalized_ir: true
    hashing_requires_normalized_ir: true
    proof_requires_normalized_hash: true
    raw_expression_evaluation_allowed: false

  canonical_order:
    - compile
    - normalize
    - hash
    - evaluate
    - proof

  mvp_rewrites:
    and:
      flatten_nested_and: true
      remove_duplicate_operands: true
      sort_operands_by_canonical_hash: true
      single_operand_collapses: true

    equivalent:
      sort_binary_operands_by_canonical_hash: true

    requires:
      preserve_operand_order: true

    forall:
      preserve_variable_domain_body_shape: true
      normalize_body: true

  hard_failures:
    - normalization_failed
    - unsupported_operator
    - undeclared_symbol
    - rewrite_overflow
    - hash_mismatch

  on_violation: SYSTEM_INVALID


===== ./afritech/core/runtime/contracts/proof_contract.yaml =====
execution:

  proof_required: true

  must_include:
    - normalized_expression_hash
    - canonical_expression
    - proof_hash
    - dependency_graph

  must_validate:
    - normalized_expression_hash_matches_normalized_s_ir
    - canonical_expression_matches_normalized_s_ir
    - proof_hash_matches_proof_payload

  on_violation: SYSTEM_INVALID


===== ./afritech/core/runtime/contracts/system_contract.yaml =====

  requires:

    - termination_guaranteed
    - rewrite_confluence
    - normalization_canonical
    - satisfiability_verified
    - proof_generated
    - proof_within_limits
    - semantic_hash_stable
    - consensus_verified

  on_violation:
    action: SYSTEM_HALT

===== ./afritech/core/runtime/policies/replay_compression.yaml =====

  prune_invalid_lineages: true
  snapshot_canonical_state: required

  replay_equivalence_required: true
  condition: replay(original) == replay(compressed)


===== ./afritech/core/runtime/policies/resource_limits.yaml =====

  cpu_budget:
    max_cycles: bounded

  memory_budget:
    max_bytes: bounded

  proof_budget:
    max_nodes: 10000

  evaluation_budget:
    max_steps: 5000

action_on_violation: TERMINATE_EVALUATION

===== ./afritech/core/runtime/system_enforcement/execution_pipeline.yaml =====
schema: afritech.runtime.system_enforcement.execution_pipeline.v1

execution_pipeline:
  authority: canonical_runtime_path
  status: frozen_for_mvp

  invariant:
    runtime_code_may_execute_semantics: true
    runtime_code_must_not_define_semantics: true
    raw_expression_evaluation_allowed: false

  stages:
    - name: compile
      input: semantic_yaml
      output: s_ir
      failure: SYSTEM_INVALID

    - name: normalize
      input: s_ir
      output: normalized_s_ir
      required_before_evaluation: true
      failure: SYSTEM_INVALID

    - name: hash
      input: normalized_s_ir
      output: normalized_expression_hash
      failure: SYSTEM_INVALID

    - name: evaluate
      input: normalized_s_ir
      output: admissibility_result
      allowed_operators:
        - AND
        - EQUIVALENT
        - REQUIRES
        - FORALL
      failure: SYSTEM_INVALID

    - name: proof
      input:
        - normalized_expression_hash
        - admissibility_result
      output: proof_artifact
      failure: SYSTEM_INVALID

    - name: admission_gate
      input: proof_artifact
      output:
        - ADMIT
        - REJECT
      failure: SYSTEM_INVALID

  non_bypassable_order:
    - compile
    - normalize
    - hash
    - evaluate
    - proof
    - admission_gate


===== ./afritech/distributed/contracts/scale_contract.yaml =====
schema: afritech.distributed.scale_contract.v1

version: 1

metadata:

  authority: OPERATIONAL

  status: ACTIVE

  mutability: CONTROLLED

  registry_class: DISTRIBUTED_SCALE_CONTRACT

  description: >
    Canonical distributed scale contract governing deterministic
    partition routing, replay-safe distributed execution,
    append-only distributed coordination, deterministic recovery,
    infrastructure-independent replay equivalence, and operational
    scale participation within AfriTech.

    Distributed infrastructure exists exclusively to:
      - increase operational throughput
      - isolate execution partitions
      - coordinate deterministic workers
      - preserve replay-safe ordering
      - support deterministic recovery

    Distributed infrastructure must never:
      - redefine constitutional truth
      - bypass replay admissibility
      - mutate core execution semantics
      - introduce probabilistic execution
      - introduce observer-relative coordination
      - create infrastructure-derived authority

    Truth authority derives exclusively from:
      - replay admissibility
      - constitutional proof
      - deterministic execution
      - invariant preservation
      - witness admissibility
      - closed-world execution semantics


# ============================================================
# DISTRIBUTED SCALE CONTRACT
# ============================================================

distributed_scale_contract:

  role:

    - partition_events
    - route_to_workers
    - preserve_order_per_partition
    - record_worker_decisions
    - support_deterministic_recovery
    - preserve_replay_equivalence
    - support_partition_reconstruction
    - preserve_append_only_execution_history
    - support_replay_safe_worker_coordination
    - preserve_infrastructure_independent_execution

  forbidden:

    - define_execution_truth
    - bypass_replay
    - mutate_core_state_directly
    - use_random_partitioning
    - use_wall_clock_as_authority
    - accept_unrecorded_worker_output
    - permit_undeclared_worker_execution
    - permit_hidden_retry_semantics
    - permit_probabilistic_execution
    - allow_dynamic_partition_authority
    - allow_first_worker_wins_resolution
    - permit_observer_relative_batch_ordering
    - permit_runtime_reflection_discovery
    - permit_unsealed_recovery_execution
    - allow_infrastructure_defined_legitimacy

  required:

    - canonical_partition_identity
    - deterministic_routing
    - replay_equivalent_worker_output
    - append_only_execution_log
    - fail_closed_on_divergence
    - deterministic_partition_ordering
    - replay_safe_worker_assignment
    - replay_safe_batch_reconstruction
    - infrastructure_independent_replay
    - deterministic_recovery_reconstruction
    - replay_bound_execution_confirmation
    - canonical_batch_identity
    - explicit_worker_registration
    - immutable_execution_transcripts


# ============================================================
# PARTITION SEMANTICS
# ============================================================

partition_semantics:

  identity:

    canonical_form:
      module_path

    deterministic_resolution:
      true

    replay_safe:
      true

    observer_independent:
      true

    examples:

      - partition.rides.region_01
      - partition.rides.region_02
      - partition.dispatch.priority
      - partition.payments.pending

  routing:

    deterministic:
      true

    replay_safe:
      true

    infrastructure_independent:
      true

    forbidden:

      - random_partition_selection
      - wall_clock_partitioning
      - worker_load_based_truth_changes
      - observer_relative_routing
      - runtime_generated_partition_identity

  ordering:

    preserve_fifo_per_partition:
      true

    canonical_sequence_required:
      true

    replay_equivalent_order_required:
      true

    forbidden:

      - nondeterministic_batch_ordering
      - speculative_reordering
      - hidden_priority_mutation


# ============================================================
# WORKER SEMANTICS
# ============================================================

worker_semantics:

  worker_identity:

    explicit_registration_required:
      true

    replay_safe_identity:
      true

    deterministic_assignment:
      true

  execution:

    deterministic_execution_required:
      true

    replay_equivalence_required:
      true

    append_only_execution_required:
      true

    provisional_until_replay_confirmation:
      true

  forbidden:

    - worker_truth_authority
    - hidden_execution_mutation
    - undeclared_worker_execution
    - probabilistic_output_generation
    - replay_bypass_execution
    - observer_relative_execution
    - filesystem_identity_execution
    - speculative_result_acceptance

  required_outputs:

    - execution_hash
    - replay_hash
    - partition_identity
    - canonical_sequence
    - normalized_input_hash
    - deterministic_output_hash


# ============================================================
# COORDINATOR SEMANTICS
# ============================================================

coordinator_semantics:

  authority:

    truth_authority:
      false

    replay_authority:
      false

    execution_authority:
      limited

  responsibilities:

    - assign_batches
    - collect_worker_results
    - preserve_canonical_ordering
    - build_execution_transcripts
    - preserve_replay_safe_coordination

  forbidden:

    - override_replay_results
    - mutate_execution_truth
    - hidden_retry_semantics
    - semantic_conflict_resolution
    - infrastructure_defined_correctness
    - speculative_result_acceptance
    - observer_relative_batch_ordering

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_batch_ordering
    - replay_safe_transcript_generation
    - append_only_batch_coordination
    - deterministic_result_collection


# ============================================================
# DISTRIBUTED REPLAY SEMANTICS
# ============================================================

distributed_replay:

  replay_requirements:

    - deterministic_worker_output
    - replay_equivalent_reconstruction
    - canonical_partition_ordering
    - append_only_execution_transcripts
    - invariant_preservation
    - deterministic_batch_reconstruction

  replay_success_condition: >
    distributed_execution_hash ==
    replay_reconstruction_hash

  replay_failure_conditions:

    - worker_divergence
    - partition_order_divergence
    - replay_hash_mismatch
    - undeclared_worker_execution
    - observer_relative_batching
    - missing_execution_transcript
    - nondeterministic_recovery
    - infrastructure_dependent_truth

  replay_effects:

    replay_failure:
      REPLAY_INVALID

    divergence:
      EXECUTION_REFUSAL

    transcript_corruption:
      BATCH_INVALID


# ============================================================
# RECOVERY SEMANTICS
# ============================================================

recovery_semantics:

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_partition_rebuild
    - replay_safe_recovery
    - canonical_recovery_ordering
    - duplicate_execution_rejection
    - deterministic_convergence
    - delayed_event_convergence
    - offline_worker_rejoin_after_replay_validation

  forbidden:

    - recovery_truth_mutation
    - partial_replay_acceptance
    - hidden_recovery_mutation
    - replay_bypass_recovery
    - infrastructure_authority_recovery

  required:

    - append_only_recovery_log
    - replay_validated_recovery
    - deterministic_reconstruction
    - canonical_rejoin_protocol


# ============================================================
# CLOSED-WORLD ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

closed_world_alignment:

  required:

    - declared_partitions
    - explicit_worker_registration
    - deterministic_partition_resolution
    - replay_safe_execution
    - canonical_batch_identity
    - append_only_execution_logs

  forbidden:

    - dynamic_worker_discovery
    - runtime_partition_creation
    - reflection_based_execution
    - undeclared_execution_surfaces
    - speculative_execution_paths
    - observer_relative_resolution

  description: >
    Distributed scale execution must remain fully compatible
    with closed-world execution semantics.


# ============================================================
# REPLAY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

replay_alignment:

  replay_safe_required:
    true

  deterministic_execution_required:
    true

  witness_alignment_required:
    true

  invariant_preservation_required:
    true

  replay_invalidators:

    - probabilistic_execution
    - replay_divergence
    - undeclared_worker_execution
    - infrastructure_defined_truth
    - partition_order_divergence
    - observer_relative_batching

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_replay_reconstruction
    - replay_equivalent_worker_execution
    - canonical_partition_reconstruction
    - deterministic_distributed_recovery


# ============================================================
# FAILURE MODES
# ============================================================

failure_modes:

  replay_divergence:

    effect:
      REPLAY_INVALID

  worker_divergence:

    effect:
      EXECUTION_REFUSAL

  undeclared_worker_execution:

    effect:
      HARD_FAIL

  partition_order_divergence:

    effect:
      BATCH_INVALID

  infrastructure_authority_escalation:

    effect:
      HARD_FAIL

  replay_bypass_attempt:

    effect:
      EXECUTION_REFUSAL

  nondeterministic_recovery:

    effect:
      RECOVERY_INVALID


# ============================================================
# CI ENFORCEMENT
# ============================================================

ci_enforcement:

  validators:

    - distributed_scale_validator
    - distributed_partition_validator
    - distributed_worker_validator
    - distributed_replay_validator
    - distributed_recovery_validator
    - distributed_transcript_validator

  required_checks:

    - no_random_partitioning
    - no_worker_truth_authority
    - no_hidden_retry_semantics
    - no_undeclared_worker_execution
    - deterministic_partition_ordering
    - replay_equivalent_reconstruction
    - append_only_execution_validation
    - infrastructure_independent_correctness

  failure_mode:
    CI_FAIL


# ============================================================
# SAFETY GUARANTEES
# ============================================================

safety:

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_distributed_execution
    - replay_safe_scaling
    - infrastructure_independent_truth
    - observer_independent_execution
    - append_only_distributed_coordination
    - invariant_preserving_recovery

  forbid:

    - infrastructure_sovereignty
    - probabilistic_execution
    - replay_bypass
    - hidden_execution_mutation
    - observer_relative_coordination
    - speculative_batch_acceptance

  enforcement:

    violation_action:
      FAIL_FAST

    replay_violation_action:
      REPLAY_INVALID

    runtime_violation_action:
      EXECUTION_REFUSAL


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  description: >
    Distributed scale infrastructure exists solely to improve:
      - throughput
      - partition isolation
      - execution coordination
      - deterministic recovery
      - operational resilience

    Distributed infrastructure does not possess:
      - truth authority
      - replay authority
      - semantic authority
      - constitutional authority

    Constitutional truth derives exclusively from:
      - deterministic replay admissibility
      - invariant preservation
      - witness admissibility
      - closed-world execution semantics
      - canonical ontology resolution

    Distributed execution is constitutionally admissible only
    when infrastructure-independent replay equivalence is
    preserved.

===== ./afritech/distributed/worker/worker_contract.yaml =====
schema: afritech.distributed.worker_contract.v1

version: 1

metadata:
  authority: OPERATIONAL
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED
  registry_class: DISTRIBUTED_WORKER_CONTRACT
  description: >
    Canonical distributed worker contract governing deterministic
    worker execution, replay-bound worker output, explicit worker
    registration, partition-safe execution, append-only transcript
    generation, and non-sovereign distributed execution within
    AfriTech.

    Distributed workers exist only to execute assigned replay-safe
    partition records and produce deterministic execution outputs.

    Workers must never define truth, bypass replay, mutate core state
    directly, or create infrastructure-derived authority.


# ============================================================
# WORKER CONTRACT
# ============================================================

worker_contract:
  truth_authority: false
  replay_authority: false
  semantic_authority: false
  constitutional_authority: false

  role:
    - consume_assigned_partition_records
    - execute_deterministically
    - preserve_partition_order
    - produce_worker_result
    - produce_execution_hash
    - produce_replay_hash
    - append_execution_transcript
    - support_replay_reconstruction
    - fail_closed_on_divergence

  forbidden:
    - define_execution_truth
    - bypass_replay
    - mutate_core_state_directly
    - consume_unassigned_partition_records
    - execute_undeclared_partition
    - produce_unrecorded_output
    - use_random_output_generation
    - use_wall_clock_as_authority
    - use_filesystem_identity_as_authority
    - perform_hidden_retry
    - accept_first_worker_wins_resolution
    - override_coordinator_ordering
    - override_replay_verification
    - emit_observer_relative_output
    - execute_without_normalized_input_hash
    - execute_without_canonical_event_hash
    - execute_without_partition_sequence
    - mutate_execution_transcript_after_write

  required:
    - explicit_worker_identity
    - declared_partition_assignment
    - canonical_partition_identity
    - canonical_event_identity
    - partition_sequence
    - normalized_input_hash
    - canonical_event_hash
    - deterministic_execution_hash
    - deterministic_output_hash
    - replay_hash
    - append_only_execution_transcript
    - replay_equivalent_output
    - fail_closed_on_divergence


# ============================================================
# WORKER IDENTITY
# ============================================================

worker_identity:
  canonical_form: module_path
  explicit_registration_required: true
  deterministic_resolution: true
  replay_safe: true
  observer_independent: true

  filesystem_identity_forbidden: true
  symbolic_identity_forbidden: true
  dynamic_discovery_forbidden: true

  allowed_examples:
    - afritech.distributed.worker.node.worker_01
    - afritech.distributed.worker.node.worker_02
    - afritech.distributed.worker.node.worker_03

  forbidden_examples:
    - worker_01
    - current_worker
    - active_worker
    - ./worker.py
    - afritech/distributed/worker/worker_node.py


# ============================================================
# PARTITION ASSIGNMENT
# ============================================================

partition_assignment:
  assignment_required: true
  deterministic_assignment_required: true
  assigned_partition_only: true
  preserve_fifo_per_partition: true
  partition_sequence_required: true

  forbidden:
    - unassigned_partition_consumption
    - dynamic_partition_claiming
    - runtime_partition_creation
    - random_worker_partition_assignment
    - worker_selected_partition_authority
    - partition_sequence_skipping
    - partition_sequence_reordering

  required_fields:
    - worker_id
    - partition_id
    - partition_sequence
    - assignment_hash


# ============================================================
# EXECUTION INPUT CONTRACT
# ============================================================

execution_input:
  required_fields:
    - event_id
    - partition_id
    - partition_sequence
    - normalized_payload_hash
    - canonical_event_hash
    - assignment_hash

  validation_required: true
  immutable: true
  replay_safe: true

  forbidden:
    - mutable_input_payload
    - unnormalized_input
    - missing_payload_hash
    - missing_event_hash
    - missing_partition_identity
    - missing_partition_sequence
    - observer_relative_input_interpretation
    - live_external_lookup_as_execution_input


# ============================================================
# EXECUTION OUTPUT CONTRACT
# ============================================================

execution_output:
  required_fields:
    - worker_id
    - event_id
    - partition_id
    - partition_sequence
    - normalized_input_hash
    - canonical_event_hash
    - deterministic_output_hash
    - execution_hash
    - replay_hash
    - status

  allowed_statuses:
    - EXECUTED
    - REFUSED
    - REPLAY_INVALID
    - DIVERGENCE_DETECTED

  deterministic_hashing_required: true
  replay_equivalence_required: true
  append_only_transcript_required: true

  forbidden:
    - output_without_replay_hash
    - output_without_execution_hash
    - output_without_input_hash
    - output_without_partition_sequence
    - mutable_output_after_write
    - hidden_side_effect_output
    - probabilistic_output
    - observer_relative_output


# ============================================================
# TRANSCRIPT REQUIREMENTS
# ============================================================

execution_transcript:
  append_only: true
  immutable_after_write: true
  canonical_ordering_required: true
  replay_reconstructable: true

  required_fields:
    - worker_id
    - event_id
    - partition_id
    - partition_sequence
    - input_hash
    - output_hash
    - execution_hash
    - replay_hash
    - transcript_hash

  forbidden:
    - transcript_mutation
    - transcript_deletion
    - transcript_reordering
    - missing_worker_identity
    - missing_partition_identity
    - missing_replay_hash
    - observer_relative_transcript_ordering


# ============================================================
# REPLAY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

replay_alignment:
  replay_safe_required: true
  deterministic_execution_required: true
  invariant_preservation_required: true
  witness_alignment_required: true
  replay_confirmation_required_before_final_acceptance: true

  replay_success_condition: >
    worker_output_hash == replay_reconstructed_output_hash

  replay_invalidators:
    - worker_output_divergence
    - missing_replay_hash
    - missing_execution_transcript
    - undeclared_worker_execution
    - unassigned_partition_execution
    - partition_sequence_divergence
    - observer_relative_execution
    - probabilistic_execution

  failure_effects:
    worker_output_divergence:
      DIVERGENCE_DETECTED

    missing_replay_hash:
      REPLAY_INVALID

    undeclared_worker_execution:
      EXECUTION_REFUSAL   # ✅ FIXED (was inconsistent)

    unassigned_partition_execution:
      EXECUTION_REFUSAL   # ✅ FIXED

    partition_sequence_divergence:
      REPLAY_INVALID


# ============================================================
# RECOVERY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

recovery_alignment:
  worker_failure_recoverable: true
  recovery_truth_authority: false
  replacement_worker_allowed: true
  replacement_requires_replay_validation: true

  recovery_required_inputs:
    - partition_id
    - partition_sequence
    - execution_transcript
    - replay_hash
    - canonical_event_hash

  forbidden:
    - recovery_without_replay
    - recovery_with_partial_transcript
    - recovery_that_mutates_past_output
    - recovery_that_skips_partition_sequence
    - recovery_that_accepts_duplicate_worker_output
    - recovery_that_creates_new_truth

  guarantees:
    - deterministic_worker_replacement
    - replay_safe_recovery
    - duplicate_output_rejection
    - canonical_partition_rebuild
    - deterministic_rejoin_after_validation


# ============================================================
# CLOSED-WORLD ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

closed_world_alignment:
  required:
    - explicit_worker_registration
    - declared_partition_assignment
    - declared_execution_surface
    - canonical_worker_identity
    - canonical_partition_identity
    - immutable_execution_input
    - append_only_execution_transcript

  forbidden:
    - dynamic_worker_discovery
    - runtime_worker_registration
    - reflection_based_worker_resolution
    - undeclared_partition_execution
    - undeclared_execution_surface
    - implicit_execution_authority
    - speculative_worker_execution

  description: >
    Worker execution must remain explicitly declared,
    deterministic, replay-safe, and closed-world aligned.


# ============================================================
# FAILURE MODES
# ============================================================

failure_modes:
  undeclared_worker_execution:
    effect: HARD_FAIL

  unassigned_partition_execution:
    effect: EXECUTION_REFUSAL

  missing_replay_hash:
    effect: REPLAY_INVALID

  worker_output_divergence:
    effect: DIVERGENCE_DETECTED

  partition_sequence_divergence:
    effect: BATCH_INVALID

  transcript_mutation:
    effect: HARD_FAIL

  hidden_retry_detected:
    effect: EXECUTION_REFUSAL

  infrastructure_authority_escalation:
    effect: HARD_FAIL


# ============================================================
# CI ENFORCEMENT
# ============================================================

ci_enforcement:
  validators:
    - distributed_worker_validator
    - distributed_replay_validator
    - distributed_transcript_validator
    - distributed_recovery_validator

  required_checks:
    - explicit_worker_registration_check
    - declared_partition_assignment_check
    - no_unassigned_partition_execution
    - no_worker_truth_authority
    - no_hidden_retry_semantics
    - worker_output_replay_hash_required
    - execution_transcript_append_only_check
    - worker_output_replay_equivalence_check
    - no_infrastructure_authority_escalation

  failure_mode: CI_FAIL


# ============================================================
# SAFETY GUARANTEES
# ============================================================

safety:
  guarantees:
    - deterministic_worker_execution
    - replay_bound_worker_output
    - partition_safe_worker_consumption
    - append_only_worker_transcripts
    - infrastructure_independent_worker_legitimacy
    - fail_closed_worker_divergence

  forbid:
    - worker_sovereignty
    - replay_bypass
    - probabilistic_worker_execution
    - observer_relative_worker_output
    - mutable_worker_transcripts
    - unassigned_partition_consumption
    - hidden_worker_retry

  enforcement:
    violation_action: FAIL_FAST
    replay_violation_action: REPLAY_INVALID
    runtime_violation_action: EXECUTION_REFUSAL


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:
  description: >
    A distributed worker is a non-sovereign operational executor.

    A worker may consume only assigned replay-safe partition
    records, execute deterministically, and emit replay-bound
    outputs.

    A worker must never define truth, bypass replay, mutate core
    state directly, create hidden retry semantics, or derive
    authority from infrastructure.

    Worker legitimacy exists only when:
      - worker identity is explicit
      - partition assignment is declared
      - input is normalized and hash-bound
      - output is deterministic and replay-bound
      - execution transcript is append-only
      - replay equivalence is preserved
      - closed-world execution semantics remain intact

      AFRIRIDE_PILOT_MODE: "controlled"


===== ./afritech/docker/docker-compose.yml =====
version: "3.9"

services:

  redis:
    image: redis:7
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"

  api:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.api
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    depends_on:
      - redis

  worker:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.worker
    depends_on:
      - redis

    deploy:
      replicas: 3   # ✅ SCALE WORKERS HERE 

===== ./afritech/epoch/epoch_path_aliases.yaml =====
schema: afritech.epoch.path_aliases.v2

version: 2

metadata:

  authority: ARCHITECTURE
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED

  registry_class: EPOCH_ALIAS_REGISTRY

  description: >
    Canonical epoch path alias registry governing deterministic
    epoch filename normalization, canonical identity mapping,
    replay-safe alias resolution, and filesystem-to-canonical
    transition semantics within AfriTech.

    This registry exists exclusively to:
      - normalize historical epoch filenames
      - preserve replay-safe epoch lineage continuity
      - support deterministic migration semantics
      - prevent ambiguous epoch identity resolution
      - enforce canonical epoch naming doctrine

    Alias mappings are filesystem compatibility artifacts only.

    Constitutional epoch identity derives exclusively from:
      - epoch semantic identity
      - canonical epoch registry lineage
      - deterministic replay admissibility
      - canonical module-path ontology

    Filesystem aliases must never independently define:
      - epoch legitimacy
      - replay authority
      - runtime authority
      - constitutional identity


# ============================================================
# PATH ONTOLOGY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

path_alignment:

  canonical_identity_form: module_path

  alias_resolution_form: filesystem_path

  forbidden_identity_forms:

    - extension_identity
    - conceptual_identity
    - relative_identity

  description: >
    Alias mappings are compatibility-layer filesystem
    artifacts only and must never override canonical
    constitutional identity semantics.


# ============================================================
# ALIAS STATUS MODEL
# ============================================================

alias_status_model:

  CANONICAL:

    replay_safe: true

    mutable: false

    redirect_required: false

    description: >
      Canonical epoch filename recognized as the authoritative
      filesystem representation.


  MIGRATING:

    replay_safe: conditional

    mutable: true

    redirect_required: true

    description: >
      Transitional compatibility alias pending deterministic
      normalization toward canonical naming semantics.


  DEPRECATED:

    replay_safe: false

    mutable: false

    redirect_required: true

    description: >
      Historical compatibility alias retained temporarily for
      deterministic replay continuity only.


  FORBIDDEN:

    replay_safe: false

    mutable: false

    redirect_required: false

    description: >
      Constitutionally forbidden alias identity.


# ============================================================
# ALIAS REGISTRY
# ============================================================

aliases:

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # LEGACY HYphenated COMPATIBILITY ALIAS
  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  EPOCH-0008.yaml:

    canonical: EPOCH_0008.yaml

    status: MIGRATING

    replay_admissible: true

    redirect_required: true

    migration_target:

      canonical_filename: EPOCH_0008.yaml

      migration_required: true

      deterministic_resolution_required: true

    description: >
      Transitional hyphenated epoch filename retained
      temporarily for replay-safe lineage continuity.


  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # CANONICAL FILENAMES
  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  EPOCH_0006.yaml:

    status: CANONICAL

    replay_admissible: true

    redirect_required: false

    description: >
      Canonical replay-safe epoch filename.


  EPOCH_0007.yaml:

    status: CANONICAL

    replay_admissible: true

    redirect_required: false

    description: >
      Canonical replay-safe epoch filename.


  EPOCH_0008.yaml:

    status: CANONICAL

    replay_admissible: true

    redirect_required: false

    description: >
      Canonical replay-safe epoch filename.


# ============================================================
# GLOBAL ALIAS RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: EPOCH_ALIAS_RULE_1

    rule: >
      Canonical epoch filenames must use deterministic
      underscore-separated normalization semantics.


  - id: EPOCH_ALIAS_RULE_2

    rule: >
      Hyphenated epoch filenames are transitional
      compatibility aliases only and must resolve
      deterministically toward canonical identities.


  - id: EPOCH_ALIAS_RULE_3

    rule: >
      Canonical epoch identities must never alias
      themselves directly or indirectly.


  - id: EPOCH_ALIAS_RULE_4

    rule: >
      Alias graphs must remain acyclic and replay-safe.


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTIONS
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertions:

  - >
    Canonical epoch identities are terminal replay-safe
    identity anchors.

  - >
    Alias mappings may preserve compatibility continuity
    but may never redefine constitutional identity.

  - >
    Replay-safe epoch resolution requires deterministic,
    acyclic alias normalization semantics.

===== ./afritech/epoch/epoch_registry.yaml =====
schema: afritech.epoch.registry.v2

version: 2

metadata:

  authority: CONSTITUTION
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED

  registry_class: CANONICAL_EPOCH_REGISTRY

  description: >
    Canonical constitutional epoch registry governing
    deterministic temporal evolution, replay-safe epoch
    transitions, lineage continuity, runtime activation,
    reseal semantics, and temporal finality within AfriTech.

    This registry is the authoritative constitutional source
    for:
      - epoch transition legality
      - temporal monotonicity
      - replay-safe epoch evolution
      - active epoch exclusivity
      - seal semantics
      - epoch lifecycle admissibility
      - registry continuity
      - deterministic temporal ordering

    No runtime, replay, governance, proof, CI, or
    enforcement component may reinterpret epoch semantics
    outside deterministic artifacts derived from this registry.


# ============================================================
# PATH ONTOLOGY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

path_alignment:

  canonical_identity_form: module_path

  forbidden_identity_forms:

    - filesystem_path
    - extension_identity
    - conceptual_identity
    - relative_identity

  description: >
    Epoch registry identity resolution requires canonical
    module-path ontology alignment.


# ============================================================
# EPOCH REGISTRY
# ============================================================

epoch_registry:

  metadata:

    registry_version: v2

    registry_authority: CONSTITUTION

    replay_admissibility_required: true

    deterministic_ordering: enforced

    description: >
      Canonical registry defining temporal evolution rules,
      replay admissibility, lifecycle constraints, reseal
      semantics, lineage continuity, and deterministic epoch
      progression within AfriTech.


  # ==========================================================
  # CORE RULES
  # ==========================================================

  rules:

    - id: EPOCH_MONOTONIC_VERSION

      rule: >
        epoch.version MUST strictly increase across all
        constitutionally admissible transitions.

      violation_class: B_TEMPORAL


    - id: EPOCH_SEAL_REQUIRED

      rule: >
        Previous epoch MUST be constitutionally sealed before
        any subsequent epoch transition.

      violation_class: B_FINALITY


    - id: EPOCH_INSTANCE_UNIQUE

      rule: >
        epoch.instance_id MUST remain globally unique across
        all epoch transitions.

      violation_class: B_IDENTITY


    - id: EPOCH_ACTIVE_SINGLETON

      rule: >
        Exactly one runtime-active epoch MAY exist at any
        constitutionally admissible moment.

      violation_class: B_RUNTIME


    - id: EPOCH_SEALED_IMMUTABLE

      rule: >
        Constitutionally sealed epochs MUST remain immutable.

      violation_class: B_IMMUTABILITY


    - id: EPOCH_NO_BACKWARD_TIME

      rule: >
        Temporal progression MUST remain monotonic under all
        runtime, replay, governance, and proof conditions.

      violation_class: B_TEMPORAL


    - id: EPOCH_REPLAY_REQUIRED

      rule: >
        All constitutionally admissible epochs MUST remain
        replay-reconstructible.

      violation_class: B_REPLAY


    - id: EPOCH_CANONICAL_IDENTITY

      rule: >
        Epoch identity resolution MUST derive exclusively from
        canonical module-path ontology.

      violation_class: B_IDENTITY


    - id: EPOCH_RESEAL_REQUIRED

      rule: >
        Epoch evolution legitimacy requires deterministic
        reseal validation and replay-safe attestation.

      violation_class: B_ATTESTATION


    - id: EPOCH_CLOSED_WORLD_REQUIRED

      rule: >
        Epoch evolution MUST preserve declared execution
        surfaces and closed-world execution semantics.

      violation_class: B_CLOSED_WORLD


  # ==========================================================
  # CONSTITUTIONAL INVARIANTS
  # ==========================================================

  invariants:

    - I30_EPOCH_MONOTONICITY
    - I30_1_NO_BACKWARD_TIME
    - I30_2_SEAL_BEFORE_TRANSITION
    - I30_3_DETERMINISTIC_EVOLUTION
    - I30_4_GLOBAL_EPOCH_CONSISTENCY
    - I31_REPLAY_ADMISSIBILITY_REQUIRED
    - I32_RESEAL_REQUIRED
    - I33_CLOSED_WORLD_EVOLUTION
    - I34_CANONICAL_IDENTITY_REQUIRED
    - I35_DETERMINISTIC_LINEAGE


  # ==========================================================
  # TRANSITION POLICY
  # ==========================================================

  transition:

    mode: STRICT

    replay_admissibility_required: true

    deterministic_transition_required: true

    constraints:

      require_sealed_previous: true

      require_version_increment: true

      require_instance_change: true

      forbid_parallel_epochs: true

      require_replay_alignment: true

      require_receipt_alignment: true

      require_registry_hash_alignment: true

      require_invariant_validation: true

      require_deterministic_trace_validation: true

      forbid_speculative_transition: true

      forbid_observer_relative_transition: true

    forbidden:

      - probabilistic_transition
      - replay_divergence
      - lineage_rewrite
      - speculative_epoch_activation
      - undeclared_execution_surface_transition


  # ==========================================================
  # EPOCH STATE MODEL
  # ==========================================================

  state:

    constitutional_status:

      ACTIVE:

        immutable: false

        replay_admissible: conditional

        description: >
          Constitutionally active epoch not yet sealed.


      SEALED:

        immutable: true

        replay_admissible: true

        description: >
          Constitutionally finalized immutable epoch.


    runtime_status:

      ACTIVE:

        execution_enabled: true

        description: >
          Runtime-active epoch participating in sovereign
          execution.


      ARCHIVED:

        execution_enabled: false

        description: >
          Historical epoch retained exclusively for replay,
          proof, and lineage continuity.


    active_rules:

      only_one_active_epoch: true

      active_epoch_must_not_be_sealed: true

      active_epoch_must_be_admissible: true

      active_epoch_must_be_replay_safe: true


    sealed_rules:

      sealed_epoch_is_immutable: true

      sealed_epoch_is_not_runtime_mutable: true

      sealed_epoch_is_replay_admissible: true

      sealed_epoch_is_not_active: true


  # ==========================================================
  # DETERMINISM
  # ==========================================================

  determinism:

    ordering: STRICT

    evaluation: DETERMINISTIC

    replay_consistency: ENFORCED

    observer_independence: REQUIRED

    deterministic_requirements:

      - canonical_identity_resolution
      - replay_safe_execution
      - deterministic_mutation_ordering
      - deterministic_transition_ordering
      - invariant_preservation

    forbidden:

      - probabilistic_execution
      - observer_relative_replay
      - dynamic_surface_aliasing
      - uncontrolled_mutation
      - reflection_based_resolution


  # ==========================================================
  # REPLAY ADMISSIBILITY
  # ==========================================================

  replay_admissibility:

    required: true

    replay_requirements:

      - deterministic_execution
      - deterministic_transition
      - canonical_identity_resolution
      - replay_traceability
      - invariant_preservation
      - admissible_execution_surfaces

    replay_invalidators:

      - replay_divergence
      - probabilistic_reconstruction
      - observer_relative_replay
      - speculative_execution
      - undeclared_execution_surfaces

    witness_requirements:

      - receipt_hash
      - replay_hash
      - execution_trace_hash
      - transcript_hash
      - mutation_trace_hash


  # ==========================================================
  # REGISTRY ALIGNMENT
  # ==========================================================

  registry_alignment:

    registry_is_source_of_truth: true

    runtime_must_match_registry: true

    replay_must_match_registry: true

    proof_must_match_registry: true

    required_fields:

      - epoch_id
      - instance_id
      - version
      - constitutional_status
      - runtime_status
      - replay_hash
      - receipt_hash
      - registry_hash

    constraints:

      canonical_identity_required: true

      deterministic_resolution_required: true

      replay_alignment_required: true

      invariant_alignment_required: true

      no_registry_gaps: true

      no_duplicate_epoch_identity: true

      no_filesystem_identity_resolution: true


  # ==========================================================
  # RESEAL LAW
  # ==========================================================

  reseal:

    reseal_required: true

    reseal_requirements:

      - registry_hash_alignment
      - replay_hash_alignment
      - receipt_generation
      - deterministic_trace_validation
      - invariant_validation

    forbidden:

      - reseal_without_attestation
      - reseal_without_replay_alignment
      - reseal_after_terminal_epoch
      - observer_relative_reseal

    guarantees:

      - immutable_lineage
      - deterministic_history
      - replay_safe_epoch_finality


  # ==========================================================
  # SAFETY GUARANTEES
  # ==========================================================

  safety:

    guarantees:

      - deterministic_epoch_ordering
      - replay_safe_lineage
      - immutable_resealed_history
      - canonical_identity_resolution
      - deterministic_transition_validation
      - observer_independent_replay
      - closed_world_epoch_evolution

    forbid:

      - epoch_regression
      - epoch_reuse
      - unsealed_transition
      - multiple_active_epochs
      - hidden_epoch_mutation
      - speculative_epoch_activation
      - filesystem_identity_resolution
      - probabilistic_transition
      - replay_divergence

    enforcement:

      violation_action: FAIL_FAST

      replay_violation_action: REPLAY_INVALID

      lineage_violation_action: REGISTRY_REJECTION

      attestation_violation_action: RESEAL_REJECTION


# ============================================================
# FAILURE EFFECTS
# ============================================================

failure_effects:

  replay_divergence:

    effect:

      - replay_invalid
      - transcript_rejection
      - receipt_non_admissible


  lineage_violation:

    effect:

      - registry_invalid
      - epoch_rejection


  temporal_regression:

    effect:

      - runtime_rejection
      - replay_invalid


  attestation_mismatch:

    effect:

      - reseal_rejection
      - proof_invalid


  undeclared_execution_surface:

    effect:

      - runtime_rejection
      - replay_invalid
      - CI_failure


# ============================================================
# CI ENFORCEMENT ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

ci_alignment:

  enforced_by:

    - afritech.ci.epoch_validation
    - afritech.ci.replay_validation
    - afritech.ci.execution_surface_validation
    - afritech.ci.import_topology_enforcement

  required_checks:

    - deterministic_epoch_ordering
    - replay_admissibility
    - invariant_alignment
    - canonical_identity_resolution
    - reseal_alignment
    - no_lineage_gaps
    - no_probabilistic_transition
    - no_filesystem_identity_resolution

  failure_mode: CI_FAIL


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  description: >
    AfriTech epoch evolution is constitutionally deterministic,
    replay-safe, monotonic, and lineage-preserving.

    Epoch legitimacy derives exclusively from:
      - deterministic temporal ordering
      - replay admissibility
      - invariant preservation
      - canonical identity resolution
      - admissible execution topology
      - constitutionally valid reseal semantics

    No epoch legitimacy may emerge from:
      - filesystem structure
      - speculative interpretation
      - observer-relative replay
      - runtime activity alone
      - undeclared execution surfaces
      - probabilistic reconstruction

    Epoch evolution must remain:
      - deterministic
      - replay-safe
      - observer-independent
      - constitutionally bounded
      - closed-world aligned

===== ./afritech/epoch/epoch_semantics.yaml =====
# Upgraded Version — `afritech/epoch/epoch_semantics.yaml`

schema: afritech.epoch.semantics.v2

version: 2

metadata:

  authority: CONSTITUTION
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED

  compiled_target: afritech.epoch.compiled.semantic_epoch
  generated_ir: afritech.epoch.compiled.epoch_ir

  enforcement_mode: HARD

  description: >
    Canonical constitutional semantic source governing
    epoch identity, epoch evolution, replay admissibility,
    reseal semantics, registry continuity, execution
    legitimacy, lineage determinism, and temporal finality
    within AfriTech.

    This file is the authoritative semantic ontology for:
      - epoch identity
      - epoch transition legality
      - replay admissibility
      - reseal obligations
      - lineage continuity
      - registry integrity
      - constitutional evolution
      - temporal finality

    No runtime, replay, governance, proof, CI, or
    enforcement component may interpret epoch semantics
    outside artifacts deterministically compiled from
    this source.


# ============================================================
# PATH ONTOLOGY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

path_alignment:

  canonical_identity_form: module_path

  forbidden_identity_forms:

    - filesystem_path
    - extension_identity
    - conceptual_identity
    - relative_identity

  description: >
    Epoch semantic identity resolution requires canonical
    module-path ontology alignment.


# ============================================================
# GLOBAL EPOCH LAW
# ============================================================

global_law:

  monotonicity:

    required: true

    rule: STRICTLY_INCREASING

    description: >
      Epoch numbers must increase monotonically without
      rollback or reuse.

    violation_class: B_TEMPORAL


  parent_lineage:

    required: true

    root_epoch_parent: null

    description: >
      Every epoch except genesis must reference exactly
      one canonical parent epoch.

    violation_class: B_LINEAGE


  replay_determinism:

    required: true

    rule: CANONICAL_REPLAY_REQUIRED

    description: >
      Epoch state must be deterministically reconstructible
      under replay-safe constitutional conditions.

    violation_class: B_REPLAY


  registry_continuity:

    required: true

    rule: NO_EPOCH_GAPS

    description: >
      Epoch registry lineage must remain continuous,
      deterministic, and gap-free.

    violation_class: B_REGISTRY


  attestation_binding:

    required: true

    rule: RECEIPT_AND_REGISTRY_HASH_MATCH

    description: >
      Epoch legitimacy requires receipt, replay,
      transcript, and registry alignment.

    violation_class: B_ATTESTATION


  temporal_finality:

    resealed_epochs_mutable: false

    description: >
      Resealed epochs become constitutionally immutable.

    violation_class: B_FINALITY


  closed_world_execution:

    required: true

    description: >
      Epoch evolution must preserve declared execution
      surfaces and deterministic execution topology.

    violation_class: B_CLOSED_WORLD


  deterministic_transition:

    required: true

    description: >
      Equivalent constitutional epoch transitions must
      produce replay-equivalent outcomes.

    violation_class: B_DETERMINISM


# ============================================================
# EPOCH STATUS MODEL
# ============================================================

epoch_status_model:

  constitutional_status:

    SEALED:

      immutable: true

      replay_admissible: true

      description: >
        Constitutionally finalized epoch state.


    ACTIVE:

      immutable: false

      replay_admissible: conditional

      description: >
        Runtime-active but not yet constitutionally sealed.


  runtime_status:

    ACTIVE:

      execution_enabled: true

      description: >
        Epoch participates in runtime execution.


    ARCHIVED:

      execution_enabled: false

      description: >
        Historical epoch retained for replay and proof.


# ============================================================
# EPOCH TYPES
# ============================================================

epoch_types:

  GENESIS:

    description: >
      Foundational epoch establishing constitutional root,
      trust anchor, replay root, and initial authority.

    permits:

      - constitutional_initialization
      - root_attestation
      - baseline_receipt_generation
      - initial_execution_surface_declaration

    prohibits:

      - parent_reference
      - replay_fork
      - authority_derivation
      - prior_lineage_reference

    reseal_required: true


  EVOLUTION:

    description: >
      Epoch introducing constitutional capability expansion
      while preserving deterministic lineage continuity.

    permits:

      - invariant_extension
      - runtime_surface_expansion
      - proof_extension
      - replay_extension
      - registry_evolution

    prohibits:

      - lineage_rewrite
      - replay_reset
      - authority_escalation
      - historical_mutation

    reseal_required: true


  NORMALIZATION:

    description: >
      Structural normalization epoch without constitutional
      capability expansion.

    permits:

      - internal_refactor
      - artifact_relayout
      - replay_alignment
      - receipt_schema_stabilization
      - ontology_normalization

    prohibits:

      - invariant_addition
      - authority_change
      - execution_surface_change
      - replay_semantic_change

    reseal_required: true


  CORRECTION:

    description: >
      Epoch repairing semantic inconsistency while preserving
      constitutional authority boundaries.

    permits:

      - semantic_repair
      - attestation_fix
      - replay_consistency_repair
      - registry_consistency_repair

    prohibits:

      - capability_addition
      - authority_broadening
      - execution_surface_extension
      - replay_semantic_expansion

    reseal_required: true


  TERMINAL:

    description: >
      Final immutable constitutional epoch.
      No further constitutional evolution permitted.

    permits:

      - proof_discharge
      - terminal_attestation
      - replay_finalization

    prohibits:

      - further_transition
      - mutation
      - reseal_after_final
      - runtime_expansion

    reseal_required: true


# ============================================================
# TRANSITION LAW
# ============================================================

transition_rules:

  GENESIS:

    allowed_next:

      - EVOLUTION
      - NORMALIZATION


  EVOLUTION:

    allowed_next:

      - EVOLUTION
      - NORMALIZATION
      - CORRECTION
      - TERMINAL


  NORMALIZATION:

    allowed_next:

      - EVOLUTION
      - NORMALIZATION
      - CORRECTION
      - TERMINAL


  CORRECTION:

    allowed_next:

      - EVOLUTION
      - NORMALIZATION
      - CORRECTION
      - TERMINAL


  TERMINAL:

    allowed_next: []


# ============================================================
# REPLAY ADMISSIBILITY
# ============================================================

replay_admissibility:

  required: true

  replay_requirements:

    - deterministic_execution
    - deterministic_transition
    - canonical_identity_resolution
    - replay_traceability
    - invariant_preservation
    - admissible_execution_surfaces

  replay_invalidators:

    - replay_divergence
    - probabilistic_reconstruction
    - observer_relative_replay
    - speculative_execution
    - undeclared_execution_surfaces

  witness_requirements:

    - receipt_hash
    - replay_hash
    - execution_trace_hash
    - mutation_trace_hash
    - transcript_hash

  description: >
    Replay admissibility governs whether epoch transitions
    remain constitutionally reconstructible.


# ============================================================
# RESEAL LAW
# ============================================================

reseal_law:

  reseal_required_for:

    - GENESIS
    - EVOLUTION
    - NORMALIZATION
    - CORRECTION
    - TERMINAL

  reseal_requirements:

    - registry_hash_alignment
    - replay_hash_alignment
    - receipt_generation
    - invariant_validation
    - deterministic_trace_validation

  forbidden:

    - reseal_without_attestation
    - reseal_without_replay_alignment
    - reseal_after_terminal_epoch

  description: >
    Resealing finalizes constitutional epoch legitimacy.


# ============================================================
# LINEAGE GUARANTEES
# ============================================================

lineage_guarantees:

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_epoch_ordering
    - replay_safe_lineage
    - gap_free_registry
    - canonical_parent_resolution
    - immutable_resealed_history

  forbidden:

    - lineage_rewrite
    - replay_reset
    - epoch_gap
    - duplicate_epoch_identity


# ============================================================
