===== ./afritech/governance/adr/ADR-0011-afriride-deterministic-retry.yaml =====
schema: afritech.governance.adr.v1

adr:
  id: ADR-0011
  title: AfriRide Deterministic Retry Observation Formalization
  status: PROPOSED
  type: CONSTITUTIONAL_EVOLUTION
  proposed_epoch: 7
  approved_at: "2026-05-14T00:00:00Z"
  registry_ref: ADR-0011

  authors:
    - AfriTech Governance

  related_artifacts:
    - ecosystems/afriride/tests/test_retry_idempotence.py
    - ecosystems/afriride/tests/test_observational_replay.py
    - ecosystems/afriride/runtime/replay.py
    - ecosystems/afriride/runtime/deterministic_executor.py

  supersedes: []

  dependencies:
    - ADR-0001
    - ADR-0002
    - ADR-0003
    - ADR-0004
    - ADR-0005
    - ADR-0006
    - ADR-0007
    - ADR-0008
    - ADR-0009

  context: >
    AfriRide Phase 1 established a deterministic execution model
    where identical RuntimeContext produces identical ExecutionResult,
    including replay-stable execution traces and deterministic
    result hashes.

    During Phase 2 observational analysis, an empirical runtime
    behavior was consistently observed:

      - re-submission of an identical intent (retry)
        deterministically results in refusal

    Replay verification confirmed that:
      - refusal behavior is deterministic
      - replay traces remain stable
      - refusal hashes remain reproducible
      - no hidden mutation occurs
      - no semantic interpretation is required

    This behavior already exists within the lawful runtime
    execution surface and does not require additional runtime
    capability.

    However, the behavior is not presently declared within
    AfriRide’s formal failure taxonomy.

    This creates a constitutional ambiguity between:

      - empirically observed deterministic runtime behavior
      - formally declared proof-valid failure semantics

  problem_statement: >
    AfriRide currently lacks a formally declared classification
    for deterministic refusal behavior arising from identical
    retry observation.

    Although runtime and replay behavior remain deterministic,
    the failure taxonomy does not explicitly acknowledge this
    observational refusal category.

    Without formal declaration:
      - replay auditors may treat refusal as undefined behavior
      - proof surfaces remain semantically incomplete
      - deterministic observational outcomes remain undocumented

  decision: >
    Formally recognize deterministic refusal under duplicate
    intent replay as part of AfriRide’s declared constitutional
    failure surface.

    This ADR does NOT introduce any runtime, semantic, or replay changes.

    This ADR ONLY elevates observed deterministic behavior into
    declared constitutional taxonomy.

  constraints:
    - no runtime logic changes are allowed
    - no replay behavior may be modified
    - no semantic interpretation may be introduced
    - no intent equivalence is defined
    - no normalization or deduplication is permitted

  classification:
    mutation_type: DECLARATIVE_ALIGNMENT
    constitutional_scope: FAILURE_TAXONOMY_ALIGNMENT
    runtime_effect: NONE
    determinism_effect: NONE
    replay_effect: NONE
    semantic_effect: NONE
    authority_effect: NONE
    execution_surface_effect: NONE
    mutation_visibility: EXPLICIT

  observational_basis:
    evidence_type: EMPIRICAL_RUNTIME_OBSERVATION

    validation_pipeline:
      - deterministic_execution_verification
      - replay_consistency_verification
      - transcript_hash_validation
      - refusal_stability_confirmation

    observed_properties:
      deterministic: true
      replay_stable: true
      hash_stable: true
      observer_independent: true
      non_mutating: true

  invariants:
    - INVARIANT-DETERMINISTIC-EXECUTION
    - INVARIANT-REPLAY-REQUIRED-FOR-VALIDITY
    - INVARIANT-NO-SILENT-MUTATION
    - INVARIANT-AUTHORITY-DECLARATION
    - INVARIANT-NO-AUTHORITY-ESCALATION
    - INVARIANT-CLOSED-WORLD
    - INVARIANT-ADR-REQUIRED

  invariant_assertions:
    - identical inputs MUST produce identical outputs
    - replay MUST yield identical traces and hashes
    - deterministic refusal MUST remain replay-stable
    - no runtime mutation is introduced
    - no semantic equivalence is defined
    - duplicate intent MUST NOT trigger normalization
    - duplicate retry MUST NOT imply canonical identity

  failure_surface:

    added_case:
      id: DUPLICATE_INTENT_REFUSAL
      failure_kind: OBSERVATIONAL_REFUSAL
      classification: DETERMINISTIC_RUNTIME_REFUSAL

      description: >
        Deterministic refusal observed under replay-stable
        re-execution of identical intent conditions.

      trigger_condition: >
        Runtime observes retry execution under identical
        deterministic RuntimeContext conditions.

      properties:
        deterministic: true
        replay_stable: true
        hash_stable: true
        observer_independent: true
        non_semantic: true
        non_normalizing: true
        non_equivalence_bound: true
        non_inferential: true
        runtime_existing_behavior: true
        replay_verified: true

      prohibited_interpretations:
        - semantic_equivalence
        - retry_identity_inference
        - canonicalization
        - request_normalization
        - deduplication_semantics
        - behavioral_generalization

  validation_pipeline:
    - runtime_admission
    - runtime_orchestration
    - runtime_activation
    - guard_engine
    - runtime_engine
    - proof_generator
    - evaluation_engine
    - replay_verification
    - registry_seal
    - taxonomy_validator

  enforced_by:
    - ecosystems.afriride.runtime.deterministic_executor
    - ecosystems.afriride.runtime.replay
    - afritech.verify.replay
    - afritech.guards.invariant_guard
    - afritech.registry.seal

  replay_alignment:
    replay_behavior_changed: false
    replay_hash_changed: false
    transcript_structure_changed: false
    replay_admissibility_changed: false
    deterministic_refusal_preexisting: true
    replay_interpretation_clarified: true

  closed_world_compliance:
    new_execution_surfaces_introduced: false
    undeclared_runtime_access: false
    semantic_runtime_extension: false
    dynamic_interpretation_introduced: false
    equivalence_resolution_added: false
    epistemic_status: OBSERVED_FACT
    semantic_commitment: NONE
    interpretation_required: false

  consequences:
    positive:
      - aligns empirical behavior with declared failure taxonomy
      - improves replay auditability
      - strengthens proof-of-continuity semantics
      - removes ambiguity from deterministic refusal interpretation
      - preserves strict determinism boundaries

    negative:
      - expands declarative taxonomy surface
      - introduces future taxonomy governance obligations
      - requires continued observational vs semantic separation discipline

  links:
    epochs:
      - EPOCH_0007

    surfaces:
      - ecosystems.afriride.runtime
      - proof
      - evaluation
      - replay_verification

    related_adrs:
      - ADR-0010

  traceability:
    related_files:
      - ecosystems/afriride/runtime/deterministic_executor.py
      - ecosystems/afriride/runtime/replay.py
      - ecosystems/afriride/tests/test_retry_idempotence.py
      - ecosystems/afriride/tests/test_observational_replay.py

  integrity:
    immutable: true
    replay_protected: true
    registry_included: true

===== ./afritech/governance/adr/ADR-0012-edge-layer-admission.yaml =====
schema: afritech.governance.adr.v1

adr:
  id: ADR-0012
  title: Edge Layer Constitutional Admission
  status: ACCEPTED
  type: CONSTITUTIONAL_EXECUTION_TOPOLOGY
  proposed_epoch: 7
  approved_at: "2026-05-21T00:00:00Z"
  registry_ref: ADR-0012

  authors:
    - AfriTech Governance

  related_artifacts:
    - afritech/edge/adapter/runtime_adapter.py
    - afritech/edge/adapter/validation.py
    - afritech/edge/normalization/normalizer.py
    - afritech/edge/normalization/validation.py
    - afritech/edge/ingestion/queue_ingestor.py
    - afritech/guards/edge_input_guard.py
    - afritech/governance/rules/RULE-EDGE-001.yaml
    - afritech/architecture/surface_authority_registry.yaml
    - afritech/architecture/surface_implementation_binding.yaml
    - afritech/architecture/implementation_registry.yaml

  supersedes: []

  dependencies:
    - ADR-0001
    - ADR-0002
    - ADR-0003
    - ADR-0004
    - ADR-0005
    - ADR-0006
    - ADR-0007
    - ADR-0008
    - ADR-0009

  context: >
    AfriRide production hardening requires controlled open-world
    interaction without allowing raw external inputs to enter
    constitutional runtime execution directly.

    External systems introduce non-determinism through HTTP payloads,
    provider responses, timestamps, queue delivery, GPS noise,
    mobile clients, payments, maps, and retry behavior.

    AfriTech must preserve deterministic execution, replay
    admissibility, closed-world execution, and canonical module-path
    identity while allowing production access to the core.

  problem_statement: >
    Without a declared edge layer, production traffic could enter
    runtime execution through informal paths, creating ambiguity
    between external-world adaptation and constitutional admission.

    The system requires explicit surfaces to contain non-determinism
    before execution admission.

  decision: >
    Introduce three canonical edge execution surfaces:

      - afritech.edge.adapter
      - afritech.edge.normalization
      - afritech.edge.ingestion

    External inputs MUST pass through adapter → normalization →
    ingestion before any execution admission.

    The edge layer MAY adapt and normalize external input, but MUST NOT:
      - redefine constitutional truth
      - alter replay authority
      - alter proof authority
      - modify core runtime semantics

  constraints:
    - edge must be the only entry point for external inputs
    - adapter must produce canonical deterministic representation
    - normalization must be replay-stable
    - ingestion must only admit validated events
    - edge ordering must be strictly enforced
    - edge must not introduce semantic interpretation
    - edge must not bypass runtime admission controls

  classification:
    mutation_type: EXECUTION_TOPOLOGY_EXTENSION
    constitutional_scope: EDGE_ADMISSION_BOUNDARY
    runtime_effect: CONTROLLED_EDGE_ADMISSION
    determinism_effect: PRESERVED
    replay_effect: PRESERVED
    semantic_effect: NONE
    authority_effect: BOUNDED_EDGE_AUTHORITY
    execution_surface_effect: DECLARED_CANONICAL_EDGE_SURFACES
    mutation_visibility: EXPLICIT

  invariants:
    - INVARIANT-NO-SILENT-MUTATION
    - INVARIANT-DETERMINISTIC-EXECUTION
    - INVARIANT-REPLAY-REQUIRED-FOR-VALIDITY
    - INVARIANT-AUTHORITY-DECLARATION
    - INVARIANT-NO-AUTHORITY-ESCALATION
    - INVARIANT-CLOSED-WORLD
    - INVARIANT-ADR-REQUIRED
    - INVARIANT-EDGE-GATED-EXECUTION
    - INVARIANT-EDGE-ORDERING-ENFORCED
    - INVARIANT-EXTERNAL-NONDETERMINISM-CONTAINED

  invariant_assertions:
    - external inputs MUST NOT enter runtime execution directly
    - adapter output MUST be canonical and deterministic
    - normalization MUST use replay-stable transformations
    - ingestion MUST admit only normalized events
    - edge ordering MUST be adapter → normalization → ingestion
    - edge MUST NOT redefine constitutional truth
    - edge MUST NOT bypass replay admissibility
    - edge MUST NOT introduce semantic interpretation

  admitted_surfaces:
    - afritech.edge.adapter
    - afritech.edge.normalization
    - afritech.edge.ingestion

  validation_pipeline:
    - edge_adapter
    - edge_normalization
    - edge_ingestion
    - runtime_admission
    - runtime_orchestration
    - runtime_activation
    - guard_engine
    - runtime_engine
    - proof_generator
    - evaluation_engine
    - replay_verification
    - registry_seal

  enforced_by:
    - afritech.edge.adapter
    - afritech.edge.normalization
    - afritech.edge.ingestion
    - afritech.guards.edge_input_guard
    - afritech.guards.guard_engine
    - afritech.runtime.admission
    - afritech.verify.replay
    - afritech.registry.seal

  required_rule:
    - RULE-EDGE-001

  required_guard:
    - afritech.guards.edge_input_guard

  required_ci_checks:
    - CI-EDGE-001
    - afritech.ci.surface_validator
    - afritech.ci.constitutional_pipeline

  forbidden:
    - direct_external_runtime_execution
    - raw_external_input_to_core
    - unnormalized_provider_response_execution
    - observer_relative_edge_ordering
    - brand_or_documentation_based_runtime_identity
    - undeclared_edge_execution_surface

  consequences:
    positive:
      - non_determinism_contained_before_runtime
      - production_input_path_formalized
      - replay_safe_edge_boundary_created
      - closed_world_execution_preserved

    negative:
      - increases governance surface area
      - introduces additional validation and CI requirements
      - requires strict pipeline discipline in production

  links:
    epochs:
      - EPOCH_0007

    surfaces:
      - afritech.edge.adapter
      - afritech.edge.normalization
      - afritech.edge.ingestion
      - runtime.admission
      - runtime.engine

  traceability:
    related_files:
      - afritech/edge/adapter/runtime_adapter.py
      - afritech/edge/normalization/normalizer.py
      - afritech/edge/ingestion/queue_ingestor.py
      - afritech/guards/edge_input_guard.py
      - afritech/governance/rules/RULE-EDGE-001.yaml

  integrity:
    immutable: true
    replay_protected: true
    registry_included: true

===== ./afritech/governance/adr/ADR-0013-audit-log-hash-linking.yaml =====
schema: afritech.governance.adr.v1

adr:
  id: ADR-0013
  title: Audit Log Hash Linking (Tamper-Proof Chain)
  status: APPROVED
  type: CONSTITUTIONAL_AUDIT_INTEGRITY
  proposed_epoch: 7
  approved_at: "2026-05-22T00:00:00Z"
  registry_ref: ADR-0013

  authors:
    - AfriTech Governance

  related_artifacts:
    - afritech/audit/hash_engine.py
    - afritech/audit/chain_validator.py
    - afritech/models/audit_log.py
    - afritech/guards/audit_chain_guard.py
    - afritech/governance/rules/RULE-AUDIT-001.yaml

  dependencies:
    - ADR-0002
    - ADR-0003
    - ADR-0005
    - ADR-0009
    - ADR-0012

# =====================================================
# CONTEXT
# =====================================================

  context: >
    AfriTech guarantees deterministic execution (ADR-0003),
    replay-valid state reconstruction (ADR-0009), and registry
    sealing (ADR-0005). However, audit logs remain vulnerable to
    undetected tampering if entries are stored independently.

    Replay correctness depends on historical integrity. Without a
    cryptographically linked audit chain, malicious or accidental
    modification of past records cannot be detected reliably.

# =====================================================
# PROBLEM STATEMENT
# =====================================================

  problem_statement: >
    The system lacks a cryptographic mechanism to enforce
    forward-linked audit history integrity.

    Without hash chaining:
      - audit entries can be altered without detection
      - replay history may be corrupted silently
      - registry sealing relies on unverified history inputs
      - forensic traceability is incomplete

# =====================================================
# DECISION
# =====================================================

  decision: >
    Introduce a cryptographic hash chain across all audit log entries.

    Each audit entry MUST:
      - include previous_hash
      - include entry_hash
      - be deterministically hashed using payload and previous_hash

    Chain integrity MUST be enforced at:
      - insertion time
      - replay verification
      - registry sealing

    Any hash mismatch MUST invalidate the audit chain
    and trigger system-level rejection.

# =====================================================
# MODEL
# =====================================================

  model:
    inputs:
      - previous_hash
      - audit_payload

    outputs:
      - entry_hash

    hash_formula: >
      entry_hash = SHA256(previous_hash + serialize(audit_payload))

# =====================================================
# CONSTRAINTS
# =====================================================

  constraints:
    - payload serialization MUST be deterministic
    - hash algorithm MUST be stable and consistent
    - ordering MUST follow temporal determinism (ADR-0002)
    - no entry may be inserted out of sequence
    - previous_hash MUST match prior entry_hash
    - genesis entry MUST have null previous_hash

# =====================================================
# INVARIANTS
# =====================================================

  invariants:
    - INVARIANT-HASH-CHAIN-INTEGRITY
    - INVARIANT-FORWARD-ONLY-LINKING
    - INVARIANT-TAMPER-DETECTION
    - INVARIANT-REPLAY-HASH-CONSISTENCY
    - INVARIANT-AUDIT-CHAIN-CONTINUITY

  invariant_assertions:
    - each entry MUST reference the previous hash
    - computed hash MUST match stored hash
    - any modification MUST break the chain
    - replay MUST recompute identical hash chain
    - chain termination MUST match registry seal

# =====================================================
# VALIDATION PIPELINE
# =====================================================

  validation_pipeline:
    - edge_ingestion
    - runtime_admission
    - runtime_engine
    - audit_log_write
    - hash_engine
    - chain_validator
    - replay_verification
    - registry_seal

# =====================================================
# ENFORCEMENT
# =====================================================

  enforced_by:
    - afritech.audit.hash_engine
    - afritech.audit.chain_validator
    - afritech.guards.audit_chain_guard
    - afritech.verify.replay
    - afritech.registry.seal

  required_rule:
    - RULE-AUDIT-001

  required_guard:
    - afritech.guards.audit_chain_guard

  required_ci_checks:
    - CI-AUDIT-CHAIN-001
    - afritech.ci.audit_integrity_validator

# =====================================================
# FAILURE BEHAVIOR
# =====================================================

  failure_model:

    hash_mismatch:
      action: REJECT
      severity: CRITICAL

    broken_chain:
      action: HALT_REPLAY
      severity: CRITICAL

    invalid_previous_hash:
      action: REJECT_INSERT
      severity: HIGH

    replay_divergence:
      action: SYSTEM_INVALIDATION
      severity: CRITICAL

# =====================================================
# DATA MODEL REQUIREMENT
# =====================================================

  audit_log_schema:
    required_fields:
      - id
      - payload
      - previous_hash
      - entry_hash
      - timestamp
      - epoch
      - status

# =====================================================
# CLASSIFICATION
# =====================================================

  classification:
    mutation_type: AUDIT_INTEGRITY_ENFORCEMENT
    constitutional_scope: HISTORY_AUTHENTICITY
    runtime_effect: AUDIT_CHAIN_VALIDATION
    determinism_effect: PRESERVED
    replay_effect: STRENGTHENED
    semantic_effect: NONE
    authority_effect: REPLAY_HARDENING
    execution_surface_effect: NONE
    mutation_visibility: EXPLICIT

# =====================================================
# CONSEQUENCES
# =====================================================

  consequences:

    positive:
      - enables tamper-proof audit history
      - guarantees integrity of replay input
      - strengthens registry sealing validity
      - enables forensic verification
      - ensures historical authenticity

    negative:
      - introduces hash computation overhead
      - requires strict ordering enforcement
      - chain corruption impacts full audit validity

# =====================================================
# LINKS
# =====================================================

  links:
    epochs:
      - EPOCH_0007

    surfaces:
      - audit_log
      - runtime_engine
      - replay_verification
      - registry_seal

    related_adrs:
      - ADR-0012

# =====================================================
# TRACEABILITY
# =====================================================

  traceability:
    related_files:
      - afritech/audit/hash_engine.py
      - afritech/audit/chain_validator.py
      - afritech/guards/audit_chain_guard.py
      - afritech/models/audit_log.py

# =====================================================
# INTEGRITY
# =====================================================

  integrity:
    immutable: true
    replay_protected: true
    registry_included: true

===== ./afritech/governance/adr/ADR-0014-cross-system-proof-format.yaml =====
id: ADR-0014
title: Cross-System Proof Format (Standard)
status: ACCEPTED
epoch: 6

context:
  problem: >
    Proofs are currently system-specific and lack interoperability.
    External systems cannot verify proofs without relying on
    internal implementation details or shared databases.

  goal: >
    Enable portable, trustless verification across independent systems,
    allowing any compliant system to validate proofs using only
    cryptographic guarantees.

  constraints:
    - Must not rely on shared database state
    - Must support external auditors
    - Must be deterministic and reproducible
    - Must be API and transport independent

decision:
  description: >
    Adopt a canonical JSON proof format including:
    - deterministic data payload
    - merkle root for integrity
    - signature for authenticity
    - public key reference for trust resolution

  format:
    version: "v1"

    required_fields:
      - version
      - system_id
      - network
      - timestamp
      - proof_type
      - data
      - merkle_root
      - hash_algorithm
      - signature
      - public_key_id

    schema:
      version: string
      system_id: string
      network: string
      timestamp: string (ISO-8601)

      proof_type: string

      data:
        logs: list

      merkle_root: string
      hash_algorithm: string

      signature: string
      public_key_id: string

      metadata: optional object

  cryptographic_requirements:
    - Hash algorithm must be deterministic (e.g. SHA-256)
    - Log ordering must be stable and canonical
    - Merkle root must be reproducible across systems
    - Signature must be generated from merkle_root only
    - Public key must be resolvable via registry

  verification_process:
    - Validate schema completeness
    - Recompute merkle root from logs
    - Compare computed root with provided root
    - Resolve public key via public_key_id
    - Verify signature using resolved key
    - Optionally validate timestamp freshness

  trust_model:
    description: >
      Trust is derived from cryptographic guarantees rather than
      system relationships.

    properties:
      - No shared database required
      - No shared secret required
      - Verification is deterministic
      - Proof origin is verifiable via signature

consequences:
  positives:
    - Cross-system interoperability
    - Independent verification capability
    - Zero-trust architecture enforcement
    - Compatibility with external auditors
    - Scalable distributed trust model

  negatives:
    - Requires key management infrastructure
    - Requires strict schema adherence
    - Requires canonical serialization discipline
    - Additional verification overhead

invariants:
  - id: INVARIANT-014
    description: >
      Any proof must be verifiable independently of the originating system.

  - id: INVARIANT-014B
    description: >
      Proof integrity must be guaranteed through deterministic merkle root
      and signature validation.

rules:
  - id: RULE-014-1
    description: Proof must include merkle_root and signature

  - id: RULE-014-2
    description: public_key_id must resolve to a valid key

  - id: RULE-014-3
    description: Logs must regenerate identical merkle root

  - id: RULE-014-4
    description: Signature must match merkle_root and public key

bindings:
  - id: BIND-014-1
    description: Cross-system verifier binding
    target: afritech.services.cross_proof_verifier.verify_cross_system_proof

  - id: BIND-014-2
    description: Public key registry binding
    target: afritech.crypto.key_registry.PUBLIC_KEY_REGISTRY

guards:
  - id: GUARD-014-1
    description: Validate proof structure
    implementation: afritech.guards.guard_cross_proof.guard_validate_proof_structure

  - id: GUARD-014-2
    description: Validate merkle integrity
    implementation: afritech.guards.guard_cross_proof.guard_validate_merkle

  - id: GUARD-014-3
    description: Validate signature authenticity
    implementation: afritech.guards.guard_cross_proof.guard_validate_signature

ci:
  tests:
    - test_cross_system_proof_valid
    - test_cross_system_proof_tampered
    - test_invalid_signature_rejected
    - test_unknown_public_key_fails
    - test_merkle_mismatch_rejected

  enforcement:
    - All proofs must pass verification tests
    - Any failed guard must block acceptance
    - Signature verification must be mandatory

extensions:
  future_adrs:
    - ADR-0015: Key Rotation System
    - ADR-0016: Multi-Signature Proof Format
    - ADR-0017: Blockchain Anchoring
    - ADR-0018: Global Trust Registry

  extensibility:
    metadata:
      description: >
        Optional metadata field can be extended without breaking
        verification logic.

    versioning:
      description: >
        Future versions must maintain backward compatibility or
        declare breaking changes explicitly.

notes:
  - Proof format is transport-agnostic (API, file, blockchain)
  - Canonical JSON serialization is REQUIRED
  - This ADR establishes the foundation for trust federation
  - Future ADRs will build on this format for advanced features

===== ./afritech/governance/adr/ADR-0015-continuity-proof-aggregation.yaml =====
schema: afritech.governance.adr.v1

adr:
  id: ADR-0015
  title: Cross-Scenario Continuity Proof Aggregation
  status: ACCEPTED
  type: CONSTITUTIONAL_PROOF_AGGREGATION
  proposed_epoch: 7
  approved_at: "2026-05-28T21:47:22Z"
  registry_ref: ADR-0015

  authors:
    - AfriTech Governance

  context:
    problem: >
      AfriTech and AfriRide produce continuity evidence across many scenarios:
      offline recovery, delayed synchronization, replay equivalence, partial
      disruption, corruption isolation, multi-region recovery, entropy events,
      and live-pilot operational traces. These proofs are useful independently,
      but decision makers also need an aggregate continuity view that preserves
      source proof authority.

    goal: >
      Define a canonical aggregation doctrine for cross-scenario continuity
      proofs so multiple scenario proofs can be summarized, compared, and
      checkpointed without weakening replay truth or mutating source evidence.

    constraints:
      - Aggregation must not redefine source proof truth.
      - Aggregation must preserve each source proof hash.
      - Aggregation must be deterministic for identical source inputs.
      - Aggregation must not convert advisory evidence into runtime authority.
      - Aggregation must remain replay-verifiable and auditor-readable.

  decision: >
    Adopt a cross-scenario continuity proof aggregation standard. The aggregate
    artifact is a deterministic index over source proof artifacts, scenario
    classifications, evidence hashes, outcome statuses, and non-claims. It may
    summarize continuity readiness, but it must never replace the underlying
    proof artifacts or become an independent source of runtime truth.

  aggregation_contract:
    artifact_type: continuity_proof_aggregate
    version: v1
    required_fields:
      - aggregate_id
      - version
      - generated_at
      - scope
      - scenario_proofs
      - aggregate_hash
      - source_hashes
      - continuity_summary
      - non_claims
      - validator

    scenario_proof_fields:
      - scenario_id
      - source_path
      - proof_type
      - proof_hash
      - result
      - replay_participating
      - continuity_property
      - failure_boundary

    continuity_summary_fields:
      - total_scenarios
      - passed_scenarios
      - failed_scenarios
      - replay_verified_count
      - continuity_properties_observed
      - excluded_claims

  constitutional_boundary:
    aggregate_authority: EVIDENCE_INDEX
    source_truth_authority: SOURCE_PROOF
    runtime_authoritative: false
    replay_authoritative: false
    governance_authoritative: true
    may_block_release_when_validator_fails: true
    may_replace_source_proof: false
    may_mutate_source_proof: false

  deterministic_aggregation_flow:
    - collect declared source proof artifacts
    - validate each source proof path and hash
    - normalize scenario identifiers
    - sort scenario entries by scenario_id and source_path
    - compute aggregate_hash from canonical JSON
    - emit continuity summary and non-claims
    - validate aggregate with CI before use

  invariants:
    - id: INVARIANT-015
      description: >
        Continuity aggregates must preserve source proof hash identity and must
        not mutate source proof content.
    - id: INVARIANT-015B
      description: >
        Aggregate readiness statements must remain bounded by the included
        scenarios and explicit non-claims.
    - id: INVARIANT-015C
      description: >
        Aggregation order must be deterministic and replay-verifiable.

  rules:
    - id: RULE-015-1
      description: Each source proof must have a stable proof_hash.
    - id: RULE-015-2
      description: Scenario entries must sort deterministically.
    - id: RULE-015-3
      description: Aggregate hash must be computed from canonical serialization.
    - id: RULE-015-4
      description: Aggregates must include explicit non-claims.
    - id: RULE-015-5
      description: Aggregate artifacts must not claim runtime authority.

  bindings:
    - id: BIND-015-1
      description: Source proof artifact binding.
      target: reports/
    - id: BIND-015-2
      description: Continuity proof document binding.
      target: docs/proof/
    - id: BIND-015-3
      description: Future aggregate validator binding.
      target: afritech.ci.continuity_invariants_validator

  ci:
    required_checks:
      - source_proof_paths_exist
      - source_hashes_are_present
      - aggregate_order_is_deterministic
      - aggregate_hash_is_stable
      - non_claims_are_explicit
      - runtime_authority_is_false

    tests:
      - test_continuity_invariants_validator
      - test_entropy_bound_execution
      - test_multiregion_gate6
      - test_afriride_marketplace_proof
      - test_mobile_pilot_e2e

  non_claims:
    - Aggregation does not prove global deployment readiness.
    - Aggregation does not replace source replay verification.
    - Aggregation does not create runtime authority.
    - Aggregation does not infer continuity outside declared scenarios.

  consequences:
    positives:
      - Continuity evidence becomes comparable across scenarios.
      - Governance can cite one aggregate while preserving source proof truth.
      - Auditors can trace every summary claim to source proof artifacts.
      - Future dashboards can display continuity readiness without creating new authority.
    negatives:
      - Aggregate maintenance adds one validation surface.
      - Scenario taxonomy must remain stable for deterministic comparison.

  supersedes: []

  depends_on:
    - ADR-0014

  next_admissible_step: >
    Introduce RULE-015 and BIND-015 only when aggregate artifacts are promoted
    from documented doctrine into enforced CI surfaces.


===== ./afritech/governance/adr/ADR-0016-multisource-consensus.yaml =====
schema: afritech.governance.adr.v1

adr:
  id: ADR-0016
  title: Multi-Source Consensus and External Trust Scoring
  status: ACCEPTED
  type: CONSTITUTIONAL_CONSENSUS_AND_TRUST_SCORING
  proposed_epoch: 7
  approved_at: "2026-05-28T21:47:22Z"
  registry_ref: ADR-0016

  authors:
    - AfriTech Governance

  context:
    problem: >
      AfriTech may receive evidence from multiple external systems, devices,
      partners, operators, and proof emitters. These sources can disagree,
      degrade, replay late, or provide incomplete data. AfriTech needs a
      governed method for comparing evidence quality without allowing external
      sources or trust scores to define constitutional truth.

    goal: >
      Define a deterministic multi-source consensus and external trust scoring
      doctrine that ranks evidence reliability, detects disagreement, and
      preserves source independence while keeping replay and governance as the
      only admissible truth authorities.

    constraints:
      - Trust scores are advisory evidence, not truth.
      - External sources must not define AfriTech constitutional authority.
      - Consensus must preserve dissent and disagreement.
      - Consensus must be deterministic for identical inputs.
      - Missing or late evidence must be classified, not silently ignored.

  decision: >
    Adopt a bounded multi-source consensus and external trust scoring model.
    The model produces advisory trust assessments over declared evidence
    sources. It may influence review priority, admission confidence, and
    operator attention, but it must not override replay, proof verification,
    governance invariants, or runtime admission rules.

  source_contract:
    artifact_type: multisource_evidence_packet
    version: v1
    required_fields:
      - source_id
      - source_type
      - issued_at
      - evidence_hash
      - evidence_type
      - trust_inputs
      - signature_status
      - replay_status
      - declared_limitations

    source_types:
      - internal_runtime
      - mobile_device
      - partner_system
      - operator_attestation
      - external_registry
      - audit_artifact

  trust_scoring_model:
    score_range:
      min: 0
      max: 100
    deterministic_inputs:
      - signature_status
      - replay_status
      - source_history
      - freshness
      - schema_validity
      - contradiction_count
      - declared_limitations
    forbidden_inputs:
      - hidden heuristics
      - operator preference
      - filesystem location
      - undocumented reputation claims
      - runtime side effects

    score_classes:
      trusted:
        range: [80, 100]
        meaning: strong advisory confidence
      review_required:
        range: [50, 79]
        meaning: admissible for review, not automatic trust
      low_confidence:
        range: [1, 49]
        meaning: weak advisory confidence
      rejected:
        range: [0, 0]
        meaning: invalid or constitutionally inadmissible evidence

  consensus_model:
    output_type: advisory_consensus_packet
    required_outputs:
      - consensus_id
      - source_ids
      - source_scores
      - agreement_set
      - disagreement_set
      - missing_sources
      - late_sources
      - confidence_summary
      - non_claims

    deterministic_flow:
      - validate source packets
      - compute per-source trust scores
      - group matching evidence hashes
      - preserve contradictions as disagreement_set
      - classify missing and late sources
      - compute confidence_summary from declared inputs
      - emit advisory packet with non-claims

  constitutional_boundary:
    advisory_only: true
    runtime_authoritative: false
    replay_authoritative: false
    proof_authoritative: false
    governance_authoritative: true
    may_trigger_review: true
    may_block_when_invalid: true
    may_override_replay: false
    may_override_invariants: false

  invariants:
    - id: INVARIANT-016
      description: >
        Multi-source consensus must preserve disagreement and must not collapse
        conflicting evidence into false equivalence.
    - id: INVARIANT-016B
      description: >
        Trust scores must be deterministic, explainable, and derived only from
        declared inputs.
    - id: INVARIANT-016C
      description: >
        External trust scores must remain advisory and must not define runtime
        or replay truth.

  rules:
    - id: RULE-016-1
      description: Every scored source must declare evidence_hash and source_id.
    - id: RULE-016-2
      description: Trust scores must include input attribution.
    - id: RULE-016-3
      description: Disagreement must remain visible in consensus output.
    - id: RULE-016-4
      description: External scores must never override replay validation.
    - id: RULE-016-5
      description: Rejected sources must include rejection reason.

  bindings:
    - id: BIND-016-1
      description: Trust confidence doctrine binding.
      target: afritech/truth/epistemic_confidence.yaml
    - id: BIND-016-2
      description: Consensus proof validation surface.
      target: afritech/ci/convergence_validator.py
    - id: BIND-016-3
      description: Multi-source consensus validation surface.
      target: afritech/ci/convergence_validator.py

  ci:
    required_checks:
      - source_packets_are_declared
      - trust_score_inputs_are_attributed
      - disagreement_is_preserved
      - rejected_sources_have_reasons
      - advisory_boundary_is_explicit
      - replay_override_is_forbidden

    tests:
      - test_distributed_convergence_engine
      - test_network_convergence_under_delay
      - test_convergence_validator
      - test_witness_forgery_detection

  non_claims:
    - Trust score does not equal truth.
    - Consensus does not eliminate dissent.
    - External evidence does not define AfriTech authority.
    - Advisory confidence does not replace replay or proof verification.

  consequences:
    positives:
      - External evidence can be ranked without becoming authoritative.
      - Disagreement becomes explicit and reviewable.
      - Trust scoring can support operator and governance workflows.
      - Future AI readiness workflows can consume confidence packets safely.
    negatives:
      - Trust inputs must be carefully versioned.
      - Consensus packets add another audit surface.

  supersedes: []

  depends_on:
    - ADR-0014
    - ADR-0015

  next_admissible_step: >
    Introduce RULE-016 and BIND-016 only after trust scoring examples and
    adversarial disagreement fixtures are declared.


===== ./afritech/governance/adr/ADR-0018-cross-system-continuity.yaml =====
schema: afritech.governance.adr.v1

adr:
  id: ADR-0018
  title: Cross-System Continuity and Federated Proof
  status: ACCEPTED
  type: CONSTITUTIONAL_FEDERATED_CONTINUITY
  proposed_epoch: 7
  approved_at: "2026-05-28T21:47:22Z"
  registry_ref: ADR-0018

  authors:
    - AfriTech Governance

  context:
    problem: >
      AfriTech systems increasingly need to preserve continuity across system
      boundaries: AfriRide, AfriProgramming, mobile clients, edge ingestion,
      partner services, audit stores, and external proof consumers. A single
      local proof is insufficient when continuity depends on evidence moving
      across independently governed systems.

    goal: >
      Define a federated proof and cross-system continuity doctrine that keeps
      evidence portable, independently verifiable, causally linked, and bounded
      by AfriTech constitutional authority.

    constraints:
      - Federated proof must not require a shared database.
      - Every participating system must retain source evidence authority.
      - Cross-system continuity must preserve causal order and hash linkage.
      - External systems must not define AfriTech runtime truth.
      - Federation must be replay-verifiable from declared proof packets.

  decision: >
    Adopt a cross-system continuity and federated proof model. Participating
    systems exchange federated proof packets containing source proof hashes,
    issuer identity, continuity links, causality metadata, signatures, and
    declared limitations. AfriTech may verify and aggregate these packets, but
    the federation layer remains a governance and proof boundary, not a runtime
    authority boundary.

  federated_proof_contract:
    artifact_type: federated_proof_packet
    version: v1
    required_fields:
      - federation_id
      - issuer_system
      - subject_system
      - issued_at
      - proof_type
      - source_proof_hash
      - continuity_link_hash
      - causal_sequence
      - signature
      - public_key_id
      - limitations
      - non_claims

    continuity_link_fields:
      - previous_packet_hash
      - current_packet_hash
      - source_event_hash
      - target_event_hash
      - replay_status
      - continuity_property

  federation_model:
    topology: DECLARED_PARTICIPANTS
    participant_requirements:
      - stable system identity
      - declared public key reference
      - proof packet schema support
      - source proof retention
      - replay status declaration

    forbidden_federation_behaviors:
      - implicit participant authority
      - unverifiable proof forwarding
      - hash-link rewriting
      - hidden participant scoring
      - external runtime truth injection

  validation_flow:
    - validate packet schema
    - resolve issuer public key
    - verify packet signature
    - verify source_proof_hash format
    - verify continuity_link_hash
    - validate causal_sequence monotonicity within packet scope
    - preserve limitations and non_claims
    - emit federation verification result

  constitutional_boundary:
    federation_authority: PROOF_TRANSPORT_AND_VALIDATION
    source_truth_authority: SOURCE_SYSTEM_PROOF
    afritech_truth_authority: AFRITECH_REPLAY_AND_GOVERNANCE
    runtime_authoritative: false
    replay_authoritative: false
    governance_authoritative: true
    external_system_may_define_afritech_truth: false
    federation_may_trigger_governance_review: true

  invariants:
    - id: INVARIANT-018
      description: >
        Federated proof packets must preserve source proof identity and causal
        continuity links across system boundaries.
    - id: INVARIANT-018B
      description: >
        Cross-system continuity must be independently verifiable without shared
        mutable database state.
    - id: INVARIANT-018C
      description: >
        External systems must not define AfriTech runtime truth through
        federation.

  rules:
    - id: RULE-018-1
      description: Federated packets must include issuer and subject system identity.
    - id: RULE-018-2
      description: Federated packets must include source_proof_hash and continuity_link_hash.
    - id: RULE-018-3
      description: Federated signatures must verify against declared public keys.
    - id: RULE-018-4
      description: Causal sequence must be preserved within packet scope.
    - id: RULE-018-5
      description: External systems must not declare AfriTech runtime authority.

  bindings:
    - id: BIND-018-1
      description: Cross-system proof verifier binding.
      target: afritech.services.cross_proof_verifier.verify_cross_system_proof
    - id: BIND-018-2
      description: Cross-system proof API binding.
      target: afritech/api/cross_proof_views.py
    - id: BIND-018-3
      description: Public key registry binding.
      target: afritech.crypto.key_registry.PUBLIC_KEY_REGISTRY
    - id: BIND-018-4
      description: Cross-system continuity test binding.
      target: tests/integration/test_cross_system_proof.py

  ci:
    required_checks:
      - federated_packet_schema_valid
      - issuer_key_resolves
      - signature_verifies
      - continuity_link_hash_present
      - source_proof_hash_preserved
      - external_runtime_authority_forbidden

    tests:
      - test_valid_cross_proof
      - test_cross_system_proof_valid
      - test_invalid_signature_rejected
      - test_unknown_public_key_fails
      - test_merkle_mismatch_rejected

  non_claims:
    - Federation does not create shared runtime authority.
    - Federation does not replace local replay.
    - Federation does not certify external systems as globally trusted.
    - Federation does not remove the need for source proof retention.

  consequences:
    positives:
      - Cross-system proof can be verified without shared databases.
      - Federated evidence becomes portable and auditable.
      - Causal continuity can be preserved across independently governed systems.
      - AfriTech can participate in trust federation without surrendering authority.
    negatives:
      - Participant identity and key lifecycle must be governed.
      - Federated proof packets require stable schema versioning.

  supersedes: []

  depends_on:
    - ADR-0014
    - ADR-0015
    - ADR-0016

  next_admissible_step: >
    Introduce RULE-018 and BIND-018 after federated packet fixtures and CI
    schema validation are declared.


===== ./afritech/governance/adr/ADR-0019-afritech-constitution-v1-binding.yaml =====
schema: afritech.governance.adr.v1

adr:
  id: ADR-0019
  title: AfriTech Constitution v1.0 Declared Binding
  status: ACCEPTED
  type: CONSTITUTIONAL_DECLARED_BINDING
  proposed_epoch: 7
  approved_at: "2026-05-28T21:47:22Z"
  registry_ref: ADR-0019

  authors:
    - AfriTech Governance

  context: >
    AfriTech Constitution v1.0 is documented, parseable, validated,
    test-preserved, pipeline-preserved, and checkpointed. The Constitution
    remains doctrine and is not yet active runtime authority.

  problem_statement: >
    The Constitution now requires a declared executable binding layer so future
    implementation work can reference it through traceable governance artifacts
    without silently promoting doctrine into runtime truth.

  decision: >
    Introduce a declared non-runtime binding layer for AfriTech Constitution v1.0.
    The binding layer admits the Constitution doctrine, machine-readable spec,
    validator, and checkpoint as governed executable surfaces for validation and
    traceability only.

  constitutional_boundary:
    runtime_authoritative: false
    governance_authoritative: true
    doctrine_authoritative: true
    binding_required_before_runtime_enforcement: true
    silent_runtime_promotion_forbidden: true

  admitted_surfaces:
    - afritech/constitution/AFRITECH_CONSTITUTION_V1.md
    - afritech/constitution/AFRITECH_CONSTITUTION_V1.yaml
    - afritech/ci/afritech_constitution_v1_validator.py
    - docs/proof/AFRITECH_CONSTITUTION_V1_CHECKPOINT.md

  linked_invariants:
    - INVARIANT-019

  required_rule:
    - RULE-019

  required_binding:
    - BIND-019

  required_ci_checks:
    - afritech.ci.afritech_constitution_v1_binding_validator

  constraints:
    - Constitution v1.0 binding must validate doctrine shape.
    - Constitution v1.0 binding must validate checkpoint preservation.
    - Constitution v1.0 binding must not invoke runtime execution.
    - Constitution v1.0 binding must not modify active canonical registry authority.
    - Runtime enforcement requires a future ADR, rule, binding, guard, and CI chain.

  consequences:
    positives:
      - Constitution v1.0 becomes traceably bindable.
      - Future implementation work can reference declared surfaces.
      - Runtime authority remains protected.
      - Governance evolution remains reversible.
    negatives:
      - One additional validation layer is required before runtime binding.

  next_admissible_step: >
    Define runtime enforcement only through a future ADR after the declared
    binding layer remains green.


===== ./afritech/governance/adr/afritech/governance/adr/ADR-0007-afriride-v0-2.yaml =====
# afritech/governance/adr/ADR-000X-afriride-v0-2.yaml

schema: afritech.governance.adr.v1

adr:
  id: ADR-000X
  title: AfriRide v0.2 Proof-of-Continuity Expansion
  status: PROPOSED
  type: CONSTITUTIONAL_EVOLUTION
  proposed_epoch: 7
  date: 2026-05-13

  authors:
    - AfriTech Governance

  related_artifacts:
    - ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/
    - ecosystems/afriride/AFRIRIDE_V0_FREEZE.md

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ CONTEXT
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  context: >
    AfriRide v0.1 established deterministic, replay-verifiable
    coordination under a single controlled failure scenario.
    That version was constitutionally frozen under Epoch 6
    as immutable Proof-of-Continuity evidence.

    AfriRide v0.2 extends this proof to a bounded and explicitly
    declared failure space, demonstrating that deterministic,
    authority-governed coordination remains replay-sound across
    multiple failure modes without mutating or weakening v0.1.

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ DECISION
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  decision: >
    Introduce AfriRide v0.2 as a new Proof-of-Continuity surface
    that expands the validated failure space while preserving
    all AfriRide v0.1 guarantees.

    AfriRide v0.2 is introduced as an additive, layered system
    operating under a new determinism envelope and execution
    runtime, without modification of the frozen constitutional
    core.

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ SCOPE
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  scope:

    added:
      - DRIVER_DROPOUT deterministic failure scenario
      - DRIVER_REJECTION_CHAIN deterministic failure scenario
      - TIMEOUT_EXCEEDED deterministic failure scenario
      - v0.2 deterministic failure taxonomy
      - v0.2 determinism envelope
      - multi-scenario continuity metrics:
          - Continuity Coverage (CC)
          - Deterministic Refusal Rate (DRR)
      - v0.2 execution handlers and runtime wrapper

    preserved:
      - AfriRide v0.1 (constitutionally frozen)
      - v0.1 determinism guarantees
      - v0.1 replay identity guarantees
      - v0.1 authority model and execution law

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ INVARIANTS
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  invariants:
    - v0.1 artifacts MUST NOT be modified
    - v0.2 execution MUST be layered externally
    - replay hash identity is mandatory
    - deterministic refusal is required on exhaustion or timeout
    - all failures MUST be declared in the failure taxonomy
    - no nondeterministic behavior is permitted
    - determinism envelope violations invalidate proof

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ EVIDENCE
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  evidence:
    executable:
      - ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/run_multi_scenario_demo.py

    scenarios:
      - ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/scenarios/driver_dropout.yaml
      - ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/scenarios/rejection_chain.yaml
      - ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/scenarios/timeout.yaml

    metrics:
      - Decision Determinism Rate (DDR): 1.0 (all scenarios)
      - Continuity Coverage (CC): 1.0
      - Deterministic Refusal Rate (DRR): 1.0

    replay:
      - identical execution and replay hashes verified
      - replay verifier status: VALID

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ CONSEQUENCES
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  consequences:

    positive:
      - expands AfriRide continuity proof to multiple failure modes
      - strengthens AfriTech institutional credibility
      - demonstrates scalable governance without historical mutation
      - preserves replay-verifiable lineage across epochs

    negative:
      - requires new constitutional epoch (Epoch 7)
      - increases governance surface area
      - introduces additional audit responsibilities

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ STATUS & NEXT STEPS
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  status_notes:
    - This ADR authorizes advancement to Epoch 7
    - AfriRide v0.2 may be frozen after ratification
    - No product, optimization, or performance claims are authorized

  approval_requirements:
    - governance review
    - replay verifier confirmation
    - epoch registry update

===== ./afritech/governance/anti_entropy.yaml =====
schema: afritech.governance.anti_entropy.v1

anti_entropy:

  description: >
    Defines risk classes that introduce implementation entropy.
    These must never occur in the system.

  risks:

    semantic_drift:
      forbidden: true

    implicit_runtime_semantics:
      forbidden: true

    proof_bypass:
      forbidden: true

    non_determinism:
      forbidden: true

    layer_contamination:
      forbidden: true

    optimization_semantic_shortcuts:
      forbidden: true

  enforcement:

    runtime: REQUIRED
    ci: REQUIRED
    code_review: REQUIRED
    architecture_freeze: REQUIRED

  executable_boundary:

    runtime_code_may:
      - execute_declared_semantics
      - normalize_declared_semantics
      - hash_normalized_semantics
      - evaluate_normalized_semantics
      - generate_proofs

    runtime_code_must_not:
      - define_semantics
      - infer_semantic_authority
      - bypass_normalization
      - evaluate_raw_expressions
      - introduce_new_operators_without_constitutional_declaration

  frozen_surfaces:
    constitution: FROZEN
    semantic_calculus: FROZEN
    meta_semantics: FROZEN

  allowed_changes_after_freeze:
    - bug_fixes
    - contradiction_resolution
    - implementation_required_clarifications

  failure:

    block_execution: true
    block_merge: true
    severity: CRITICAL


===== ./afritech/governance/autonomous_constitution.yaml =====
schema: afritech.governance.autonomous_constitution.v1

autonomous_constitution:

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # PURPOSE
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  description: >
    Defines a self-evolving constitutional system capable of:
      - detecting structural inefficiencies
      - generating governance improvements
      - validating upgrades via consensus
      - applying safe constitutional mutations

  model: SELF_UPGRADING_CONSTITUTIONAL_SYSTEM

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # CORE PRINCIPLES
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  principles:

    - constitution_is_mutable_but_controlled
    - all_changes_must_be_deterministically_justified
    - evolution_requires_consensus
    - replay_validity_must_be_preserved
    - backward_compatibility_must_be_enforced

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # EVOLUTION TRIGGERS
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  triggers:

    - repeated_consensus_failures
    - performance_degradation
    - governance_rule_conflicts
    - security_violations
    - persistent_node_disagreements
    - drift_pattern_detection

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # PROPOSAL GENERATION
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  proposal_engine:

    description: >
      Automatically generates constitutional amendments.

    inputs:

      - system_metrics
      - failure_patterns
      - node_reputation_history
      - replay_inconsistencies

    output:

      proposal:

        id: "ADR-AUTO-XXXX"

        type: GOVERNANCE_CHANGE

        changes:

          - modify_rule
          - adjust_threshold
          - introduce_new_constraint
          - deprecate_existing_rule

    invariant:

      - proposals_must_be_deterministic
      - proposals_must_be_replayable

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # SIMULATION LAYER
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  simulation:

    description: >
      Proposed changes are executed in a simulated environment.

    requirements:

      - deterministic_re-execution
      - replay_verification
      - historical_data_testing

    validation:

      must_pass:

        - all_previous_replay_cases
        - current_execution_paths
        - consensus_verification

      failure_effect: proposal_rejected

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # CONSENSUS APPROVAL
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  approval:

    model: MULTI_NODE_CONSENSUS

    requirements:

      - 2/3 quorum agreement
      - no critical replay regression
      - no security degradation

    voting:

      unit: node

      process:

        - evaluate_proposal
        - simulate_execution
        - submit_vote

    rejection_conditions:

      - replay_failure
      - increased divergence
      - invariant_violation

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # SAFE APPLICATION
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  application:

    description: >
      Approved changes are applied in a controlled manner.

    steps:

      - create_new_epoch
      - update_registry
      - regenerate_attestation
      - issue_new_certificate

    constraints:

      - atomic_change_application
      - rollback_possible
      - version_history_preserved

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # VERSIONING SYSTEM
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  versioning:

    version_scheme: SEMANTIC_CONSTITUTION

    components:

      - MAJOR: breaking governance change
      - MINOR: additive rule
      - PATCH: adjustment

    invariant:

      - version_must_increase_monotonically

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # SELF-VALIDATION LOOP
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  evolution_loop:

    cycle:

      - observe_system
      - detect_patterns
      - generate_proposal
      - simulate_proposal
      - consensus_vote
      - apply_or_reject

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # CONSTRAINTS (CRITICAL)
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  constraints:

    - no_change_may_break_replay
    - no_change_may_introduce_nondeterminism
    - no_change_may_bypass_authority_rules
    - no_change_may alter surface_registry without validation

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # FAIL-SAFE MECHANISMS
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  fail_safe:

    rollback:

      description: >
        If post-upgrade failure is detected,
        system reverts to previous version.

      trigger:

        - consensus_failure_post_upgrade
        - replay_regression

    freeze:

      description: >
        Evolution disabled if instability threshold exceeded.

      condition:

        - repeated_failed_proposals > threshold

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # GOVERNANCE MEMORY (ADR AUTO-GENERATION)
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  adr_generation:

    enabled: true

    description: >
      Every accepted proposal becomes an ADR.

    structure:

      - id
      - change_summary
      - justification
      - simulation_results
      - consensus_result

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # FINAL ASSERTION
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  final_assertion: >
    The constitution evolves itself.

    It observes its own behavior,
    improves its own rules,
    validates its own changes,
    and applies them without human intervention.

    No evolution without proof.
    No change without consensus.
    No regression without rollback.

===== ./afritech/governance/binding_schema.yaml =====
schema: afritech.binding.v1

binding_manifest:

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # SOURCE ARTIFACT ANCHORING (CRITICAL ROOT INPUTS)
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  source_artifacts:

    genesis_hash: sha256
    trace_schema_hash: sha256
    state_schema_hash: sha256
    invariants_hash: sha256

    # OPTIONAL EXTENSIONS
    proof_system_hash: sha256
    runtime_surface_hash: sha256

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # DERIVED BINDINGS (NO MANUAL DEFINITIONS)
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  bindings:

    - id: string

      type:
        enum:
          - TRACE_BINDING
          - STATE_BINDING
          - AUTHORITY_BINDING
          - PROOF_BINDING
          - RUNTIME_BINDING

      target: string
      constraint: string

      derived_from:
        - string

      # -------------------------------------------------------------
      # CRYPTOGRAPHIC IDENTITY
      # -------------------------------------------------------------

      binding_hash: sha256
      binding_version: string

      # -------------------------------------------------------------
      # EXECUTION CONTEXT
      # -------------------------------------------------------------

      scope:

        applies_to:
          - TRACE
          - STATE
          - EXECUTION
          - VALIDATION

        enforcement_level:
          enum:
            - STRICT
            - MANDATORY
            - OPTIONAL

      # -------------------------------------------------------------
      # VALIDATION RULE
      # -------------------------------------------------------------

      validation:

        required: true
        failure_policy:

          type: HARD_FAILURE
          effect: SYSTEM_REJECTION

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # ROOT BINDING COMMITMENT
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  root_binding_hash: sha256

  # sha256 over:
  #   ordered list of binding_hash values

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # DETERMINISM + REPLAY GUARANTEE
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  determinism:

    canonical_serialization: json_sorted_compact
    hashing_algorithm: sha256

    replay:

      expected:

        - identical_binding_list
        - identical_binding_hashes
        - identical_root_binding_hash

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # SYSTEM INVARIANTS (AUTHORITY CLOSURE)
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  invariants:

    - I21_BINDING_DERIVATION
      # all bindings MUST be machine-generated

    - I21_1_NO_MANUAL_BINDINGS
      # no human-authored bindings allowed

    - I21_2_CANONICAL_DERIVATION
      # identical inputs MUST produce identical bindings

    - I21_3_BINDING_HASH_INTEGRITY
      # each binding_hash MUST match canonical recomputation

    - I21_4_ROOT_HASH_CORRECT
      # root_binding_hash MUST match recomputed value

    - I21_5_AUTHORITY_DERIVED_FROM_GENESIS
      # authority MUST trace back to genesis_hash

    - I21_6_NO_ORPHAN_BINDINGS
      # every binding MUST derive from source artifacts

    - I21_7_REPLAY_EQUALITY
      # replay MUST regenerate identical manifest

    - I21_8_BINDING_ENUM_EXHAUSTIVENESS
      # all system layers MUST be covered by bindings

    - I21_9_IMMUTABILITY
      # bindings MUST NOT change after generation

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # VALIDATION CONTRACT
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  validation:

    required_at_system_boot: true
    required_for_runtime_execution: true
    required_for_proof_verification: true

    enforce_hash_integrity: true
    enforce_derivation: true
    enforce_total_binding_coverage: true

    failure_policy:

      type: HARD_FAILURE
      effect: SYSTEM_NON_EXISTENCE

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # TRACE + STATE INTEGRATION
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  integration:

    trace:

      requirement:
        trace.event.step MUST map to binding.target

    state_machine:

      requirement:
        transitions MUST comply with binding constraints

    proof:

      requirement:
        all proofs MUST validate against binding constraints

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # SECURITY MODEL
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  security:

    authority_model: DERIVED_ONLY
    tamper_resistance: CRYPTOGRAPHIC

    guarantees:

      - no hidden authority paths
      - no privilege escalation
      - no drift in derived bindings
      - no manual override possible

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # METADATA (NON-AUTHORITATIVE)
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  metadata:

    version: v1
    environment: production

    description: >
      Binding manifest schema defining the deterministic derivation
      of all authority relationships within AfriTech.

    notes: >
      This artifact MUST be generated automatically. Any manual
      modification invalidates the entire system.

===== ./afritech/governance/bindings/BIND-014-cross-proof.yaml =====
id: BIND-014

description: >
  Defines the binding between cross-system proof validation rules (RULE‑014)
  and their concrete implementation within the AfriTech platform.
  Ensures that all proof verification logic is executed through
  governed, auditable components.

bindings:

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ CORE VERIFICATION ENGINE
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  - id: BIND-014-1
    name: cross_proof_verifier
    description: >
      Primary verification entry point for cross-system proofs.
      Responsible for orchestrating all validation steps.
    target: afritech.services.cross_proof_verifier.verify_cross_system_proof
    responsibilities:
      - schema validation
      - merkle recomputation
      - signature verification
      - rule enforcement
    linked_rules:
      - RULE-014-1
      - RULE-014-6
      - RULE-014-7

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ KEY REGISTRY
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  - id: BIND-014-2
    name: public_key_registry
    description: >
      Resolves public keys used for signature verification.
    target: afritech.crypto.key_registry.PUBLIC_KEY_REGISTRY
    responsibilities:
      - provide public key lookup by public_key_id
      - enforce key presence
    linked_rules:
      - RULE-014-5

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ MERKLE ENGINE
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  - id: BIND-014-3
    name: merkle_engine
    description: >
      Computes deterministic merkle root from logs.
    target: afritech.crypto.merkle.compute_merkle_root
    responsibilities:
      - rebuild merkle tree
      - ensure deterministic hashing
    linked_rules:
      - RULE-014-6

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ SIGNATURE VERIFICATION
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  - id: BIND-014-4
    name: signature_verifier
    description: >
      Validates signature against merkle_root and public key.
    target: afritech.crypto.signature_utils.verify_signature
    responsibilities:
      - cryptographic signature validation
      - reject invalid signatures
    linked_rules:
      - RULE-014-7

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ STRUCTURE VALIDATION GUARD
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  - id: BIND-014-5
    name: proof_structure_guard
    description: >
      Validates proof structure and required fields.
    target: afritech.guards.guard_cross_proof.guard_validate_proof_structure
    responsibilities:
      - ensure required fields exist
      - ensure schema compliance
    linked_rules:
      - RULE-014-1
      - RULE-014-2
      - RULE-014-3
      - RULE-014-4

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ MERKLE VALIDATION GUARD
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  - id: BIND-014-6
    name: merkle_validation_guard
    description: >
      Ensures recomputed merkle root matches provided value.
    target: afritech.guards.guard_cross_proof.guard_validate_merkle
    responsibilities:
      - compare computed vs provided root
    linked_rules:
      - RULE-014-6

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ SIGNATURE VALIDATION GUARD
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  - id: BIND-014-7
    name: signature_validation_guard
    description: >
      Confirms signature verification result.
    target: afritech.guards.guard_cross_proof.guard_validate_signature
    responsibilities:
      - enforce signature validity
    linked_rules:
      - RULE-014-7

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ✅ EXECUTION FLOW
# ---------------------------------------------------------

execution_flow:
  - step: validate_structure
    binding: BIND-014-5

  - step: compute_merkle
    binding: BIND-014-3

  - step: validate_merkle
    binding: BIND-014-6

  - step: resolve_key
    binding: BIND-014-2

  - step: verify_signature
    binding: BIND-014-4

  - step: enforce_signature
    binding: BIND-014-7

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ✅ FAILURE POLICY
# ---------------------------------------------------------

failure_policy:
  on_any_failure: REJECT_PROOF
  behavior:
    - stop_processing: true
    - log_incident: true
    - emit_alert: true

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ✅ TRACEABILITY
# ---------------------------------------------------------

linked_rules:
  - RULE-014

linked_invariants:
  - INVARIANT-014
  - INVARIANT-014B
  - INVARIANT-014C

linked_adr:
  - ADR-0014

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ✅ CI ENFORCEMENT
# ---------------------------------------------------------

ci:
  enforcement:
    - All bindings must resolve to valid Python paths
    - All referenced functions must exist
    - All guards must return boolean values
    - Verification flow must execute without side effects

  tests:
    - test_binding_paths_valid
    - test_verifier_executes_all_steps
    - test_missing_binding_fails
    - test_invalid_guard_output_fails
    - test_successful_proof_verification    

===== ./afritech/governance/bindings/BIND-019-constitution-v1.yaml =====
id: BIND-019

description: >
  Declares the non-runtime executable binding surface for AfriTech Constitution
  v1.0. This binding makes the doctrine validateable through CI while preserving
  the boundary that Constitution v1.0 is not active runtime authority.

bindings:
  - id: BIND-019-1
    name: constitution_v1_doctrine
    target: afritech/constitution/AFRITECH_CONSTITUTION_V1.md
    binding_type: DOCUMENT
    runtime_authoritative: false
    responsibilities:
      - preserve human-readable canonical doctrine
      - provide source language for future governance work
    linked_rules:
      - RULE-019-1

  - id: BIND-019-2
    name: constitution_v1_machine_spec
    target: afritech/constitution/AFRITECH_CONSTITUTION_V1.yaml
    binding_type: MACHINE_SPEC
    runtime_authoritative: false
    responsibilities:
      - provide parseable doctrine structure
      - support deterministic validation
    linked_rules:
      - RULE-019-2

  - id: BIND-019-3
    name: constitution_v1_validator
    target: afritech.ci.afritech_constitution_v1_validator
    binding_type: CI_VALIDATOR
    runtime_authoritative: false
    responsibilities:
      - validate Constitution v1.0 structure
      - validate hierarchy and canonical relationship
    linked_rules:
      - RULE-019-3

  - id: BIND-019-4
    name: constitution_v1_checkpoint
    target: docs/proof/AFRITECH_CONSTITUTION_V1_CHECKPOINT.md
    binding_type: PROOF_DOCUMENT
    runtime_authoritative: false
    responsibilities:
      - preserve green checkpoint evidence
      - record next admissible binding step
    linked_rules:
      - RULE-019-4

execution_flow:
  - step: validate_doctrine_exists
    binding: BIND-019-1
  - step: validate_machine_spec
    binding: BIND-019-2
  - step: run_constitution_validator
    binding: BIND-019-3
  - step: validate_checkpoint
    binding: BIND-019-4
  - step: assert_non_runtime_authority
    binding: BIND-019-1

failure_policy:
  on_any_failure: REJECT_BINDING
  behavior:
    - stop_binding_progression: true
    - preserve_existing_runtime_authority: true
    - require_governance_review: true

linked_rules:
  - RULE-019

linked_invariants:
  - INVARIANT-019

linked_adr:
  - ADR-0019

  enforcement:
    - Binding targets must resolve.
    - Constitution v1.0 validator must pass.
    - Runtime authoritative flags must remain false.
    - Checkpoint proof must record green test, validator, and pipeline status.
  tests:
    - test_constitution_v1_binding_validator_passes
    - test_constitution_v1_binding_is_not_runtime_authoritative
    - test_constitution_v1_binding_targets_resolve

===== ./afritech/governance/development_rules.yaml =====
schema: afritech.governance.development_rules.v1

rules:

  D1_NO_SHORTCUT:
    statement: >
      If correctness depends on a shortcut, the implementation must be rejected.

  D2_EXPLAINABILITY:
    statement: >
      Every evaluation must be traceable, normalized, and provable.

  D3_REPRODUCIBILITY:
    statement: >
      Any result must be reproducible across environments and runs.

  D4_NO_MAGIC:
    statement: >
      No hidden behavior, implicit logic, or silent inference is allowed.

review_requirements:

  - "Does this introduce semantic meaning in code?"
  - "Can this be expressed in S-IR?"
  - "Is this deterministic?"
  - "Are proofs preserved?"
  - "Is normalization enforced?"
