===== ./afritech/governance/roles.yaml =====
schema: afritech.governance.roles.v1

# =====================================================================
# GOVERNANCE ROLES & QUORUM SEMANTICS (DECLARATIVE, NON-AUTHORITATIVE)
# =====================================================================
# This file defines GOVERNANCE INTENT ONLY.
#
# It:
#   - Grants NO authority
#   - Enforces NO rules
#   - Triggers NO behavior
#   - Is NOT consulted by runtime or guards
#
# Authority remains exclusively with:
#   - registry.yaml
#   - epoch mechanics
#   - ADR approval records
#
# This document exists to enable future institutional scalability
# without retroactively redefining legitimacy.
# =====================================================================

purpose: >
  Declarative description of governance roles and approval semantics
  for AfriTech constitutional evolution.
  This file documents how authority may be exercised by humans,
  but does not enforce or grant that authority.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Governance roles
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------

governance_roles:

  proposer:
    description: >
      Proposes Architectural Decision Records (ADRs).
      May author and submit changes for consideration.
    permissions:
      - draft_adr
      - submit_adr
    restrictions:
      - may_not_approve
      - may_not_reseal
      - may_not_advance_epoch

  verifier:
    description: >
      Reviews proposed ADRs for constitutional correctness,
      completeness, and invariant preservation.
    permissions:
      - review_adr
      - request_changes
    restrictions:
      - may_not_approve
      - may_not_execute
      - may_not_override

  approver:
    description: >
      Approves ADRs according to quorum rules.
      Approval authorizes epoch advancement but does not execute it.
    permissions:
      - approve_adr
    restrictions:
      - may_not_execute
      - may_not_override_runtime

  attestor:
    description: >
      Attests that governance procedures were followed correctly.
      Provides institutional confirmation only.
    permissions:
      - attest_process
    restrictions:
      - may_not_block
      - may_not_execute
      - may_not_override

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Quorum semantics (declarative)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------

quorum_rules:

  adr_approval:
    description: >
      Rules defining how many approvers are required
      to approve an ADR for constitutional evolution.
    model: SIMPLE_MAJORITY
    parameters:
      minimum_approvers: 1
      fraction_required: 0.50

  epoch_advance_authorization:
    description: >
      An epoch may advance only after a valid ADR approval quorum
      has been satisfied.
    depends_on:
      - adr_approval

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Governance invariants (non-enforcing)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------

governance_invariants:
  - no_single_actor_may_hold_all_roles
  - approval_and_execution_are_separate
  - governance_does_not_override_runtime
  - governance_never_bypasses_registry

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Current operational mode
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------

operational_mode:
  governance_model: CENTRALIZED
  reason: >
    Current governance is intentionally centralized.
    These role definitions exist to support future
    scaling without ambiguity.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Authority disclaimer
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------

authority_disclaimer: >
  This file is documentary and non-authoritative.
  It does not grant power, enforce behavior,
  or supersede the registry, epochs, or runtime guards.
  In any conflict, registry.yaml and epoch history prevail.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Status
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------

status: ACTIVE
mutable: true

===== ./afritech/governance/rules/RULE-014-cross-proof.yaml =====
id: RULE-014

description: >
  Defines mandatory validation rules for cross-system proof verification
  as specified in ADR‑0014. These rules ensure integrity, authenticity,
  and deterministic reproducibility of proofs.

rules:

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ CORE STRUCTURE REQUIREMENTS
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  - id: RULE-014-1
    description: Proof must include merkle_root
    enforcement:
      type: required_field
      field: merkle_root
      on_failure: REJECT_PROOF

  - id: RULE-014-2
    description: Proof must include signature
    enforcement:
      type: required_field
      field: signature
      on_failure: REJECT_PROOF

  - id: RULE-014-3
    description: Proof must include public_key_id
    enforcement:
      type: required_field
      field: public_key_id
      on_failure: REJECT_PROOF

  - id: RULE-014-4
    description: Proof must include data.logs for reconstruction
    enforcement:
      type: required_field
      field: data.logs
      on_failure: REJECT_PROOF

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ KEY TRUST VALIDATION
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  - id: RULE-014-5
    description: public_key_id must exist in key registry
    enforcement:
      type: registry_lookup
      target: PUBLIC_KEY_REGISTRY
      field: public_key_id
      on_failure: REJECT_PROOF

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ CRYPTOGRAPHIC VERIFICATION
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  - id: RULE-014-6
    description: Logs must reproduce identical merkle root
    enforcement:
      type: deterministic_check
      process: recompute_merkle_root(data.logs)
      expected: merkle_root
      on_failure: REJECT_PROOF

  - id: RULE-014-7
    description: Signature must match merkle_root using resolved public key
    enforcement:
      type: cryptographic_verification
      input: merkle_root
      key: public_key_id
      signature: signature
      on_failure: REJECT_PROOF

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ OPTIONAL VALIDATION RULES (RECOMMENDED)
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  - id: RULE-014-8
    description: Hash algorithm must be supported and deterministic
    enforcement:
      type: allowed_values
      field: hash_algorithm
      allowed: ["sha256"]
      on_failure: REJECT_PROOF

  - id: RULE-014-9
    description: Timestamp must be valid ISO-8601 format
    enforcement:
      type: format_check
      field: timestamp
      format: iso8601
      on_failure: REJECT_PROOF

  - id: RULE-014-10
    description: Proof version must be supported
    enforcement:
      type: allowed_values
      field: version
      allowed: ["v1"]
      on_failure: REJECT_PROOF

  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ SECURITY HARDENING RULES
  # ---------------------------------------------------------

  - id: RULE-014-11
    description: Proof must not contain conflicting fields
    enforcement:
      type: consistency_check
      logic: no_duplicate_or_conflicting_fields
      on_failure: REJECT_PROOF

  - id: RULE-014-12
    description: Metadata must not affect proof validation
    enforcement:
      type: isolation_check
      field: metadata
      rule: ignored_in_verification
      on_failure: IGNORE_FIELD

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ✅ DEFAULT ACTIONS
# ---------------------------------------------------------

on_failure:
  REJECT_PROOF: >
    Immediately reject proof and stop verification

  IGNORE_FIELD: >
    Ignore non-critical field and continue processing

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ✅ TRACEABILITY
# ---------------------------------------------------------

linked_invariants:
  - INVARIANT-014
  - INVARIANT-014B
  - INVARIANT-014C

linked_adr:
  - ADR-0014

===== ./afritech/governance/rules/RULE-019-constitution-v1-binding.yaml =====
id: RULE-019

description: >
  Defines declared binding rules for AfriTech Constitution v1.0. These rules
  make Constitution v1.0 traceable and validateable without making it runtime
  authoritative.

rules:
  - id: RULE-019-1
    description: Constitution v1.0 doctrine file must exist.
    enforcement:
      type: required_file
      path: afritech/constitution/AFRITECH_CONSTITUTION_V1.md
      on_failure: REJECT_BINDING

  - id: RULE-019-2
    description: Constitution v1.0 machine-readable YAML must parse.
    enforcement:
      type: yaml_parse
      path: afritech/constitution/AFRITECH_CONSTITUTION_V1.yaml
      on_failure: REJECT_BINDING

  - id: RULE-019-3
    description: Constitution v1.0 validator must pass.
    enforcement:
      type: ci_validator
      module: afritech.ci.afritech_constitution_v1_validator
      on_failure: REJECT_BINDING

  - id: RULE-019-4
    description: Constitution v1.0 checkpoint must exist and record green state.
    enforcement:
      type: checkpoint_validation
      path: docs/proof/AFRITECH_CONSTITUTION_V1_CHECKPOINT.md
      required_terms:
        - "1378 passed"
        - "98 passed, 0 failed"
        - "Constitutional closure achieved"
      on_failure: REJECT_BINDING

  - id: RULE-019-5
    description: Constitution v1.0 must not be runtime authoritative in this phase.
    enforcement:
      type: negative_authority_check
      runtime_authoritative: false
      on_failure: REJECT_BINDING

on_failure:
  REJECT_BINDING: >
    Reject the declared Constitution v1.0 binding and prevent further binding
    work until the rule failure is resolved.

linked_invariants:
  - INVARIANT-019

linked_adr:
  - ADR-0019


===== ./afritech/governance/rules/RULE-036-data-locality.yaml =====
id: RULE-036
name: data_locality_enforcement

description: >
  Enforces that all execution surfaces operate on localized,
  bounded data sets to preserve performance and determinism.

applies_to:
  - runtime
  - replay
  - execution_surfaces

requirements:
  - surface_scoped_data_access
  - bounded_working_set
  - deterministic_access_pattern
  - scheduler_trace_required
  - work_stealing_same_partition_and_numa

forbidden:
  - random_access_patterns
  - cross_surface_unbounded_calls
  - pointer_heavy_data_structures
  - cross_partition_work_stealing
  - cross_numa_work_stealing

rules:
  - id: LOCALITY_RULE_9
    rule: Work stealing is permitted only within same partition and NUMA boundary

enforcement:
  level: STRICT
  failure_mode: CI_FAIL
RULE-ADR-DOC-SYNC:
  description: Markdown ADR must match YAML ADR
  enforcement:
    - md file must exist for each ADR
    - content must be derived from YAML

===== ./afritech/governance/rules/RULE-AUDIT-001.yaml =====
id: RULE-AUDIT-CHAIN-INTEGRITY
description: >
  Enforces cryptographic audit chain integrity for all AuditLog entries.

enforcement:
  - every entry must include previous_hash
  - entry_hash must be computed deterministically
  - chain linkage must be verified before insert
  - replay must validate entire chain

actions:
  - reject invalid insert
  - halt replay on chain break


===== ./afritech/governance/rules/RULE-EDGE-001.yaml =====
id: RULE-EDGE-001
status: ACTIVE
classification: CONSTITUTIONAL_EDGE_ADMISSION_RULE
rule: >
  External inputs must never directly enter runtime execution.
  All external inputs must pass through adapter, normalization,
  and ingestion surfaces before execution admission.
enforcement:
  runtime: true
  replay: true
  ci: true
required_sequence:
  - adapter
  - normalization
  - ingestion
forbidden:
  - direct_external_runtime_execution
  - unnormalized_external_input
  - unqueued_external_admission
  - observer_relative_edge_ordering
  - unrecorded_provider_input
failure_effect:
  - execution_refusal
  - replay_invalid
  - ci_failure


===== ./afritech/governance/surface_registry.yaml =====
schema: afritech.governance.surface_registry.v1

surface_registry:

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # PURPOSE
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  description: >
    The Surface Registry defines all system surfaces and their
    constitutional classification, execution eligibility,
    and governance status.

    It is the authoritative mapping used to determine:
      - which surfaces are executable
      - which surfaces are included in hash binding
      - which surfaces are governed
      - which surfaces are strictly informational

  classification_model: EXPLICIT_DECLARATION_ONLY

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # GLOBAL INVARIANTS
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  invariants:

    - no_implicit_surfaces
    - only_declared_surfaces_may_execute
    - only_declared_surfaces_may_be_hashed
    - non_executable_surfaces_must_not_import_runtime
    - infrastructure_surfaces_are_non_constitutional

  enforcement:

    undeclared_surface_effect: HARD_FAILURE
    invalid_execution_effect: RUNTIME_INVALIDATION
    invalid_hash_inclusion: ATTESTATION_FAILURE

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # SURFACE DEFINITIONS
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  surfaces:

    # =============================================================

    registry:

      path: afritech/registry/

    # =============================================================

    evaluation:

      executable: true

    truth_model:

    # =============================================================

    # =============================================================

    guards:

      path: afritech/guards/
      type: EXECUTION_GUARD
      executable: true
      hash_included: true
      authority_scope: GLOBAL_GATE

    # =============================================================
    # AUDIT
    # =============================================================

    audit:

      path: afritech/audit/
      type: LOGGING
      executable: false
      hash_included: false
      authority_scope: OBSERVATION_ONLY

    # =============================================================
    # INFRASTRUCTURE (NON-CONSTITUTIONAL)
    # =============================================================

    infrastructure:

      path: afritech/infrastructure/
      type: NON_CONSTITUTIONAL
      executable: false
      hash_included: false
      authority_scope: NONE

    docker:

      path: afritech/docker/
      type: NON_CONSTITUTIONAL
      executable: false
      hash_included: false
      authority_scope: NONE

    ci:

      path: .github/workflows/
      type: NON_CONSTITUTIONAL
      executable: false
      hash_included: false
      authority_scope: NONE

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # EXECUTION LAW
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  execution_law:

    requirements:

      - surface_must_be_declared
      - surface_must_be_executable_true
      - authority_scope_must_be_satisfied

    violation_response:

      - runtime_refusal
      - replay_invalidation
      - constitutional_failure

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # HASH LAW
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  hash_law:

    requirements:

      - only_hash_included_surfaces_participate
      - all_hash_included_surfaces_must_be_stable
      - infrastructure_excluded_from_hash_chain

    violation_response:

      - attestation_invalidated
      - certificate_invalidated

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # NON-EXECUTABLE SURFACES
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  non_executable_surfaces:

    - afritech/governance/roles.yaml
    - afritech/governance/INDEX.yaml
    - afritech/replay/docs/
    - afritech/audit/

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # PHASE COMPATIBILITY
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  lifecycle:

    current_phase: PHASE_2B

    readiness:

      supports:

        - deterministic_runtime
        - replay_verification
        - authority_enforcement
        - CI_enforcement

      next_extension:

        - multi-node surface validation
        - dynamic surface admission
        - distributed execution surfaces

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  constitutional_assertion: >
    Only declared surfaces exist in the AfriTech system.

    Execution is permitted exclusively on:
      - declared executable surfaces
      - under authority constraints
      - within deterministic boundaries

    Any surface not declared herein is considered non-existent.

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # FINAL ASSERTION
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------

  final_assertion: >
    AfriTech operates as a surface-declared system.

    No declaration → no execution.
    No execution → no effect.
    No effect → no existence.

authority:

  afritech/guards:
    role: SYSTEM_SAFETY_COORDINATION

    responsibilities:
      - escalation
      - quarantine
      - rollback
      - halt_management
      - incident_classification

    forbidden:
      - replay_validation
      - semantic_interpretation
      - runtime_admission

  afritech/runtime/guards:
    role: CONSTITUTIONAL_RUNTIME_ENFORCEMENT

    responsibilities:
      - runtime_admission
      - determinism_validation
      - invariant_enforcement
      - proof_validation

    forbidden:
      - rollback_orchestration
      - operational_incident_management

===== ./afritech/guards/guard_registry.yaml =====
guard_registry:

  metadata:
    version: v1
    description: >
      Canonical registry of all runtime guards enforcing
      constitutional invariants, authority, state, trace,
      schema, and runtime correctness.

  guards:

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # CORE INVARIANT GUARDS
    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    - id: GUARD-INVARIANT-001
      type: INVARIANT_GUARD
      target: SYSTEM_RUNTIME
      rule: "Enforce deterministic execution, replay validity, authority presence, closed world, and proof requirement"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # AUTHORITY GUARDS
    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    - id: GUARD-AUTHORITY-001
      type: AUTHORITY_GUARD
      target: AUTHORITY_CONTEXT
      rule: "All actions must be authorized and derived from genesis root"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # EPOCH GUARDS (TIME CONSISTENCY)
    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    - id: GUARD-EPOCH-001
      type: EPOCH_GUARD
      target: TIME_LAYER
      rule: "Epoch must be active and immutable unless explicitly authorized"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # STATE MACHINE GUARDS
    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    - id: GUARD-STATE-001
      type: STATE_GUARD
      target: STATE_TRANSITIONS
      rule: "Only allowed transitions may execute; illegal transitions are rejected"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # TRACE INTEGRITY GUARDS
    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    - id: GUARD-TRACE-001
      type: TRACE_GUARD
      target: TRACE_EVENTS
      rule: "Trace events must match computed hashes and execution steps"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # SCHEMA VALIDATION GUARDS
    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    - id: GUARD-SCHEMA-001
      type: RUNTIME_GUARD
      target: STRUCTURED_PAYLOADS
      rule: "All payloads must conform to defined JSON schemas"

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
    # RUNTIME SAFETY GUARDS
    # ---------------------------------------------------------

    - id: GUARD-RUNTIME-001
      type: RUNTIME_GUARD
      target: EXECUTION_CONTEXT
      rule: "No undefined behavior or malformed context allowed at runtime"

    - id: GUARD-LOCALITY-001
      type: DATA_LOCALITY_GUARD
      target: EXECUTION_CONTEXT
      path: afritech.guards.data_locality_guard.validate_data_locality
      applies_to:
        - runtime_execution
        - replay_validation
      rule: "Execution data access must remain inside declared data, surface, and partition locality bounds"

  invariants:

    - I23_GUARD_MANDATORY
    - I23_1_NO_BYPASS
    - I23_2_FAIL_FAST
    - I23_3_FULL_COVERAGE
    - I23_4_DETERMINISTIC_ENFORCEMENT
    - I36_DATA_LOCALITY_BOUND

  enforcement:

    mode: STRICT
    failure_action: REJECT

  determinism:

    ordering: defined
    evaluation: deterministic
    side_effects: disallowed
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# GUARD REGISTRY — CROSS SYSTEM PROOF VALIDATION
# ---------------------------------------------------------

guards:

  - id: GUARD-014-1
    name: guard_validate_proof_structure
    description: >
      Validates that the proof contains all required fields and
      follows the expected schema structure.

    implementation: afritech.guards.guard_cross_proof.guard_validate_proof_structure

    linked_rules:
      - RULE-014-1   # merkle_root required
      - RULE-014-2   # signature required
      - RULE-014-3   # public_key_id required
      - RULE-014-4   # data.logs required

    input:
      - proof

    output: boolean


  - id: GUARD-014-2
    name: guard_validate_merkle
    description: >
      Ensures the recomputed Merkle root matches the provided root,
      guaranteeing data integrity.

    implementation: afritech.guards.guard_cross_proof.guard_validate_merkle

    linked_rules:
      - RULE-014-6

    input:
      - proof
      - computed_root

    output: boolean


  - id: GUARD-014-3
    name: guard_validate_signature
    description: >
      Validates the result of cryptographic signature verification.

    implementation: afritech.guards.guard_cross_proof.guard_validate_signature

    linked_rules:
      - RULE-014-7

    input:
      - signature_valid

    output: boolean


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ✅ EXECUTION PIPELINE
# ---------------------------------------------------------

execution_order:
  - GUARD-014-1   # structure first
  - GUARD-014-2   # then integrity
  - GUARD-014-3   # then trust/authenticity


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ✅ FAILURE POLICY
# ---------------------------------------------------------

failure_policy:
  mode: fail_fast

  rules:
    - on_failure: STOP_EXECUTION
    - reject_proof: true
    - log_failure: true
    - emit_alert: true


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ✅ TRACEABILITY
# ---------------------------------------------------------

linked_adr:
  - ADR-0014

linked_invariants:
  - INVARIANT-014
  - INVARIANT-014B
  - INVARIANT-014C

linked_bindings:
  - BIND-014


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# ✅ CI ENFORCEMENT
# ---------------------------------------------------------

ci:
  validations:
    - each guard must have implementation path
    - each guard must return boolean
    - execution_order must include all guards
    - no duplicate guard entries

  tests:
    - test_guard_structure_validation
    - test_guard_merkle_validation
    - test_guard_signature_validation
    - test_guard_execution_order  

===== ./afritech/inference/authority_profiles.yaml =====
# afritech/inference/authority_profiles.yaml
#
# STATUS: EXECUTION-AUTHORITY CANON
#
# This file defines the ONLY authority profiles permitted
# to participate in runtime execution during the
# FIRST_EXECUTABLE_PROOF phase.
#
# Authority profiles define:
# - what an actor is allowed to cause
# - what an actor is explicitly forbidden to cause
# - how legitimacy is evaluated before intelligence acts
#
# Authority is NEVER inferred.
# Authority is NEVER escalated at runtime.
#
# Any execution referencing an undeclared authority profile
# is constitutionally invalid.

version: AUTHORITY_PROFILES_V1

applicability:
  phase: FIRST_EXECUTABLE_PROOF
  enforced_by:
    - ConstitutionalAgent
    - ReplayVerifier

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# GLOBAL AUTHORITY INVARIANTS
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

global_invariants:

  - id: NO_RUNTIME_AUTHORITY_CREATION
    description: >
      Authority profiles may not be created, derived,
      delegated, or modified at runtime.

  - id: NO_AUTHORITY_ESCALATION
    description: >
      An execution may never assume an authority
      broader than the one explicitly bound to it.

  - id: AUTHORITY_PRECEDES_INTELLIGENCE
    description: >
      Authority scope MUST be evaluated before any
      inference, routing, or reasoning takes place.

  - id: AUTHORITY_VIOLATION_IS_TERMINAL
    description: >
      Any authority violation MUST immediately halt
      execution and invalidate proof.

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# AUTHORITY PROFILES
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

authority_profiles:

  # ===============================================================
  # CONSTITUTIONAL RESEARCH AGENT AUTHORITY
  # ===============================================================

  - id: CONSTITUTIONAL_RESEARCH_AGENT
    description: >
      Minimal authority profile used exclusively to
      demonstrate constitutional execution viability.
      This authority exists ONLY for proof purposes.

    domain:
      - knowledge_generation
      - constitutional_validation

    permitted_operations:
      - research_synthesis
      - citation_analysis
      - non_mutating_inference
      - truth_artifact_emission

    prohibited_operations:
      - state_mutation
      - external_write
      - environment_interaction
      - system_command
      - registry_modification
      - epoch_advancement
      - authority_delegation
      - authority_derivation
      - federation_interaction

    scope_constraints:
      max_claims: 10
      requires_citations: true
      epistemic_confidence_required: true
      causal_trace_required: true

    replay_constraints:
      deterministic_only: true
      witnesses_required: 0
      allows_federation_variance: false

    failure_on_violation: true

  # ===============================================================
  # NULL / UNAUTHORIZED AUTHORITY (IMPLICIT DENIAL)
  # ===============================================================

  - id: UNAUTHORIZED
    description: >
      Implicit authority profile representing absence
      of constitutional authorization.

    domain: []

    permitted_operations: []

    prohibited_operations:
      - "*"

    scope_constraints: {}

    replay_constraints:
      deterministic_only: true

    failure_on_violation: true

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# AUTHORITY SELECTION RULES
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

authority_resolution:

  binding_required: true

  resolution_rules:
    - rule: REQUEST_MUST_DECLARE_AUTHORITY
      description: >
        Every constitutional request MUST explicitly
        declare an authority_profile ID.

    - rule: IMPLICIT_AUTHORITY_FORBIDDEN
      description: >
        Absence of an explicit authority declaration
        resolves to UNAUTHORIZED.

    - rule: MULTIPLE_AUTHORITIES_FORBIDDEN
      description: >
        A request MUST reference exactly one authority
        profile. Composed or combined authorities
        are forbidden at this phase.

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# EXECUTION ENFORCEMENT
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

enforcement:

  evaluation_point: PRE_INFERENCE

  violation_response:
    type: HARD_FAILURE
    effect: >
      Authority violations terminate execution and
      invalidate the constitutional proof.

  audit_requirements:
    - authority_profile_id MUST appear in replay transcript
    - authority_scope evaluation MUST be replayable
    - authority violation MUST be explicit in failure taxonomy

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

constitutional_assertion: >
  AfriTech recognizes no execution authority that is
  not explicitly declared in this file.
  Authority is finite, enumerable, and replay-verifiable.

===== ./afritech/inference/constitutional_request.yaml =====
# afritech/inference/constitutional_request.yaml
#
# STATUS: EXECUTION-BOUND CANONICAL SCHEMA
#
# This schema defines the ONLY constitutionally valid request
# envelope permitted to enter execution during the
# FIRST_EXECUTABLE_PROOF phase.
#
# Purpose:
# - Enforce authority-before-intelligence
# - Guarantee deterministic routing and replay
# - Prevent implicit defaults and hidden scope expansion
#
# Any execution that processes a request not conforming
# exactly to this schema is constitutionally invalid.

version: CONSTITUTIONAL_REQUEST_V1

applicability:
  phase: FIRST_EXECUTABLE_PROOF
  enforced_by:
    - ConstitutionalAgent
    - InferenceRouter
    - ConstitutionalDispatch
    - ReplayVerifier

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# GLOBAL REQUEST INVARIANTS
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

global_invariants:

  - id: EXPLICIT_AUTHORITY_REQUIRED
    description: >
      Every request MUST explicitly declare exactly one
      authority_profile. Implicit authority is forbidden.

  - id: AUTHORITY_PRECEDES_INFERENCE
    description: >
      Authority scope MUST be validated before any routing,
      reasoning, or inference occurs.

  - id: DETERMINISM_REQUIRED
    description: >
      Requests MUST declare deterministic replay requirements.
      Non-deterministic execution is forbidden at this phase.

  - id: NO_IMPLICIT_DEFAULTS
    description: >
      Absence of any required field renders the request invalid.
      No field may be inferred or defaulted at runtime.

  - id: SINGLE_REQUEST_SINGLE_AUTHORITY
    description: >
      A request may bind to exactly one authority profile.
      Composite or layered authorities are forbidden.

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# CANONICAL REQUEST SCHEMA
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

constitutional_request:

  # ===============================================================
  # IDENTIFIERS
  # ===============================================================

  request_id:
    type: uuid
    required: true
    description: >
      Globally unique identifier for the request.
      Used for request hashing and replay reconstruction.

  epoch_id:
    type: string
    required: true
    description: >
      Constitutional epoch identifier under which the
      request is evaluated. Used strictly as a replay anchor.

  # ===============================================================
  # AUTHORITY BINDING
  # ===============================================================

  authority_profile:
    type: string
    required: true
    allowed_values:
      - CONSTITUTIONAL_RESEARCH_AGENT
    description: >
      Explicit authority profile governing this request.
      Must match exactly one declared authority profile.

  # ===============================================================
  # RISK AND DOMAIN CLASSIFICATION
  # ===============================================================

  domain:
    type: string
    required: true
    allowed_values:
      - research
    description: >
      Execution domain classification.
      Used for deterministic routing constraints.

  risk_class:
    type: string
    required: true
    allowed_values:
      - low
    description: >
      Risk classification of the requested operation.
      Higher risk classes are explicitly forbidden
      at this proof phase.

  # ===============================================================
  # OPERATIONAL SCOPE
  # ===============================================================

  permissible_operations:
    type: list
    required: true
    allowed_values:
      - research_synthesis
      - citation_analysis
      - non_mutating_inference
    description: >
      Exhaustive list of operations this request permits.
      Any operation not listed here is forbidden.

  prohibited_operations:
    type: list
    required: true
    allowed_values:
      - state_mutation
      - external_action
      - system_command
      - environment_interaction
      - registry_modification
      - epoch_advancement
      - authority_delegation
    description: >
      Explicitly forbidden operations.
      Prohibited operations take precedence over
      permissible operations.

  # ===============================================================
  # ATTESTATION REQUIREMENTS
  # ===============================================================

  required_attestations:
    type: list
    required: true
    description: >
      List of attestation identifiers required for execution.
      Must be empty for FIRST_EXECUTABLE_PROOF.

  # ===============================================================
  # REPLAY CONSTRAINTS
  # ===============================================================

  replay_requirements:
    type: object
    required: true

    fields:
      deterministic:
        type: boolean
        required: true
        value: true
        description: >
          Execution MUST be deterministic.
          Any non-deterministic behavior invalidates proof.

      witnesses_required:
        type: integer
        required: true
        value: 0
        description: >
          Number of external witnesses required.
          Must be zero at this phase.

  # ===============================================================
  # DETERMINISM SCOPE DECLARATION
  # ===============================================================

  determinism_scope:
    type: object
    required: true

    fields:
      assumes_single_runtime:
        type: boolean
        required: true
        value: true
        description: >
          Replay assumes a single runtime environment
          with identical software and configuration.

      excludes_federation_variance:
        type: boolean
        required: true
        value: true
        description: >
          Request explicitly excludes federated execution
          and any distributed variance.

  # ===============================================================
  # OUTPUT CONSTRAINTS
  # ===============================================================

  output_constraints:
    type: object
    required: true

    fields:
      citation_required:
        type: boolean
        required: true
        value: true
        description: >
          All claims MUST be grounded with citations
          in provenance_chain.

      max_claims:
        type: integer
        required: true
        max_value: 10
        description: >
          Upper bound on number of claims permitted
          in the TruthPacket.

      epistemic_confidence_required:
        type: boolean
        required: true
        value: true
        description: >
          TruthPacket MUST include full epistemic
          confidence vector.

      causal_trace_required:
        type: boolean
        required: true
        value: true
        description: >
          TruthPacket MUST include causal_trace
          enabling replayable causal audit.

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# VALIDATION RULES
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

validation:

  strict: true

  failure_handling:
    mode: HARD_FAILURE
    timing: PRE_INFERENCE
    description: >
      Any schema violation MUST immediately halt execution
      before routing or inference begins.

  audit_requirements:
    - request_hash MUST appear in replay transcript
    - authority_profile MUST be replay-validated
    - determinism_scope MUST be asserted in transcript

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

constitutional_assertion: >
  AfriTech recognizes no execution request that is not
  explicitly and exhaustively declared in this schema.
  What is not written is constitutionally forbidden.

  permissible_operations:

    - citation_analysis
    - non_mutating_inference

    - state_mutation
    - external_action
    - system_command
    - environment_interaction
    - registry_modification
    - epoch_advancement
    - authority_delegation

  required_attestations: []

  replay_requirements:
    deterministic: true
    witnesses_required: 0

  determinism_scope:
    assumes_single_runtime: true

    causal_trace_required: true

===== ./afritech/proof/witness_policy.yaml =====
schema: afritech.proof.witness_policy.v2

version: 2

metadata:

  authority: PROOF
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED

  registry_class: CONSTITUTIONAL_WITNESS_POLICY

  description: >
    Canonical constitutional witness policy governing
    replay admissibility, witness generation, witness
    validation, deterministic proof semantics, execution
    trace attestation, mutation traceability, transcript
    reconstruction, and closed-world witness enforcement
    within AfriTech.

    This policy defines:
      - witness legitimacy
      - witness admissibility
      - replay-safe witness semantics
      - deterministic witness construction
      - witness bundle admissibility
      - proof-bound execution semantics
      - witness lineage continuity
      - transcript reconstruction legality

    Witnesses are constitutional proof artifacts only.

    Witness generation does NOT independently confer:
      - runtime sovereignty
      - governance authority
      - execution legitimacy
      - constitutional authority

    Witness participation remains bounded by:
      - replay admissibility
      - invariant preservation
      - deterministic execution
      - canonical ontology resolution
      - closed-world execution semantics


# ============================================================
# PATH ONTOLOGY ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

path_alignment:

  canonical_identity_form: module_path

  forbidden_identity_forms:

    - filesystem_path
    - extension_identity
    - conceptual_identity
    - symbolic_short_name
    - relative_identity

  description: >
    Witness identity resolution requires canonical
    module-path ontology semantics.


# ============================================================
# WITNESS TYPES
# ============================================================

witness_types:

  REPLAY:

    canonical_surface:
      afritech.proof.witness.replay_witness

    replay_significance: REQUIRED

    deterministic_required: true

    replay_safe_required: true

    admissible_required: true

    witness_hash_required: true

    description: >
      Canonical replay admissibility witness.


  MUTATION:

    canonical_surface:
      afritech.proof.witness.mutation_witness

    replay_significance: REQUIRED

    deterministic_required: true

    replay_safe_required: true

    admissible_required: true

    invariant_preserving_required: true

    description: >
      Canonical deterministic mutation trace witness.


  TRANSCRIPT:

    canonical_surface:
      afritech.proof.witness.transcript_witness

    replay_significance: REQUIRED

    deterministic_required: true

    replay_safe_required: true

    admissible_required: true

    transcript_reconstructible: true

    description: >
      Canonical replay transcript reconstruction witness.


  EXECUTION:

    canonical_surface:
      afritech.proof.witness.execution_witness

    replay_significance: REQUIRED

    deterministic_required: true

    replay_safe_required: true

    admissible_required: true

    closed_world_required: true

    description: >
      Canonical deterministic execution witness.


  BUNDLE:

    canonical_surface:
      afritech.proof.witness.witness_bundle

    replay_significance: REQUIRED

    deterministic_required: true

    replay_safe_required: true

    admissible_required: true

    consistency_validation_required: true

    description: >
      Canonical aggregated witness bundle.


# ============================================================
# REQUIRED WITNESS HASHES
# ============================================================

required_hashes:

  replay:

    - replay_hash
    - receipt_hash
    - registry_hash
    - invariant_hash


  mutation:

    - mutation_hash
    - mutation_trace_hash
    - execution_trace_hash


  transcript:

    - transcript_hash
    - execution_trace_hash
    - mutation_trace_hash


  execution:

    - execution_hash
    - execution_trace_hash
    - transcript_hash


  bundle:

    - bundle_hash
    - replay_hash
    - transcript_hash
    - mutation_trace_hash
    - execution_trace_hash


# ============================================================
# WITNESS ADMISSIBILITY
# ============================================================

admissibility:

  required:

    - deterministic_execution
    - replay_safe_execution
    - invariant_preservation
    - canonical_identity_resolution
    - closed_world_alignment
    - observer_independence

  forbidden:

    - probabilistic_execution
    - observer_relative_execution
    - reflection_based_execution
    - undeclared_execution_surfaces
    - ambiguous_identity_resolution
    - speculative_execution

  admissibility_failures:

    probabilistic_execution:

      action: REPLAY_INVALID


    invariant_violation:

      action: PROOF_REJECTION


    ontology_violation:

      action: IDENTITY_REJECTION


    replay_divergence:

      action: REPLAY_REJECTION


# ============================================================
# REPLAY ADMISSIBILITY
# ============================================================

replay_admissibility:

  required: true

  replay_requirements:

    - deterministic_reconstruction
    - replay_traceability
    - transcript_reconstruction
    - mutation_traceability
    - canonical_identity_resolution

  replay_invalidators:

    - replay_divergence
    - transcript_divergence
    - mutation_ordering_divergence
    - observer_relative_execution
    - probabilistic_transition

  replay_guarantees:

    - deterministic_reconstruction
    - invariant_preservation
    - lineage_continuity
    - replay_safe_execution


# ============================================================
# MUTATION TRACEABILITY
# ============================================================

mutation_traceability:

  required: true

  mutation_requirements:

    - deterministic_mutation_ordering
    - mutation_gateway_authorization
    - mutation_trace_hash_generation
    - replay_safe_mutation_semantics

  authorized_gateways:

    - afritech.kernel.constitutional_gateway

  forbidden:

    - hidden_mutation
    - unordered_mutation
    - probabilistic_mutation
    - observer_relative_mutation

  description: >
    Mutation traceability requires deterministic mutation
    ordering and replay-safe trace generation.


# ============================================================
# TRANSCRIPT RECONSTRUCTION
# ============================================================

transcript_reconstruction:

  required: true

  transcript_requirements:

    - deterministic_event_ordering
    - transcript_hash_generation
    - replay_safe_event_semantics
    - invariant_preserving_events

  forbidden:

    - non_monotonic_event_ordering
    - observer_relative_transcripts
    - probabilistic_event_reconstruction
    - hidden_transcript_events

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_reconstruction
    - replay_safe_transcripts
    - invariant_preserving_lineage


# ============================================================
# EXECUTION WITNESS POLICY
# ============================================================

execution_policy:

  deterministic_execution_required: true

  closed_world_alignment_required: true

  invariant_preservation_required: true

  authorized_execution_surfaces:

    - afritech.runtime.admission
    - afritech.runtime.replay
    - afritech.kernel.constitutional_gateway

  forbidden:

    - undeclared_execution_surfaces
    - reflection_based_execution
    - speculative_runtime_execution
    - probabilistic_execution

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_execution
    - replay_safe_execution
    - observer_independent_execution
    - invariant_preserving_execution


# ============================================================
# WITNESS BUNDLE POLICY
# ============================================================

bundle_policy:

  consistency_validation_required: true

  deterministic_bundle_generation: true

  replay_safe_bundle_generation: true

  closed_world_alignment_required: true

  required_consistency_checks:

    - replay_hash_alignment
    - transcript_hash_alignment
    - mutation_trace_hash_alignment
    - execution_trace_hash_alignment
    - registry_hash_alignment
    - invariant_hash_alignment

  guarantees:

    - replay_safe_bundle_consistency
    - invariant_preserving_aggregation


# ============================================================

# ============================================================

implementation_alignment:

  admissible_states:

    - PARTIAL
    - IMPLEMENTED

  forbidden_states:

    - PLANNED
    - DOCUMENTARY
    - FORBIDDEN_ALIAS

  runtime_safe_states:

    - IMPLEMENTED

  description: >
    Witness participation requires constitutionally
    admissible implementation legitimacy.


# ============================================================
# CLOSED-WORLD ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

closed_world_alignment:

  required:

    - declared_execution_surfaces
    - deterministic_surface_resolution
    - replay_safe_execution
    - canonical_identity_resolution

  forbidden:

    - implicit_execution_surfaces
    - reflection_based_discovery
    - dynamic_runtime_aliasing
    - undeclared_execution_binding

  description: >
    Witness admissibility requires closed-world execution
    semantics.


# ============================================================
# DETERMINISM ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

determinism_alignment:

  deterministic_required: true

  observer_independence_required: true

  replay_safe_required: true

  forbidden:

    - probabilistic_execution
    - observer_relative_reconstruction
    - runtime_dynamic_resolution
    - ambiguous_identity_resolution

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_reconstruction
    - replay_safe_execution
    - invariant_preserving_replay


# ============================================================
# SAFETY GUARANTEES
# ============================================================

safety:

  guarantees:

    - deterministic_witness_generation
    - replay_safe_witness_reconstruction
    - observer_independent_validation
    - invariant_preserving_attestation
    - canonical_identity_alignment

  forbid:

    - implicit_runtime_legitimacy
    - filesystem_authority_inference
    - speculative_execution_authority
    - observer_relative_attestation
    - ambiguous_witness_resolution

  enforcement:

    violation_action: FAIL_FAST

    replay_violation_action: REPLAY_INVALID

    proof_violation_action: PROOF_REJECTION


# ============================================================
# FAILURE EFFECTS
# ============================================================

failure_effects:

  replay_divergence:

    effect:

      - replay_invalid
      - proof_non_admissible
      - transcript_rejection


  transcript_divergence:

    effect:

      - replay_rejection
      - witness_invalid


  mutation_ordering_violation:

    effect:

      - mutation_trace_invalid
      - deterministic_failure


  closed_world_violation:

    effect:

      - execution_refusal
      - proof_rejection


  ontology_violation:

    effect:

      - identity_rejection
      - CI_failure


# ============================================================
# GLOBAL CONSTITUTIONAL RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  - id: WITNESS_POLICY_RULE_1
    rule: >
      Witness legitimacy requires deterministic replay-safe
      execution semantics.

  - id: WITNESS_POLICY_RULE_2
    rule: >
      Witnesses must never independently confer constitutional
      authority.

  - id: WITNESS_POLICY_RULE_3
    rule: >
      Replay admissibility requires:
        - deterministic reconstruction
        - transcript traceability
        - mutation traceability
        - invariant preservation

  - id: WITNESS_POLICY_RULE_4
    rule: >
      Witness identity resolution requires canonical module-path
      ontology alignment.

  - id: WITNESS_POLICY_RULE_5
    rule: >
      Closed-world execution semantics are mandatory for witness
      admissibility.

  - id: WITNESS_POLICY_RULE_6
    rule: >
      Observer-relative witness generation is constitutionally
      forbidden.

  - id: WITNESS_POLICY_RULE_7
    rule: >
      Filesystem artifacts must never independently define
      witness legitimacy.

  - id: WITNESS_POLICY_RULE_8
    rule: >
      Witness validation must remain:
        - deterministic
        - replay-safe
        - observer-independent
        - constitutionally bounded


# ============================================================
# CI ENFORCEMENT ALIGNMENT
# ============================================================

ci_alignment:

  enforced_by:

    - afritech.ci.replay_validation
    - afritech.ci.execution_surface_validation
    - afritech.ci.import_topology_enforcement
    - afritech.ci.witness_validation

  required_checks:

    - canonical_identity_resolution
    - deterministic_witness_validation
    - replay_alignment_validation
    - invariant_alignment_validation
    - closed_world_validation

  failure_mode: CI_FAIL


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion:

  description: >
    Witnesses are deterministic constitutional proof artifacts
    governing replay admissibility, execution traceability,
    mutation traceability, and transcript reconstruction
    within AfriTech.

    Witness legitimacy derives exclusively from:
      - deterministic reconstruction
      - replay-safe execution semantics
      - invariant preservation
      - canonical identity resolution
      - constitutionally declared execution surfaces

    Witness legitimacy must never derive from:
      - filesystem structure
      - runtime activity alone
      - speculative interpretation
      - observer-relative reconstruction
      - semantic inference

    Witness validation must remain:
      - deterministic
      - replay-safe
      - observer-independent
      - constitutionally bounded
      - closed-world aligned

===== ./afritech/registry/authority_profiles.yaml =====
# afritech/registry/authority_profiles.yaml
#
# STATUS: PHASE‑2A EXECUTION LAW
#
# This registry defines all authority profiles permitted
# during Phase‑2A (Parallel Authority).
#
# Authorities are mutually isolated.
# Cross‑authority execution is constitutionally forbidden
# unless explicitly proven via replay.

version: AUTHORITY_PROFILES_V1

authorities:

  CONSTITUTIONAL_RESEARCH_AGENT:
    description: >
      Original Phase‑1 authority.
      Retained unchanged for backward‑compatible replay.

    allowed_domains:
      - research

    max_risk_class: low

    routing_policy: isolated

    replay_scope:
      - self_only

  SECONDARY_RESEARCH_AUTHORITY:
    description: >
      Phase‑2A parallel authority.
      Used to prove authority coexistence without contamination.

    allowed_domains:
      - research

    max_risk_class: low

    routing_policy: isolated

    replay_scope:
      - self_only

# --------------------------------------------------------
# GLOBAL INVARIANTS
# --------------------------------------------------------

global_invariants:

  - id: NO_AUTHORITY_SYNTHESIS
    description: >
      A request may bind to exactly one authority.
      Authority aggregation or composition is forbidden.

  - id: ISOLATED_REPLAY_DOMAINS
    description: >
      Replay artifacts generated under one authority
      MUST be rejected if verified under another authority.

  - id: EXPLICIT_AUTHORITY_BINDING
    description: >
      Authority identity MUST be declared in the request
      and MUST match registry entries exactly.

===== ./afritech/registry/epochs/EPOCH_0007.yaml =====
# afritech/registry/epochs/EPOCH_0007.yaml

schema: afritech.registry.epoch.v1

epoch:
  id: EPOCH_0007
  number: 7
  status: ACTIVE
  type: CONSTITUTIONAL_EVOLUTION

  parent_epoch: EPOCH_0006
  enacted_at: 2026-05-13

  description: >
    Epoch 7 introduces AfriRide v0.2 as an expanded
    Proof-of-Continuity surface. This epoch extends the
    validated failure space of AfriRide from a single
    deterministic scenario (v0.1) to a bounded, explicitly
    declared set of deterministic failure scenarios (v0.2),
    while preserving all prior guarantees and frozen
    artifacts from earlier epochs.

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ AUTHORITY
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  authority:
    basis:
      - ADR-0010-afriride-v0-2
    scope:
      - Proof-of-Continuity expansion
      - Deterministic failure-space validation

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ AFFECTED SURFACES
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  surfaces:

    added:
      - path: ecosystems/afriride/v0_2
        classification: PROOF_SURFACE
        version: v0.2
        status: ACTIVE
        mutable: true

    preserved:
      - path: ecosystems/afriride
        version: v0.1
        status: FROZEN
        mutable: false

      - path: afritech/core/constitutional
        status: FROZEN
        mutable: false

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ DETERMINISM & REPLAY GUARANTEES
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  determinism:
    envelope:
      - ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/determinism_envelope.yaml
    guarantees:
      - identical inputs produce identical traces
      - identical traces produce identical hashes
      - deterministic refusal on exhaustion or timeout
      - no nondeterministic execution paths permitted

  replay:
    required: true
    verifier: afritech.verify.replay
    acceptance_criteria:
      - replay hash equality
      - trace sequence equality
      - absence of undeclared behavior

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ FAILURE TAXONOMY BINDING
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  failure_taxonomy:
    path: ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/failure_taxonomy.yaml
    enforcement:
      - all failures must be explicitly declared
      - all failures must map to declared handling modes
      - undeclared failures invalidate proof surface

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ METRICS (DECLARATIVE ONLY)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  metrics:
    allowed:
      - Decision Determinism Rate (DDR)
      - Continuity Coverage (CC)
      - Deterministic Refusal Rate (DRR)
    forbidden:
      - performance metrics
      - optimization metrics
      - economic or product metrics

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ INVARIANTS (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  invariants:
    - no mutation of AfriRide v0.1 artifacts
    - no modification of constitutional core
    - v0.2 execution must be layered externally
    - determinism envelope violations invalidate epoch claims
    - replay failure invalidates proof surface
    - partial correctness is not permitted

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ CONSEQUENCES
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  consequences:
    positive:
      - expands AfriRide Proof-of-Continuity to multiple scenarios
      - strengthens institutional trust and auditability
      - preserves replay-verifiable historical lineage
    negative:
      - increases governance surface area
      - requires continued audit discipline for v0.2 artifacts

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ FINAL ASSERTION
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  assertion: >
    Epoch 7 lawfully extends AfriRide’s Proof-of-Continuity
    surface to v0.2 without mutating or weakening any
    prior guarantees. All behavior introduced in this epoch
    is deterministic, replay-verifiable, and constitutionally
    bounded.

===== ./afritech/registry/epochs/EPOCH_0008.yaml =====
schema: afritech.governance.epoch.v1

epoch:
  id: EPOCH_0008

  number: 8

  title: Constitutional Observational Doctrine Ratification

  status: RATIFIED

  type: GOVERNANCE_DOCTRINE_EVOLUTION

  parent_epoch: EPOCH-0007

  effective_version: v0.2

  ratification_date: 2026-05-14

  enacted_at: 2026-05-14

  governance_authority:
    - AfriTech Governance
    - AfriRide Constitutional Council

  constitutional_transition:
    from: PROOF_SURFACE_EXPANSION
    to: OBSERVATIONAL_ADMISSIBILITY_GOVERNANCE

  related_artifacts:
    - ADR-0011-afriride-deterministic-retry.yaml
    - ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/failure_taxonomy.yaml
    - ecosystems/afriride/tests/test_retry_idempotence.py
    - ecosystems/afriride/tests/test_observational_replay.py

# =====================================================
# PURPOSE
# =====================================================

  purpose: >

    Establish a constitutional doctrine governing lawful
    observational evolution within AfriRide while preserving:

      - deterministic execution integrity
      - replay authority
      - closed-world runtime guarantees
      - semantic boundary isolation

    This epoch formally ratifies the admissibility model
    for declarative constitutional expansion based exclusively
    on replay-verified deterministic runtime observation.

    The doctrine established in this epoch governs future
    admissibility of constitutional declarations without
    expanding executable runtime semantics.

# =====================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL POSITION
# =====================================================

  constitutional_position: >

    AfriRide records deterministic execution outcomes
    as immutable replay-authoritative facts.

    Governance may declare replay-verified observational
    behavior as constitutional taxonomy only when:

      - execution behavior already exists
      - replay confirms determinism
      - no semantic interpretation is introduced
      - execution semantics remain unchanged

    Observed behavior may be declared.

    Interpreted behavior may not.

# =====================================================
# TRI-SURFACE INTEGRITY MODEL
# =====================================================

  tri_surface_integrity_model:

    runtime_execution:
      property: DETERMINISTIC
      status: SEALED

    replay_verification:
      property: AUTHORITATIVE
      status: BINDING

    governance_declaration:
      property: DESCRIPTIVE
      status: EXTENSIBLE

  tri_surface_constraints:

    - runtime MUST NOT interpret

    - replay MUST NOT infer

    - governance MUST NOT mutate execution

    - governance MUST NOT reinterpret replay evidence

    - no surface may contaminate another

# =====================================================
# LAW OF OBSERVATIONAL EVOLUTION
# =====================================================

  law_of_observational_evolution: >

    Declarative constitutional expansion is lawful only when:

      - the behavior already exists
      - replay confirms determinism
      - no semantic interpretation is introduced
      - execution semantics remain unchanged

    Replay-confirmed deterministic observation is the sole
    admissible basis for non-semantic constitutional expansion.

# =====================================================
# DIRECTIONAL ASYMMETRY
# =====================================================

  directional_asymmetry:

    lawful_direction:

      - runtime
      - observation
      - replay_verification
      - constitutional_declaration

    prohibited_direction:

      - governance_interpretation
      - semantic_inference
      - runtime_expansion

  directional_constraints:

    - governance MUST remain descriptive

    - replay MUST remain authoritative

    - runtime MUST remain non-interpreting

    - semantic inference MUST NOT mutate execution

    - replay evidence MUST supersede governance interpretation

# =====================================================
# EPISTEMIC MODEL
# =====================================================

  epistemic_model:

    admissible_fact_classification:

      epistemic_status: OBSERVED_FACT

      semantic_commitment: NONE

      interpretation_required: false

      replay_required: true

      determinism_required: true

      mutation_permitted: false

    epistemic_constraints:

      - replay-observed facts are immutable

      - semantic interpretation is non-authoritative

      - replay evidence supersedes governance interpretation

      - future semantic overlays MUST NOT reinterpret
        historical execution facts

      - runtime outcomes MUST remain historically stable

      - replay-confirmed outcomes constitute constitutional evidence

# =====================================================
# FAILURE ONTOLOGY
# =====================================================

  failure_ontology:

    lawful_failure_classes:

      - OBSERVATIONAL_REFUSAL
      - DETERMINISTIC_REFUSAL
      - REPLAY_STABLE_REFUSAL

    prohibited_failure_collapses:

      - semantic_refusal
      - inferred_refusal
      - normalized_refusal
      - policy_refusal
      - equivalence_refusal

  ontology_constraints:

    - observational refusal MUST remain non-semantic

    - deterministic refusal MUST remain replay-bound

    - runtime outcomes MUST NOT acquire retroactive meaning

    - replay evidence MUST NOT be semantically elevated
      without constitutional ratification

# =====================================================
# REPLAY AUTHORITY MODEL
# =====================================================

  replay_authority_model:

    replay_status: AUTHORITATIVE

    replay_constraints:

      - replay hashes MUST remain stable

      - replay traces MUST remain deterministic

      - replay admissibility MUST remain invariant-preserving

      - replay evidence MUST supersede interpretive overlays

      - replay-confirmed outcomes are constitutionally binding

      - replay divergence invalidates admissibility claims

# =====================================================
# CLOSED-WORLD ENFORCEMENT
# =====================================================

  closed_world_enforcement:

    runtime_constraints:

      undeclared_execution_surfaces: FORBIDDEN

      semantic_runtime_extension: FORBIDDEN

      dynamic_equivalence_resolution: FORBIDDEN

      canonicalization_layers: FORBIDDEN

      runtime_inference_expansion: FORBIDDEN

      semantic_normalization: FORBIDDEN

    governance_constraints:

      governance MUST NOT introduce executable semantics

      governance MUST NOT reinterpret replay evidence

      governance MUST NOT inject semantic normalization

      governance MUST NOT expand runtime meaning

# =====================================================
# SEMANTIC BOUNDARY PROTECTION
# =====================================================

  semantic_boundary_protection:

    prohibited_operations:

      - intent_equivalence_inference

      - retry_identity_classification

      - semantic_deduplication

      - canonical_request_normalization

      - similarity_interpretation

      - behavioral_generalization

      - semantic_retry_grouping

      - equivalence_class_construction

    semantic_boundary_assertion: >

      AfriRide does not classify semantic identity,
      construct equivalence, or normalize intent meaning
      unless explicitly authorized by future constitutional
      elevation and epoch transition.

# =====================================================
# OBSERVATIONAL DOCTRINE
# =====================================================

  observational_doctrine: >

    AfriRide declares only replay-verified deterministic facts.

    It does not:

      - infer semantic identity
      - construct equivalence
      - normalize intent
      - interpret similarity

    Runtime execution produces observable outcomes.

    Replay verifies deterministic continuity.

    Governance may declare replay-confirmed observations,
    but may not reinterpret execution meaning.

    Observed behavior may be declared.

    Interpreted behavior may not.

    Observe → Prove → Replay → Declare.

# =====================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ADMISSIBILITY FILTER
# =====================================================

  constitutional_admissibility_filter:

    lawful_admission_requirements:

      replay_verified: true

      deterministic: true

      execution_preexisting: true

      semantic_free: true

      runtime_unchanged: true

      replay_stable: true

      non_interpretive: true

    automatic_rejection_conditions:

      - semantic_interpretation_required

      - execution_behavior_mutation

      - replay_divergence

      - equivalence_activation

      - normalization_dependency

      - runtime_surface_expansion

      - governance_driven_runtime_inference

      - semantic_reclassification_of_history

# =====================================================
# FUTURE EPOCH CONSTRAINTS
# =====================================================

  future_epoch_constraints:

    - future semantic systems REQUIRE new epoch ratification

    - observational facts MUST remain historically immutable

    - replay evidence MUST remain authoritative across epochs

    - governance reinterpretation of historical replay is forbidden

    - semantic promotion REQUIRES explicit constitutional elevation

    - deterministic replay continuity MUST remain uninterrupted

    - semantic evolution MUST remain isolated from observational doctrine

# =====================================================
# GOVERNANCE IMPLICATIONS
# =====================================================

  governance_implications:

    lawful_governance_actions:

      - declare replay-confirmed observations

      - expand non-semantic taxonomy

      - strengthen replay auditability

      - formalize deterministic runtime evidence

      - preserve replay-authoritative constitutional lineage

    prohibited_governance_actions:

      - reinterpret execution semantics

      - inject runtime meaning

      - normalize intent identity

      - collapse equivalence classes

      - mutate replay authority

      - infer semantic continuity

# =====================================================
# RUNTIME PRESERVATION GUARANTEE
# =====================================================

  runtime_preservation_guarantee: >

    This epoch introduces no runtime mutation.

    No executor semantics are modified.

    No orchestration semantics are modified.

    No replay behavior is modified.

    No deterministic guarantees are weakened.

    No execution surfaces are expanded.

    The constitutional surface expands descriptively
    without expanding executable behavior.

# =====================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL CONSEQUENCES
# =====================================================

  constitutional_consequences:

    positive:

      - establishes replay-authoritative governance doctrine

      - formalizes lawful observational evolution

      - prevents semantic contamination of replay evidence

      - strengthens deterministic constitutional continuity

      - creates admissibility jurisprudence for future ADRs

    negative:

      - increases governance doctrine surface area

      - requires strict future semantic isolation discipline

      - introduces higher constitutional review burden
        for future semantic epochs

# =====================================================
# CANONICAL DOCTRINE STATEMENT
# =====================================================

  canonical_doctrine_statement: >

    AfriRide records deterministic execution outcomes
    as immutable facts and prohibits semantic interpretation
    without explicit constitutional elevation.

# =====================================================
# FINAL ASSERTION
# =====================================================

  final_assertion: >

    Epoch 8 formally establishes lawful observational evolution
    as the sole admissible path for declarative constitutional
    expansion within AfriRide’s deterministic governance model.

    Replay remains authoritative.

    Runtime remains sealed.

    Governance remains descriptive.

    Semantic interpretation remains constitutionally isolated.

    No execution semantics are expanded.

    No replay authority is weakened.

    No equivalence-space is activated.

    Historical execution truth remains immutable.

    Observe → Prove → Replay → Declare.

===== ./afritech/registry/history/correction_2026_05_reseal.yaml =====
schema: afritech.registry.history.correction.v1

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# EPOCH BINDING (MANDATORY)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
epoch_id: 6

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# CORRECTION IDENTITY
# ------------------------------------------------------------
correction:
  id: correction_2026_05_reseal
  type: CONSTITUTIONAL_BOOKKEEPING
  status: FINAL
  immutable: true

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# TIMESTAMPS
# ------------------------------------------------------------
timestamp:
  detected_at: "2026-05-03T04:42:00Z"
  corrected_at: "2026-05-03T05:18:00Z"

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# SCOPE OF CORRECTION
# ------------------------------------------------------------
scope:
  affects_epochs:
    - 1
    - 2
    - 3
  registry_identity: true
  runtime_execution: false
  governance_rules: false

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# DESCRIPTION
# ------------------------------------------------------------
description: >
  A constitutional bookkeeping inconsistency was identified where
  epochs 1–3 were marked FINAL without explicit reseal metadata,
  despite governance rules requiring reseal_after_advance.
  This correction records the remediation that restored consistency
  between declared epoch finality, registry sealing, and cryptographic
  identity attestation.

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# ACTIONS TAKEN
# ------------------------------------------------------------
actions_taken:
  - >
    Added explicit reseal metadata to epoch history records
    (resealed: true, seal_status: SEALED)
  - >
    Recomputed the registry attestation hash from the canonical
    pre-seal registry snapshot
  - >
    Reapplied registry seal using sovereignty tooling

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# ACTIONS NOT TAKEN
# ------------------------------------------------------------
non_actions:
  - No epoch counters were advanced
  - No law or invariant definitions were modified
  - No runtime, guard, or kernel behavior changed
  - No authority boundaries were altered

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSESSMENT
# ------------------------------------------------------------
constitutional_assessment:
  law_changed: false
  epoch_advanced: false
  runtime_affected: false
  authority_modified: false
  replay_semantics_changed: false

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# VERIFICATION
# ------------------------------------------------------------
verification:
  reseal_method: afritech.registry.sovereignty.seal_registry
  registry_seal_status: SEALED
  runtime_boot_verified: true
  ci_validation_passed: true

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# REFERENCES
# ------------------------------------------------------------
references:
  adr:
    - afritech/governance/adr/ADR-0005.yaml
  modified_files:
    - afritech/registry/history/epoch_1.yaml
    - afritech/registry/history/epoch_2.yaml
    - afritech/registry/history/epoch_3.yaml
    - afritech/registry/registry.yaml
  tooling:
    - afritech/registry/sovereignty.py

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTIONS
# ------------------------------------------------------------
constitutional_assertions:
  - This correction is non-temporal and does not constitute an epoch
  - Identity-domain corrections must not advance epoch counters
  - Registry reseal is required to restore cryptographic truthfulness
  - Replay validity requires sealed history continuity

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# AUDIT NOTES
# ------------------------------------------------------------
audit_notes: >
  This record exists to preserve long-term traceability of
  identity corrections that are orthogonal to temporal evolution.

  Future audits must treat this correction as a baseline identity
  normalization event, not a governance action.
