===== ./afritech/registry/surface_aliases.yaml =====
schema: afritech.registry.surface_aliases.v1

version: 1

metadata:

  authority: REGISTRY
  status: ACTIVE
  mutability: CONTROLLED

  description: >
    Surface alias registry providing deterministic
    filesystem-to-surface resolution mappings.

    Aliases represent FULL filesystem paths and resolve
    deterministically to canonical registry artifacts.

    This registry is strictly a compatibility layer and
    does not define execution or constitutional authority.


# ============================================================
# ALIASES
# ============================================================

aliases:

  afritech/registry/attestation.py:

    resolved_to:
      - afritech/registry/attestation.yaml

    status: MIGRATING

    ontology: FILESYSTEM_TO_FILESYSTEM_COMPATIBILITY

    description: >
      Deprecated Python surface redirected to canonical YAML
      attestation registry.


# ============================================================
# RESOLUTION RULES
# ============================================================

resolution:

  ordering: DETERMINISTIC

  single_target_required: true

  observer_independent: true

  replay_safe: true

  constraints:

    - no_multiple_primary_targets
    - deterministic_lookup_only
    - no_runtime_resolution
    - no_reflection


# ============================================================
# VALIDATION RULES
# ============================================================

validation:

  required:

    - alias_must_be_filesystem_path
    - resolved_targets_must_exist
    - deterministic_resolution_required

  forbidden:

    - ambiguous_resolution
    - multi-branch_resolution
    - runtime_dynamic_aliasing
    - alias_chains

  failure_mode: FAIL_FAST


# ============================================================
# CLOSED-WORLD SEMANTICS
# ============================================================

closed_world:

  enforced: true

  description: >
    Only declared aliases are valid. No implicit or
    dynamically discovered surfaces are allowed.


# ============================================================
# CONSTITUTIONAL ASSERTION
# ============================================================

constitutional_assertion: >

  Surface aliases are deterministic compatibility mappings only.

  Canonical surface authority derives exclusively from:
    - registered canonical artifacts
    - attested registry state
    - deterministic replay-safe resolution

  Filesystem aliases must never:
    - define execution authority
    - override canonical registry identity
    - introduce ambiguity
    - depend on runtime resolution


===== ./afritech/replay/ci/ADMISSION_REQUIRED.yaml =====
## ============================================================
## AfriTech CI Rule
## Runtime Admission Enforcement
## ============================================================
## Rule ID: CI-RUNTIME-ADMISSION-001
## Purpose:
## Ensure that no runtime execution can occur without
## explicit constitutional admission via RuntimeCertificate.
##
## Scope:
## - afritech/runtime/main.py
## - afritech/runtime/admission/
##
## This rule is normative and enforced in CI.
## ============================================================

metadata:
  rule_id: CI-RUNTIME-ADMISSION-001
  rule_version: "1.0.0"
  applies_to:
    - Phase-1
    - Phase-2A
    - Phase-2B
  last_updated: "2026-05-08"

## ============================================================
## GOVERNANCE PRINCIPLE
## ============================================================

principle:
  statement: >
    No AfriTech runtime may exist or execute without
    successful constitutional admission via a valid
    RuntimeCertificate.

  rationale: >
    Runtime existence is not implicit. It must be explicitly
    granted through cryptographically verifiable admission.
    This prevents unauthorized execution, configuration drift,
    and non-attested runtime behavior.

  enforcement_mode: static_analysis_and_ci_execution
  failure_action: ci_fail

## ============================================================
## REQUIRED RUNTIME BEHAVIOR
## ============================================================

required_behavior:

  description: >
    The runtime entrypoint MUST invoke the
    RuntimeAdmissionEngine before any execution path is reachable.

  admission_call:

    required_file: afritech/runtime/main.py

    required_import:
      - 'from afritech\\.runtime\\.admission\\.admission_engine import RuntimeAdmissionEngine'

    required_invocation:
      - 'RuntimeAdmissionEngine\\('
      - 'admit\\('

    required_flow:
      description: >
        Admission MUST occur before any guarded or unguarded
        execution is invoked.

      ordering:
        first:
          - _admit_runtime
        before:
          - any_execution_call

## ============================================================
## PROHIBITED BEHAVIOR
## ============================================================

prohibited_behavior:

  description: >
    Any execution path that bypasses runtime admission
    is constitutionally invalid.

  forbidden_patterns:

    - direct_execution_without_admission
    - guard_execution_without_admission
    - runtime_boot_without_certificate_check

## ============================================================
## TARGETED FILES
## ============================================================

targets:

  runtime_entrypoint:
    path: afritech/runtime/main.py

  admission_engine:
    path: afritech/runtime/admission/admission_engine.py

## ============================================================
## CI VALIDATION CHECKS
## ============================================================

ci_checks:

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # AE-R1: Ensure admission engine import exists
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: AE-R1

    description: >
      Verify that runtime entrypoint imports RuntimeAdmissionEngine.

    target: afritech/runtime/main.py

    pattern_required:
      - 'from afritech\\.runtime\\.admission\\.admission_engine import RuntimeAdmissionEngine'

    failure_message: >
      Runtime entrypoint does not import RuntimeAdmissionEngine.

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # AE-R2: Ensure admission invocation exists
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: AE-R2

    description: >
      Verify that RuntimeAdmissionEngine is instantiated
      and admit() is invoked.

    target: afritech/runtime/main.py

    pattern_required:
      - 'RuntimeAdmissionEngine\\('
      - '\\.admit\\('

    failure_message: >
      Runtime entrypoint does not invoke admission engine.

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # AE-R3: Ensure admission precedes execution
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: AE-R3

    description: >
      Verify that admission occurs before any execution call.

    target: afritech/runtime/main.py

    required_order:
      first: _admit_runtime
      before:
        - run_guarded
        - dispatch
        - executor

    failure_message: >
      Runtime admission does not precede execution logic.

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # AE-R4: Ensure no direct execution without admission
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: AE-R4

    description: >
      Prevent direct execution bypassing admission.

    target: afritech/runtime/main.py

    pattern_forbidden:
      - 'run_guarded\\(.*\\)$'
      - 'dispatch\\('

    enforce_context: absence_of_admission_call

    failure_message: >
      Direct execution detected without prior admission.

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # AE-R5: Certificate path enforcement
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: AE-R5

    description: >
      Ensure runtime references a RuntimeCertificate path.

    target: afritech/runtime/main.py

    pattern_required:
      - 'certificates/runtime_epoch_'

    failure_message: >
      Runtime does not reference a valid certificate path.

## ============================================================
## RUNTIME GUARANTEE (ASSERTION)
## ============================================================

runtime_assertion:

  statement: >
    Any runtime execution without prior admission MUST be treated
    as non-existent and constitutionally invalid.

  enforcement_effect:
    - execution_refused
    - runtime_invalidated
    - replay_rejected

## ============================================================
## CI OUTCOME
## ============================================================

ci_outcome:

  on_pass:
    result: success
    message: "Runtime admission enforcement passed."

  on_fail:
    result: failure
    message: >
      Runtime violates constitutional admission requirements.
      See CI logs for specific violations.


===== ./afritech/replay/ci/ATTESTATION_BINDING_ENFORCEMENT.yaml =====
## ============================================================
## AfriTech CI Rule
## Attestation ↔ Certificate Binding Enforcement
## ============================================================
## Rule ID: CI-ATTEST-BIND-001
## Purpose:
## Ensure that RuntimeCertificates are strictly bound to
## the current attested constitutional state.
##
## This rule guarantees:
## - No drift between attestation and runtime
## - No stale certificate usage
## - No inconsistent system identity
##
## Scope:
## - afritech/registry/attestation.yaml
## - afritech/proof/certificates/
##
## This rule is normative and enforced in CI.
## ============================================================

metadata:
  rule_id: CI-ATTEST-BIND-001
  rule_version: "1.0.0"
  applies_to:
    - Phase-1
    - Phase-2A
    - Phase-2B
  last_updated: "2026-05-08"

## ============================================================
## GOVERNANCE PRINCIPLE
## ============================================================

principle:

  statement: >
    A RuntimeCertificate is valid ONLY if it is bound
    to the currently attested constitutional state.

  rationale: >
    Attestation defines the authoritative identity of the system.
    RuntimeCertificates derive their legitimacy from attestation.
    Any mismatch invalidates runtime legitimacy entirely.

  enforcement_mode: ci_execution
  failure_action: ci_fail


## ============================================================
## REQUIRED BINDING CONDITIONS
## ============================================================

binding_requirements:

  description: >
    The RuntimeCertificate MUST match the attestation
    across all constitutionally relevant hashes.

  required_alignments:

    - registry_hash
    - epoch_hash
    - kernel_root_hash
    - execution_surfaces_hash
    - authority_profiles_hash
    - replay_verifier_hash

  epoch_alignment_required: true
  attestation_status_required: SEALED


## ============================================================
## TARGETED FILES
## ============================================================

targets:

  attestation:
    path: afritech/registry/attestation.yaml

  certificates:
    path: afritech/proof/certificates/
    pattern: "*.cert"


## ============================================================
## CI VALIDATION CHECKS
## ============================================================

ci_checks:

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # AB-F1: Attestation must exist
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: AB-F1

    description: >
      Verify that attestation.yaml exists.

    target: afritech/registry/attestation.yaml

    must_exist: true

    failure_message: >
      Missing attestation.yaml — constitutional state undefined.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # AB-F2: Attestation must be SEALED
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: AB-F2

    description: >
      Verify attestation is sealed.

    target: afritech/registry/attestation.yaml

    required_field:
      path: attestation.status
      value: SEALED

    failure_message: >
      Attestation is not SEALED — certificate invalid.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # AB-F3: Certificate ↔ Attestation hash match
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: AB-F3

    description: >
      Verify all certificate bindings match attestation bindings.

    enforcement: semantic_validation

    validation_method:
      type: python_hook
      function: validate_attestation_certificate_binding

    failure_message: >
      RuntimeCertificate does not match attestation hashes.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # AB-F4: Epoch consistency
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: AB-F4

    description: >
      Verify certificate epoch matches attestation epoch.

    target_pattern: "afritech/proof/certificates/*.cert"

    compare_fields:

      - attestation.epoch
      - runtime_certificate.epoch

    failure_message: >
      Certificate epoch does not match attested epoch.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # AB-F5: Prevent stale certificate usage
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: AB-F5

    description: >
      Ensure certificate issued_at >= attestation issued_at.

    enforcement: semantic_validation

    validation_method:
      type: python_hook
      function: validate_certificate_freshness

    failure_message: >
      Certificate predates attestation — stale certificate detected.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # AB-F6: Attestation hash binding must exist in certificate
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: AB-F6

    description: >
      Ensure certificate explicitly references attestation.

    target_pattern: "afritech/proof/certificates/*.cert"

    required_fields:
      - runtime_certificate.constitutional_binding.attestation_hash

    failure_message: >
      Certificate missing attestation binding.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # AB-F7: Prevent orphan certificates
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: AB-F7

    description: >
      Ensure no certificate exists without matching attestation.

    enforcement: semantic_validation

    validation_method:
      type: python_hook
      function: detect_orphan_certificates

    failure_message: >
      Orphan RuntimeCertificate detected.


## ============================================================
## VALIDATION FUNCTIONS (REFERENCE)
## ============================================================

validation_contract:

  validate_attestation_certificate_binding:

    description: >
      Compare attestation.bindings with
      certificate.constitutional_binding.

    expected_result: >
      Exact match across all binding fields.


  validate_certificate_freshness:

    description: >
      Ensure:
        certificate.issued_at >= attestation.issued_at

    expected_result: >
      Certificate must not predate attestation.


  detect_orphan_certificates:

    description: >
      Ensure every certificate references an existing
      attestation and matching epoch.

    expected_result: >
      No orphan certificates exist.


## ============================================================
## BINDING INVARIANT
## ============================================================

binding_invariant:

  statement: >
    Runtime existence is derived from attestation.

    Therefore:
      certificate MUST match attestation exactly.

  violation_effect:

    - runtime_invalidated
    - certificate_invalidated
    - execution_refused
    - replay_invalidated
    - trust_chain_broken


## ============================================================
## CI OUTCOME
## ============================================================

ci_outcome:

  on_pass:
    result: success
    message: "Attestation binding validation passed."

  on_fail:
    result: failure
    message: >
      RuntimeCertificate is not correctly bound to
      attested constitutional state. Execution is forbidden.

===== ./afritech/replay/ci/AUDIT_EMISSION_ENFORCEMENT.yaml =====
# ============================================================
# Afritech CI Rule
# Verifier Audit Emission Enforcement
# ============================================================
#
# Rule ID: CI-REPLAY-AUDIT-001
#
# Purpose:
#   Ensure that every replay verifier run emits a JSON audit
#   artifact via the audit side-channel.
#
# Scope:
#   Phase-2B (and later)
#
# This rule is normative and enforced in CI.
# ============================================================

metadata:
  rule_id: CI-REPLAY-AUDIT-001
  rule_version: "1.0.0"
  applies_to:
    - Phase-2B
  last_updated: "2026-05-05"

# ============================================================
# GOVERNANCE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

principle:

  statement: >
    Every invocation of the replay verifier MUST result in the
    emission of a machine-readable audit artifact, regardless
    of replay acceptance or refusal.

  rationale: >
    Audit artifacts provide an immutable, non-evidentiary record
    of verifier behavior for CI, compliance, and archival purposes,
    without contaminating stdout-based runtime evidence.

  enforcement_mode: static_analysis_and_ci_execution
  failure_action: ci_fail

# ============================================================
# REQUIRED AUDIT BEHAVIOR
# ============================================================

required_behavior:

  description: >
    The replay verification workflow MUST call the canonical
    audit export function exactly once per verifier run.

  audit_emission:
    function: export_verifier_audit
    module: afritech.replay.audit.export

  emission_timing:
    description: >
      Audit emission MUST occur after verifier execution and
      MUST reflect the final ReplayVerdict.

# ============================================================
# TARGETED FILES
# ============================================================

targets:

  cli_entrypoint:
    path: afritech/replay/run_verify.py

  audit_export_module:
    path: afritech/replay/audit/export.py

# ============================================================
# CI VALIDATION CHECKS
# ============================================================

ci_checks:

  - check_id: AE-F1
    description: >
      Verify that run_verify.py imports the audit export function.
    target: afritech/replay/run_verify.py
    pattern_required:
      - 'from afritech\\.replay\\.audit\\.export import export_verifier_audit'
    failure_message: >
      Replay verifier CLI does not import audit export function.

  - check_id: AE-F2
    description: >
      Verify that export_verifier_audit is invoked in the
      replay verification execution path.
    target: afritech/replay/run_verify.py
    pattern_required:
      - 'export_verifier_audit\\('
    failure_message: >
      Replay verifier run does not emit an audit artifact.

  - check_id: AE-F3
    description: >
      Verify audit emission is not routed to stdout.
    target: afritech/replay/audit/export.py
    pattern_forbidden:
      - 'print\\('
      - 'sys\\.stdout'
    failure_message: >
      Audit export must not write to stdout.

  - check_id: AE-F4
    description: >
      Verify that audit export writes JSON files only.
    target: afritech/replay/audit/export.py
    pattern_required:
      - 'json.dumps'
    failure_message: >
      Audit export does not produce canonical JSON output.

# ============================================================
# CI OUTCOME
# ============================================================

ci_outcome:

  on_pass:
    result: success
    message: "All replay verifier runs emit audit artifacts."

  on_fail:
    result: failure
    message: >
      Replay verifier run missing mandatory audit emission.
      See CI logs for details on the specific violation.

===== ./afritech/replay/ci/AUDIT_SCHEMA_VALIDATION_ENFORCEMENT.yaml =====
# ============================================================
# Afritech CI Rule
# Audit Schema Validation Enforcement
# ============================================================
#
# Rule ID: CI-REPLAY-AUDIT-SCHEMA-001
#
# Purpose:
#   Ensure that every replay verifier audit artifact conforms
#   exactly to the canonical JSON-Schema definition.
#
# Scope:
#   Phase-2B (and later)
#
# This rule is normative and enforced in CI.
# ============================================================

metadata:
  rule_id: CI-REPLAY-AUDIT-SCHEMA-001
  rule_version: "1.0.0"
  applies_to:
    - Phase-2B
  last_updated: "2026-05-05"

# ============================================================
# GOVERNANCE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

principle:

  statement: >
    All replay verifier audit artifacts MUST conform to the
    canonical JSON-Schema. Non-conforming audit output is
    considered a governance failure.

  rationale: >
    Schema validation ensures structural consistency,
    machine auditability, and prevents silent drift between
    verifier behavior and governance documentation.

  enforcement_mode: ci_execution
  failure_action: ci_fail

# ============================================================
# CANONICAL SCHEMA SOURCE
# ============================================================

canonical_schema:

  path: afritech/replay/audit/schema/REPLAY_VERIFIER_AUDIT.schema.json
  format: json-schema
  versioned: true

# ============================================================
# AUDIT ARTIFACT TARGETS
# ============================================================

audit_artifacts:

  directory:
    description: >
      Directory where replay verifier audit files are written.
    path: afritech/replay/audit/logs
    file_pattern: "replay_audit_*.json"

# ============================================================
# CI VALIDATION CHECKS
# ============================================================

ci_checks:

  - check_id: AS-F1
    description: >
      Verify that at least one audit file is emitted during
      replay verifier CI execution.
    target_directory: afritech/replay/audit/logs
    minimum_files: 1
    failure_message: >
      No replay verifier audit artifacts found.

  - check_id: AS-F2
    description: >
      Validate every audit file against the canonical schema.
    schema: afritech/replay/audit/schema/REPLAY_VERIFIER_AUDIT.schema.json
    targets:
      directory: afritech/replay/audit/logs
      pattern: "replay_audit_*.json"
    validator: json_schema_validator
    failure_message: >
      One or more replay verifier audit artifacts failed schema validation.

  - check_id: AS-F3
    description: >
      Ensure audit artifacts contain no additional properties
      beyond those defined in the schema.
    enforcement: strict
    failure_message: >
      Audit artifact contains undefined or extra fields.

# ============================================================
# CI TOOLING ASSUMPTIONS
# ============================================================

tooling:

  json_schema_validator:
    acceptable_tools:
      - ajv
      - python-jsonschema
      - spectral
    invocation_mode: strict
    allow_additional_properties: false

# ============================================================
# CI OUTCOME
# ============================================================

ci_outcome:

  on_pass:
    result: success
    message: "Replay verifier audit artifacts conform to schema."

  on_fail:
    result: failure
    message: >
      Replay verifier audit schema validation failed.

===== ./afritech/replay/ci/AUTHORITY_BEFORE_INFERENCE_ENFORCEMENT.yaml =====
# ============================================================
# Afritech CI Rule
# Authority Isolation Must Precede Inference Validation
# ============================================================
#
# Rule ID: CI-REPLAY-AUTH-ORDER-001
#
# Purpose:
#   Ensure that authority isolation is enforced BEFORE any
#   inference binding validation occurs in the replay verifier.
#
# Scope:
#   Phase-2B (applies transitively to Phase-2A)
#
# This rule is normative and enforced in CI.
# ============================================================

metadata:
  rule_id: CI-REPLAY-AUTH-ORDER-001
  rule_version: "1.0.0"
  applies_to:
    - Phase-2A
    - Phase-2B
  last_updated: "2026-05-05"

# ============================================================
# GOVERNANCE PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

principle:

  statement: >
    Authority isolation is a foundational replay invariant and
    MUST be enforced before any inference-related validation.
    Inference validation MUST NOT occur across authority domains.

  rationale: >
    Inference binding has meaning only within a single authority-
    scoped replay domain. Validating inference prior to authority
    isolation risks cross-authority contamination.

  enforcement_mode: static_analysis
  failure_action: ci_fail

# ============================================================
# CANONICAL ORDER REQUIREMENT
# ============================================================

required_validation_order:

  description: >
    The replay verifier MUST validate authority isolation
    before validating inference bindings.

  required_sequence:
    - _validate_authority
    - _validate_environment
    - _validate_request
    - _validate_inference_binding

  forbidden_sequences:
    - inference_validation_before_authority
    - inference_validation_without_authority_validation

# ============================================================
# TARGETED FILES
# ============================================================

targets:

  verifier:
    path: afritech/replay/verify.py

# ============================================================
# CI VALIDATION CHECKS
# ============================================================

ci_checks:

  - check_id: AO-F1
    description: >
      Verify that _validate_authority is invoked before
      _validate_inference_binding in verify().
    target: afritech/replay/verify.py
    required_order:
      first: _validate_authority
      before: _validate_inference_binding
    failure_message: >
      Authority validation must occur before inference validation.

  - check_id: AO-F2
    description: >
      Verify that inference binding validation is not reachable
      unless authority validation has already executed.
    target: afritech/replay/verify.py
    pattern_forbidden:
      - '_validate_inference_binding\\(.*\\).*_validate_authority'
    failure_message: >
      Inference validation appears reachable before authority validation.

  - check_id: AO-F3
    description: >
      Verify that no inference-related failure modes are emitted
      prior to authority mismatch handling.
    target: afritech/replay/verify.py
    forbidden_failure_modes_before_authority:
      - inference_input_mismatch
      - model_identity_mismatch
      - nondeterministic_inference
    failure_message: >
      Inference failure emitted before authority isolation.

# ============================================================
# CI OUTCOME
# ============================================================

ci_outcome:

  on_pass:
    result: success
    message: "Authority isolation precedes inference validation."

  on_fail:
    result: failure
    message: >
      Replay verifier violates authority-first governance ordering.

===== ./afritech/replay/ci/CERTIFICATE_SIGNATURE_VALIDATION.yaml =====
## ============================================================
## AfriTech CI Rule
## Runtime Certificate Signature Validation
## ============================================================
## Rule ID: CI-CERT-SIGNATURE-001
## Purpose:
## Ensure that every RuntimeCertificate is:
## - structurally valid
## - cryptographically signed
## - bound to the attested constitutional state
##
## Scope:
## - afritech/proof/certificates/
## - afritech/proof/runtime_certificate.py
##
## This rule is normative and enforced in CI.
## ============================================================

metadata:
  rule_id: CI-CERT-SIGNATURE-001
  rule_version: "1.0.0"
  applies_to:
    - Phase-1
    - Phase-2A
    - Phase-2B
  last_updated: "2026-05-08"

## ============================================================
## GOVERNANCE PRINCIPLE
## ============================================================

principle:
  statement: >
    No RuntimeCertificate may authorize runtime execution
    unless its signature, payload integrity, and constitutional
    bindings are cryptographically valid.

  rationale: >
    The RuntimeCertificate is the sole authorization artifact
    for runtime existence. If its integrity is not enforced,
    the entire constitutional model collapses.

  enforcement_mode: ci_execution
  failure_action: ci_fail

## ============================================================
## REQUIRED CERTIFICATE PROPERTIES
## ============================================================

required_properties:

  description: >
    Every RuntimeCertificate MUST satisfy all structural,
    cryptographic, and binding constraints prior to acceptance.

  structural_requirements:

    required_fields:
      - certificate_id
      - issued_at
      - epoch
      - constitutional_binding
      - runtime_identity
      - admission_guarantees
      - execution_scope
      - replay_constraints
      - signature

  cryptographic_requirements:

    signature_required: true
    payload_hash_required: true
    certificate_root_required: true

  binding_requirements:

    - registry_hash_present
    - epoch_hash_present
    - kernel_root_hash_present
    - execution_surfaces_hash_present
    - authority_profiles_hash_present
    - replay_verifier_hash_present

## ============================================================
## TARGETED FILES
## ============================================================

targets:

  certificate_directory:
    path: afritech/proof/certificates
    pattern: "*.cert"

  certificate_handler:
    path: afritech/proof/runtime_certificate.py

## ============================================================
## CI VALIDATION CHECKS
## ============================================================

ci_checks:

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # CS-F1: Ensure certificate exists
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: CS-F1

    description: >
      Verify that at least one RuntimeCertificate exists.

    target_directory: afritech/proof/certificates
    minimum_files: 1

    failure_message: >
      No RuntimeCertificate found. Runtime cannot be admitted.

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # CS-F2: Validate certificate structure
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: CS-F2

    description: >
      Ensure certificate contains all required top-level fields.

    target_pattern: "afritech/proof/certificates/*.cert"

    required_fields:
      - runtime_certificate.certificate_id
      - runtime_certificate.epoch
      - runtime_certificate.constitutional_binding
      - runtime_certificate.runtime_identity
      - runtime_certificate.signature

    failure_message: >
      RuntimeCertificate missing required fields.

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # CS-F3: Validate signature fields present
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: CS-F3

    description: >
      Ensure signature fields exist and are not placeholders.

    target_pattern: "afritech/proof/certificates/*.cert"

    required_fields:
      - runtime_certificate.signature.payload_hash
      - runtime_certificate.signature.signature
      - runtime_certificate.signature.certificate_root

    forbidden_values:
      - "<SHA256(canonical serialization)>"
      - "<CRYPTOGRAPHIC_SIGNATURE>"
      - "<RUNTIME_ROOT_PUBLIC_KEY_HASH>"

    failure_message: >
      RuntimeCertificate contains unresolved or placeholder signature fields.

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # CS-F4: Payload hash integrity
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: CS-F4

    description: >
      Validate that payload_hash matches canonical serialization.

    enforcement: semantic_validation

    validation_method:
      type: python_hook
      function: validate_certificate_payload_hash

    failure_message: >
      RuntimeCertificate payload_hash does not match serialized content.

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # CS-F5: Signature verification
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: CS-F5

    description: >
      Verify cryptographic signature against certificate_root.

    enforcement: semantic_validation

    validation_method:
      type: python_hook
      function: verify_certificate_signature

    failure_message: >
      RuntimeCertificate signature verification failed.

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # CS-F6: Binding consistency check
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: CS-F6

    description: >
      Ensure certificate binding matches actual system state.

    enforcement: semantic_validation

    validation_method:
      type: python_hook
      function: validate_constitutional_binding

    failure_message: >
      RuntimeCertificate does not match registry, epoch, or surfaces.

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # CS-F7: Prevent unsigned certificate execution
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: CS-F7

    description: >
      Ensure runtime_certificate.py enforces signature verification.

    target: afritech/proof/runtime_certificate.py

    pattern_required:
      - 'verify_signature'
      - 'validate_payload_hash'

    failure_message: >
      Certificate handler does not enforce cryptographic validation.

## ============================================================
## VALIDATION FUNCTIONS (REFERENCE CONTRACT)
## ============================================================

validation_contract:

  validate_certificate_payload_hash:

    description: >
      Canonically serialize certificate fields defined in
      signature_scope and compute SHA256 hash.

    expected_result: >
      Computed hash must equal signature.payload_hash.

  verify_certificate_signature:

    description: >
      Verify signature using certificate_root public key.

    expected_result: >
      Signature MUST validate against payload_hash.

  validate_constitutional_binding:

    description: >
      Compare binding hashes against actual files:
      - registry.yaml
      - EXECUTION_SURFACES.yaml
      - authority_profiles.yaml

    expected_result: >
      All hashes MUST match.

## ============================================================
## RUNTIME GUARANTEE (ASSERTION)
## ============================================================

runtime_assertion:

  statement: >
    No RuntimeCertificate with invalid signature,
    mismatched payload_hash, or inconsistent binding
    MAY authorize runtime execution.

  enforcement_effect:
    - runtime_invalidated
    - execution_refused
    - replay_rejected
    - attestation_chain_broken

## ============================================================
## CI OUTCOME
## ============================================================

ci_outcome:

  on_pass:
    result: success
    message: "RuntimeCertificate validation passed."

  on_fail:
    result: failure
    message: >
      RuntimeCertificate failed integrity or signature validation.
      Execution is constitutionally forbidden.
      See CI logs for details.


===== ./afritech/replay/ci/CERTIFICATE_VERIFICATION_ENFORCEMENT.yaml =====
## ============================================================
## AfriTech CI Rule
## Runtime Certificate Verification Enforcement
## ============================================================
## Rule ID: CI-CERT-VERIFY-001
## Purpose:
## Ensure that RuntimeCertificates are not only valid in CI,
## but also actively verified at runtime via enforced logic.
##
## Scope:
## - afritech/runtime/admission/admission_engine.py
## - afritech/proof/runtime_certificate.py
##
## This rule is normative and enforced in CI.
## ============================================================

metadata:
  rule_id: CI-CERT-VERIFY-001
  rule_version: "1.0.0"
  applies_to:
    - Phase-1
    - Phase-2A
    - Phase-2B
  last_updated: "2026-05-08"


## ============================================================
## GOVERNANCE PRINCIPLE
## ============================================================

principle:

  statement: >
    RuntimeCertificate validation MUST be actively enforced
    at runtime. Structural correctness alone is insufficient.

  rationale: >
    A valid certificate that is not verified during runtime
    admission represents a broken security model.
    Verification must be executed—not assumed.

  enforcement_mode: static_analysis
  failure_action: ci_fail


## ============================================================
## REQUIRED VERIFICATION BEHAVIOR
## ============================================================

required_behavior:

  description: >
    The runtime must perform explicit verification of:
      - payload_hash integrity
      - cryptographic signature validity
      - constitutional binding consistency

  required_functions:

    - verify_signature
    - validate_payload_hash
    - validate_constitutional_binding

  enforcement_location:

    admission_engine:
      path: afritech/runtime/admission/admission_engine.py

    certificate_handler:
      path: afritech/proof/runtime_certificate.py


## ============================================================
## TARGETED FILES
## ============================================================

targets:

  admission_engine:
    path: afritech/runtime/admission/admission_engine.py

  certificate_handler:
    path: afritech/proof/runtime_certificate.py


## ============================================================
## CI VALIDATION CHECKS
## ============================================================

ci_checks:

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # CV-F1: Ensure signature verification exists
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: CV-F1

    description: >
      Verify that cryptographic signature validation logic is implemented.

    targets:
      - afritech/runtime/admission/admission_engine.py
      - afritech/proof/runtime_certificate.py

    pattern_required:
      - 'verify_signature'
      - 'signature'
      - 'certificate_root'

    failure_message: >
      Signature verification logic is missing or incomplete.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # CV-F2: Ensure payload hash validation exists
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: CV-F2

    description: >
      Verify that certificate payload hash integrity is validated.

    targets:
      - afritech/runtime/admission/admission_engine.py
      - afritech/proof/runtime_certificate.py

    pattern_required:
      - 'payload_hash'
      - 'compute_sha256'
      - 'validate_payload'

    failure_message: >
      Certificate payload hash validation is missing.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # CV-F3: Ensure binding validation logic exists
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: CV-F3

    description: >
      Ensure that constitutional binding hashes are checked
      against actual system files.

    target: afritech/runtime/admission/admission_engine.py

    pattern_required:
      - '_validate_constitutional_binding'
      - 'registry_hash'
      - 'epoch_hash'
      - 'execution_surfaces_hash'

    failure_message: >
      Constitutional binding validation is incomplete.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # CV-F4: Ensure verification occurs BEFORE admission succeeds
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: CV-F4

    description: >
      Ensure that verification is executed before admission returns success.

    target: afritech/runtime/admission/admission_engine.py

    required_order:
      sequence:
        - _validate_signature
        - _validate_constitutional_binding
        - _validate_admission_guarantees

    failure_message: >
      Verification steps are not executed in required order.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # CV-F5: Prevent bypass of verification
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: CV-F5

    description: >
      Ensure that admission cannot succeed without running verification.

    target: afritech/runtime/admission/admission_engine.py

    pattern_forbidden:
      - 'return True$'

    enforce_context: absence_of_validation_calls

    failure_message: >
      Admission may return success without verification.


## ============================================================
## VERIFICATION INVARIANT
## ============================================================

verification_invariant:

  statement: >
    A RuntimeCertificate is valid ONLY if:
      - payload integrity is verified
      - signature is cryptographically confirmed
      - constitutional bindings are consistent

  violation_effect:

    - runtime_invalidated
    - execution_refused
    - replay_rejected
    - certificate_rejected


## ============================================================
## CI OUTCOME
## ============================================================

ci_outcome:

  on_pass:
    result: success
    message: "Runtime certificate verification enforcement passed."

  on_fail:
    result: failure
    message: >
      Runtime certificate verification is incomplete or bypassed.
      Execution is constitutionally invalid.

===== ./afritech/replay/ci/FAILURE_TAXONOMY_ENFORCEMENT.yaml =====
# ============================================================
# Afritech CI Rule
# Failure Taxonomy Enforcement
# ============================================================
#
# Rule ID: CI-REPLAY-FAIL-001
#
# Purpose:
#   Ensure that *every* replay verifier failure is mapped to
#   the canonical failure taxonomy.
#
# Scope:
#   - afritech/replay/verify.py
#   - afritech/replay/failures.py
#
# This rule is normative and enforced in CI.
# ============================================================

metadata:
  rule_id: CI-REPLAY-FAIL-001
  rule_version: "1.0.0"
  applies_to:
    - Phase-1
    - Phase-2A
    - Phase-2B
  last_updated: "2026-05-05"

# ============================================================
# ENFORCEMENT PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

principle:

  statement: >
    The replay verifier MUST NOT emit any failure that is not
    explicitly defined in the canonical failure taxonomy.

  enforcement_mode: static_analysis
  failure_action: ci_fail

# ============================================================
# CANONICAL SOURCES
# ============================================================

canonical_sources:

  failure_definitions:
    path: afritech/replay/failures.py
    required_items:
      - ReplayFailureMode
      - ReplayInvariant
      - DivergenceLocation

  failure_taxonomy_doc:
    path: afritech/replay/docs/FAILURE_TAXONOMY.yaml

# ============================================================
# REQUIRED VERIFIER CONTRACT
# ============================================================

verifier_contract:

  allowed_failure_emission:

    description: >
      Replay verifier code MAY ONLY construct ReplayFailure
      instances using values sourced from ReplayFailureMode,
      ReplayInvariant, and DivergenceLocation enums.

    disallowed_patterns:
      - string literal failure modes
      - ad-hoc invariant strings
      - ad-hoc divergence locations

  required_construction_pattern:

    python:
      allowed:
        - ReplayFailure(
            ReplayFailureMode.<ENUM>,
            ReplayInvariant.<ENUM>,
            DivergenceLocation.<ENUM>,
            <details>
          )

      forbidden:
        - ReplayFailure("...", "...", "...", "...")

# ============================================================
# CI VALIDATION CHECKS
# ============================================================

ci_checks:

  - check_id: CT-F1
    description: >
      Verify that verify.py imports ReplayFailureMode,
      ReplayInvariant, and DivergenceLocation.
    target: afritech/replay/verify.py
    failure_message: >
      Verifier does not import canonical failure enums.

  - check_id: CT-F2
    description: >
      Verify that verify.py does not construct ReplayFailure
      with string literals.
    pattern_forbidden:
      - 'ReplayFailure\(\s*"[a-zA-Z_]+"'
    target: afritech/replay/verify.py
    failure_message: >
      Verifier constructs ReplayFailure using string literals.

  - check_id: CT-F3
    description: >
      Verify that every ReplayFailureMode enum appears in
      FAILURE_TAXONOMY.yaml.
    target:
      - afritech/replay/failures.py
      - afritech/replay/docs/FAILURE_TAXONOMY.yaml
    failure_message: >
      Failure mode missing from canonical taxonomy document.

  - check_id: CT-F4
    description: >
      Verify no failure modes appear in taxonomy that are not
      defined in ReplayFailureMode.
    target:
      - afritech/replay/failures.py
      - afritech/replay/docs/FAILURE_TAXONOMY.yaml
    failure_message: >
      Taxonomy references undefined failure mode.

# ============================================================
# CI OUTCOME
# ============================================================

ci_outcome:

  on_pass:
    result: success
    message: "Replay failure taxonomy enforcement passed."

  on_fail:
    result: failure
    message: >
      Replay verifier failure semantics violate canonical taxonomy.
      See CI logs for details on the specific violation.

===== ./afritech/replay/ci/INFRASTRUCTURE_ENFORCEMENT.yaml =====
## ============================================================
## AfriTech CI Rule
## Infrastructure Non-Interference Enforcement
## ============================================================
## Rule ID: CI-INFRA-ENFORCE-001
## Purpose:
## Ensure infrastructure surfaces remain strictly
## non-constitutional and cannot influence runtime execution.
##
## Scope:
## - afritech/docker/
## - .github/workflows/
## - environment configs
##
## ============================================================

metadata:
  rule_id: CI-INFRA-ENFORCE-001
  rule_version: "1.0.0"
  applies_to:
    - Phase-2B
  last_updated: "2026-05-08"

# ============================================================
# PRINCIPLE
# ============================================================

principle:

  statement: >
    Infrastructure MUST NOT influence runtime execution,
    replay outcomes, or constitutional validity.

  enforcement_mode: static_analysis
  failure_action: ci_fail


# ============================================================
# TARGETS
# ============================================================

targets:

  infrastructure_dirs:

    - afritech/docker/
    - .github/workflows/

  runtime_dirs:

    - afritech/runtime/
    - afritech/inference/
    - afritech/replay/


# ============================================================
# CORE RULES
# ============================================================

rules:

  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # INF-1: No infrastructure imports in runtime
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: INF-1

    description: >
      Runtime must not import infrastructure modules.

    target_dirs: 
      - afritech/runtime/
      - afritech/inference/

    forbidden_patterns:

      - "docker"
      - "os\\.environ"
      - "subprocess"
      - "dotenv"

    failure_message: >
      Runtime depends on infrastructure or environment.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # INF-2: No environment-driven execution
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: INF-2

    description: >
      Prevent execution logic based on environment variables.

    target_dirs:
      - afritech/runtime/
      - afritech/inference/

    forbidden_patterns:

      - "os\\.getenv"
      - "os\\.environ\\["
      - "env\\."

    failure_message: >
      Environment-based execution detected.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # INF-3: No infrastructure paths in registry/certificate
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: INF-3

    description: >
      Infrastructure must not appear in constitutional bindings.

    target_files:

      - afritech/registry/registry.yaml
      - afritech/registry/attestation.yaml
      - afritech/proof/certificates/*.cert

    forbidden_patterns:

      - "docker"
      - ".github"
      - "workflow"

    failure_message: >
      Infrastructure detected in constitutional binding.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # INF-4: Replay determinism enforcement
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: INF-4

    description: >
      Ensure infrastructure cannot affect replay output.

    target_dirs:

      - afritech/runtime/

    forbidden_patterns:

      - "time\\."
      - "random\\."
      - "uuid\\."
      - "datetime\\.now"

    failure_message: >
      Non-deterministic dependency detected.


  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  # INF-5: No execution logic inside infrastructure
  # ------------------------------------------------------------
  - check_id: INF-5

    description: >
      Infrastructure files must not contain runtime logic.

    target_dirs:

      - afritech/docker/
      - .github/workflows/

    forbidden_patterns:

      - "def run"
      - "execute"
      - "engine"

    failure_message: >
      Execution logic found in infrastructure.


# ============================================================
# RUNTIME ASSERTION
# ============================================================

runtime_assertion:

  statement: >
    Runtime execution must be fully independent
    from all infrastructure layers.

  violations:

    - runtime_invalidated
    - replay_invalidated
    - certificate_rejected


# ============================================================
# CI OUTCOME
# ============================================================

ci_outcome:

  on_pass:
    result: success
    message: "Infrastructure isolation verified."

  on_fail:
    result: failure
    message: >
      Infrastructure is influencing execution.
      This violates constitutional invariants.

metadata:
