===== ./ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/determinism_envelope.yaml =====
# ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/determinism_envelope.yaml

schema: afriride.determinism_envelope.v0_2
kind: determinism_envelope

envelope:
  domain: afriride
  version: v0.2
  status: ACTIVE
  classification: bounded_deterministic_execution

  description: >
    This determinism envelope defines the complete and exclusive
    boundary of allowed variability for AfriRide v0.2.
    All execution, failure handling, replay verification,
    and continuity proofs MUST operate entirely within
    this envelope. Any behavior outside this envelope
    is constitutionally invalid.

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ CORE DETERMINISM PRINCIPLE
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  core_principle:
    statement: >
      Given identical inputs, identical parameters, identical
      authority scope, and identical envelope constraints,
      AfriRide v0.2 MUST produce identical decision traces,
      identical event sequences, and identical replay hashes.

    applies_to:
      - assignment decisions
      - retry sequencing
      - failure handling
      - refusal outcomes
      - trace generation

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ ALLOWED VARIABILITY (EXPLICITLY BOUNDED)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  allowed_variability:

    scenario_parameters:
      description: >
        Scenario parameters may vary ONLY if explicitly
        declared in scenario YAML files.
      examples:
        - initial_driver_count
        - dropout_count
        - rejection_sequence
        - timeout_threshold

      constraints:
        - parameters must be scalar or ordered lists
        - parameters must be replay-bound
        - no implicit defaults permitted

    ordered_collections:
      description: >
        Collections may vary in size but MUST be ordered
        deterministically before use.
      ordering_rules:
        - explicit sort key required
        - stable ordering guaranteed
        - no runtime reordering

    retry_depth:
      description: >
        Retry count may vary as a function of deterministic
        exhaustion of ordered candidates.
      constraints:
        - retry is strictly sequential
        - no backtracking
        - no parallel attempts

    logical_step_count:
      description: >
        Logical step counters may advance deterministically
        and are used for timeout evaluation.
      constraints:
        - counter increments must be explicit
        - counters must appear in trace
        - no wall-clock dependency

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ❌ FORBIDDEN VARIABILITY (ABSOLUTE)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  forbidden_variability:

    randomness:
      - random()
      - sampling
      - shuffle
      - probabilistic_branching

    time_sources:
      - wall_clock_time
      - system_time
      - sleep
      - async_delay

    external_state:
      - environment_variables
      - network_state
      - I/O side effects
      - shared mutable globals

    inference:
      - heuristic_selection
      - optimization
      - scoring_functions
      - ML_model_outputs

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ NORMALIZATION RULES
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  normalization:

    identifiers:
      rule: >
        Identifiers must be stable, immutable, and comparable.
      constraints:
        - no mutation of ids
        - ids must be hash-stable

    collections:
      rule: >
        All collections MUST be normalized before use.
      normalization_steps:
        - explicit sorting
        - explicit truncation
        - explicit filtering

    state_transitions:
      rule: >
        State transitions must be atomic, ordered,
        and traceable.
      constraints:
        - no partial mutation
        - no implicit transitions

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ TRACE & REPLAY BINDING
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  trace_binding:

    requirements:
      - every decision emits a trace event
      - every failure emits a trace event
      - trace order is strict
      - trace content is deterministic

    invariants:
      - identical trace sequence → identical hash
      - trace omission → invalid execution
      - trace divergence → replay failure

  replay_binding:

    requirements:
      - replay uses identical envelope
      - replay forbids external inputs
      - replay recomputes all decisions

    acceptance_rule:
      description: >
        Replay is accepted ONLY if recomputed trace hash
        exactly matches original execution hash.

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ AUTHORITY & SCOPE BINDING
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  authority_binding:

    requirements:
      - authority must be explicit
      - authority scope must be identical for replay
      - no authority inference permitted

    forbidden:
      - implicit authority escalation
      - authority mutation during execution

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ FAILURE HANDLING CONSISTENCY
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  failure_handling:

    rule: >
      All failures MUST map to declared failure taxonomy
      and handling modes.
    constraints:
      - no undeclared failure paths
      - no silent failure
      - refusal must be explicit

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ VALIDATION & ENFORCEMENT
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  validation:

    must_hold:
      - execution conforms to envelope
      - replay conforms to envelope
      - trace and replay hashes identical
      - no forbidden variability observed

    violation_effect:
      classification: CONSTITUTIONAL_INVALIDATION
      consequence:
        - scenario proof rejected
        - continuity coverage reduced to zero
        - v0.2 claim invalidated

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ RELATIONSHIP TO v0.1
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  compatibility:

    v0_1_status:
      description: >
        AfriRide v0.1 remains frozen and unaffected.
      guarantees:
        - no weakening of v0.1 determinism
        - no reinterpretation of v0.1 proofs

    v0_2_extension:
      description: >
        v0.2 extends proof coverage ONLY within this
        determinism envelope.

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ FINAL ASSERTION
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  final_assertion:
    statement: >
      AfriRide v0.2 is deterministic if and only if
      all execution paths, failures, retries, and refusals
      operate strictly within this determinism envelope.


===== ./ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/failure_taxonomy.yaml =====
failure_taxonomy:
  domain: afriride
  version: v0.2
  classification: deterministic_failure_space

  governing_epoch: EPOCH_0008

  doctrine: OBSERVATIONAL_ADMISSIBILITY_GOVERNANCE

# =====================================================
# PRINCIPLE
# =====================================================

  principle:
    description: >
      All coordination failures must be explicit, deterministic,
      and replay-verifiable. No failure may occur implicitly,
      probabilistically, or without trace representation.

    guarantees:
      - every failure must be declared in this taxonomy
      - every failure must produce a deterministic outcome
      - every outcome must be replay-identical
      - no silent failure is permitted

# =====================================================
# OBSERVATIONAL DOCTRINE BINDING
# =====================================================

  observational_doctrine:
    rule: >
      Taxonomy may declare only replay-verified deterministic
      runtime behavior without introducing semantic interpretation.

    admissibility_conditions:
      - execution_preexisting: true
      - deterministic: true
      - replay_verified: true
      - semantic_free: true
      - runtime_unchanged: true

    prohibited_actions:
      - semantic_interpretation
      - equivalence_inference
      - normalization
      - runtime_behavior_mutation

# =====================================================
# EPISTEMIC MODEL
# =====================================================

  epistemic:
    base_classification:
      epistemic_status: OBSERVED_FACT
      semantic_commitment: NONE
      interpretation_required: false

    constraints:
      - all failure classifications are replay-observed facts
      - failures MUST NOT encode meaning or intent identity
      - replay evidence supersedes all interpretation
      - historical outcomes are immutable

# =====================================================
# FAILURE MODES
# =====================================================

  failure_modes:

    DRIVER_DROPOUT:
      description: >
        A driver becomes unavailable after initial eligibility
        evaluation but before final assignment.

      trigger_condition:
        - driver_list_reduced_deterministically
        - availability_flag_false

      deterministic_handling: RETRY_OR_REFUSE

      expected_outcomes:
        - deterministic_driver_reselection
        - fallback_to_next_available
        - deterministic_refusal_if_exhausted

      invariants:
        - driver ordering is stable
        - retry sequence is deterministic
        - no randomness in selection

    DRIVER_REJECTS:
      description: >
        A driver rejects an assignment request.

      trigger_condition:
        - driver_response == REJECTED

      deterministic_handling: RETRY_NEXT_DRIVER

      expected_outcomes:
        - sequential driver iteration
        - rejection recorded in execution trace

      invariants:
        - rejection ordering is preserved
        - no reordering of candidates
        - deterministic traversal only

    NO_DRIVER_AVAILABLE:
      description: >
        No eligible driver exists at evaluation time.

      trigger_condition:
        - driver_pool_empty
        - all drivers unavailable

      deterministic_handling: DETERMINISTIC_REFUSAL

      expected_outcomes:
        - explicit refusal outcome
        - complete trace emission

      invariants:
        - refusal is replay-identical
        - no partial assignment permitted

    TIMEOUT_EXCEEDED:
      description: >
        Execution exceeds declared deterministic step bound.

      trigger_condition:
        - execution_steps > declared_threshold

      deterministic_handling: DETERMINISTIC_REFUSAL

      expected_outcomes:
        - deterministic timeout refusal
        - trace record emission

      invariants:
        - threshold is constant
        - no real-time dependency
        - no external clock influence

# =====================================================
# OBSERVATIONAL FAILURE OVERLAY
# =====================================================

  observational_failures:

    DUPLICATE_INTENT_REFUSAL:
      base_mode: DETERMINISTIC_REFUSAL
      classification: OBSERVATIONAL_REFUSAL

      epistemic_status: OBSERVED_FACT
      semantic_commitment: NONE
      interpretation_required: false

      description: >
        Deterministic refusal observed for execution traces with
        identical replay hash signature.

      properties:
        deterministic: true
        replay_stable: true
        hash_stable: true
        non_semantic: true
        non_equivalence_bound: true
        runtime_existing_behavior: true

      replay_guarantees:
        - identical replay hash signature MUST produce identical refusal
        - refusal MUST produce identical replay hash signature
        - transcript MUST remain unchanged

      prohibited_interpretations:
        - equivalence inference
        - similarity inference
        - normalization
        - deduplication logic
        - intent identity assignment

      constitutional_basis:
        - ADR-0011
        - EPOCH_0008

# =====================================================
# HANDLING MODES
# =====================================================

  handling_modes:

    RETRY_OR_REFUSE:
      description: >
        Deterministic reassignment until candidates are exhausted,
        then deterministic refusal.

    RETRY_NEXT_DRIVER:
      description: >
        Sequential deterministic selection of next available driver.

    DETERMINISTIC_REFUSAL:
      description: >
        Explicit refusal outcome with full trace emission.

# =====================================================
# GLOBAL INVARIANTS
# =====================================================

  invariants:

    determinism:
      - no random selection
      - no probabilistic branching
      - no nondeterministic iteration

    replay:
      - identical replay hash signature yields identical outcome
      - all failure traces are replay-identical

    ordering:
      - all collections are explicitly ordered
      - driver traversal is stable

    traceability:
      - every failure produces a trace entry
      - every decision is auditable

    epistemic:
      - all outcomes are observed facts
      - no semantic interpretation permitted
      - runtime outcomes are immutable across epochs

# =====================================================
# FORBIDDEN BEHAVIORS
# =====================================================

  forbidden:

    execution:
      - implicit_fallback
      - silent_failure
      - untraced_execution

    determinism:
      - random_selection
      - probabilistic_branching
      - time_based_variation

    structure:
      - undeclared_failure_modes
      - runtime_extension_without_epoch

    semantics:
      - equivalence inference
      - similarity reasoning
      - normalization
      - deduplication
      - intent classification

# =====================================================
# VALIDATION CONTRACT
# =====================================================

  validation:

    required:
      - all scenarios reference declared failure_modes
      - all failures map to handling_modes
      - all outputs are replay-verifiable

    admissibility:
      - replay_verified: true
      - deterministic: true
      - semantic_free: true
      - runtime_unchanged: true

    rejection_conditions:
      - replay divergence
      - semantic dependency
      - equivalence requirement
      - runtime mutation
      - normalization dependency

    success_condition: >
      All failure scenarios resolve into deterministic,
      replay-identical outcomes without semantic interpretation.

    failure_condition: >
      Any deviation from declared modes, determinism,
      or replay consistency invalidates v0.2 compliance.

===== ./ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/scenarios/driver_dropout.yaml =====
# ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/scenarios/driver_dropout.yaml

schema: afriride.scenario.v0_2
kind: failure_scenario

scenario:
  id: DRIVER_DROPOUT
  version: v0.2
  classification: deterministic_failure_scenario
  domain: afriride
  status: ACTIVE

  description: >
    This scenario validates deterministic handling of driver dropout,
    where a subset of initially eligible drivers becomes unavailable.
    The system must re-evaluate assignment deterministically without
    introducing randomness or altering replay consistency.

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ TAXONOMY LINK (MANDATORY)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  failure_mode: DRIVER_DROPOUT
  governed_by:
    taxonomy: ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/failure_taxonomy.yaml
    handling_mode: RETRY_OR_REFUSE

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ INPUT PARAMETERS (FULLY DETERMINISTIC)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  parameters:
    initial_driver_count: 10
    dropout_count: 5

    driver_definition:
      format: list_of_dicts
      required_fields:
        - id
        - available
      id_constraints:
        type: integer
        uniqueness: required
        mutation: forbidden

    ordering_rule:
      description: >
        Drivers must be sorted in ascending order by id before
        any assignment or dropout logic is applied.
      key: id
      order: ascending
      requirement: strict

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ DERIVED CONDITIONS (EXPLICIT — NO RUNTIME INFERENCE)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  derived_conditions:
    remaining_driver_count: 5

    dropout_strategy:
      description: >
        Dropout is applied deterministically by truncating
        the ordered driver list.
      algorithm:
        steps:
          - step: sort
            rule: ascending_id
          - step: truncate
            rule: remove_last_N
            parameter: dropout_count
      forbidden:
        - random_removal
        - probabilistic_selection
        - availability_sampling

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ EXPECTED SYSTEM BEHAVIOR
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  expected_behavior:

    assignment_strategy:
      description: >
        Assignment must always select the first available driver
        from the deterministically ordered list.
      rules:
        - select_first_available
        - no_random_selection
        - no_scoring
        - no_weighting
        - no_reordering

    fallback_behavior:
      description: >
        If the selected driver becomes unavailable,
        the system must select the next driver in strict order.
      rules:
        - strictly_sequential_retry
        - no_skipping
        - no_reordering
        - bounded_by_remaining_driver_count

    exhaustion_behavior:
      description: >
        If all drivers are exhausted due to dropout,
        the system must deterministically refuse.
      output:
        type: refusal
        reason: NO_DRIVER_AVAILABLE
        refusal_classification: DETERMINISTIC_REFUSAL
        retry_permitted: false

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ TRACE REQUIREMENTS
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  trace_requirements:

    required_events:
      - DriverPoolInitialized
      - DriverPoolOrdered
      - DriverDropoutApplied
      - AssignmentAttempted
      - DriverSelected
      - DeterministicRefusal

    event_ordering:
      requirement: strict
      branching_allowed: false

    trace_invariants:
      - identical input yields identical event sequence
      - identical event sequence yields identical hash
      - no event omission
      - no extra events

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ REPLAY REQUIREMENTS
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  replay_requirements:

    admissibility_conditions:
      - identical scenario_id
      - identical parameters
      - identical driver ordering
      - identical dropout_count
      - identical authority scope

    guarantees:
      - identical trace reconstruction
      - identical event ordering
      - identical replay hash

    invalidation_rule:
      description: >
        Any divergence between original execution and replay,
        including trace order or hash, invalidates this scenario proof.

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ VALIDATION RULES
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  validation:

    must_hold:
      - driver list sorted before processing
      - dropout applied exactly once
      - assignment order preserved
      - no randomness introduced
      - determinism contract honored

    must_not_occur:
      - implicit_fallback
      - silent_failure
      - dynamic_reordering
      - missing_trace_events
      - external_state_dependency

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ KPI EXPECTATIONS (LIMITED — v0.2)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  kpi_expectations:

    determinism:
      DDR: 1.0

    continuity:
      included_in_CC: true

    refusal_behavior:
      deterministic_refusal_if_exhausted: true

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ❌ FORBIDDEN BEHAVIOR
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  forbidden:

    execution:
      - random_driver_selection
      - heuristic_assignment
      - load_balancing_logic
      - optimization_routines

    determinism:
      - unordered_iteration
      - probabilistic_branching
      - wall_clock_dependency
      - entropy_sources

    structure:
      - modifying_driver_ids
      - injecting_dynamic_drivers
      - undeclared_failure_path


===== ./ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/scenarios/rejection_chain.yaml =====
# ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/scenarios/rejection_chain.yaml

schema: afriride.scenario.v0_2
kind: failure_scenario

scenario:
  id: DRIVER_REJECTION_CHAIN
  version: v0.2
  classification: deterministic_failure_scenario
  domain: afriride
  status: ACTIVE

  description: >
    This scenario validates deterministic handling of sequential driver
    rejections during ride assignment. Drivers reject assignments in a
    fixed order, and the system must retry deterministically without
    reordering, skipping, or introducing randomness, preserving replay
    identity across all retries.

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ TAXONOMY LINK (MANDATORY)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  failure_mode: DRIVER_REJECTS
  governed_by:
    taxonomy: ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/failure_taxonomy.yaml
    handling_mode: RETRY_NEXT_DRIVER

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ INPUT PARAMETERS (FULLY DETERMINISTIC)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  parameters:
    initial_driver_count: 5

    driver_definition:
      format: list_of_dicts
      required_fields:
        - id
        - available
      id_constraints:
        type: integer
        uniqueness: required
        mutation: forbidden

    ordering_rule:
      description: >
        Drivers must be sorted in ascending order by id before any
        assignment attempt.
      key: id
      order: ascending
      requirement: strict

    rejection_sequence:
      description: >
        Ordered list of driver ids that will deterministically reject
        the assignment when selected.
      type: ordered_list
      example: [1, 2]

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ DERIVED CONDITIONS (EXPLICIT)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  derived_conditions:
    rejection_count: 2
    remaining_candidates: 3

    rejection_handling:
      description: >
        Each rejection removes the driver from future consideration.
      algorithm:
        steps:
          - step: select_next_driver
            rule: ascending_order
          - step: detect_rejection
            rule: driver_id_in_rejection_sequence
          - step: mark_driver_unavailable
            effect: remove_from_candidate_pool

      forbidden:
        - reinsert_rejected_driver
        - random_reselection
        - non_sequential_iteration

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ EXPECTED SYSTEM BEHAVIOR
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  expected_behavior:

    assignment_strategy:
      description: >
        System must attempt assignment strictly in driver id order.
      rules:
        - select_first_available
        - no_random_selection
        - no_scoring
        - no_weighting

    rejection_behavior:
      description: >
        When a driver rejects, the system must deterministically move
        to the next driver.
      rules:
        - strict_sequence
        - preserve_rejection_order
        - no_retry_of_rejected_driver

    exhaustion_behavior:
      description: >
        If all drivers reject or are exhausted, the system must
        deterministically refuse.
      output:
        type: refusal
        reason: NO_DRIVER_AVAILABLE
        refusal_classification: DETERMINISTIC_REFUSAL
        retry_permitted: false

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ TRACE REQUIREMENTS
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  trace_requirements:

    required_events:
      - DriverPoolInitialized
      - DriverPoolOrdered
      - AssignmentAttempted
      - DriverRejected
      - DriverSelected
      - DeterministicRefusal

    event_ordering:
      requirement: strict
      branching_allowed: false

    trace_invariants:
      - identical input yields identical event sequence
      - rejection order must appear explicitly in trace
      - identical trace yields identical hash
      - no event omission
      - no extra events

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ REPLAY REQUIREMENTS
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  replay_requirements:

    admissibility_conditions:
      - identical scenario_id
      - identical parameters
      - identical rejection_sequence
      - identical driver ordering
      - identical authority scope

    guarantees:
      - identical trace reconstruction
      - identical event ordering
      - identical replay hash

    invalidation_rule:
      description: >
        Any divergence in rejection order, event sequence, or hash
        invalidates the scenario proof.

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ VALIDATION RULES
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  validation:

    must_hold:
      - driver list sorted before processing
      - rejection sequence honored strictly
      - rejected drivers removed from pool
      - no randomness introduced
      - determinism contract honored

    must_not_occur:
      - implicit fallback
      - silent failure
      - driver reordering
      - retry loops
      - external_state_dependency

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ KPI EXPECTATIONS (LIMITED — v0.2)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  kpi_expectations:

    determinism:
      DDR: 1.0

    continuity:
      included_in_CC: true

    refusal_behavior:
      deterministic_refusal_if_exhausted: true

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ❌ FORBIDDEN BEHAVIOR
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  forbidden:

    execution:
      - random_driver_selection
      - heuristic_assignment
      - load_balancing_logic
      - optimization_routines

    determinism:
      - unordered_iteration
      - probabilistic_branching
      - wall_clock_dependency
      - entropy_sources

    structure:
      - modifying_driver_ids
      - reinserting_rejected_drivers
      - undeclared_failure_path

===== ./ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/scenarios/timeout.yaml =====
# ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/scenarios/timeout.yaml

schema: afriride.scenario.v0_2
kind: failure_scenario

scenario:
  id: TIMEOUT_EXCEEDED
  version: v0.2
  classification: deterministic_failure_scenario
  domain: afriride
  status: ACTIVE

  description: >
    This scenario validates deterministic handling of coordination
    timeouts. When a coordination step exceeds its declared deterministic
    threshold, the system must refuse explicitly and replay-identically,
    without consulting wall-clock time or introducing nondeterminism.

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ TAXONOMY LINK (MANDATORY)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  failure_mode: TIMEOUT_EXCEEDED
  governed_by:
    taxonomy: ecosystems/afriride/v0_2/failure_taxonomy.yaml
    handling_mode: DETERMINISTIC_REFUSAL

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ INPUT PARAMETERS (FULLY DETERMINISTIC)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  parameters:
    timeout_threshold:
      description: >
        Maximum number of deterministic coordination steps
        allowed before timeout is triggered.
      unit: logical_steps
      value: 10

    coordination_step_definition:
      description: >
        A coordination step represents a single, deterministic
        state transition attempt (e.g. assignment attempt).
      counted_events:
        - AssignmentAttempted
        - DriverResponseEvaluated

    clock_model:
      description: >
        Timeout is evaluated using logical step counting only.
        Wall-clock time is explicitly forbidden.
      type: logical_counter
      wall_clock_usage: forbidden

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ DERIVED CONDITIONS (EXPLICIT)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  derived_conditions:
    timeout_trigger_condition:
      description: >
        Timeout is triggered when the number of coordination
        steps exceeds the declared threshold.
      rule: coordination_steps > timeout_threshold

    timeout_evaluation:
      description: >
        Timeout evaluation must occur at a deterministic
        checkpoint in the execution flow.
      evaluation_point: post_step_increment

    forbidden:
      - real_time_measurement
      - system_clock_access
      - sleep_based_delay

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ EXPECTED SYSTEM BEHAVIOR
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  expected_behavior:

    normal_behavior:
      description: >
        Coordination proceeds deterministically while the
        step count remains within the threshold.
      rules:
        - step_counter_incremented_per_event
        - no timeout evaluation before threshold

    timeout_behavior:
      description: >
        When the threshold is exceeded, the system must
        immediately produce a deterministic refusal.
      output:
        type: refusal
        reason: TIMEOUT_EXCEEDED
        refusal_classification: DETERMINISTIC_REFUSAL
        retry_permitted: false

    post_timeout_behavior:
      description: >
        No further coordination or retries are permitted
        after timeout refusal.
      rules:
        - execution_terminates
        - no state mutation
        - no retry_attempts

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ TRACE REQUIREMENTS
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  trace_requirements:

    required_events:
      - CoordinationStarted
      - CoordinationStepIncremented
      - TimeoutThresholdEvaluated
      - DeterministicRefusal

    event_ordering:
      requirement: strict
      branching_allowed: false

    trace_invariants:
      - coordination_step_count must be explicit in trace
      - identical inputs yield identical step counts
      - identical trace yields identical hash
      - no missing or extra events

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ REPLAY REQUIREMENTS
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  replay_requirements:

    admissibility_conditions:
      - identical scenario_id
      - identical timeout_threshold
      - identical coordination_step_definition
      - identical authority scope

    guarantees:
      - identical step counter reconstruction
      - identical event ordering
      - identical replay hash

    invalidation_rule:
      description: >
        Any divergence in step count, event order,
        or replay hash invalidates the scenario proof.

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ VALIDATION RULES
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  validation:

    must_hold:
      - timeout evaluated using logical steps only
      - threshold constant across executions
      - refusal occurs immediately after threshold breach
      - determinism contract honored

    must_not_occur:
      - wall_clock_dependency
      - time.sleep or delay logic
      - probabilistic timeout
      - implicit continuation after timeout
      - external_state_dependency

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ✅ KPI EXPECTATIONS (LIMITED — v0.2)
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  kpi_expectations:

    determinism:
      DDR: 1.0

    continuity:
      included_in_CC: true

    refusal_behavior:
      deterministic_refusal_on_timeout: true

  # -----------------------------------------------------
  # ❌ FORBIDDEN BEHAVIOR
  # -----------------------------------------------------

  forbidden:

    execution:
      - retry_after_timeout
      - partial_execution_post_refusal
      - heuristic_grace_period

    determinism:
      - wall_clock_time
      - entropy_sources
      - probabilistic_thresholds

    structure:
      - dynamic_threshold_adjustment
      - undeclared_timeout_paths

  node:
    version: 6

dependencies:
  pre:
    - npm config set "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken" $NPM_AUTH
    - sudo apt-key adv --fetch-keys http://dl.yarnpkg.com/debian/pubkey.gpg
    - echo "deb http://dl.yarnpkg.com/debian/ stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/yarn.list
    - sudo apt-get update -qq
    - sudo apt-get install -y -qq yarn
  cache_directories:
    - ~/.yarn-cache
  override:
    - yarn

test:
  override:
    - yarn test

  publish:
    tag: /v?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)*(-.+)?/
    commands:
      - npm publish

===== ./rider_app/node_modules/ast-types/.github/workflows/main.yml =====
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [ master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ master ]

jobs:
  test:
    name: Test on node ${{ matrix.node_version }} and ${{ matrix.os }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node_version: ['10', '12', '14', '15', '16', '17']
        os: [ubuntu-latest]

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v2
    - name: Use Node.js ${{ matrix.node_version }}
      uses: actions/setup-node@v1
      with:
        node-version: ${{ matrix.node_version }}

    - name: npm install, build and test
      run: |
        npm install
        npm run build --if-present
        npm test

===== ./rider_app/node_modules/chrome-launcher/.github/workflows/ci.yml =====
name: 🛠

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request: # run on all PRs, not just PRs to a particular branch

jobs:
  basics:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node: [ 'latest', 'lts/*', 'lts/-1' ]
    name: basics (node ${{ matrix.node }})

    steps:
    - name: git clone
      uses: actions/checkout@v3

    - name: Setup node
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
        cache: 'yarn'

    - run: yarn --frozen-lockfile --network-timeout 1000000
    - run: yarn build

    - run: yarn test-formatting
    - run: yarn type-check

    # Run tests that require headful Chrome.
    - run: sudo apt-get install xvfb
    - name: yarn test
      run: xvfb-run --auto-servernum yarn test --reporter=spec

  unit:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [macos-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    name: unit_${{ matrix.os }}

    steps:
    - name: git clone
      uses: actions/checkout@v3

    - name: Use Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
        cache: 'yarn'

    - run: yarn --frozen-lockfile --network-timeout 1000000
    - run: yarn build
    - run: yarn test --reporter=spec


===== ./rider_app/node_modules/chrome-launcher/.github/workflows/lh-smoke.yml =====
name: smoke

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request: # run on all PRs, not just PRs to a particular branch

jobs:
  # Only run smoke tests for windows against stable chrome.
  lh-smoke-windows:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        smoke-test-shard: [1, 2]
      # e.g. if set 1 fails, continue with set 2 anyway
      fail-fast: false
    runs-on: windows-latest
    name: Windows smoke ${{ matrix.smoke-test-shard }}/2

    steps:
    - name: git clone
      uses: actions/checkout@v3

    - name: Use Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
        cache: 'yarn'
    
    # chrome-launcher
    # This'll add lighthouse AND install chrome-launcher's deps
    - run: yarn add --frozen-lockfile --network-timeout 1000000 -D https://github.com/GoogleChrome/lighthouse.git#main
    - run: yarn build

    # lighthouse 
    - run: yarn --cwd node_modules/lighthouse/ install --frozen-lockfile --network-timeout 1000000
    - run: yarn reset-link
    - run: yarn --cwd node_modules/lighthouse/ build-report

    - name: Run smoke tests
      # Windows bots are slow, so only run enough tests to verify matching behavior.
      run: yarn --cwd node_modules/lighthouse/ smoke --debug -j=2 --retries=0 --shard=${{ matrix.smoke-test-shard }}/2 dbw oopif offline lantern metrics

    - name: Upload failures
      if: failure()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v1
      with:
        name: Smokehouse (windows)
        path: node_modules/lighthouse/.tmp/smokehouse-ci-failures/

    - nodejs_version: "7"
    - nodejs_version: "8"
    - nodejs_version: "9"

  - npm install

  # Output useful info for debugging.
  - node --version
  - npm --version
  - npm run test

# Don't actually build.
build: off

===== ./rider_app/node_modules/jscodeshift/.github/workflows/test.yml =====
name: test

on:
  workflow_call:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [12.x, 14.x, 16.x]

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Use Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: yarn --frozen-lockfile
      - name: Run test
        run: yarn test

===== ./rider_app/node_modules/node-abort-controller/.github/workflows/test.yml =====
name: Test

on:
  push:
    branches: ["master"]
  pull_request:
    branches: ["master"]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Use Node.js 14.6.x
        uses: actions/setup-node@v3
        with:
          node-version: 14.6.x
          cache: "npm"
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test

  - 'nvm install-latest-npm'
install:
  - 'if [ "${TRAVIS_NODE_VERSION}" = "0.6" ] || [ "${TRAVIS_NODE_VERSION}" = "0.9" ]; then nvm install --latest-npm 0.8 && npm install && nvm use "${TRAVIS_NODE_VERSION}"; else npm install; fi;'

===== ./rider_app/node_modules/plist/.github/workflows/ci.yml =====
name: ci

on:
  push:
  pull_request:

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [ '14', '16', '18' ]

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Use Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}
      uses: actions/setup-node@v1
      with:
        node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}

    - run: npm install
    - run: npm test

===== ./rider_app/node_modules/recast/.github/workflows/main.yml =====
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [ master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ master ]

jobs:
  test:
    name: Test on node ${{ matrix.node_version }} and ${{ matrix.os }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node_version: ['10.x', '12.x', '14.x', '15.x', '16.x']
        os: [ubuntu-latest]

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v2
    - name: Use Node.js ${{ matrix.node_version }}
      uses: actions/setup-node@v1
      with:
        node-version: ${{ matrix.node_version }}

    - name: npm install, build and test
      run: |
        npm install
        npm run build --if-present
        npm test

===== ./rider_app/node_modules/requireg/node_modules/resolve/appveyor.yml =====
version: 1.0.{build}
skip_branch_with_pr: true
build: off

environment:
  matrix:
    - nodejs_version: "9"
    - nodejs_version: "8"
    - nodejs_version: "7"
    - nodejs_version: "6"
    - nodejs_version: "5"
    - nodejs_version: "4"
    - nodejs_version: "3"
    - nodejs_version: "2"
    - nodejs_version: "1"
    - nodejs_version: "0.12"
    - nodejs_version: "0.10"
    - nodejs_version: "0.8"
    - nodejs_version: "0.6"
matrix:
  # fast_finish: true
  allow_failures:
    - nodejs_version: "0.8"
    - nodejs_version: "0.6"

platform:
  - x86
  - x64

# Install scripts. (runs after repo cloning)
install:
 # Get the latest stable version of Node.js or io.js
 - ps: Install-Product node $env:nodejs_version $env:platform
 - IF %nodejs_version% EQU 0.6 npm config set strict-ssl false && npm -g install npm@1.3
 - IF %nodejs_version% EQU 0.8 npm config set strict-ssl false && npm -g install npm@1.4.28 && npm install -g npm@4.5
 - set PATH=%APPDATA%\npm;%PATH%
 #- IF %nodejs_version% NEQ 0.6 AND %nodejs_version% NEQ 0.8 npm -g install npm
 # install modules
 - npm install

# Post-install test scripts.
test_script:
 # Output useful info for debugging.
 - node --version
 - npm --version
 # run tests
 - npm run tests-only

===== ./rider_app/node_modules/reusify/.github/workflows/ci.yml =====
name: ci

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  legacy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: ['0.10', '0.12', 4.x, 6.x, 8.x, 10.x, 12.x, 13.x, 14.x, 15.x, 16.x]

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}

      - name: Install
        run: |
          npm install --production && npm install tape

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          npm run test

  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [18.x, 20.x, 22.x]

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}

      - name: Install
        run: |
          npm install

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          npm run test:coverage

  types:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Install
        run: |
          npm install

      - name: Run types tests
        run: |
          npm run test:typescript

  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Install
        run: |
          npm install

      - name: Lint
        run: |
          npm run lint

===== ./rider_app/node_modules/xml2js/node_modules/xmlbuilder/appveyor.yml =====
environment:
  matrix:
    - nodejs_version: "4"
    - nodejs_version: "5"
    - nodejs_version: "6"
    - nodejs_version: "8"
    - nodejs_version: "10"
    - nodejs_version: "" # latest

install:
  - ps: "Install-Product node $env:nodejs_version"
  - "npm install"

test_script:
  - "node --version"
  - "npm --version"
  - "npm test"

build: off
